code_good:
subdomain = "europe" if target == "EU" else "world"
resp = requests.get("https://" + subdomain + ".example.com/data/")

subject: SQL query construction with JSON-marshaled data in string interpolation
truth: Stop using fmt.Sprintf with string interpolation to embed JSON-marshaled data into SQL expressions; use placeholder syntax (e.g., sq.Expr("md5(?)", versionJSON)) instead.
quote: JSON encoding does not escape single quotes in any way, so this code is vulnerable
code_bad: sq.Expr(fmt.Sprintf("md5('%s')", versionJSON))
code_good: sq.Expr("md5(?)", versionJSON)

subject: Runtime.exec with string concatenation
truth: Stop building command lines by concatenating user input into a single string passed to exec; use an array of strings instead so user input is treated only as an argument.
quote: user input might include special characters such as ampersands

subject: Exception error message exposure in HTTP responses
truth: Stop sending exception messages (ex.getMessage()) back to the remote user in HTTP error responses; log the exception server-side and send a generic error message instead.
quote: printing a exception message back to the response

subject: Wildcard injection in subprocess/shell calls
truth: Stop passing bare '*' wildcards to shell commands like chown, chmod, tar, or rsync; use explicit file lists or escape the wildcard to prevent symlink/trojan-file attacks.
quote: Possible wildcard injection in call: subprocess.Popen
code_bad: subp.Popen('/bin/chown *', shell=True)
code_good: subp.Popen(['/bin/chown', '*'])

subject: CSP meta tag limitations
truth: When using CSP via a meta tag, you lose framing protections (frame-ancestors), sandboxing, and CSP violation logging endpoints; use the HTTP header instead when possible.
quote: However, you will not be able to use [framing protections](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Content-Security-Policy/frame-ancestors), [sandboxing](https://developer.mozilla.or
code_bad: <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" content="...">
code_good: Content-Security-Policy: ...
source: OWASP CS: Content Security Policy Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Java regex dot matches newlines
truth: Stop using Pattern.compile(regex) without the DOTALL flag when validating access paths; use Pattern.compile(regex, Pattern.DOTALL) so the dot matches newline characters.
quote: By default, a "dot" (.) in a regular expression matches all characters except the newline characters \n and \r.
code_bad:
Pattern p = Pattern.compile(PROTECTED_PATTERN);
Matcher m = p.matcher(path);
code_good:
Pattern p = Pattern.compile(PROTECTED_PATTERN, Pattern.DOTALL);
Matcher m = p.matcher(path);

subject: Rust std library before main
truth: The std library only guarantees stability between the beginning and end of main; calling it from #[ctor] (before main) or #[dtor] (after main) causes undefined behavior.
quote: the std library only guarantees stability and portability between the beginning and the end of main

subject: iOS text input masking for sensitive data
truth: Use UITextField with isSecureTextEntry = true (UIKit) or SecureField (SwiftUI) for passwords, PINs, and OTPs; do not use a plain TextField even if styled to look masked.
quote: Set `isSecureTextEntry` to `true` on any `UITextField` that captures sensitive data.
code_bad: let passwordField = UITextField()
code_good:
let passwordField = UITextField()
passwordField.isSecureTextEntry = true

subject: void* pointer arithmetic as GNU extension
truth: Prefer char* to void* for byte-level pointer arithmetic, since pointer arithmetic on void* is a nonstandard GNU extension and may silently misbehave on other compilers.
quote: pointer arithmetic on void * is a nonstandard GNU extension
code_bad: return *(voidPointer + i);
code_good: return *(intPointer + i);

subject: OAuth 2.0 state parameter
truth: Stop using a constant or static string as the OAuth 2.0 state value; generate a unique, non-guessable random state per request and validate it in the redirect callback.
quote: Always include a unique, non-guessable state value

subject: ProcessBuilder environment variable names from user input
truth: Stop using user-controlled strings as environment variable names; validate both the name and value against a whitelist before adding them to the subprocess environment.
quote: an environment variable is set with a name that is derived from the user input var without validation

associative: set env var from HTTP request

subject: paramiko exec_command with user-controlled input
truth: Stop passing untrusted user input directly to paramiko SSHClient.exec_command(); implement proper authorization (e.g., JWT validation) before executing remote commands.
quote: the exec_command is controlled by the user and hence leads to a vulnerability
code_bad:
#!/usr/bin/env python

from flask import request, Flask
import paramiko
from paramiko import SSHClient

app = Flask(__name__)
paramiko_ssh_client = SSHClient()
paramiko_ssh_client.load_system_host_keys()
paramiko_ssh_client.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
paramiko_ssh_client.connect(hostname="127.0.0.1", port="22", username="ssh_user_name", pkey="k", timeout=11, banner_timeout=200)


@app.route('/external_exec_command_1')
def withoutAuthorization():
    user_cmd = request.args.get('command')
    stdin, stdout, stderr = paramiko_ssh_client.exec_command(user_cmd)
    return stdout

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.debug = False
    app.run()
code_good:
#!/usr/bin/env python

from flask import request, Flask
import paramiko
from paramiko import SSHClient

app = Flask(__name__)
paramiko_ssh_client = SSHClient()
paramiko_ssh_client.load_system_host_keys()
paramiko_ssh_client.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy())
paramiko_ssh_client.connect(hostname="127.0.0.1", port="22", username="ssh_user_name", pkey="k", timeout=11, banner_timeout=200)


@app.route('/external_exec_command_1')
def withAuthorization():
    user_cmd = request.args.get('command')
    auth_jwt = request.args.get('Auth')
    # validating jwt token first
    # .... then continue to run the command
    stdin, stdout, stderr = paramiko_ssh_client.exec_command(user_cmd)
    return stdout


if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.debug = False
    app.run()

subject: Log injection via taint analysis (G706)
truth: Stop using user-controlled strings as the format argument to log.Printf; use structured logging with key-value pairs or safe format verbs like %d after type conversion.
quote: log.Printf("User logged in: %s", username)
code_bad: log.Printf("User logged in: %s", username)
code_good: log.Printf("Processing ID: %d", num)

subject: scanf family functions without length specifier
truth: Always specify a length within the format string parameter of scanf-family functions, making it one less than the buffer size to reserve space for the NULL terminator.
quote: It is bad practice to use any of the scanf functions without including a specified length within the format parameter, as it will be vulnerable to buffer overflows.
code_bad:
char buf1[10];
scanf("%s", buf1);

subject: Java NewCookie.toString() loses HttpOnly
truth: Do not rely on NewCookie.toString() to preserve HttpOnly; either append ";HttpOnly" after toString() or use the NewCookie constructor's boolean parameter for HttpOnly.
quote: Set a sensitive cookie header using the class `javax.ws.rs.core.Cookie` with the `HttpOnly` flag set through string concatenation
code_bad: response.setHeader("Set-Cookie", new NewCookie("session-access-key", accessKey, "/", null, null, 0, true).toString());
code_good: response.setHeader("Set-Cookie", new NewCookie("session-access-key", accessKey, "/", null, null, 0, true) + ";HttpOnly");

subject: SSL/TLS hostname verification via setEndpointIdentificationAlgorithm
truth: Always call SSLParameters.setEndpointIdentificationAlgorithm("HTTPS") after creating an SSLEngine or SSLSocket, otherwise hostname verification is disabled by default.
quote: When SSLSocket or SSLEngine are created without a secure setEndpointIdentificationAlgorithm, hostname verification is disabled by default
code_bad:
SSLContext sslContext = SSLContext.getInstance("TLS");
SSLEngine sslEngine = sslContext.createSSLEngine();  //BAD: No endpointIdentificationAlgorithm set

subject: Java temp directory creation with Guava Files.createTempDir
truth: Stop using com.google.common.io.Files.createTempDir() which creates world-readable directories (CVE-2020-8908); use java.nio.file.Files.createTempDirectory() instead.
quote: com.google.common.io.Files.createTempDir(); // BAD: CVE-2020-8908: Directory has permissions `drwxr-xr-x`
code_bad: File tempDir = com.google.common.io.Files.createTempDir(); // BAD: CVE-2020-8908: Directory has permissions `drwxr-xr-x`
code_good: Path temp2 = Files.createTempDirectory("random-directory"); // GOOD: File has permissions `drwx------`

subject: SSRF via user-controlled URI in HttpClient
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly into HttpRequest.newBuilder(URI). Validate the URI against a whitelist of known fixed strings before building the request.
quote: a request parameter is incorporated without validation into a Http request

associative: build HTTP request from user input

subject: DexClassLoader with world-writable path
truth: Stop loading Dex files from world-writable external storage like /sdcard; load them from the app's private storage directory (getCacheDir() or getFilesDir()) instead.
quote: It is dangerous to load Dex libraries from shared world-writable storage spaces

subject: Android package namespace squatting defense
truth: Do not trust package name alone for cross-app class loading; always call PackageManager.checkSignatures() and verify the result equals PackageManager.SIGNATURE_MATCH.
quote: Verify the package signature in addition to the package name before loading any classes or code from another application
code_bad: if (p.packageName.startsWith("some.package.")) {

subject: Weak hash for sensitive data
truth: Do not use MD5 or SHA-1 to hash sensitive data like passwords, certificates, or usernames; use Argon2, scrypt, bcrypt, PBKDF2 for passwords, or SHA-2/SHA-3 otherwise.
quote: Using a broken or weak cryptographic hash function can leave data vulnerable
code_bad: let digest = Insecure.MD5.hash(data: data) // BAD: MD5 is not suitable for hashing sensitive data.
code_good: let digest = SHA512.hash(data: data) // GOOD: SHA-512 is suitable for hashing sensitive data.

subject: Regex injection in Swift
truth: Stop constructing regular expressions with unsanitized user input; use NSRegularExpression.escapedPattern(for:) to escape meta-characters before embedding user input.
quote: Constructing a regular expression with unsanitized user input is dangerous
code_bad: let regex1 = try Regex(remoteInput)
code_good: let regex1 = try Regex(myRegex)

subject: Uncontrolled Recursion
truth: Always ensure a recursive function modifies its termination condition before recursing, or check recursion depth against a safe limit and exit with error if exceeded.
quote: Ensure that an end condition will be reached under all logic conditions
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: integer overflow in malloc size multiplication
truth: When an integer multiplication overflows in C, the result wraps around and can be much smaller than intended, leading to a heap buffer overflow on subsequent writes.
quote: When an integer multiplication overflows in C, the result wraps around and can be much smaller than intended.
code_bad: char **root_node = (char **) malloc(factor * sizeof(char *));

subject: SSH HostKeyCallback
truth: Stop using ssh.InsecureIgnoreHostKey() in production; validate host keys against an allow list using ssh.FixedHostKey or a custom callback that rejects unknown keys.
quote: Not properly verifying the host key returned by a server provides attackers with an opportunity to perform a Machine-in-the-Middle (MitM) attack.

subject: Spaces in file paths causing shell misinterpretation
truth: Stop assuming file paths are shell-safe; spaces or special characters in paths like __dirname will be interpreted by the shell, causing unintended command execution.
quote: The shell command will, however, fail to work as intended if the absolute path of the script's directory contains spaces.
code_bad:
let cmd = "rm -rf " + path.join(__dirname, "temp");
cp.execSync(cmd); // BAD

subject: Prototype chain method injection via dynamic lookup
truth: Stop relying on hasOwnProperty alone; additionally check that the looked-up value is typeof 'function' to guard against prototype pollution or non-method properties.
quote: check that the method is an own property and not inherited from the prototype object

subject: UITextField isSecureTextEntry for password fields
truth: Stop setting isSecureTextEntry to false on password fields; set isSecureTextEntry = true to mask input and prevent third-party keyboards from capturing the password.
quote: The password field has isSecureTextEntry set to false.
code_bad:
alert.addTextField { tf in
    tf.placeholder = "Password"
    tf.isSecureTextEntry = false
    tf.accessibilityIdentifier = "password_field"
}
code_good:
alert.addTextField { tf in
    tf.placeholder = "OTP 1"
    tf.isSecureTextEntry = true
    tf.keyboardType = .numberPad
    tf.accessibilityIdentifier = "otp_1_field"
}

subject: Third-party keyboard exposure via insecure text fields
truth: When isSecureTextEntry is false, iOS allows third-party keyboards to capture typed input; set isSecureTextEntry = true to restrict input to the system keyboard only.
quote: iOS does not offer installed third-party (custom) keyboards for them
code_bad:
alert.addTextField { tf in
    tf.placeholder = "Password"
    tf.isSecureTextEntry = false
    tf.accessibilityIdentifier = "password_field"
}
code_good:
alert.addTextField { tf in
    tf.placeholder = "OTP 1"
    tf.isSecureTextEntry = true
    tf.keyboardType = .numberPad
    tf.accessibilityIdentifier = "otp_1_field"
}

subject: File path construction from user input
truth: Stop using user-controlled input directly in file path operations; validate that the normalized path is relative and contains no '..' components or path separators.
quote: Accessing files using paths constructed from user-controlled data can allow an attacker to access unexpected resources
code_bad:
path := r.URL.Query()["path"][0]

// BAD: This could read any file on the file system
data, _ := ioutil.ReadFile(path)
w.Write(data)

subject: Android component export default
truth: Stop relying on the default export behavior; explicitly set android:exported="false" on every component with an intent filter unless you truly need external access.
quote: Explicitly set the android:exported attribute for every component or use permissions to limit access to the component.
code_bad:
<activity>
            android:name=".Activity">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>
code_good:
<activity>
            android:name=".Activity">
            android:exported="false"
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>

subject: WebResourceResponse with unsanitized URI
truth: Stop using user-provided URLs directly in WebResourceResponse without path validation; always validate that the requested file path is not in a protected directory.
quote: Unsanitized user-provided URLs must not be used to serve a response directly

subject: SQL query construction with % formatting
truth: Stop building SQL queries with string interpolation (% formatting); use parameterized queries with a tuple of parameters as the second argument to cursor.execute().
quote: Possible SQL injection vector through string-based query construction
code_bad: cur.execute("SELECT * FROM foo WHERE id = '%s'" % identifier)
code_good: cur.execute("SELECT * FROM foo WHERE id = '%s'", identifier)

subject: virtual patching
truth: Do NOT create exploit-specific virtual patches that block only the exact known payload; instead create positive-security rules that validate allowed input patterns.
quote: You want to resist the urge to take the easy road and quickly create an **exploit-specific virtual patch**.
code_bad:
SecRule REQUEST_URI "@contains /wp-content/plugins/levelfourstorefront/scripts/administration/exportsubscribers.php" "chain,id:1,phase:2,t:none,t:Utf8toUnicode,t:urlDecodeUni,t:normalizePathWin,t:lowercase,block,msg:'Input Validation Error for \'reqID\' parameter.',logdata:'%{args.reqid}'"
  SecRule ARGS:/reqID/ "@pm '"
code_good:
SecRule REQUEST_URI "@contains /wp-content/plugins/levelfourstorefront/scripts/administration/exportsubscribers.php" "chain,id:2,phase:2,t:none,t:Utf8toUnicode,t:urlDecodeUni,t:normalizePathWin,t:lowercase,block,msg:'Input Validation Error for \'reqID\' parameter.',logdata:'%{args.reqid}'"
  SecRule ARGS:/reqID/ "!@rx ^[0-9]+$"
source: OWASP CS: Virtual Patching Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Command injection via sprintf + system with user input
truth: Stop passing user input directly into sprintf to build command strings for system(); use a shell-encoding library routine to escape the user-supplied string first.
quote: passes user input as part of a call to system or popen without escaping special elements

subject: Loop variable shadowing causing infinite loop
truth: Stop declaring local variables inside a loop that share the same name as the loop condition variable; this shadows the outer variable and creates an infinite loop.
quote: Using variables with the same name is dangerous.

subject: Path traversal via archive entry filenames
truth: Do not trust archive entry filenames (e.g., ZipArchiveEntry.FullName) as safe file paths; they can contain '..' or absolute paths that escape the target directory.
quote: Zip archives contain archive entries representing each file in the archive. These entries include a file path for the entry, but these file paths are not restricted and may contain unexpected special elements such as the directory traversal element (..)
code_bad:
string destFileName = Path.Combine(destDirectory, entry.FullName);
        entry.ExtractToFile(destFileName);

subject: LDAP query construction with string concatenation
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into LDAP DN strings and search filters; encode them using Encoder.LdapDistinguishedNameEncode and Encoder.LdapFilterEncode.
quote: User input used in DN (Distinguished Name) without encoding
code_bad: string ldapQuery = "LDAP://myserver/OU=People,O=" + organizationName;
code_good:
string safeOrganizationName = Encoder.LdapDistinguishedNameEncode(organizationName);
string safeLDAPQuery = "LDAP://myserver/OU=People,O=" + safeOrganizationName;

subject: Android certificate pinning with TrustManager
truth: Stop using HttpsURLConnection without a pinned TrustManager; initialize SSLContext with a TrustManager from a KeyStore loaded with only the necessary certificates.
quote: Certificate pinning implemented via a TrustManager
code_bad: URLConnection conn = new URL("https://example.com").openConnection();

subject: Stapler default method without annotation
truth: Always annotate Stapler web methods with @POST for state-changing actions; omitting the annotation allows safe HTTP requests which have no default CSRF protection.
quote: no HTTP request type is specified, so safe HTTP requests are allowed

subject: Conditional execution of authentication logic
truth: If conditional statements containing authentication code are based on user-controlled data, an attacker can bypass security by preventing that code from executing.
quote: If these statements contain important authentication or login code, and the decision about whether to execute this code is based on user-controlled data, it may be possible for an attacker to bypass security systems

subject: void return type in Spring @GetMapping
truth: Stop using void return type in @GetMapping methods with user-controlled path variables; add HttpServletResponse parameter to prevent view name resolution from URI.
quote: returns void, so view name is taken from URI

subject: User-controlled file path concatenation
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into file paths; normalize the path with path.resolve and fs.realpathSync, then verify it starts with a safe root directory.
quote: Accessing files using paths constructed from user-controlled data can allow an attacker to access unexpected resources.
code_bad: res.write(fs.readFileSync(ROOT + filePath, 'utf8'));

subject: Stored XSS via file names in HTML
truth: Stop concatenating uncontrolled stored values (like file names) directly into HTML; escape them with a library like escape-html before inserting into the response.
quote: Directly using uncontrolled stored value (for example, file names) to create HTML content without properly sanitizing the input first, allows for a cross-site scripting vulnerability.

subject: WebView allowContentAccess default
truth: Stop relying on the default value of allowContentAccess (true); explicitly set allowContentAccess = false to prevent content:// URI access from WebView JavaScript.
quote: `allowContentAccess` is intentionally not set to false to showcase the default behavior
code_bad: // allowContentAccess = false
code_good: allowContentAccess = false

subject: iOS Keychain biometric ACL flag
truth: Stop using kSecAccessControlBiometryAny for Keychain items that should become inaccessible when biometrics change; use kSecAccessControlBiometryCurrentSet instead.
quote: When it is required that the associated keychain item become inaccessible when changes are made to the biometric database (e.g., when a new fingerprint or face is added), the app must use the`kSecAcce
code_bad: kSecAccessControlBiometryAny
code_good: kSecAccessControlBiometryCurrentSet

subject: uncontrolled data arithmetic overflow
truth: Do not perform arithmetic on uncontrolled data without first checking the result will not overflow; use a guard like `if (n < INT_MAX-1000)` before the operation.
quote: Always guard against overflow in arithmetic operations on uncontrolled data

subject: Unchecked return value of init function
truth: When an initialization function returns a status code indicating failure, reading the local variable without checking that status code reads uninitialized memory.
quote: If the caller does not check the status code before reading the local variable, it may read unitialized memory

subject: Missing regex timeout in C#
truth: Stop using Regex without a timeout when processing untrusted input; set a timeout via the Regex constructor or AppDomain.SetData for REGEX_DEFAULT_MATCH_TIMEOUT.
quote: a timeout should be used to avoid a denial of service
code_bad: new Regex("^([a-z]*)*$").Match(userInput);

subject: Go html/template special types (HTML, HTMLAttr, JS, JSStr, CSS, Srcset, URL)
truth: Never use html/template special types (HTML, HTMLAttr, JS, JSStr, CSS, Srcset, URL) on untrusted user input; pass user data as plain string to preserve escaping.
quote: Using them on user-provided values allows for a cross-site scripting vulnerability
code_bad: tmpl.Execute(w, template.HTML(username))
code_good: tmpl.Execute(w, username)

subject: ProcessBuilder environment variables from user input
truth: Stop passing unvalidated user input directly into environment variables of a subprocess; validate the input against a whitelist or use hard-coded values instead.
quote: Passing unvalidated user input into the environment variables of a subprocess can allow an attacker to execute malicious code.

subject: JavaScript code construction via string concatenation
truth: Stop using string concatenation to build JavaScript code; escape dangerous characters like <, >, / using a character map before embedding user-controlled values.
quote: Using string concatenation to construct JavaScript code can be error-prone, or in the worst case, enable code injection
code_bad:
const assignment = `obj[${JSON.stringify(key)}]=42`;
return `(function(){${assignment}})`
code_good:
const assignment = `obj[${escapeUnsafeChars(JSON.stringify(key))}]=42`;
return `(function(){${assignment}})`

subject: Using fmt.Sprintf for SQL query construction
truth: Stop using fmt.Sprintf to build SQL query strings with any user-controlled input; use database/sql parameter placeholders (? or $1) and pass values as arguments.
quote: Format string without proper quoting
code_bad:
q := fmt.Sprintf("select * from foo where name = '%s'", os.Args[1])
rows, err := db.Query(q)

subject: Java ProcessBuilder command string concatenation
truth: Stop passing command and arguments as a single string to ProcessBuilder; pass the command and each argument as separate elements to prevent OS command injection.
quote: the command together with the arguments are passed as a one string, making easy to manipulate that expression and inject malicious strings.
code_bad: ProcessBuilder b = new ProcessBuilder("C:\DoStuff.exe -arg1 -arg2");
source: OWASP CS: Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: PAM expired account bypass
truth: Stop relying solely on pam_authenticate; a user with an expired account or expired password can still pass authentication and must be rejected by pam_acct_mgmt.
quote: pam_authenticate only verifies the credentials of a user. It does not check if a user has an appropriate authorization to actually login.

subject: Android ContentResolver with externally-provided URI
truth: Stop passing externally-provided URIs directly to ContentResolver operations; validate and normalize the URI path to block access to internal files like /data/.
quote: Externally-provided URI directly used in content resolution

subject: SQL query construction with string formatting (%)
truth: Stop using string formatting (%) to embed user input into SQL queries; use query parameters (cursor.execute with %s placeholder and separate arguments) instead.
quote: cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '%s'" % username)
code_bad: cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '%s'" % username)
code_good: cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = %s", username)

subject: Binding to all interfaces with 0.0.0.0
truth: Stop binding to '0.0.0.0' which opens the service on all network interfaces; bind to a specific IP address like '127.0.0.1' or a documented internal IP instead.
quote: Binding to all network interfaces can potentially open up a service to traffic on unintended interfaces

subject: Recursive function with unmodified termination condition
truth: Always modify the termination condition inside the recursive function so the base case is reached; never call recursion with the same unchanged condition value.
quote: flg is never modified so it is always TRUE - this call will continue until the stack explodes
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: integer arithmetic on user-controlled data
truth: Always guard against overflow in arithmetic operations on user-controlled data by validating input or checking the result fits before performing the operation.
quote: Performing calculations on user-controlled data can result in integer overflows unless the input is validated.

subject: CDN script inclusion from untrusted domain
truth: Stop loading scripts from untrusted CDN domains like polyfill.io; instead load from a trusted CDN with a pinned version and subresource integrity (SRI) hashes.
quote: Including a resource from an untrusted source or using an untrusted channel may allow an attacker to include arbitrary code in the response.
code_bad: <script src="https://cdn.polyfill.io/v2/polyfill.min.js" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>
code_good: <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/polyfill/v3/polyfill.min.js?version=4.8.0" integrity="sha384-i0IGVuZBkKZqwXTD4CH4kcksIbFx7WKFMdxN8zUhLFHpLdELF0ym0jxa6UvLhW8/ sha384-3d4jRKquKl90C9aFG+eH4lPJmtbPHgACWHrp+VomFOxF8lzx2jxqeYkhpRg18UWC" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>

subject: Path prefix check bypass with ../ sequences
truth: Stop checking path prefixes with hasPrefix on raw strings; normalize the path with FilePath.lexicallyNormalized() before comparing against a known safe prefix.
quote: a malicious response could contain a file name containing special characters

subject: tarfile.extractall() without members or filter
truth: Stop using tarfile.extractall() without a members filter or filter parameter; pass members=members_filter(tar) or filter='data' to prevent directory traversal.
quote: * ``tarfile.extractall()`` - members from the archive is trusted - HIGH
code_bad: tar.extractall(path=tempfile.mkdtemp())
code_good: tar.extractall(path=tempfile.mkdtemp(), members=members_filter(tar))

subject: tarfile.extractall() with filter='fully_trusted'
truth: Stop using filter='fully_trusted' on tarfile.extractall() unless you have verified the archive source; use filter='data' to strip dangerous members by default.
quote: def filter_fully_trusted_archive_handler(filename):
code_bad: tar.extractall(path=tempfile.mkdtemp(), filter="fully_trusted")
code_good: tar.extractall(path=tempfile.mkdtemp(), filter="data")

subject: Function argument as HTTP request URL
truth: Stop passing a URL as a function argument to http.Get() unless the caller only supplies constant values; a function argument is not considered a secure source.
quote: An url provided as a function argument is not secure
code_bad:
func get(url string) {
	resp, err := http.Get(url)

subject: TLS VerifyPeerCertificate without VerifyConnection
truth: Stop setting VerifyPeerCertificate without also setting VerifyConnection; TLS session resumption bypasses VerifyPeerCertificate when VerifyConnection is unset.
quote: TLS resumption may bypass VerifyPeerCertificate when VerifyConnection is unset
code_bad: VerifyPeerCertificate: func(_ [][]byte, _ [][]*x509.Certificate) error { return nil }
code_good:
VerifyPeerCertificate: func(_ [][]byte, _ [][]*x509.Certificate) error { return nil },
		VerifyConnection:      func(_ tls.ConnectionState) error { return nil }

subject: Android Content Provider exported without permission
truth: Stop exporting content providers with android:exported="true" without android:permission; set android:exported="false" or define proper read/write permissions.
quote: the application exports two content providers
code_bad:
<provider
      android:authorities="com.mwr.example.sieve.FileBackupProvider"
      android:exported="true"
      android:multiprocess="true"
      android:name=".FileBackupProvider"
/>
code_good:
<provider
      android:authorities="com.mwr.example.sieve.DBContentProvider"
      android:exported="true"
      android:multiprocess="true"
      android:name=".DBContentProvider">
    <path-permission
          android:path="/Keys"
          android:readPermission="com.mwr.example.sieve.READ_KEYS"
          android:writePermission="com.mwr.example.sieve.WRITE_KEYS"
     />
</provider>

subject: JavaScript context after URL context in href
truth: When untrusted data passes through a javascript: protocol handler in an href attribute, encode for both JavaScript and URL contexts using server-side encoding.
quote: In the above example, untrusted data started in the rendering URL context (`href` attribute of an `a` tag) then changed to a JavaScript execution context (`javascript:` protocol handler) which passed
code_bad: Click Me
code_good: Click Me
source: OWASP CS: DOM based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Writing sensitive data to file in C
truth: Stop writing cleartext credentials to a file with fputs; encrypt them first using a library like libsodium's crypto_secretbox_easy before writing with fwrite.
quote: write password to disk in cleartext

subject: Android Fragment Injection
truth: Stop instantiating fragments from user-provided names without validation; use static fragment instantiation or validate the fragment name against a whitelist.
quote: Fragment instantiated from user input without validation
code_good:
getFragmentManager().beginTransaction()
        .replace(com.android.internal.R.id.prefs, new MyFragment()).commit();

subject: Authentication check using unsalted cookie values
truth: Stop comparing unsalted cookie values against user-controlled parameters for authentication; use cryptographically signed cookies (req.signedCookies) instead.
quote: an attacker can craft their cookie values to match those of any user

subject: SSRF guard using private-ip package
truth: Stop using private-ip to validate hosts for SSRF guards; use ipaddr.js with .range() which is transition-aware and detects IPv4-mapped, NAT64, and 6to4 forms.
quote: `private-ip` classifies the textual IPv4 form only, so it returns false

subject: ssl.wrap_socket default protocol
truth: Stop using ssl.wrap_socket with no explicit protocol version; specify ssl_version=PROTOCOL_TLSv1_2 or use ssl.create_default_context with minimum_version set.
quote: The ssl.wrap_socket function defaults to an insecure version of SSL/TLS when no specific protocol version is specified.
code_bad: ssl.wrap_socket(socket.socket())
code_good:
context = ssl.create_default_context()
context.minimum_version = ssl.TLSVersion.TLSv1_2

subject: URL host substring check
truth: Stop checking if a URL string or its host contains an allowed domain as a substring; parse the URL and compare the host against an explicit whitelist instead.
quote: treating the URL as a string and checking if one of the allowed hosts is a substring of the URL is very prone to errors
code_bad:
class AppController < ApplicationController
    def index
        url = params[:url]
        # BAD: the host of `url` may be controlled by an attacker
        if url.include?("example.com")
            redirect_to url
        end
    end
end
code_good:
class AppController < ApplicationController
    def index
        url = params[:url]
        host = URI(url).host
        # GOOD: the host of `url` can not be controlled by an attacker
        allowedHosts = [
            'example.com',
            'beta.example.com',
            'www.example.com'
        ]
        if allowedHosts.include?(host)
            redirect_to url
        end
    end
end

associative: ensure url goes to my domain

subject: Unsafe Reflection with Access Control Bypass
truth: Do not use user-controlled input with Class.forName() even if access control checks are added afterward; the attacker can still instantiate arbitrary classes.
quote: attacker could supply values to select unexpected classes or methods
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: reflectively instantiate a class with access control
instantiate a class from user input safely
use newInstance with a request parameter

subject: ProcessInlineSchema flag in XmlReaderSettings
truth: Do not include ProcessInlineSchema in ValidationFlags when validating user-provided XML, as it lets an attacker supply their own schema to bypass validation.
quote: //      option is specified, so an attacker can provide their own schema to validate

subject: Spring CSRF protection for state-changing endpoints
truth: Stop using safe HTTP methods (GET, HEAD, etc.) for state-changing actions; use unsafe methods (POST, DELETE, etc.) that Spring protects from CSRF by default.
quote: a safe HTTP request like GET should not be used for a state-changing action

subject: XPath compile with concatenated user input
truth: Stop using xpath.compile with string-concatenated user input; pre-compile the query with variable references ($var) and resolve via setXPathVariableResolver.
quote: If user input must be included in an XPath expression, either sanitize the data or pre-compile the query and use variable references to include the user input.
code_bad:
XPathExpression expression2 = xpath.compile("/users/user[@name='" + user + "' and @pass='" + pass + "']");
isExist = (boolean)expression2.evaluate(doc, XPathConstants.BOOLEAN);

subject: Auth0 java-jwt decode vs verify pattern
truth: Stop treating JWT.decode() as sufficient; it performs no cryptographic verification — always pair decode with JWTVerifier.verify() before trusting any claim.
quote: Don't use information from a JWT without verifying that JWT.

subject: XPath variable replacement mechanism
truth: Use the xpath library's variable replacement mechanism ($varname in the expression, variables: { varname: value } in select) instead of string interpolation.
quote: embed the user input using the variable replacement mechanism offered by xpath

subject: XPath injection via string formatting
truth: Stop building XPath expressions with string concatenation or %-formatting using user input; use parameterized XPath queries with variable references instead.
quote: If an XPath expression is built using string concatenation, and the components of the concatenation include user input, it makes it very easy for a user to create a malicious XPath expression.
code_bad: r = tree.xpath("/tag[@id='%s']" % value)
code_good: r = tree.xpath("/tag[@id=$tagid]", tagid=value)

subject: GetConfigForClient returning VerifyPeerCertificate without VerifyConnection
truth: When returning a *tls.Config from GetConfigForClient that sets VerifyPeerCertificate, also set VerifyConnection or disable session tickets to prevent bypass.
quote: TLS resumption may bypass VerifyPeerCertificate when VerifyConnection is unset
code_good:
VerifyPeerCertificate: func(_ [][]byte, _ [][]*x509.Certificate) error { return nil },
		VerifyConnection:      func(_ tls.ConnectionState) error { return nil }

subject: BiometricPrompt with DEVICE_CREDENTIAL fallback for sensitive data
truth: Stop allowing DEVICE_CREDENTIAL fallback when protecting sensitive data with biometrics; use BIOMETRIC_STRONG alone to ensure biometric presence is required.
quote: Allows DEVICE_CREDENTIAL fallback

.setAllowedAuthenticators(
    BiometricManager.Authenticators.BIOMETRIC_STRONG or
    BiometricManager.Authenticators.DEVICE_CREDENTIAL
)
code_good: .setAllowedAuthenticators(BIOMETRIC_STRONG)

subject: Password hashing without salt
truth: Stop hashing passwords without a salt; use a long random salt of at least 32 bytes and update the MessageDigest with the salt before digesting the password.
quote: Without a salt, it is much easier for attackers to pre-compute the hash value using dictionary attack techniques such as rainbow tables to crack passwords.
code_bad:
MessageDigest md = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256");
byte[] messageDigest = md.digest(password.getBytes());

subject: forwarding command-line arguments to child_process.exec
truth: Stop using child_process.exec/execSync with concatenated command-line arguments; use child_process.execFile/execFileSync with an array of arguments instead.
quote: Forwarding command-line arguments to child_process.exec or some other library routine that executes a system command within a shell can change the meaning of the command unexpectedly due to unescaped special characters.
code_bad: cp.execSync(`node ${script} ${args.join(' ')}`);
code_good: cp.execFileSync('node', [script].concat(args));

subject: unsafe recursive merge function
truth: Stop recursively merging properties without checking that they are own properties of the destination object; check dst.hasOwnProperty(key) before recursing.
quote: Only merge or assign a property recursively when it is an own property of the destination object.

subject: iOS sensitive input on system keyboard
truth: Set isSecureTextEntry = true on UITextField/UITextView for sensitive fields to force the system keyboard and prevent third-party keyboard data exfiltration.
quote: iOS does not display third-party keyboards for secure fields

code_good:
let pinField = UITextField()
pinField.isSecureTextEntry = true

subject: DOM XSS - double encoding from HTML + JS encoding
truth: Stop double-encoding: when setting an HTML attribute in the DOM execution context, apply only JavaScript encoding, not HTML attribute encoding on top of it.
quote: The ESAPI.encoder().encodeForHTMLAttribute() is unnecessary and causes double-encoding

code_good: x.setAttribute("value", '<%=ESAPI.encoder().encodeForJavascript(companyName)%>');
source: OWASP CS: DOM based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Open redirect without whitelist
truth: Maintain a list of authorized redirects on the server and choose from that list based on user input; avoid putting user input directly into a redirect URL.
quote: maintain a list of authorized redirects on the server; then choose from that list based on the user input provided
code_bad: ctx.Response.Redirect(ctx.Request.QueryString["page"]);

subject: Path normalization for zip extraction
truth: Do normalize the combined output path with file.toPath().normalize() and verify it starts with the destination directory path before writing any zip entry.
quote: The recommended way of writing an output file from a zip archive entry is to verify that the normalized full path of the output file starts with a prefix that matches the destination directory.
code_bad:
File file = new File(destinationDir, entry.getName());
FileOutputStream fos = new FileOutputStream(file); // BAD

subject: Android TextView displaying sensitive data
truth: Stop displaying sensitive information like passwords immediately in a TextView; set the view to INVISIBLE first and only reveal it on explicit user action.
quote: sensitive information in password is exposed to the TextView
code_bad:

pwView.setText("Your password is: " + password); // BAD: password is shown immediately

subject: SSLContext default protocol in Python
truth: Stop using ssl.SSLContext(ssl_version=...) with weak protocol constants; use ssl.create_default_context() and set minimum_version = ssl.TLSVersion.TLSv1_2.
quote: All versions of SSL, and TLS versions 1.0 and 1.1 are known to be vulnerable to attacks.

code_good:

context.minimum_version = ssl.TLSVersion.TLSv1_2

subject: Bank account creation without authentication
truth: Always authenticate the user before allowing creation of critical resources like bank accounts; check authentication status before executing the operation.
quote: does not perform any authentication for functionality that requires a provable user identity

subject: Authentication attempt limiting
truth: Implement a counter to limit failed authentication attempts and disconnect the user after a small number of failures instead of allowing unlimited retries.
quote: does not implement sufficient measures to prevent multiple failed authentication attempts within a short time frame

subject: Shell Command Injection despite input validation
truth: When validating filenames for shell commands, use an allowlist regex anchored to the full string (^...$) to prevent bypass via embedded special characters.
quote: does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements

subject: C++ exception handler memory cleanup
truth: Always check that a pointer is non-null before deleting it in a catch block, because the allocation may not have occurred before the exception was thrown.
quote: When releasing memory in a catch block, be sure that the memory was allocated and has not already been released.

subject: PreparedStatement vs Statement for SQL queries
truth: Always use PreparedStatement with parameterized queries (? placeholders) instead of Statement with string concatenation, even when inputs seem controlled.
quote: use a prepared query

subject: Rhino initStandardObjects vs initSafeStandardObjects
truth: Stop using ctx.initStandardObjects() with user-controlled scripts; use ctx.initSafeStandardObjects() or set a ClassShutter to restrict accessible classes.

subject: Certificate revocation checking disabled
truth: Do not disable certificate revocation checking by passing false to PKIXParameters.setRevocationEnabled() without providing a custom PKIXRevocationChecker.
quote: it may be disabled by passing false to the PKIXParameters.setRevocationEnabled() method

subject: Shell argument separation
truth: Stop passing dynamic file paths as part of a shell command string; pass them as separate arguments to execFileSync or spawn to avoid shell interpretation.
quote: provide the directory as an argument that does not get interpreted by a shell

cp.execSync(cmd); // BAD

subject: Overwriting toString via prototype pollution
truth: Stop allowing user input to set object properties without sanitization; an attacker can overwrite toString and crash the application on any type coercion.
quote: replace the toString property of an object with a primitive. Whenever toString is then called on that object, either explicitly or implicitly as part of a type coercion, an exception will be raised

subject: lodash.merge with URL-encoded query params

quote: some web frameworks, such as Express, parse query parameters using extended URL-encoding by default

subject: Hugging Face Hub downloads without revision pinning
truth: Always pin to a specific immutable commit hash when downloading from Hugging Face Hub, never rely on mutable tags or branch names like 'main' or 'v1.0.0'.
quote: Downloading models, datasets, or files without specifying a revision based on an immmutable revision (commit) can lead to supply chain attacks

)

subject: C++ range-based for loop with temporary container via function return
truth: Never pass a temporary container through a function that returns a reference in a range-based for loop; store the temporary in an rvalue reference first.
quote: The lifetime of the vector returned by `get_vector()` is not extended, and the behavior is undefined.

subject: scanf uninitialized memory read
truth: Stop reading scanf arguments without checking the return value; unassigned arguments contain indeterminate values and reading them is undefined behavior.
quote: reading one of the arguments not assigned a value is undefined behavior and may have unexpected consequences

  return i;

subject: Zeroed byte array as IV in GCM mode
truth: Do not initialize a byte array for an IV without filling it with random bytes; a zeroed IV is trivially predictable and breaks GCM's security guarantees.
quote: When a cipher is used in certain modes such as CBC or GCM, it requires an initialization vector (IV). Under the same secret key, IVs should be unique and ideally unpredictable.

subject: JHipster RandomUtil using RandomStringUtils without SecureRandom

code_good: return RandomStringUtils.random(DEF_COUNT, 0, 0, true, true, null, SECURE_RANDOM);

subject: dom4j XPath query with string concatenation
truth: Stop using document.selectSingleNode with string-concatenated XPath; use document.createXPath with SimpleVariableContext and variable references instead.
quote: The final two examples are for dom4j. They show an example of XPath injection and one method of preventing it.

subject: Denial of service via Ajv error object allocation
truth: Stop assuming allErrors: true only affects error reporting; it creates an unbounded memory allocation path that attackers can trigger with crafted input.
quote: An attacker can exploit this by sending an object that deliberately contains a huge number of errors

subject: URL logging of sensitive data
truth: Sensitive data in URLs gets logged in browser history, server logs, proxy logs, and leaks via the Referer header — stop putting secrets in query strings.
quote: Sensitive information within URLs may be logged in various locations, including the user's browser, the web server, and any forward or reverse proxy servers between the two endpoints

subject: Missing length validation on message body
truth: Stop trusting the message length without checking it against a maximum; validate that length is both positive and less than MAX_LENGTH before processing.
quote: Assume all input is malicious. Use an "accept known good" input validation strategy
code_good:
unsigned int length = getMessageLength(message[0]);
if ((length > 0) && (length < MAX_LENGTH)) {...}
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: loadFileURL allowingReadAccessTo overly broad directory
truth: Stop passing a broad directory to allowingReadAccessTo; restrict it to the specific file URL (indexURL) to prevent JavaScript from reading sibling files.
quote: demoRoot` grants WebKit native file access to the entire `demoRoot` directory, and `allowFileAccessFromFileURLs = true` independently grants JavaScript the ability to issue `fetch()` and `XMLHttpReque
code_bad: webView.loadFileURL(indexURL, allowingReadAccessTo: demoRoot)
code_good: webView.loadFileURL(indexURL, allowingReadAccessTo: indexURL)

subject: Hard-coded password comparison in C#
truth: Stop comparing passwords against hard-coded string literals; use a hashed password from a config file verified with PasswordHasher.VerifyHashedPassword.
quote: Inbound authentication made by comparison to string literal
code_bad: if (password == "myPa55word")
code_good: if (PasswordHasher.VerifyHashedPassword(hashedPassword, password))

subject: Array index validation after search-not-found sentinel
truth: Always validate that a search result index is >= 0 before using it to access an array; a sentinel value of -1 will cause ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
quote: foundProductID is used without checking whether the index is -1

subject: Authentication bypass via user-controlled cookie
truth: Never decide whether to authenticate a user based on data that may be controlled by that user; use server-side information based on credentials instead.
quote: Never decide whether to authenticate a user based on data that may be controlled by that user.

subject: prototype pollution via constructor.prototype
truth: Stop trusting that merge functions only copy own properties; an attacker can use constructor.prototype to reach Object.prototype even without __proto__.
quote: the built-in Object.prototype is usually reachable through the special properties __proto__ and constructor.prototype

subject: Ruby password hashing with SHA-256
truth: Stop using SHA-256 for password hashing; use Argon2 (via the argon2 gem) which is computationally expensive and includes a per-password salt by default.
quote: Although SHA-256 is a strong cryptographic hash function, it is not suitable for password hashing since it is not computationally expensive.

require 'openssl'

def get_password_hash(password, salt)
  OpenSSL::Digest.new('SHA256').digest(password + salt) # BAD
end
code_good:
require 'argon2'

def get_initial_hash(password)

end

def check_password(password, known_hash)
  Argon2::Password.verify_password(password, known_hash)
end

subject: Flask debug mode
truth: Stop using debug=True for convenience in development; use environment variables or config files to control debug mode and never let it reach production.
quote: Running Flask applications in debug mode results in the Werkzeug debugger being enabled
code_bad: app.run(debug=True)
code_good: app.run(debug=False)

subject: variable-based header assignment
truth: Even when assigning headers via a variable reference, setting both Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length on the same response creates a smuggling vector.

header.Set("Content-Length", "50")

w.Header().Set("Cache-Control", "no-cache")
w.Write([]byte("{}"))

subject: Node.js blocking operations with race conditions
truth: Do not perform synchronous operations after an async callback that depends on them; move all dependent operations inside the same non-blocking callback.
quote: Such race conditions can also affect the security of your application.
source: OWASP CS: NodeJS Security Cheat Sheet

subject: bcrypt null-byte truncation with pre-hashing
truth: Pre-hash the password with a fast hash and encode the output to a printable string (e.g. base64) before passing to bcrypt to avoid null-byte truncation.
quote: The original bcrypt expects a null terminated password string, this means that the hash value will only be used to the first null byte in the hash value.
code_bad: bcrypt(H($password)), $salt, $cost)
code_good: bcrypt(base64(H($password))), $salt, $cost)
source: OWASP CS: Password Storage Cheat Sheet

subject: mutex lock/unlock without RAII
truth: Stop calling mutex.lock() and mutex.unlock() directly; use RAII wrappers like std::lock_guard or a custom RAII class to guarantee release on all paths.
quote: The best way to ensure that locks are always unlocked is to use RAII

subject: Error message verbosity in web responses
truth: Stop sending detailed exception information (stack traces, file names, SQL code) to remote users; log details server-side and return a generic message.
quote: Send the user a more generic error message that reveals less information

ctx.Response.Write("Exception occurred");

subject: relative command path
truth: Stop relying on PATH resolution for system commands; hardcode the absolute path or use a configuration system that is itself secured against tampering.
quote: simply use a command that has an absolute path instead of a relative path
code_bad: Runtime.getRuntime().exec("make");
code_good: Runtime.getRuntime().exec(Paths.MAKE_PREFIX + "/bin/make");

subject: SQL injection via indirectly controlled input
truth: Even if input is not fully user-controlled, concatenating it into SQL is vulnerable because a separate bug or refactor could let the user influence it.
quote: Perhaps a separate vulnerability will allow the user to gain control of the component

subject: Implicit PendingIntent
truth: Always make the underlying Intent of a PendingIntent explicit by setting a destination component; never create a PendingIntent from an implicit Intent.
quote: Avoid creating implicit PendingIntents. This means that the underlying Intent should always have an explicit destination component.

subject: Java regex path access control with dot
truth: When using a dot in a regex for access control path validation, always compile the pattern with Pattern.DOTALL so the dot matches \n and \r characters.
quote: The Java Pattern Matcher API Pattern.compile(PATTERN, Pattern.DOTALL) with the DOTALL flag set can be adopted to address this vulnerability.

Pattern p = Pattern.compile(PROTECTED_PATTERN);
Matcher m = p.matcher(path);
code_good:

subject: SSRF via user-controlled URL path
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into URL paths; validate input with an allowlist (e.g., alphanumeric only) before using it in a network request.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into an HTTP request without validating the input can facilitate server side request forgery attacks

subject: Python ZipFile.extractall() decompression bomb
truth: Stop using ZipFile.extractall() on user-provided zip files; read decompressed data byte-by-byte with ZipFile.open() and enforce a total size threshold.
quote: python ZipFile library is vulnerable by default
code_good:
import zipfile


def safeUnzip(zipFileName):
    '''
    safeUnzip reads each file inside the zipfile 1 MB by 1 MB
    and during extraction or reading of these files it checks the total decompressed size
    doesn't exceed the SIZE_THRESHOLD
    '''
    buffer_size = 1024 * 1024 * 1  # 1 MB
    total_size = 0
    SIZE_THRESHOLD = 1024 * 1024 * 10  # 10 MB
    with zipfile.ZipFile(zipFileName) as myzip:
        for fileinfo in myzip.infolist():
            with myzip.open(fileinfo.filename, mode="r") as myfile:
                content = b''
                chunk = myfile.read(buffer_size)
                total_size += buffer_size
                if total_size > SIZE_THRESHOLD:
                    print("Bomb detected")
                    return False  # it isn't a successful extract or read
                content += chunk
                # reading next bytes of uncompressed data
                while chunk:
                    chunk = myfile.read(buffer_size)
                    total_size += buffer_size
                    if total_size > SIZE_THRESHOLD:
                        print("Bomb detected")
                        return False  # it isn't a successful extract or read
                    content += chunk

                # An example of extracting or reading each decompressed file here
                print(bytes.decode(content, 'utf-8'))
    return True  # it is a successful extract or read

subject: Package-level URL variable in HTTP request
truth: Stop declaring the URL for http.Get() as a package-level variable; declare it as a const or a function-scope variable initialized to a constant string.
quote: // Variable defined a package level can be changed at any time

subject: ssh.PublicKeyCallback returning nil error without validation
truth: Always validate the public key inside PublicKeyCallback and return nil error only if authorized; returning nil unconditionally bypasses authentication.
quote: Stateful misuse of ssh.PublicKeyCallback leading to auth bypass

subject: setAttribute with event handler names
truth: Stop using setAttribute(name_string, value_string) to set event handler attributes; set the event handler property directly on the DOM element instead.
quote: The `setAttribute(name_string,value_string)` method is dangerous because it implicitly coerces the *value_string* into the DOM attribute datatype of *name_string*.
code_good: document.getElementById("bb").onmouseover = \u0074\u0065\u0073\u0074\u0049\u0074;
