code_good: Test stub = (Test) UnicastRemoteObject.exportObject(obj, 0, csf, ssf);

subject: Fragment instantiation from user input
truth: Do not instantiate fragments with externally-provided names; validate the fragment name against a whitelist or use a static fragment reference.
quote: do not instantiate classes (including fragments) with user-provided names unless the name has been properly validated
code_good:
getFragmentManager().beginTransaction()
        .replace(com.android.internal.R.id.prefs, new MyFragment()).commit();

subject: DocumentBuilderFactory XML parsing
truth: Disable DTD parsing via factory.setFeature("http://apache.org/xml/features/disallow-doctype-decl", true) before calling parse on untrusted XML.
quote: most of them are vulnerable to XXE because their default settings enable parsing of external entities

subject: JSONP endpoint without request body enforcement
truth: Require a request body (POST content or multipart file) in JSONP endpoints to prevent cross-domain information leakage via simple GET requests.
quote: When these two methods process the request, there must be a request body in the request, which does not meet the conditions of Jsonp injection.

subject: Express.js template rendering with user-controlled objects
truth: Stop passing user-controlled objects directly to res.render(); construct the object explicitly with only the properties needed by the template.
quote: Directly using user-controlled objects as arguments to template engines might allow an attacker to do local file reads or even remote code execution.

subject: Math.random() for security-sensitive values
truth: Stop using Math.random() for security-sensitive values; use crypto.getRandomValues() in the browser or crypto.randomBytes() in Node.js instead.
quote: Using a cryptographically weak pseudo-random number generator to generate a security-sensitive value, such as a password, makes it easier for an attacker to predict the value.

subject: PAM authorization bypass
truth: Stop using only pam_authenticate for login checks; always call pam_acct_mgmt after pam_authenticate to verify the user is authorized to log in.
quote: A call to pam_authenticate should be followed by a call to pam_acct_mgmt to check if a user is allowed to login.
code_bad:
def authenticate(username, password, service='login'):
    def my_conv(n_messages, messages, p_response, app_data):
        """
        Simple conversation function that responds to any prompt where the echo is off with the supplied password
        """
        ...

    handle = PamHandle()
    conv   = PamConv(my_conv, 0)
    retval = pam_start(service, username, byref(conv), byref(handle))

    retval = pam_authenticate(handle, 0)
    return retval == 0
code_good:
def authenticate(username, password, service='login'):
    def my_conv(n_messages, messages, p_response, app_data):
        """
        Simple conversation function that responds to any prompt where the echo is off with the supplied password
        """
        ...

    handle = PamHandle()
    conv   = PamConv(my_conv, 0)
    retval = pam_start(service, username, byref(conv), byref(handle))

    retval = pam_authenticate(handle, 0)
    if retval == 0:
        retval = pam_acct_mgmt(handle, 0)
    return retval == 0

subject: unbounded regex input length causing DoS
truth: Limit input length to a reasonable maximum (e.g., 1000 characters) before applying a regex that may have polynomial worst-case time complexity.
quote: mitigate performance issues by limiting the length to 1000 characters, which will always finish in a reasonable amount of time

subject: SSRF via user-controlled subdomain
truth: Stop using user input to construct any part of a URL (scheme, host, port, path, query); an attacker can inject # or .. to redirect the request.
quote: By using evil.com# as the target value, the requested URL will be https://evil.com#.example.com/data/
code_bad: resp = requests.get("https://" + target + ".example.com/data/")
code_good:
subdomain = "europe" if target == "EU" else "world"
resp = requests.get("https://" + subdomain + ".example.com/data/")

subject: Ruby OpenSSL weak algorithm string
truth: Stop passing weak algorithm names like 'des', 'rc4', 'blowfish' to OpenSSL::Cipher.new; use the strong class alias OpenSSL::Cipher::AES128.new.
quote: Using broken or weak cryptographic algorithms may compromise security guarantees
code_bad: cipher = OpenSSL::Cipher.new('des') # BAD: weak encryption
code_good: cipher = OpenSSL::Cipher::AES128.new # GOOD: strong encryption

subject: Storing sensitive data in database with SQLx
truth: Stop storing sensitive data in cleartext in the database; encrypt each sensitive field (e.g., with AES-256-GCM) before binding it to the query.
quote: Sensitive information that is stored unencrypted in a database is accessible to an attacker who gains access to that database.

subject: Wildcard injection with os.system
truth: Stop passing bare '*' wildcards to os.system calls; use subprocess with argument lists or glob with explicit paths to avoid wildcard injection.
quote: Vulnerable to wildcard injection
code_bad: o.system('/bin/chown *')
code_good: subp.Popen(['/bin/chown', '*'])

subject: markupsafe.Markup with dynamic content
truth: Stop passing dynamic/untrusted content directly to markupsafe.Markup; interpolate the resulting Markup object or use markupsafe.escape instead.
quote: ``markupsafe.Markup`` does not perform any escaping, so passing dynamic content, like f-strings, variables or interpolated strings will potentially lead to XSS vulnerabilities
code_bad: Markup(f"unsafe {content}")
code_good: escape(content)

subject: SSRF via user-controlled URL in http.NewRequestWithContext
truth: Stop passing user-controlled or env-var URLs to http.NewRequestWithContext; use a hardcoded path or validate the full URL against an allowlist.
quote:
target := os.Getenv("IMPORT_URL")
	ctx := context.Background()
	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, target, http.NoBody)

subject: While loop with no upper bound on iterations
truth: Always validate that the loop decrement/increment value is positive and non-zero, and add a maximum iteration guard to prevent unbounded loops.
quote: while (inventoryCount > minimumCount) { inventoryCount = inventoryCount - rateSold; days++; }
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: DOM XSS - HTML attribute encoding in execution context
truth: In a DOM execution context, JavaScript-encode untrusted data before inserting into non-executing HTML attributes; do NOT HTML-attribute-encode.
quote: using HTML Attribute encoding in an execution context will break the application display of data
code_bad: x.setAttribute("value", '<%=ESAPI.encoder().encodeForJavascript(ESAPI.encoder().encodeForHTMLAttribute(companyName))%>');
code_good: x.setAttribute("value", '<%=ESAPI.encoder().encodeForJavascript(companyName)%>');
source: OWASP CS: DOM based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Java NewCookie constructor HttpOnly parameter
truth: Use the NewCookie(String, String, String, String, int, boolean, boolean) constructor with the last boolean parameter set to true for HttpOnly.
quote: Set a sensitive cookie header using the class `javax.ws.rs.core.Cookie` with the `HttpOnly` flag set through the constructor
code_bad: response.setHeader("Set-Cookie", new NewCookie("session-access-key", accessKey, "/", null, null, 0, true).toString());
code_good:
NewCookie accessKeyCookie = new NewCookie("session-access-key", accessKey, "/", null, null, 0, true, true);
response.setHeader("Set-Cookie", accessKeyCookie.toString());

subject: Static initialization vector (IV) in AES/GCM cipher
truth: Stop using a static or zeroed byte array as the initialization vector; use SecureRandom to generate a random IV for each encryption operation.
quote: If the same IV is used with the same secret key, then the same plaintext results in the same ciphertext.

subject: URL host substring check after parsing
truth: Even after parsing the host, do not use substring matching on the host value — an attacker can register a subdomain like example.com.evil.com.
quote: This is still not a sufficient check as the following URLs bypass it: http://evil-example.com http://example.com.evil-example.net

subject: Express.js CSRF protection
truth: Stop relying on cookie-based session authentication alone for state-changing POST requests; install and use a CSRF middleware like lusca.csrf.
quote: Use a middleware package such as lusca.csrf to protect against CSRF attacks.

subject: libxmljs XML parsing with noent option
truth: Stop using the `noent: true` option when parsing untrusted XML with libxmljs; omit it or set it to false to disable external entity expansion.
quote: the parser is invoked with the noent option set to true
code_bad:
let xmlSrc = req.body,
    doc = libxml.parseXml(xmlSrc, { noent: true });
code_good:
let xmlSrc = req.body,
    doc = libxml.parseXml(xmlSrc);

subject: XPath injection via string concatenation
truth: Stop building XPath expressions with string concatenation; use variable references ($varName) and pass variables in the select options object.
quote: If an XPath expression is built using string concatenation, and the components of the concatenation include user input, it makes it very easy for a user to create a malicious XPath expression.

subject: User-controlled data in shell commands
truth: Stop passing user-controlled data into shell command strings; use subprocess.run with an argument list to prevent arbitrary command execution.
quote: a malicious user to provide the input http://example.org; cat /etc/passwd in order to execute the command cat /etc/passwd

subject: Parameterized XPath with lxml
truth: Use lxml's variable reference syntax ($varname) and pass values as keyword arguments to tree.xpath() instead of embedding user input directly.
quote: use variable references to safely embed it without altering the structure of the expression
code_bad: r = tree.xpath("/tag[@id='%s']" % value)
code_good: r = tree.xpath("/tag[@id=$tagid]", tagid=value)

subject: Storing sensitive data to disk in Ruby
truth: Stop writing sensitive information (passwords, tokens, secrets) directly to files or logs in plain text; always mask or redact before storing.
quote: Sensitive information that is stored unencrypted is accessible to an attacker who gains access to the storage.
code_bad:
class UserSession
  def login(username, password)
    # ...
    logfile = File.open("login_attempts.log")
    logfile.puts "login with password: #{password})"
  end
end
code_good:
class UserSession
  def login(username, password)
    # ...
    password_escaped = password.sub(/.*/, "[redacted]")
    logfile = File.open("login_attempts.log")
    logfile.puts "login with password: #{password_escaped})"
  end
end

subject: gets() function
truth: Stop using gets() — it provides no way to limit input size and will overflow any buffer; replace with fgets() and specify the maximum length.
quote: The gets function provides no way to limit the amount of data that is read and stored

subject: C++ double-free in catch chain
truth: After deleting memory in a catch handler that rethrows, set the pointer to NULL to prevent a subsequent catch handler from double-freeing it.
quote: When releasing memory in a catch block, be sure that the memory was allocated and has not already been released.

subject: Spring implicit view manipulation with non-String return type
truth: Stop relying on implicit view name resolution from user-controlled URI paths; use @ResponseBody or @RestController to prevent SpEL injection.
quote: unrestricted view name manipulation vulnerability in Spring Framework could lead to attacker-controlled arbitrary SpEL expressions being evaluated

subject: Servlet exception propagation
truth: Do not declare servlet methods with 'throws IOException, ServletException' for user-controlled input paths; catch exceptions locally instead.
quote: Even though the request-handling methods of Servlet are declared throws IOException, ServletException, it's a bad idea to let such exceptions be thrown.

subject: URL host substring check
truth: Parse the URL and check the host against an explicit whitelist of allowed hosts; do not use substring matching on the raw URL string or host.
quote: treating the URL as a string and checking if one of the allowed hosts is a substring of the URL is very prone to errors

subject: Inherited method invocation via dynamic access
truth: Guard against invocation of inherited methods like toString and valueOf by using hasOwnProperty instead of a simple property existence check.
quote: It is also good practice to prevent invocation of inherited methods like toString and valueOf.
code_bad: window[message.name](message.payload);

subject: node-expat lacks entity expansion controls
truth: Stop using node-expat for untrusted XML; it provides no way to disable entity expansion, making it inherently vulnerable to XML bomb attacks.
quote: node-expat does not provide a way of controlling entity expansion

subject: Ruby module_eval with string interpolation for class variables and methods
truth: Stop using module_eval with string interpolation to set class variables and define methods; use class_variable_set and define_method instead.
quote: A safer approach is to use class_variable_set and class_variable_get along with define_method.
code_bad:
klass.module_eval <<-CODE
  @@#{name} = target

  def #{name}(*args)
    @@#{name}.#{name}(*args)
  end
CODE
code_good:
klass.class_variable_set(var, target)
klass.define_method(name) do |*args|
  self.class.class_variable_get(var).send(name, *args)
end

subject: Hard-coded credentials in Ruby (Rack)
truth: Stop comparing passwords against plaintext string literals; use hashed and salted passwords from a config file with OpenSSL::KDF.pbkdf2_hmac.
quote: Inbound authentication made by comparison to string literal
code_bad: if password == 'myPa55word'
code_good:
hash = OpenSSL::Digest::SHA256.new
dk = OpenSSL::KDF.pbkdf2_hmac(
  password, salt: salt, hash: hash, iterations: 100_000, length: hash.digest_length
)
hashed_input = dk.unpack('H*').first
if hashed_password == hashed_input

subject: typed except with pass
truth: Stop silently passing on typed exceptions like ZeroDivisionError; log the error or handle it, as silent suppression hides operational issues.
quote: suppress errors

subject: Django mark_safe with try/except flow
truth: Stop calling mark_safe() on variables that may have been assigned in exception handlers or else/finally blocks where user data could flow in.
quote: safestring.mark_safe(my_insecure_str)

subject: filepath.WalkDir callback with os.Remove(path)
truth: Stop using the WalkDir callback path argument directly in os.Remove; use os.OpenRoot to get a root handle and call root.Remove(path) instead.
quote: Filesystem TOCTOU race risk in filepath.Walk/WalkDir callbacks
code_bad: return os.Remove(path)
code_good: return root.Remove(path)

associative: remove all files in directory walk

subject: object[x] accessor with untrusted input
truth: Stop using untrusted input directly as an object property key via object[x] accessors; validate or restrict the key to a known set of values.
quote: ### GUIDELINE \#8 - Limit access to object properties when using object\[x\] accessors
code_bad:
var myMapType = {};
myMapType[<%=untrustedData%>] = "moreUntrustedData";
source: OWASP CS: DOM based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SqlParameter with dynamic SQL
truth: Always use SqlParameter objects with parameterized queries (using @named placeholders) instead of concatenating user input into SQL strings.
quote: The third way builds a query, query2, with a single string literal that includes a parameter (@category). The parameter is then given a value by calling Parameters.Add. This version is immune to injection attacks, because any special characters are not given any special treatment.
code_bad:
var query1 = "SELECT ITEM,PRICE FROM PRODUCT WHERE ITEM_CATEGORY='"
  + categoryTextBox.Text + "' ORDER BY PRICE";

subject: backslash escape in Go regexp string literals
truth: Use raw string literals (backticks) for Go regexp patterns to avoid escape sequence confusion between \b (backspace) and \b (word boundary).
quote: regexp.Compile("\b") matches a backspace, whereas regexp.Compile("\\b") matches the start of a word
code_bad: var hostRe = regexp.MustCompile("\bforbidden.host.org")
code_good: var hostRe = regexp.MustCompile(`\bforbidden.host.org`)

subject: Open redirect via // or /\ bypass
truth: Do not assume a URL starting with '/' is safe; browsers interpret '//example.com' and '/\example.com' as absolute URLs to arbitrary domains.
quote: browsers interpret URLs beginning with // or /\ as absolute URLs

subject: strconv.ParseInt with oversized bit size then downcast
truth: When using strconv.ParseInt, specify the bit size matching the target type instead of always using 64 and then converting to a smaller type.
quote: If the provided number is greater than the maximum value of type int32, the resulting value from the conversion will be different from the actual provided value.

subject: Android Intent Redirection
truth: Do not start an arbitrary component from a user-provided Intent without validating either the destination component or the origin component.
quote: A user-provided Intent is used to launch an arbitrary component
code_bad:
Intent forwardIntent = (Intent) getIntent().getParcelableExtra("forward_intent");
startActivity(forwardIntent);

subject: Auth0 java-jwt JWT verification
truth: Stop using JWT.decode() alone for authentication; always call JWT.require().build().verify() to verify the token before trusting its claims.
quote: NOT OK: only decode, no verification

subject: Allowlist validation for system prompt user input
truth: If user input must influence the system prompt, validate it against a fixed allowlist of permitted values before including it in the prompt.
quote: validate it against a fixed allowlist of permitted values

subject: Math.random() for password generation
truth: Stop using Math.random() to generate passwords, tokens, or any security-sensitive value; it is predictable and not cryptographically secure.
quote: The random number generator used (Math.random) is not cryptographically secure, so it may be possible for an attacker to predict the generated password.
code_bad:
var suffix = Math.random();
var password = "myPassword" + suffix;
code_good:
var suffix = window.crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint32Array(1))[0];
var password = "myPassword" + suffix;

subject: Express.js async/await error handling
truth: Use async/await with a top-level try/catch in request handlers instead of callbacks to prevent uncaught exceptions from crashing the server.
quote: switch to async/await syntax instead of using callbacks, which allows wrapping the entire request handler in a try/catch block

subject: SSRF from user-controlled URL construction in Flask
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly into requests.get() URL construction; validate the input or use an allowlist of authorized URLs.
quote: To guard against SSRF attacks you should avoid putting user-provided input directly into a request URL
code_bad: resp = requests.get("https://" + target + ".example.com/data/")
code_good:
subdomain = "europe" if target == "EU" else "world"
resp = requests.get("https://" + subdomain + ".example.com/data/")

subject: Stack trace exposure in Rails error handling
truth: Stop rendering exception backtraces directly to the HTTP response; log the backtrace server-side and render a generic error message instead.
quote: the exception is exposed to the remote user by rendering it as an HTTP response
code_bad:
def update_bad(id)
    do_computation()
  rescue => e
    # BAD
    render body: e.backtrace, content_type: "text/plain"
  end
code_good:
def update_good(id)
    do_computation()
  rescue => e
    # GOOD
    logger.error e.backtrace
    render body: "Computation failed", content_type: "text/plain"
  end

subject: SSRF via interpolated user input in URL string
truth: Stop interpolating user-controlled parameters directly into HTTP request URLs; maintain a server-side allowlist of authorized URLs or paths.
quote: avoid putting user-provided input directly into a request URL
code_bad:
users_service_domain = params[:users_service_domain]
response = Excon.post("#{users_service_domain}/logins", body: {user_id: user}).body
code_good:
path = if params[:users_service_path] == "v1/users"
         "v1/users"
       else 
         "v2/users"
       end
response = Excon.post("users-service/#{path}", body: {user_id: user}).body

subject: Regex injection via string interpolation in Swift
truth: Stop embedding user input directly into regex pattern strings via interpolation; escape with NSRegularExpression.escapedPattern(for:) first.
quote: use a sanitization function such as NSRegularExpression::escapedPattern(for:)
code_bad:
let regexStr = "abc|\(remoteInput)"
let regex2 = try NSRegularExpression(pattern: regexStr)

subject: iOS UITextField autocorrectionType default
truth: Stop using autocorrectionType = .default on text fields collecting sensitive data; set autocorrectionType = .no to prevent keyboard caching.
quote: - The name input has `autocorrectionType` set to `default`, making it eligible for keyboard caching.
code_bad: tf.autocorrectionType = .default
code_good: tf.autocorrectionType = .no

subject: Kotlin string interpolation in Log calls
truth: Stop using Kotlin string interpolation inside Log calls; string construction bytecode survives even when Log calls are stripped by ProGuard.
quote: the code that constructs the string may remain in the bytecode
code_bad: Log.v("Private key tag", "Private key [byte format]: $key")
code_good: SecureLog.v("Private key [byte format]: ", key)

subject: Universal Link URL validation in iOS
truth: Stop trusting webpageURL without validating its scheme and components; use NSURLComponents to parse and validate the URL before handling it.
quote: In addition, remember that if the URL includes parameters, they should not be trusted before being carefully sanitized and validated (even when coming from trusted domain).

subject: Docker .dockerignore for Node.js images
truth: Always include a .dockerignore file to prevent node_modules, .env, credentials, and sensitive files from being copied into the Docker image.
quote: /usr/src/app` from the 2nd Dockerfile stage, we’re also copying over any local node\_modules/ to the Docker image.
code_bad:
FROM node@sha256:b2da3316acdc2bec442190a1fe10dc094e7ba4121d029cb32075ff59bb27390a
WORKDIR /usr/src/app
COPY . /usr/src/app
RUN npm ci
CMD "npm" "start"
code_good:
.dockerignore
node_modules
npm-debug.log
Dockerfile
.git
.gitignore
source: OWASP CS: Node.js Docker Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: PHP escapeshellcmd allows argument injection via extra parameters
truth: Do not rely on escapeshellcmd() to prevent argument injection; it only prevents command chaining but allows extra parameters to be injected.
quote: `escapeshellcmd()` will still allow this extra parameter meaning the attacker can override the original `--directory-prefix` option, save the file in the current directory and then achieve remote comm
source: OWASP CS: OS Command Injection Defense Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Unvalidated redirect via user-supplied URL parameter
truth: Stop redirecting to user-supplied URL parameters without validation; use a whitelist of allowed destinations or an indirect mapping instead.
quote: The following Java code receives the URL from the parameter named `url` ([GET or POST](https://docs.oracle.com/javaee/7/api/javax/servlet/ServletRequest.html#getParameter-java.lang.String-)) and redir
code_bad: response.sendRedirect(request.getParameter("url"));
code_good: response.sendRedirect("http://www.mysite.com");
source: OWASP CS: Unvalidated Redirects and Forwards Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Bean Validation custom error messages
truth: Stop passing validated bean properties directly to buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate; escape them or use parameterized messages instead.
quote: Bean properties (normally user-controlled) are passed directly to `buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate`
code_bad: constraintContext.buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate(value).addConstraintViolation().disableDefaultConstraintViolation();
code_good:
String escaped = InterpolationHelper.escapeMessageParameter(value);
constraintContext.buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate(escaped).addConstraintViolation().disableDefaultConstraintViolation();

subject: Bean Validation EL interpolation
truth: Use HibernateConstraintValidatorContext.addMessageParameter() with template placeholders instead of string concatenation in error messages.
quote: Use parameterized messages instead of string concatenation
code_bad: constraintContext.buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate(value).addConstraintViolation().disableDefaultConstraintViolation();

subject: MVEL expression validation
truth: Stop assuming MVEL expressions are safe just because they parse; validate untrusted input with allow-lists or deny-lists before evaluation.
quote: the user-provided expression is validated before evaluation
code_bad: MVEL.eval(expression);
code_good:
validateExpression(expression);
MVEL.eval(expression);

subject: FLAG_IMMUTABLE on PendingIntent
truth: Use FLAG_IMMUTABLE when creating a PendingIntent to prevent the destination component from modifying empty fields of the underlying Intent.
quote: Create the PendingIntent using the flag FLAG_IMMUTABLE whenever possible, to prevent the destination component from modifying empty fields of the underlying Intent.

subject: MyBatis annotation parameter binding
truth: Always use #{xxx} for parameter binding in MyBatis annotations; ${xxx} performs raw string substitution and is vulnerable to SQL injection.
quote: use the syntax #{xxx} whenever possible
code_bad:
@Select("select * from test where name = ${name}")
public Test bad1(String name);
code_good:
@Select("select * from test where name = #{name}")
public Test good1(String name);

subject: Shell command construction with environment values
truth: Stop using cp.execSync with string concatenation for shell commands; use cp.execFileSync with separate command and arguments array instead.
quote: Dynamically constructing a shell command with values from the local environment, such as file paths, may inadvertently change the meaning of the shell command

subject: jQuery plugin option handling
truth: Stop passing user-controlled option values directly to jQuery(); use jQuery.find() instead to always interpret the value as a CSS selector.
quote: the call to jQuery interprets sourceSelector as HTML if it is a string that starts with <

subject: JSON.stringify as sole sanitizer for JS code injection
truth: Do not rely on JSON.stringify alone when embedding strings into JavaScript code; it does not escape </script> or other dangerous sequences.
quote: However, if key contains </script>, then the generated code will break out of a </script> if inserted into a </script> tag.
code_bad:
const assignment = `obj[${JSON.stringify(key)}]=42`;
return `(function(){${assignment}})`
code_good:
const assignment = `obj[${escapeUnsafeChars(JSON.stringify(key))}]=42`;
return `(function(){${assignment}})`

subject: Array.fill with user-controlled size
truth: Always validate and limit user-controlled array sizes before calling new Array(n).fill() to prevent memory exhaustion and unresponsiveness.
quote: the subsequent filling of the array will take a long time, causing the application to be unresponsive, or even run out of memory
code_bad: let dogs = new Array(size).fill("dog"); // BAD

subject: CrossOriginProtection bypass patterns
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input from query parameters directly to AddInsecureBypassPattern; use a fixed allowlist of safe path prefixes.
quote: Unsafe CrossOriginProtection bypass patterns
code_bad:
pattern := r.URL.Query().Get("bypass")
cop.AddInsecureBypassPattern(pattern)
code_good: cop.AddInsecureBypassPattern("/healthz")

subject: Logging sensitive data with android.util.Log
truth: Stop logging sensitive data with android.util.Log; remove all Log calls from production builds using ProGuard or a custom logging facility.
quote: Applications will often use the Log Class and Logger Class to create logs.
code_bad: Log.v("Private key tag", "Private key [byte format]: " + key);
code_good: SecureLog.v("Private key [byte format]: ", key);

subject: assigning event handlers with JavaScript-encoded strings
truth: Do not assign event handler properties to JavaScript-encoded strings; assign them to a function reference or a valid variable name instead.
quote: The following does NOT work because the event handler is being set to a string.
code_bad: document.getElementById("bb").onclick = "\u0061\u006c\u0065\u0072\u0074\u0028\u0037\u0029";
code_good: document.getElementById("bb").onmouseover = \u0074\u0065\u0073\u0074\u0049\u0074;
source: OWASP CS: DOM based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: recursive mutex locking
truth: Stop locking a non-recursive mutex inside a recursive function; split into a public locking wrapper and a private unlocked implementation.
quote: A non-recursive mutex cannot be locked until it has been unlocked by its previous owner

subject: SQL query construction via string concatenation
truth: Stop building SQL queries by concatenating string components; use PreparedStatement with parameterized queries (wildcards like ?) instead.
quote: The first way involves building a query, query1, by concatenating the result of getCategory with some string literals.

subject: Storing sensitive data in cookies or properties without encryption
truth: Stop storing sensitive information in cleartext in cookies, Properties, or any serializable object; always encrypt or hash before storage.
quote: store data in a cookie in cleartext form

subject: JWT handler with onPlaintextJwt override
truth: Stop overriding onPlaintextJwt in JwtHandlerAdapter which bypasses signature verification; override onPlaintextJws or onClaimsJws instead.
quote: The second 'BAD' case uses a JwtHandlerAdapter where the onPlaintextJwt method is overriden, so it will not validate the signature

subject: Spring view name from user input
truth: Stop returning user-controlled strings as view names from @Controller methods; annotate with @ResponseBody or use @RestController instead.
quote: unrestricted view name manipulation vulnerability in Spring Framework could lead to attacker-controlled arbitrary SpEL expressions being evaluated

subject: Spring URL redirect with user input
truth: Stop putting user input directly into a redirect URL; instead, validate the input against a server-side allowlist of authorized redirects.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into a URL redirect request without validating the input can facilitate phishing attacks.

subject: Express.js reflected XSS
truth: Stop writing user-controlled request parameters directly into res.send() without escaping; use escape-html or a contextual output encoder.
quote: a request parameter is incorporated without validation into the response
code_bad: res.send("Unknown user: " + req.params.id);
code_good: res.send("Unknown user: " + escape(req.params.id));

subject: Ajv allErrors: true for debugging in production
truth: Do not enable allErrors: true in production; gate it behind an environment variable like REST_DEBUG so it only activates during debugging.
quote: A solution is to not pass in allErrors: true, which means ajv will only report the first error, not all of them
code_bad: let ajv = new Ajv({ allErrors: true });
code_good: let ajv = new Ajv({ allErrors: process.env['REST_DEBUG'] });

subject: Express.js missing X-Frame-Options header
truth: Stop sending responses without setting X-Frame-Options; call res.set('X-Frame-Options', 'DENY') before every res.send/res.json/res.render.
quote: Set the X-Frame-Options HTTP header to DENY, to instruct web browsers to block attempts to load the site in a frame

subject: Using req.cookies for authentication decisions
truth: Stop using req.cookies for authentication decisions; use req.signedCookies to ensure cookie values are server-controlled and tamper-proof.
quote: Using user-controlled data in a permissions check may allow a user to gain unauthorized access

subject: lodash.merge with untrusted input
truth: Stop using lodash.merge (or similar unsafe merge functions) with untrusted input; use lodash >= 4.17.12 or switch to a safe merge library.
quote: use of an unsafe merge or extend function to recursively copy properties from an untrusted source object

subject: Hugging Face snapshot_download without commit pinning
truth: Always pin snapshot_download to an immutable commit hash revision, never use mutable branch names like 'main' or PR refs like 'refs/pr/1'.
quote: snapshot_download(repo_id="org/model_name", revision="main")
code_bad: unsafe_snapshot_no_revision = snapshot_download(repo_id="org/model_name")
code_good:
safe_snapshot_commit = snapshot_download(
    repo_id="org/model_name",
    revision="5d0f2e8a7f1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3b4c5d"
)

subject: markupsafe.Markup with percent-format strings
truth: Stop passing percent-formatted strings with dynamic content to markupsafe.Markup; use Markup.format() on an escaped Markup object instead.
quote: ``markupsafe.Markup`` does not perform any escaping, so passing dynamic content, like f-strings, variables or interpolated strings will potentially lead to XSS vulnerabilities
code_bad: flask.Markup("unsafe %s" % content)
code_good: Markup("safe {}").format(content)

subject: Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length ordering
truth: Do not set both Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length on a response regardless of header order; pick exactly one transfer-coding mechanism.
quote: conflicting headers
code_bad:
w.Header().Set("Content-Length", "100")
w.Header().Set("Transfer-Encoding", "chunked")
code_good:
w.Header().Set("Transfer-Encoding", "chunked")
w.Write([]byte("response body"))

associative: set content length then transfer encoding

subject: File path from tainted input (env var, query param, stdin)
truth: Stop passing unsanitized user/taint input directly to file I/O functions; sanitize the path with filepath.Clean() or filepath.Rel() first.
quote: File path provided as taint input
code_bad: body, err := ioutil.ReadFile(f)
code_good: _, err := os.OpenFile(filepath.Clean(filePath), os.O_RDONLY, 0600)

subject: XSS via unescaped user input in HTTP response
truth: Stop writing user-controlled input directly into HTTP responses without escaping HTML; use html.EscapeString() or equivalent sanitization.
quote: XSS via taint analysis
code_bad:
name := r.URL.Query().Get("name") fmt.Fprintf(w, "

Hello %s

", name)
code_good:
name := r.URL.Query().Get("name") fmt.Fprintf(w, "

Hello %s

", html.EscapeString(name))

subject: Unsafe Reflection in Java
truth: Do not use user-controlled input directly with Class.forName() to instantiate classes; use an allowlist or indirect selection map instead.
quote: Do not use user-controlled inputs to select and load classes or code
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: load a class by name from user input
use reflection with a request parameter
get a class from a string parameter
dynamically invoke a method by name
select a command class from a URL parameter

subject: Java deserialization of untrusted data
truth: Never deserialize untrusted data with ObjectInputStream.readObject(); use a safe validation mechanism or prevent deserialization entirely.
quote: deserializes untrusted data without sufficiently ensuring that the resulting data will be valid
code_good:
private final void readObject(ObjectInputStream in) throws java.io.IOException {
throw new java.io.IOException("Cannot be deserialized"); }
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: read an object from a file

subject: BiometricPrompt with DEVICE_CREDENTIAL fallback in Android
truth: Stop using DEVICE_CREDENTIAL or setDeviceCredentialAllowed(true) with BiometricPrompt for sensitive operations; use BIOMETRIC_STRONG only.
quote: Using DEVICE_CREDENTIAL allows fallback to PIN/pattern/password
code_bad:
val prompt1 = BiometricPrompt.Builder(context)
    .setTitle("Test 1: Device Credential")
    .setSubtitle("Using DEVICE_CREDENTIAL (Security: FAIL)")
    .setDescription("This allows also PIN/pattern/password authentication")
    .setAllowedAuthenticators(
        BIOMETRIC_STRONG or
        DEVICE_CREDENTIAL
    )
    .setDeviceCredentialAllowed(true)
    .build()
code_good:
val prompt2 = BiometricPrompt.Builder(context)
    .setTitle("Test 2: Biometric Strong")
    .setSubtitle("Using BIOMETRIC_STRONG (Security: PASS)")
    .setDescription("This only allows Class 3 biometrics")
    .setAllowedAuthenticators(BIOMETRIC_STRONG)
    .setNegativeButton("Cancel", context.mainExecutor) { _, _ ->
        Log.d("MASTG-TEST", "BIOMETRIC_STRONG authentication cancelled")
        authResult2 = "User cancelled authentication"
        latch2.countDown()
    }
    .build()

subject: SharedPreferences.getString() without integrity validation
truth: Stop loading data from SharedPreferences and trusting it directly without integrity validation; always verify HMAC before using the value.
quote: The implementation of `loadPlain(...)` reads from `SharedPreferences` and returns the value directly:

subject: Kotlin StringBuilder in Log calls
truth: Stop using StringBuilder inside Log calls in Kotlin; the string construction bytecode persists in release builds even after Log stripping.
quote: the (unused) string leaks plain text data into memory
code_bad: Log.v("Private key tag", StringBuilder("Private key [byte format]: ").append(key).toString())
code_good: SecureLog.v("Private key [byte format]: ", key)

subject: read() return value and null termination
truth: Check the return value of read() and insert a null terminator at the last valid position before using the buffer with C string functions.
quote: In addition to checking that the read succeeds (the condition count >= 0), a null terminator is inserted before the call to strcpy.

subject: Out-of-bounds pointer dereference
truth: Ensure that pointer dereferences are properly guarded to ensure that they cannot be used to read or write past the end of the allocation.
quote: out-of-bounds read or write operation, which can cause program instability

subject: SSRF prevention via allowlist pattern
truth: Do not validate or sanitize the user-supplied URL; instead, map user input to a fixed server-side string before constructing the request.
quote: it is recommended to maintain a list of authorized request targets and choose from that list based on the user input provided

subject: RabbitMQ ConnectionFactory hostname verification
truth: Always call connectionFactory.enableHostnameVerification() after connectionFactory.useSslProtocol() to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.
quote: Hostname verification for rabbitmq ConnectionFactory is not enabled
code_bad:
com.rabbitmq.client.ConnectionFactory connectionFactory = new com.rabbitmq.client.ConnectionFactory();
connectionFactory.useSslProtocol(); //BAD: Hostname verification for rabbitmq ConnectionFactory is not enabled

subject: Using SQLCipher instead of plain SQLiteDatabase
truth: Replace SQLiteDatabase.openOrCreateDatabase with net.sqlcipher.database.SQLiteDatabase.openOrCreateDatabase and pass a database password.
quote: Use SQLCipher or similar libraries to add encryption capabilities to SQLite

subject: SASL authentication with ldap:// encrypts credentials
truth: When using ldap://, set SECURITY_AUTHENTICATION to a SASL mechanism like DIGEST-MD5 or GSSAPI instead of 'simple' to encrypt credentials.
quote: Use the ldaps:// protocol to send credentials through SSL or use SASL authentication.

subject: Regex injection via user input
truth: Always sanitize user input with Pattern.quote() before embedding it in a regular expression to prevent regex injection and ReDoS attacks.
quote: Constructing a regular expression with unsanitized user input is dangerous

subject: Sensitive data in GET query string
truth: Stop sending sensitive information like passwords in GET query strings; use HTTP POST to send sensitive data in the request body instead.
quote: Sensitive information such as user passwords should not be transmitted within the query string of the requested URL

subject: System prompt injection via user-controlled persona
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into system-level prompts; supply user-controlled values in messages with the 'user' role instead.
quote: user input is used directly in a system-level prompt

subject: HTTP response header injection via user input
truth: Stop writing user-controlled input directly into HTTP header names or values without stripping line break characters (\n, \r) and colons.
quote: Directly writing user input (for example, an HTTP request parameter) to an HTTP header can lead to an HTTP response-splitting vulnerability.
code_bad:
@app.route("/example_bad")
def example_bad():
    rfs_header = request.args["rfs_header"]
    response = Response()
    custom_header = "X-MyHeader-" + rfs_header
    # BAD: User input is used as part of the header name.
    response.headers[custom_header] = "HeaderValue" 
    return response
code_good:
@app.route("/example_good")
def example_bad():
    rfs_header = request.args["rfs_header"]
    response = Response()
    custom_header = "X-MyHeader-" + rfs_header.replace("\n", "").replace("\r","").replace(":","")
    # GOOD: Line break characters are removed from the input.
    response.headers[custom_header] = "HeaderValue" 
    return response

subject: SHA-256 for password hashing
truth: Stop using SHA-256 for password hashing; use Argon2 (or scrypt, bcrypt, PBKDF2) instead because SHA-256 is not computationally expensive.
quote: Although SHA-256 is a strong cryptographic hash function, it is not suitable for password hashing since it is not computationally expensive
code_good:
from argon2 import PasswordHasher

def get_initial_hash(password: str):
    ph = PasswordHasher()
    return ph.hash(password) # GOOD

def check_password(password: str, known_hash):
    ph = PasswordHasher()
    return ph.verify(known_hash, password) # GOOD

subject: Explicit BouncyCastle provider in Cipher.getInstance
truth: Stop specifying the 'BC' provider in Cipher.getInstance; omit the provider argument to use the default AndroidOpenSSL/Conscrypt provider.
quote: Explicitly specifying BouncyCastle provider
code_bad: val cipherBC = Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding", "BC")
code_good: val cipher = Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding")

subject: PendingIntent with implicit intent and missing FLAG_IMMUTABLE
truth: Stop using implicit intents with mutable PendingIntent; always use FLAG_IMMUTABLE with an explicit intent targeting a specific component.
quote: Missing FLAG_IMMUTABLE
code_bad:
val implicitIntent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
val mutablePendingIntent = PendingIntent.getActivity(
    context,
    0,
    implicitIntent,
    PendingIntent.FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT  // Missing FLAG_IMMUTABLE
)

subject: process/library invocation with user-controlled path
truth: Use hard-coded string literals for the command or library path instead of passing user input directly to system, dlopen, or LoadLibrary.
quote: use hard-coded string literals for the command to run or library to load
code_bad: void* handle = dlopen(lib, RTLD_LAZY);

subject: setuid/setgid without return code check
truth: Always check the return value of setuid, setgid, setgroups, and initgroups; failing to do so silently leaves elevated privileges active.
quote: The code attempts to drop privilege in an incorrect order by erroneous dropping user privilege before groups. This has security impact if the return codes are not checked.

subject: Directly writing user input to HTTP response
truth: Stop writing raw user input to HTTP responses; always HTML-encode untrusted data using WebUtility.HtmlEncode before writing to the page.
quote: Directly writing user input (for example, an HTTP request parameter) to a webpage, without properly sanitizing the input first, allows for a cross-site scripting vulnerability.

subject: Groovy script evaluation with untrusted input
truth: Stop evaluating untrusted input with GroovyShell, Eval, or GroovyClassLoader; use a sandboxed ClassLoader that blocks dangerous classes.
quote: avoid using untrusted input in a Groovy evaluation

subject: Android Dex loading storage location
truth: Do not use /sdcard or any external/shared storage for Dex files; always use getCacheDir() or getFilesDir() which are private to the app.
quote: Loading a file from private storage instead of a world-writable one can prevent this issue
code_bad: File file = new File("/sdcard/updater.apk");
code_good: File file = new File(getCacheDir() + "/updater.apk");

subject: Storing API methods in global scope
truth: Do not store API methods in the global scope; put them in an API object or Map and use hasOwnProperty to whitelist allowed method names.
quote: Instead of storing the API methods in the global scope, put them in an API object or Map.

subject: JavaScript object property assignment from user input
truth: Stop using user-controlled strings directly as object property keys; prepend a marker character like '$' to prevent prototype pollution.
quote: prepend untrusted input with a marker character such as $, before using it in properties accesses
code_bad:
var prop = req.query.userControlled; // BAD
myObj[prop] = function() {};
code_good:
var prop = "$" + req.query.userControlled; // GOOD
myObj[prop] = function() {};

subject: Nokogiri XML parsing with NOENT option enables XXE
truth: Stop using Nokogiri::XML::ParseOptions::NOENT or config.noent when parsing untrusted XML; omit the option or use config.nonoent instead.
quote: the parser is invoked with the noent option set
code_bad: doc = Nokogiri::XML.parse(xmlSrc, nil, nil, Nokogiri::XML::ParseOptions::NOENT) # BAD
code_good: doc = Nokogiri::XML.parse(xmlSrc) # GOOD

subject: markupsafe.Markup with direct variable
truth: Stop passing untrusted variables directly to markupsafe.Markup(); use markupsafe.escape() or interpolate into an existing Markup object.
quote: ``markupsafe.Markup`` does not perform any escaping, so passing dynamic content, like f-strings, variables or interpolated strings will potentially lead to XSS vulnerabilities
code_bad: Markup(content)
code_good: escape(content)

subject: Command Injection via system() call with user input
truth: Never pass unsanitized user input directly to system() or shell execution; validate against a strict allowlist of safe characters first.
quote: does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended command
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: run a shell command with a variable
execute system command with user input
build a command string with arguments
list directory contents with user-supplied path

subject: code before first case label
truth: Stop placing executable statements between the switch opening brace and the first case label; that code is dead and will never execute.
quote: i1=c1*2; // BAD: the code will not be executed

subject: Buffer access with incorrect length value
truth: Stop hard-coding a literal size for a buffer argument; use sizeof(buf) to derive the correct length from the actual buffer declaration.
quote: You need to limit the value of the length argument
code_bad:
char buf[256];
X509_NAME_oneline(X509_get_subject_name(peer),buf,1024); // BAD
code_good:
char buf[256];
X509_NAME_oneline(X509_get_subject_name(peer),buf,sizeof(buf)); // GOOD

subject: ValidationType.DTD on user-provided XML
truth: Do not set ValidationType to DTD for user-provided XML; set it to Schema so validation uses a known schema, not the document's own DTD.
quote: If it is set to DTD, the document is only validated against the DTD specified in the user-provided document itself - which could be specified as anything by a malicious user.

subject: Assembly.LoadFile with user input
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly to Assembly.LoadFile; validate the path against a whitelist of known safe assemblies first.
quote: Load assembly based on user input

subject: Go stored XSS with io.WriteString
truth: Stop writing stored values directly to HTTP responses; import "html" and call html.EscapeString() on every externally-controlled value.
quote: Sanitizing the file names prevents the vulnerability
code_bad: io.WriteString(w, file.Name()+"\n")
code_good: io.WriteString(w, html.EscapeString(file.Name())+"\n")

subject: strconv.Atoi to int32 conversion
truth: Stop using strconv.Atoi when you need a specific bit size; use strconv.ParseInt or strconv.ParseUint with the correct bit size instead.
quote: If a string is parsed into an int using strconv.Atoi, and subsequently that int is converted into another integer type of a smaller size, the result can produce unexpected values.

subject: Open redirect validation via URL parsing
truth: Do not assume a relative path is safe; parse the URL against a known base origin and verify the host stays the same before redirecting.
quote: check that we don't redirect to a different host

subject: loading external scripts over HTTP
truth: Always use HTTPS URLs when loading external scripts and consider adding subresource integrity (SRI) checks via the integrity attribute.
quote: Including a resource from an untrusted source or using an untrusted channel may allow an attacker to include arbitrary code in the response.
code_bad: <script src="http://code.jquery.com/jquery-3.6.0.slim.min.js" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>
code_good: <script src="https://code.jquery.com/jquery-3.6.0.slim.min.js" integrity="sha256-u7e5khyithlIdTpu22PHhENmPcRdFiHRjhAuHcs05RI=" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>

subject: SSRF via user-controlled hostname in outgoing HTTP request
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into the hostname of an outgoing HTTP request URL; pick the hostname from an allow-list instead.
quote: Avoid user input in the hostname of the URL. Pick the hostname from an allow-list instead of constructing it directly from user input.

subject: os.path.join does not prevent path traversal
truth: Stop assuming os.path.join(base, user_input) is safe; a user input like '/etc/passwd' or '../../etc/passwd' escapes the base directory.
quote: Note that a user could also use an absolute path here, since the result of os.path.join("/server/static/images/", "/etc/passwd") is "/etc/passwd".
code_bad: data = open(os.path.join(base_path, filename), 'rb').read()
code_good:
fullpath = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(base_path, filename))
if not fullpath.startswith(base_path):
    raise Exception("not allowed")
data = open(fullpath, 'rb').read()

subject: LDAP authentication with empty password
truth: Stop using an empty string or None as the password when binding to an LDAP server; use a non-empty password to prevent anonymous binds.
quote: anonymous binds causes an empty or None-set password to result in a successful authentication
code_bad: ldap_connection.bind('cn=root', "")
code_good: ldap_connection.bind('cn=root', "SecurePa$$!")

subject: Client IP from X-Forwarded-For header
truth: Stop trusting the first entry of X-Forwarded-For for security checks; use the last entry instead, as reverse proxies append to the end.
quote: the less-trustworthy first one is used
code_good:
client_ip = request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for')
client_ip = client_ip.split(',')[client_ip.split(',').length - 1]
if not client_ip == '127.0.0.1':
    raise Exception('ip illegal')

subject: CORS with allow_credentials=True and wildcard origins
truth: Do not set allow_origins to '*' when allow_credentials=True; any external site can then make authenticated requests on behalf of users.
quote: can allow any Peer B to send requests to the misconfigured Peer A on behalf of the user
code_bad:
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware

app = FastAPI()

origins = [
    "*"
]

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=origins,
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)


@app.get("/")
async def main():
    return {"message": "Hello World"}
code_good:
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware

app = FastAPI()

origins = [
    "http://localhost.tiangolo.com",
    "https://localhost.tiangolo.com",
    "http://localhost",
    "http://localhost:8080",
]

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=origins,
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)


@app.get("/")
async def main():
    return {"message": "Hello World"}

subject: Passing remote data to WKWebView JavaScript
truth: Do not inject remote data into JavaScript source strings; pass remote data objects via the arguments dictionary of callAsyncJavaScript.
quote: send it to the web view as data using an API such as WKWebView.callAsyncJavaScript with the arguments dictionary to pass remote data objects
code_bad: _ = try await webview.evaluateJavaScript("console.log(" + remoteData + ")") // BAD
code_good:
_ = try await webview.callAsyncJavaScript(
    "console.log(data)",
    arguments: ["data": remoteData], // GOOD
    contentWorld: .page
)

subject: XSS via unescaped form value written directly to response
truth: Stop writing r.FormValue() data directly to the response writer without escaping; use html.EscapeString or structured output like JSON.
quote: XSS via taint analysis

data := r.FormValue("data")
w.Write([]byte(data))
code_good:
data := r.FormValue("data")
jsonData, _ := json.Marshal(data)
w.Write(jsonData)

subject: atoi for port conversion
truth: Stop using atoi for port/network conversions; use istringstream with range checks instead because atoi silently fails on invalid input.
quote: Notice `atoi` and friends are not used because they can silently fail.
source: OWASP CS: C-Based Toolchain Hardening Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Shared cache without tenant isolation
truth: Stop using bare cache keys without tenant context; prefix all cache keys with a tenant identifier to prevent cross-tenant data leakage.
quote: Prefix all cache keys with tenant identifier
code_good:
def _build_key(self, tenant_id: str, key: str) -> str:
    """Build tenant-scoped cache key."""
    # Validate key format to prevent injection
    if not key or any(c in key for c in ['{', '}', '\n', '\r']):
        raise ValueError("Invalid cache key format")
    
    # Use hash of tenant_id to prevent key enumeration
    tenant_hash = hashlib.sha256(tenant_id.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]
    return f"t:{tenant_hash}:{key}"
source: OWASP CS: Multi-Tenant Application Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: CSS selectors revealing application features
truth: Stop using descriptive CSS selector names that reveal features or roles; rewrite selectors to be obscure or use build-time obfuscation.
quote: attackers could map these selectors to actual features of a web application
code_bad: #UserPage .Toolbar .addUserButton
code_good: #page_u header button:first-of-type
source: OWASP CS: Securing Cascading Style Sheets Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: C++ iterator to expired temporary container
truth: Never create an iterator to a temporary container when the iterator is expected to be used after the container's lifetime has expired.
quote: Using an iterator owned by a container after the lifetime of the container has expired can lead to undefined behavior

subject: Logging sensitive data in Go
truth: Stop logging sensitive information like passwords in cleartext; omit them entirely or log only non-sensitive fields like the username.
quote: Sensitive information that is logged unencrypted is accessible to an attacker who gains access to the logs.
code_bad: log.Printf("Registering new user %s with password %s.\n", user, pw)
code_good: log.Printf("Registering new user %s.\n", user)

subject: Trust boundary violation in Java servlets
truth: Always validate user-supplied input before writing it to the session; never write request parameters directly into session attributes.
quote: The input is written to the session without being sanitized.

subject: Android file leak via unprotected intent data path
truth: Stop loading file content from an external activity result without checking that the returned URI path does not start with /data/data.
quote: the intent could refer to paths which are accessible to the receiver application, but are intended to be application-private

subject: Unvalidated user input in server-side HTTP request
truth: Stop passing user-controlled strings directly into server-side HTTP requests; validate with an allowlist (numeric/alphanumeric) first.
quote: avoid putting user input directly into a network request. If using user input is necessary, then is mandatory to validate them.

subject: Paramiko SSH client missing host key policy
truth: Do not set the missing host key policy to AutoAddPolicy or WarningPolicy; use RejectPolicy to throw an exception on unknown host keys.
quote: Do not accept unknown host keys. In particular, do not set the default missing host key policy for the Paramiko library to either AutoAddPolicy or WarningPolicy.
code_bad: client.set_missing_host_key_policy(AutoAddPolicy)
code_good: client.set_missing_host_key_policy(RejectPolicy)

subject: Ruby shell command injection via exported functions
truth: Exported functions that build shell commands with string interpolation expose callers to command injection; pass arguments separately.
quote: a malicious user to provide the input http://example.org; cat /etc/passwd in order to execute the command cat /etc/passwd
code_bad: system("wget #{path}")
code_good: system("wget", path)

subject: URL redirect host validation
truth: Stop redirecting to any user-supplied URL; parse the URI and verify the host is either absent (relative) or matches a known good host.
quote: check that the target URL does not redirect to a different host by checking that the URL is either relative or on a known good host
code_bad:
class HelloController < ActionController::Base
  def hello
    redirect_to params[:url]
  end
end
code_good:
require 'uri'

class HelloController < ActionController::Base
  KNOWN_HOST = "example.org"

  def hello
    begin
      target_url = URI.parse(params[:url])

      # Redirect if the URL is either relative or on a known good host
      if !target_url.host || target_url.host == KNOWN_HOST
        redirect_to target_url.to_s
      else
        redirect_to "/error.html" # Redirect to error page if the host is not known
      end
    rescue URI::InvalidURIError
      # Handle the exception, for example, by redirecting to a safe page
      redirect_to "/error.html"
    end
  end
end

subject: Format string construction via string interpolation
truth: Stop building format strings dynamically with string interpolation or concatenation; use constant format strings with %@ placeholders.
quote: Use a constant string literal for the format string to prevent the possibility of data flow from an untrusted source
code_bad: print(String(format: "User input: " + inputString)) // vulnerable
code_good: print(String(format: "User input: %@", inputString)) // fixed

subject: CrossOriginProtection bypass patterns
truth: Stop using wildcard patterns like "/" or "/*" in AddInsecureBypassPattern; use explicit, narrow path prefixes like "/healthz" instead.
quote: Unsafe CrossOriginProtection bypass patterns
code_bad: cop.AddInsecureBypassPattern("/")
code_good: cop.AddInsecureBypassPattern("/healthz")

subject: Using String/BigInteger for secrets in Android memory
truth: Stop using immutable types like String and BigInteger for secrets; use byte[] or char[] and overwrite them with Arrays.fill after use.
quote: Don't use immutable structures (e.g., `String` and `BigInteger`) to represent secrets.

subject: Java Servlet error page exposes exception details
truth: Stop exposing exception.getMessage() to the client; log the exception server-side and return a generic response without error details.
quote: String errorMessage = exception.getMessage();
source: OWASP CS: Error Handling Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SSL certificate verification result
truth: Always call SSL_get_verify_result after SSL_get_peer_certificate and check that the result equals X509_V_OK before trusting the peer.
quote: it is unsafe to use that information without checking if the certificate is valid
code_bad: X509 *cert = SSL_get_peer_certificate(ssl); // BAD (SSL_get_verify_result is never called)

subject: temporary std::string c_str() pointer
truth: Stop storing c_str() from a temporary std::string; declare the string as a named local variable so its lifetime outlives the pointer.
quote: the memory returned by `c_str()` is freed when the temporary string is destroyed
code_bad:
const char* p = std::string("hello").c_str();
c_api(p);

subject: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) in ASP.NET WebForms
truth: Always verify that the current user is authorized to access the resource identified by the ID parameter before performing any action.
quote: If the methods do not check that the current user is authorized to access the specified resource, an attacker can access a resource by guessing or otherwise determining the linked ID parameter.

subject: Piggybacking on prior authentication across channels
truth: Do not assume a user authenticated on one channel is the same user connecting on a separate channel; authenticate on the new channel.
quote: an attacker may be able to connect to the alternate channel before the legitimate user does

subject: XQuery injection via string concatenation
truth: Stop building XQuery expressions by concatenating user input into the query string; use bindString to parameterize the query instead.
quote: The software uses external input to dynamically construct an XQuery expression

subject: Go HTTP server ReadHeaderTimeout
truth: Set ReadHeaderTimeout on http.Server to prevent slow-header attacks; omitting it leaves the server vulnerable to resource exhaustion.
quote: Detect ReadHeaderTimeout not configured as a potential risk

subject: Mass Assignment via automatic parameter binding
truth: Stop binding HTTP request parameters directly to domain objects; use a Data Transfer Object (DTO) that only includes editable fields.
quote: avoid binding input directly to domain objects
source: OWASP CS: Mass Assignment Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Pointer arithmetic with sizeof scaling
truth: Stop manually scaling pointer offsets by sizeof(T); pointer arithmetic is already automatically scaled by the size of the data type.
quote: Pointer arithmetic in C and C++ is automatically scaled according to the size of the data type
code_bad: return *(intPointer + (i * sizeof(int)));
code_good: return *(intPointer + i);

subject: temporary file creation with hardcoded predictable path
truth: Stop using hardcoded predictable paths like /tmp/name.tmp for temporary files; use mkstemp with a template ending in XXXXXX instead.
quote: Working with a file, without checking its existence and its rights, as well as working with names that can be predicted, may not be safe.
code_bad: fp = fopen("/tmp/name.tmp","w"); // BAD

subject: ASP.NET customErrors mode
truth: Set customErrors mode to 'On' or 'RemoteOnly' in Web.config; do not leave it 'Off' which leaks stack traces via default error pages.
quote: Set the customErrors to On to prevent the default error page from being displayed
code_bad:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <customErrors mode="Off">
      ...
    </customErrors>
  </system.web>
</configuration>
code_good:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <customErrors mode="On">
      ...
    </customErrors>
  </system.web>
</configuration>

subject: Token validation defaults
truth: Do not disable token validation by setting properties to false; the secure default is true, so remove the assignment or set to true.
quote: remove the assignment to false because the default value is true.

subject: JHipster RandomUtil using insecure PRNG for authentication tokens
truth: Stop using RandomStringUtils.randomAlphanumeric/randomNumeric for authentication tokens; use SecureRandom-backed randomness instead.
quote: CVSS v3.0 Base Score of 9.8/10
code_bad: return RandomStringUtils.randomAlphanumeric(DEF_COUNT); // BAD: RandomStringUtils does not use SecureRandom

subject: Library functions constructing HTML via innerHTML
truth: Stop using innerHTML with string concatenation for user-controlled input; use safe DOM APIs like innerText or createElement instead.
quote: does not escape unsafe HTML

subject: Accidental exposure of package.json _where field
truth: Stop exposing the entire node_modules folder; an attacker can read the _where field from package.json revealing absolute file paths.
quote: the _where field from a package.json file, which gives access to the absolute path of the file
code_bad: app.use('/node_modules', express.static(path.resolve(__dirname, '../node_modules')));
code_good:
app.use("jquery", express.static('./node_modules/jquery/dist'));
app.use("bootstrap", express.static('./node_modules/bootstrap/dist'));

subject: Jinja2 select_autoescape
truth: Use select_autoescape(['html', 'xml']) instead of manually setting autoescape to handle escaping correctly for different file types.
quote: Jinja2 provides the function select_autoescape to make sure that the correct auto-escaping is chosen
code_bad: unsafe_env = Environment(loader=loader)
code_good: safe2_env = Environment(loader=loader, autoescape=select_autoescape())

subject: ssl.wrap_socket deprecated
truth: Stop using ssl.wrap_socket (deprecated since Python 3.7); use ssl.create_default_context or ssl.SSLContext with minimum_version set.
quote: Note that ssl.wrap_socket has been deprecated in Python 3.7.
code_bad: ssl.wrap_socket(socket.socket())
code_good:
context = ssl.create_default_context()
context.minimum_version = ssl.TLSVersion.TLSv1_2

subject: LDAP3 connection with password=None
truth: Stop passing password=None when creating an LDAP3 Connection; always provide a non-empty password to avoid anonymous authentication.
quote: anonymous binds causes an empty or None-set password to result in a successful authentication
code_bad: conn = Connection(srv, user='user_dn', password=None)
code_good: conn = Connection(srv, user='user_dn', password="SecurePa$$!")

subject: Parsed host substring check
truth: Stop checking if the parsed host contains an allowed domain as a substring; use an explicit whitelist of complete hostnames instead.
quote: This is still not a sufficient check as the following URLs bypass it: http://evil-example.com http://example.com.evil-example.net
code_bad:
class AppController < ApplicationController
    def index
        url = params[:url]
        host = URI(url).host
        # BAD: the host of `url` may be controlled by an attacker
        if host.include?("example.com")
            redirect_to url
        end
    end
end
code_good:
class AppController < ApplicationController
    def index
        url = params[:url]
        host = URI(url).host
        # GOOD: the host of `url` can not be controlled by an attacker
        allowedHosts = [
            'example.com',
            'beta.example.com',
            'www.example.com'
        ]
        if allowedHosts.include?(host)
            redirect_to url
        end
    end
end

subject: URI.open in Ruby
truth: Stop using URI.open with user input; use Net::HTTP.get(URI.parse(...)) or an HTTP client instead to prevent shell command injection.
quote: The same vulnerability applies to
code_bad: URI.open(web_page) # BAD - calls `Kernel.open` internally
code_good: Net::HTTP.get(URI.parse(web_page))

subject: Hardcoded IV/nonce in AES encryption
truth: Stop using a static, hardcoded IV/nonce for AES encryption; generate a random nonce using crypto/rand for each encryption operation.
quote: Use of hardcoded IV/nonce for encryption
code_bad: aesOFB := cipher.NewOFB(block, []byte("ILoveMyNonceAlot"))
code_good:
iv := make([]byte, 16)
rand.Read(iv)
block, _ := aes.NewCipher([]byte("12345678123456781234567812345678"))
_ = cipher.NewCTR(block, iv)

subject: Open redirect via user-controlled URL parameter
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly to http.Redirect; validate the redirect target against an allowlist or use a fixed path.
quote: Open redirect via taint analysis
code_bad:
target := r.URL.Query().Get("next")
	http.Redirect(w, r, target, http.StatusFound)
code_good: http.Redirect(w, r, "/dashboard", http.StatusFound)

subject: Uninitialized variable in conditional path (C)
truth: Always initialize all variables at declaration, especially pointers, to prevent use of indeterminate values on unhandled code paths.
quote: stack variables are not initialized by default
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: set a string variable inside an if
use a variable after conditional assignment

subject: unsigned subtraction compared to zero with signed cast fix
truth: Stop comparing unsigned subtraction to zero; cast the operands to a signed type large enough to hold the result before subtracting.
quote: consider casting the result of the subtraction to a signed type

subject: std::string::c_str() lifetime
truth: Store the concatenated std::string in a named variable before calling c_str() on it, so the underlying buffer outlives the pointer.
quote: the underlying std::string object that represents the concatenated string is destroyed as soon as the call to c_str returns

subject: missing upper bound check on user-controlled allocation size
truth: Stop passing user-controlled integers directly to make() without first enforcing a maximum size limit to prevent memory exhaustion.
quote: Implement a maximum allowed value for size allocations with the built-in make function to prevent excessively large allocations.

subject: partial path traversal via getCanonicalPath()
truth: Stop using getCanonicalPath() with startsWith() to check directory containment; use Path#normalize() and Path#startsWith() instead.
quote: getCanonicalPath() is not slash-terminated

subject: Non-slash-terminated canonical path prefix check
truth: getCanonicalPath() returns a non-slash-terminated string, so startsWith() allows sibling directory access instead of only children.
quote: getCanonicalPath() returns a non-slash-terminated path string

subject: Android WebView JavaScript default
truth: Do not assume JavaScript is needed; it is disabled by default and should remain disabled unless loading only trusted HTTPS content.
quote: JavaScript execution is disabled by default. You can explicitly disable it by calling setJavaScriptEnabled(false) on the settings of the WebView.
code_bad:
WebSettings settings = webview.getSettings();
settings.setJavaScriptEnabled(true); // BAD: webview has JavaScript enabled
code_good:
WebSettings settings = webview.getSettings();
settings.setJavaScriptEnabled(false); // GOOD: webview has JavaScript disabled

subject: SpEL expression evaluation with user-controlled input
truth: Stop calling expression.getValue() without a restricted context; always pass a SimpleEvaluationContext when user input is involved.
quote: If user input must be included in the expression, it should be then evaluated in a limited context that doesn't allow arbitrary method invocation.

subject: Android content provider unintended access via Intent reflection
truth: Reflecting a user-provided Intent via setResult can grant arbitrary access to your content providers even if they are not exported.
quote: the application may be unintentionally giving arbitrary access to its content providers, even if they are not exported

subject: RSA cipher without OAEP padding
truth: Use OAEP padding (e.g., RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-1AndMGF1Padding) instead of NoPadding or PKCS1Padding when instantiating an RSA Cipher.
quote: Using an outdated padding scheme such as PKCS1, or no padding at all, can weaken the encryption by making it vulnerable to a padding oracle attack.
code_bad: Cipher rsa = Cipher.getInstance("RSA/ECB/NoPadding");
code_good: Cipher rsa = Cipher.getInstance("RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-1AndMGF1Padding");

subject: Zip entry path used directly as filesystem path
truth: Do not use the entry path from a zip archive directly as a filesystem path; always validate that it does not contain '..' elements.
quote: If these file paths are used to create a filesystem path, then a file operation may happen in an unexpected location.

subject: Temporary file naming with timestamps
truth: Stop using Date.now() or Math.random() to name temp files; use tmp.fileSync() which guarantees a non-existing, non-accessible file.
quote: there is no guarantee that the file does not already exist
code_bad:
const file = path.join(os.tmpdir(), "test-" + (new Date()).getTime() + ".txt");
fs.writeFileSync(file, "content");
code_good:
const file = tmp.fileSync().name;
fs.writeFileSync(file, "content");

subject: Passing arguments as array to subprocess.run
truth: Stop building shell commands with string concatenation; pass arguments as an array to subprocess.run to avoid shell interpretation.
quote: provide the dynamic arguments to the shell as an array to APIs such as subprocess.run to avoid interpretation by the shell

subject: Flask redirect with user input
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly to redirect(); validate the target against an allowlist or check that it has no netloc.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into a URL redirect request without validating the input can facilitate phishing attacks.

subject: String(format:) with concatenated user input
truth: Stop concatenating user input into the format string; pass user-controlled strings as format arguments using %@ specifiers instead.
quote: Passing untrusted format strings to functions that use printf style formatting can lead to buffer overflows and data representation problems
code_bad: print(String(format: "User input: " + inputString)) // vulnerable
code_good: print(String(format: "User input: %@", inputString)) // fixed

subject: Hardcoded encryption keys in Swift
truth: Stop using hardcoded arrays or constant strings as encryption keys; use SecRandomCopyBytes to generate random key material instead.
quote: Hardcoded keys should not be used for creating encryption ciphers
code_bad:
let key: Array<UInt8> = [0x2a, 0x3a, 0x80, 0x05]
let keyString = "this is a constant string"
let ivString = getRandomIV()
_ = try AES(key: key, blockMode: CBC(AES.randomIV(AES.blockSize)), padding: padding)
_ = try AES(key: keyString, iv: ivString)
_ = try Blowfish(key: key, blockMode: CBC(Blowfish.randomIV(Blowfish.blockSize)), padding: padding)
_ = try Blowfish(key: keyString, iv: ivString)

subject: PHP authorization variable initialization
truth: Always explicitly initialize boolean authorization variables to false before checking credentials; never rely on implicit defaults.
quote: initializes or sets a resource with a default that is intended to be changed
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: check if user is logged in

subject: unbounded allocation from user input
truth: Reject the request before proceeding further if the input value fails the range guard, so subsequent calculations cannot overflow.
quote: If the input value fails this guard then reject the request before proceeding further.
code_bad: char **root_node = (char **) malloc(factor * sizeof(char *));

subject: Expired SSL certificate acceptance
truth: Stop treating expired certificates as valid; an expired certificate should not be accepted as it is more likely to be compromised.
quote: An expired certificate should not be accepted as it is more likely to be compromised than a valid certificate

subject: Conditionally uninitialized variable from init function
truth: Always check the return status code of an initialization function before reading the local variable it was supposed to initialize.
quote: the code does not check the return value from the function call to initDeviceConfig

subject: vector erase inside loop with iterator invalidation
truth: Stop using the old iterator after vector::erase; assign the return value of erase to the iterator and do not increment separately.
quote: the iterator is invalidated after the call to `erase`

subject: Storing sensitive data in cookies in cleartext
truth: Stop storing sensitive information in cookies as cleartext; use MachineKey.Protect to encrypt the value before setting the cookie.
quote: Setting a cookie value with cleartext sensitive data
code_bad: ctx.Response.Cookies["AccountName"].Value = accountName;
code_good: ctx.Response.Cookies["AccountName"].Value = Protect(accountName, "Account name");

subject: MVEL expression from socket input
truth: Stop reading untrusted data from a socket and passing it directly to MVEL.eval(); the expression can invoke arbitrary JVM methods.
quote: the user-provided expression is directly evaluated

subject: Android component with intent filter
truth: Always explicitly set android:exported on every component that has an intent filter; omitting it implicitly exports the component.
quote: If the android:exported attribute is omitted from the component when an intent filter is included, then the component will be implicitly exported.
code_bad:
<activity>
            android:name=".Activity">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>
code_good:
<activity>
            android:name=".Activity">
            android:exported="false"
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>

subject: Java RMI deserialization filter
truth: Set an ObjectInputFilter via UnicastRemoteObject.exportObject(Remote, int, ObjectInputFilter) to restrict deserialization classes.
quote: Set a filter for incoming serialized data by wrapping remote objects using either UnicastRemoteObject.exportObject(Remote, int, ObjectInputFilter) or UnicastRemoteObject.exportObject(Remote, int, RMIClientSocketFactory, RMIServerSocketFactory, ObjectInputFilter) methods.
code_bad: registry.bind("unsafe", new RemoteObjectImpl());

subject: Spring redirect: string concatenation with 'redirect:' prefix
truth: Stop using string concatenation with 'redirect:' prefix and user input; validate the redirect target against an allowlist instead.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into a URL redirect request without validating the input can facilitate phishing attacks.

subject: shell commands requiring pipes or I/O redirection
truth: When a shell is needed (pipes, redirections), escape user input with shellQuote.quote() before embedding it in the command string.
quote: use shell-quote to escape any special characters in the input before embedding it in the command
