subject: Client-side request forgery via path traversal in fetch URL
truth: Restrict user input in the URL pathname to prevent path traversal attacks; do not concatenate unsanitized user input into the URL.
quote: the format of the message ID is not checked, and an attacker can abuse this to alter the endpoint targeted by the request

associative: build api url with user input

subject: Hard-coded credentials in Ruby (Rack)
truth: Stop storing plaintext passwords in source code; load hashed passwords and salts from a separate config file (e.g., YAML) instead.
quote: Remove hard-coded credentials, such as user names, passwords and certificates, from source code, placing them in configuration files
code_bad: if password == 'myPa55word'
code_good:
config_file = YAML.load_file('config.yml')
hashed_password = config_file['hashed_password']
salt = [config_file['salt']].pack('H*')

subject: torch.load weights_only=False
truth: Stop using torch.load with weights_only=False; this explicitly enables unsafe pickle deserialization and arbitrary code execution.
quote: Example of loading with weights_only=False (should trigger B614)
code_bad: unsafe_model.load_state_dict(torch.load('model_weights.pth', weights_only=False))
code_good: safe_model.load_state_dict(torch.load('model_weights.pth', weights_only=True))

subject: Newline stripping before logging in Go
truth: Stop assuming user input is safe for logs; strip \n and \r characters using strings.ReplaceAll before logging plain-text entries.
quote: line breaks should be removed from user input, using strings.Replace or similar

subject: JWT library default behavior
truth: Do not assume a JWT library verifies signatures by default; use ParseWithClaims which explicitly performs signature verification.
quote: Always verify the signature by using the appropriate methods provided by the JWT library, or use a library that verifies it by default.
code_bad: jwt.NewParser().ParseUnverified(token, &User{})

subject: MVEL expression evaluation
truth: Stop evaluating untrusted user input directly with MVEL.eval(); validate the expression against an allow-list or deny-list first.
quote: the user-provided expression is directly evaluated
code_bad: MVEL.eval(expression);
code_good:
validateExpression(expression);
MVEL.eval(expression);

subject: Android KeyStore biometric key generation
truth: Stop setting setUserAuthenticationRequired(false); set it to true to require biometric authentication before the key can be used.
quote: setUserAuthenticationRequired should be set to true; otherwise, the key can be used without user authentication.

subject: WebView debugging enabled in production
truth: Guard WebView.setWebContentsDebuggingEnabled(true) behind a debug-build check; never enable unconditionally in production builds.
quote: debugging is always enabled
code_bad: WebView.setWebContentsDebuggingEnabled(true);

subject: ReDoS via complex number regex without input length limit
truth: When a regex cannot be easily rewritten to avoid catastrophic backtracking, limit input length (e.g., 1000 chars) to prevent DoS.
quote: It is not immediately obvious how to rewrite this regular expression to avoid the problem.

subject: Multi-character regex sanitization for script tags
truth: Stop matching the entire script tag with a multi-character regex; instead match single characters '<' and '>' to remove all tags.
quote: A fix for this issue is to rewrite the regular expression to match single characters ("<" and ">") instead of the entire unsafe text.
code_bad: str.replace(/<script\b[^<]*(?:(?!<\/script>)<[^<]*)*<\/script>/g, "");

subject: Jinja2 SandboxedEnvironment for untrusted templates
truth: If constructing a template from user input is absolutely necessary, use SandboxedEnvironment.from_string() instead of Template().
quote: A sandboxed environment is used to construct the template
code_bad:
from django.urls import path
from django.http import HttpResponse
from jinja2 import Template, escape


def a(request):
    template = request.GET['template']

    # BAD: Template is constructed from user input. 
    t = Template(template)

    name = request.GET['name']
    html = t.render(name=escape(name))
    return HttpResponse(html)


urlpatterns = [
    path('a', a),
]
code_good:
from django.urls import path
from django.http import HttpResponse
from jinja2 import escape
from jinja2.sandbox import SandboxedEnvironment


def a(request):
    env = SandboxedEnvironment()
    template = request.GET['template']

    # GOOD: A sandboxed environment is used to construct the template. 
    t = env.from_string(template)

    name = request.GET['name']
    html = t.render(name=escape(name))
    return HttpResponse(html)


urlpatterns = [
    path('a', a),
]

subject: Full SSRF via subdomain injection in Python requests
truth: Stop concatenating user input into the hostname portion of a URL; use server-side mapping from input to a fixed subdomain string.
quote: user has full control of URL
code_bad: resp = requests.get("https://" + target + ".example.com/data/")
code_good:
subdomain = "europe" if target == "EU" else "world"
resp = requests.get("https://" + subdomain + ".example.com/data/")

subject: Rust ctor/dtor functions calling std library
truth: Do not call any part of the std library from a #[ctor] or #[dtor] function; use a non-std alternative like libc_println! instead.
quote: Calling functions and methods in the Rust std library from a #[ctor] or #[dtor] function is not safe

subject: Django mark_safe with format() and user data
truth: Stop using mark_safe() with .format() on strings that contain any user-controlled variables; use only hardcoded format arguments.
quote: safestring.mark_safe('{} {}'.format(my_insecure_str, 'STR'))
code_bad: safestring.mark_safe('{} {}'.format(my_insecure_str, 'STR'))
code_good: safestring.mark_safe('{}'.format('secure'))

subject: TLS session resumption bypasses VerifyPeerCertificate
truth: Disable session tickets (SessionTicketsDisabled = true) when using VerifyPeerCertificate alone, or pair it with VerifyConnection.
quote: TLS resumption may bypass VerifyPeerCertificate when VerifyConnection is unset
code_bad: VerifyPeerCertificate: func(_ [][]byte, _ [][]*x509.Certificate) error { return nil }
code_good: cfg.SessionTicketsDisabled = true

subject: Keyboard cache enabled on sensitive text input fields
truth: Disable keyboard suggestions on sensitive input fields by setting android:inputType to textNoSuggestions or a password variation.
quote: the keyboard cache will not be shown when the input field is selected
code_bad: findViewById(R.id.KeyBoardCache).setInputType(InputType.TYPE_CLASS_TEXT)
code_good:
<EditText
        android:id="@+id/KeyBoardCache"
        android:inputType="textNoSuggestions" />

subject: innerHTML with untrusted data
truth: Never use innerHTML with untrusted data; use textContent for text-only updates or sanitize with DOMPurify before using innerHTML.
quote: never use innerHTML with untrusted data
code_bad: document.getElementById('content').innerHTML = data; // DANGER! The server may have returned a payload that executes scripts, for example: .
source: OWASP CS: AJAX Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SQL connection string construction
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into SQL connection strings; use SqlConnectionStringBuilder to safely include user input.
quote: Direct use of user input in a connection string passed to SqlConnection
code_bad:
string connectionString = "server=(local);user id=" + userName + ";password= pass;";
SqlConnection sqlConnectionBad = new SqlConnection(connectionString);

subject: allocation size calculation without overflow guard
truth: Always guard against integer overflow in allocation size calculations by validating input data length before computing the size.
quote: Always guard against overflow in arithmetic operations involving potentially large numbers

subject: SSH host key callback returning nil
truth: Stop writing HostKeyCallback functions that unconditionally return nil; always validate the host key against a known allow list.
quote: a semantically similar callback, should not be used in production code

subject: Android APK installation from external storage
truth: Stop installing APKs using an Intent with data from external/uncontrolled storage; use PackageInstaller or FileProvider instead.
quote: If the file used in the Intent is from a location that is not controlled by the application (for example, an SD card that is universally writable), this can result in the unintended installation of untrusted applications.

subject: Android cleartext password storage
truth: Stop storing passwords or sensitive data in cleartext on the Android filesystem; encrypt individual fields or use EncryptedFile.
quote: sensitive information should not be saved in cleartext

subject: Timing side-channel via String.equals() on secrets
truth: Stop using String.equals() for secret comparison; use MessageDigest.isEqual() which runs in constant time regardless of content.
quote: the comparison time should not depend on the content of the input
code_bad: return Key.equals(request.getHeader("X-Auth-Token"));
code_good: return MessageDigest.isEqual(Key.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8), token.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8));

subject: jQuery HTML string auto-detection
truth: Stop relying on jQuery's auto-detection of HTML strings; jQuery() silently parses strings starting with < as HTML, creating XSS.
quote: the call to jQuery interprets sourceSelector as HTML if it is a string that starts with <
code_bad: var source = jQuery(options.sourceSelector),
code_good: var source = jQuery.find(options.sourceSelector),

subject: prototype pollution via __proto__
truth: An attacker can inject properties into Object.prototype by passing {"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}} to an unsafe merge function.
quote: if src is the object {"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}}, it will inject the property isAdmin: true in Object.prototype.

subject: Rust Actix-Web HTML response with user input
truth: Stop writing user input directly into HTML responses; use html_escape::encode_text() to encode untrusted input before inclusion.
quote: Directly writing user input (for example, an HTTP request parameter) to a webpage, without properly sanitizing the input first, allows for a cross-site scripting vulnerability.

subject: Regex anchors in URL validation
truth: Always anchor regular expressions with ^ and $ when validating untrusted input like URLs to prevent bypass via embedded matches.
quote: Use anchors to ensure that regular expressions match at the expected locations.
code_bad: let regex = try Regex(#"https?://www\.example\.com"#) // BAD: the host of `url` may be controlled by an attacker
code_good: let regex = try Regex(#"^https?://www\.example\.com"#) // GOOD: the host of `url` can not be controlled by an attacker

subject: Command injection via Process with user-controlled arguments
truth: Stop passing unsanitized user input to Process arguments; validate the command against an allowlist of hard-coded strings first.
quote: Constructing a system command with unsanitized user input is dangerous
code_bad:
var task = Process()
task.launchPath = "/bin/bash"
task.arguments = ["-c", userControlledString] // BAD

task.launch()

subject: context.WithTimeout without defer cancel()
truth: Always call the cancel function returned by context.WithTimeout/WithCancel/WithDeadline via defer cancel() to release resources.
quote:
child, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)
	defer cancel()
code_bad: child, _ := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)
code_good:
child, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)
	defer cancel()

subject: Laravel rate limiting without per-user/per-IP scoping
truth: Always scope rate limits with ->by() to tie limits to a user ID or IP, otherwise the limit applies globally across all requests.
quote: Limit::perMinute(5)->by($request->user()?->id ?: $request->ip())
code_bad: Limit::perMinute(5)
code_good: Limit::perMinute(5)->by($request->user()?->id ?: $request->ip())
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: postMessage targetOrigin wildcard
truth: Always specify the exact targetOrigin in postMessage; never use the wildcard '*' which allows any origin to receive the message.
quote: Passing to the `targetOrigin` wildcard `*` causes any origin to receive the message.
code_bad: popup.postMessage('secret message!', '*');
code_good: popup.postMessage('secret message!', 'https://sub.example.com');
source: OWASP CS: Cross-site leaks Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SSL certificate revocation check
truth: A revoked or invalid certificate may indicate an attacker; always verify the result of SSL_get_verify_result against X509_V_OK.
quote: A certificate that has been revoked may indicate that data is coming from an attacker
code_bad: X509 *cert = SSL_get_peer_certificate(ssl); // BAD (SSL_get_verify_result is never called)

subject: Wrapper functions that add checks around standard library functions
truth: Stop calling the raw standard library function directly; always use the wrapper function that adds the additional safety check.
quote: The presence of a shell function with additional check indicates the possible risks of the call. Use this check everywhere.
code_bad: fclose(fe); // BAD
code_good: myFclose(fe); // GOOD

subject: mbtowc length argument
truth: Stop passing a hardcoded constant like 4 as the length argument to mbtowc; use MB_LEN_MAX instead to avoid out-of-range access.
quote: Using a function to convert multibyte or wide characters with an invalid length argument may result in an out-of-range access error or unexpected results
code_bad: mbtowc(&wc, ptr, 4)); // BAD:we can get unpredictable results
code_good: mbtowc(&wc, ptr, MB_LEN_MAX); // GOOD

subject: Concatenating query string parameters into HTTP responses
truth: Do not concatenate query string parameters directly into Response.Write; use WebUtility.HtmlEncode to sanitize the value first.
quote: Sanitizing the user-controlled data using the WebUtility.HtmlEncode method prevents the vulnerability
code_bad:
ctx.Response.Write(
    "The page \"" + ctx.Request.QueryString["page"] + "\" was not found.");

subject: XmlReader.Create without XmlReaderSettings
truth: Always pass an XmlReaderSettings instance with ValidationType set to Schema when calling XmlReader.Create on user-provided XML.
quote: User provided XML is processed without any validation,
code_bad: XmlReader.Create(new StringReader(userProvidedXml));

subject: hostname regexp without escaping dots
truth: Always escape the . meta-character with \\. when constructing hostname regexps for security checks, or use raw string literals.
quote: the unescaped . allows for any character before example.com
code_bad: re := "^((www|beta).)?example.com/"
code_good: re := "^((www|beta)\\.)?example\\.com/"

subject: Go fmt.Fprintf with user input
truth: Stop using fmt.Fprintf to write user-controlled strings to http.ResponseWriter; always wrap the value with html.EscapeString().
quote: Sanitizing the user-controlled data prevents the vulnerability
code_bad: fmt.Fprintf(w, "%q is an unknown user", username)
code_good: fmt.Fprintf(w, "%q is an unknown user", html.EscapeString(username))

subject: Go http.Cookie default HttpOnly=false
truth: Do not rely on http.Cookie defaults; explicitly set HttpOnly: true on every cookie that should not be accessible to JavaScript.
quote: Cookies without the HttpOnly flag set are accessible to client-side scripts

subject: uncontrolled slice allocation size from user input
truth: Always validate and cap user-controlled input used as a slice size in make() against a maximum allowed value before allocation.
quote: Using untrusted input to allocate slices with the built-in make function could lead to excessive memory allocation
code_bad: result := make([]string, collectionSize)

subject: Velocity template injection via user-controlled template string
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input as the template string to Velocity.evaluate(); use a fixed/constant template string instead.
quote: code is controlled by the user
code_bad: Velocity.evaluate(context, w, "mystring", code);
code_good: Velocity.evaluate(context, w, "mystring", s); // GOOD: s is a constant string

subject: Spring ResponseEntity redirect with user-controlled Location header
truth: Stop setting the Location header or URI from user input directly; validate the redirect target against a server-side allowlist.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into a URL redirect request without validating the input can facilitate phishing attacks.

subject: Rejection sampling to fix modulo bias
truth: When you must use modulo on crypto random bytes, discard bytes >= 250 so the modulo operates on a uniformly random 0-249 range.
quote: the random byte is discarded if the value is greater than or equal to 250

subject: X-Forwarded-For first entry vs last entry
truth: Do not use the first comma-separated value of X-Forwarded-For; use the last entry which is appended by a trusted reverse proxy.
quote: the last entry of a X-Forwarded-For header value may be more trustworthy than the rest of it
code_good:
client_ip = request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for')
client_ip = client_ip.split(',')[client_ip.split(',').length - 1]
if not client_ip == '127.0.0.1':
    raise Exception('ip illegal')

subject: Core Data sensitive data storage
truth: Stop storing sensitive information in cleartext via Core Data setValue; encrypt it first using a modern algorithm like AES-GCM.
quote: BAD: sensitive information saved in cleartext
code_bad: databaseObject.setValue(creditCardNo, forKey: "myCreditCardNo")
code_good: databaseObject.setValue(encrypt(creditCardNo, encryptionKey), forKey: "myCreditCardNo")

subject: PendingIntent.getBroadcast with implicit intent and no flags
truth: Stop using implicit intents with PendingIntent.getBroadcast and no flags; always set FLAG_IMMUTABLE and use an explicit intent.
quote: No flags - mutable by default on API < 31
code_bad:
val broadcastIntent = Intent("com.example.CUSTOM_ACTION")
val broadcastPendingIntent = PendingIntent.getBroadcast(
    context,
    2,
    broadcastIntent,
    0  // No flags - mutable by default on API < 31
)

subject: insecure randomness in file upload filename generation
truth: Stop using Math.random() for filename generation in file uploads; use crypto.randomBytes(16) as the library's internal default.
quote: A file upload library's README generates filenames with `Math.random()` while the library's own default uses `crypto.randomBytes(16)`.
code_bad: Math.random()
code_good: crypto.randomBytes(16)
source: OWASP CS: NPM Security best practices
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SHA-1 hashing in gitoxide
truth: Stop using plain SHA-1 without collision detection; use sha1collisiondetection (sha1_checked) to detect known SHA-1 collisions.
quote: gitoxide uses the `sha1_smol` or `sha1` crate, both of which implement standard SHA-1 without any mitigations for collision attacks.

subject: dlopen with user input
truth: Stop passing user input directly to dlopen; validate the input against a whitelist of hard-coded library paths before loading.
quote: the user can cause arbitrary code to be loaded
code_bad: void* handle = dlopen(lib, RTLD_LAZY);

subject: Netty DefaultHttpRequest constructor
truth: Always use the DefaultHttpRequest(version, method, uri) constructor without the boolean parameter to enable header validation.
quote: Disables the internal request splitting verification
code_bad: private final DefaultHttpRequest badRequest = new DefaultHttpRequest(httpVersion, method, uri, false);
code_good: private final DefaultHttpRequest goodResponse = new DefaultHttpRequest(httpVersion, method, uri);

subject: Multiplication overflow guard pattern
truth: Guard multiplication by checking the operand is less than MAX_VALUE divided by the multiplier before performing the operation.
quote: use a guard to ensure no overflows occur
code_bad: int scaled = data * 10;

subject: PreferenceActivity isValidFragment
truth: Stop overriding isValidFragment to return true unconditionally; only return true for whitelisted trusted fragment class names.
quote: BAD: any Fragment name can be provided.

subject: Apache Shiro permissions check with user-controlled data
truth: Stop concatenating user input into permission strings; use fixed permission checks that do not depend on user-controlled data.
quote: do not use data that is controlled by that user in the permissions check
code_bad:
if(subject.isPermitted("domain:sublevel:" + whatDoTheyWantToDo))
	doIt();
code_good:
if(subject.isPermitted("domain:sublevel:whatTheMethodDoes"))
	doIt();

subject: git ls-remote with user-controlled URL
truth: Validate that URLs start with a known safe prefix like 'git@' or 'https://' before passing them to git ls-remote via execFile.
quote: Some shell commands, like git ls-remote, can execute arbitrary commands if a user provides a malicious URL that starts with --upload-pack.
code_bad:
const remote = req.query.remote;
cp.execFile("git", ["ls-remote", remote]); // NOT OK

subject: Sensitive data in GET query parameters
truth: Stop reading sensitive information (passwords, tokens, secrets) from GET query parameters; use POST with request body instead.
quote: Sensitive information such as user passwords should not be transmitted within the query string of the requested URL

subject: setTimeout with user-controlled delay
truth: Always validate and limit user-controlled delay values passed to setTimeout to prevent indefinite waits and memory exhaustion.
quote: a large delay essentially makes the application wait indefinitely before executing the function
code_bad: setTimeout(f, delay); // BAD

subject: Unicode normalization without input size limit
truth: Limit the size of any incoming data before passing it through NFKC or NFKD Unicode normalization to prevent denial of service.
quote: Ensure limiting the size of any incoming data that would go through a costly operations, including a Windows Unicode normalization with NFKC or NFKD.

subject: SQLite3 C API SQL injection in Swift
truth: Stop using sqlite3_exec with string-interpolated queries; use sqlite3_prepare_v2 with sqlite3_bind_text for parameter binding.
quote: A better way to do this is with a prepared statement, binding userControlledString to a parameter of that statement.
code_bad: let result = sqlite3_exec(db, unsafeQuery, nil, nil, nil) // BAD

subject: Path Traversal via direct file_get_contents in PHP
truth: Never pass user-controlled input directly to file_get_contents(); use an allowlist mapping of safe keys to file paths instead.
quote: The product uses external input to construct a pathname
code_bad:
$filename = $_GET['file']; // User-controlled input

echo file_get_contents($filename); // Read and display the file contents
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: read a file from GET parameter

subject: Dynamic class instantiation from user input
truth: Never instantiate classes using user-controlled strings; use an allowlist mapping of expected values to known classes instead.
quote: check the input against an allowlist of acceptable values
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Android insecure random for authentication tokens
truth: Stop using java.util.Random for security-relevant contexts like authentication tokens; use java.security.SecureRandom instead.
quote: The app insecurely uses random numbers for generating authentication tokens.
code_bad: val random1 = Random().nextDouble()
code_good: val random4 = SecureRandom().nextInt(21)

subject: Spring @ExceptionHandler returning exception details
truth: Stop leaking exception details in error responses; use ProblemDetail with a generic message and log the exception server-side.
quote: return ProblemDetail.forStatusAndDetail(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, "An error occur, please retry");
source: OWASP CS: Error Handling Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Node.js Docker CMD patterns
truth: Stop using npm/yarn/scripts as CMD; invoke node directly with an init system like dumb-init to handle PID 1 signal forwarding.
quote: Nothing happened, right? That’s because the npm client doesn’t forward any signals to the node process that it spawned.
code_bad: CMD "npm" "start"
code_good: CMD ["dumb-init", "node", "server.js"]
source: OWASP CS: Node.js Docker Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SQL injection via string interpolation in ActiveRecord where clause
truth: Stop building SQL queries with string interpolation; use parameterized queries with '?' placeholders and sanitize LIKE inputs.
quote: The statement is injectable because the name parameter is not escaped.
code_bad: @projects = Project.where("name like '" + name + "'");
code_good: @projects = Project.where("name like ?", "%#{ActiveRecord::Base.sanitize_sql_like(params[:name])}%")
source: OWASP CS: Ruby on Rails Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Array index validation in C/C++
truth: Always validate array index bounds (i < 0 || ndata <= i) before indexing when the index is derived from user-controlled data.
quote: Always check the bounds of array indexing expressions, especially if the index value is derived from user-controlled data.

subject: Using fputs/fwrite/fprintf directly on sensitive buffers
truth: Stop writing cleartext sensitive data with fputs/fwrite; encrypt the buffer first using crypto_secretbox_easy before writing.
quote: Sensitive information that is stored unencrypted is accessible to an attacker who gains access to the storage
code_bad: fputs(cleartextCredentials, file);
code_good: fwrite(ciphertext, 1, ciphertext_len, file);

subject: integer overflow in size computation leading to negative allocation
truth: Validate the size of the data from which the numbers are computed before performing arithmetic that feeds into an allocation.
quote: When passed a value whose JSON encoding is close to the maximum value of type int in length, the computation of size will overflow, producing a negative value

subject: MAC/signature comparison in Java
truth: Stop using Arrays.equals() for MAC or digital signature validation; use MessageDigest.isEqual() for constant-time comparison.
quote: A constant-time algorithm should be used for checking a MAC or a digital signature
code_bad: return Arrays.equals(signature, actual);
code_good: return MessageDigest.isEqual(signature, actual);

subject: JSONP callback parameter
truth: Stop using user-controlled callback parameter directly in JSONP response without Referer/Origin or random token verification.
quote: The software uses external input as the function name to wrap JSON data and returns it to the client as a request response. When there is a cross-domain problem, this could lead to information leakage.

subject: HTML entity decoding order
truth: When decoding HTML entities, decode & last (not first) to prevent double-unescaping that can reintroduce injected markup.
quote: the decoding function should decode the ampersand last

subject: HTML meta-character sanitization completeness
truth: Do not assume sanitizing only < and > is sufficient; when the value is placed in an HTML attribute, " must also be sanitized.
quote: some programs only sanitize < and > since those are the most common dangerous characters
code_bad: id = id.replace(/<|>/g, ""); // BAD
code_good: id = id.replace(/<|>|&|"/g, ""); // GOOD

subject: Multi-character regex sanitization
truth: Stop applying a multi-character regex replacement only once; apply it repeatedly until no more replacements can be performed.
quote: when the regular expression matches multiple consecutive characters, replacing it just once can result in the unsafe text reappearing in the sanitized input

subject: Express.js redirect with user input
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly to res.redirect(); validate against a whitelist or check the host stays the same.
quote: a request parameter is incorporated without validation into a URL redirect

subject: Regular expression with ambiguous alternation inside repetition
truth: Avoid alternations inside repetitions where branches can match the same string in multiple ways; rewrite to remove ambiguity.
quote: contains a repetition of the form r* or r+ where the sub-expression r is ambiguous

subject: WKWebView password input
truth: Stop using HTML <input type='password'> inside a WKWebView; overlay a native UITextField with isSecureTextEntry=true instead.
quote: Any JavaScript running on the page, including injected XSS payloads, can read it with `document.querySelector('input[type=password]').value`.
code_bad: <input type="password">
code_good:
let secureField = UITextField(frame: frame)
secureField.isSecureTextEntry = true
secureField.borderStyle = .roundedRect
secureField.becomeFirstResponder()

webView.superview?.addSubview(secureField)

subject: Spring MVC block-listing approach for mass assignment
truth: Prefer allow-listing with setAllowedFields() over block-listing with setDisallowedFields() to avoid missing dangerous fields.
quote: binder.setDisallowedFields(["isAdmin"]);
code_bad: binder.setDisallowedFields(["isAdmin"]);
code_good: binder.setAllowedFields(["userid","password","email"]);
source: OWASP CS: Mass Assignment Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: PHP escapeshellcmd vs escapeshellarg for user input
truth: Stop using escapeshellcmd() when user input is a single argument; use escapeshellarg() instead to prevent argument injection.
quote: It is always preferable to use `escapeshellarg()` rather than `escapeshellcmd()` when dealing with user input.
source: OWASP CS: OS Command Injection Defense Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: umask(0666) with chmod(pathname, 0666)
truth: Do not set umask to the same value as the chmod mode; the umask will zero out all permissions, making the file inaccessible.
quote:
umask(0666);
chmod(pathname, 0666); // BAD
code_bad:
umask(0666);
chmod(pathname, 0666); // BAD
code_good:
umask(0022);
chmod(pathname, 0666); // GOOD

subject: switch case value out of range
truth: Stop writing switch cases with values that cannot occur given the condition guarding the switch; the case will be dead code.
quote: the code will not be executed

subject: Double descriptor close after socket()
truth: After closing a file descriptor, always set it to -1 to prevent double-close; never guard with if(fs) because fd 0 is valid.
quote: if(fs) close(fs); // BAD
code_bad:
close(fs);
  if(fs) close(fs); // BAD
code_good:
close(fs);
  fs = -1; // GOOD

subject: Go command injection via git clone flags
truth: Stop passing user input directly to git clone; add a -- argument before the user-controlled value to prevent flag injection.
quote: add a -- argument to ensure subsequent arguments are not interpreted as flags
code_bad: cmd := exec.Command("git", "clone", repoURL, outputPath)
code_good: cmd := exec.Command("git", "clone", "--", repoURL, outputPath)

subject: Stored XSS in Go HTTP responses
truth: Always use html.EscapeString() when writing externally-controlled stored values (file names, DB contents) to HTTP responses.
quote: Directly using externally-controlled stored values (for example, file names or database contents) to create HTML content without properly sanitizing the input first, allows for a cross-site scripting
code_bad: io.WriteString(w, file.Name()+"\n")
code_good: io.WriteString(w, html.EscapeString(file.Name())+"\n")

subject: Netty DefaultHttpHeaders constructor
truth: Always use the DefaultHttpHeaders() constructor without arguments to enable CRLF validation; never pass false to disable it.
quote: Disables the internal response splitting verification
code_bad: private final DefaultHttpHeaders badHeaders = new DefaultHttpHeaders(false);
code_good: private final DefaultHttpHeaders goodHeaders = new DefaultHttpHeaders();

subject: Android WebView content access
truth: Stop enabling setAllowContentAccess(true); call setAllowContentAccess(false) to block content:// URL access from JavaScript.
quote: Allowing access to content providers via content:// URLs may allow JavaScript to access protected content.

subject: Android WebView SSL error handler
truth: Never call SslErrorHandler.proceed() unconditionally in onReceivedSslError; always verify the certificate before proceeding.
quote: If the onReceivedSslError method of an Android WebViewClient always calls proceed on the given SslErrorHandler, it trusts any certificate.

subject: File path construction from HTTP request parameters
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into file paths; validate the path against allowed prefixes and block path traversal.
quote: no file download validation
code_good: if (!filePath.contains("..") && filePath.hasPrefix("/pages")) { ... }

subject: Servlet exception handling
truth: Catch IOExceptions and RuntimeExceptions in servlet doGet/doPost methods instead of letting them propagate to the container.
quote: Failure to catch exceptions in a servlet could lead to exposure of sensitive information because when a servlet throws an exception, the servlet container typically sends debugging information back to the user.

subject: Zip extraction without path validation
truth: Stop writing files from zip entries without checking that the entry path does not contain '..' directory traversal elements.
quote: Extracting files from a malicious zip file, or similar type of archive, is at risk of directory traversal attacks if filenames from the archive are not properly validated.

subject: Modulo bias on crypto random bytes
truth: Stop using modulo (%) on crypto.randomBytes values; the result is biased because 256 is not evenly divisible by most ranges.
quote: using some mathematical operations on these cryptographically secure random numbers can create biased results
code_good:
const cryptoRandomString = require('crypto-random-string');

const digits = cryptoRandomString({length: 10, type: 'numeric'});

subject: Django SECRET_KEY hardcoded constant
truth: Stop hardcoding constant SECRET_KEY values in Django; use get_random_secret_key() from django.core.management.utils instead.
quote: Unsafe Django SECRET_KEY Example
code_bad:
settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    SECRET_KEY="constant1",
    ROOT_URLCONF=__name__,
)
global_settings.SECRET_KEY = "constant2"
settings.SECRET_KEY = "constant3"
code_good:
settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    SECRET_KEY=get_random_secret_key(),
    ROOT_URLCONF=__name__,
)
global_settings.SECRET_KEY = get_random_secret_key()
settings.SECRET_KEY = get_random_secret_key()

subject: SQL injection in Rust with SQLx
truth: Stop building SQL queries with format!() or string concatenation; use sqlx::query() with ? placeholders and .bind() instead.
quote: If a database query (such as an SQL query) is built from user-provided data without sufficient sanitization, a user may be able to run malicious database queries.
code_good:
let prepared_query = "SELECT * FROM people WHERE firstname=?";

let _ = sqlx::query(prepared_query_1).bind(&remote_controlled_string).fetch_all(&mut conn).await?; // GOOD (prepared statement with bound parameter)

subject: Hard-coded cryptographic keys in Rust
truth: Stop using hard-coded arrays of zeros or static bytes as cryptographic keys; use Aes256Gcm::generate_key with OsRng instead.
quote: Using hard-coded keys for encryption
code_bad:
let key: [u8;32] = [0;32]; // BAD: Using hard-coded keys for encryption
let cipher = Aes256Gcm::new(&key.into());
code_good:
let key = Aes256Gcm::generate_key(aes_gcm::aead::OsRng); // GOOD: Using randomly generated keys for encryption
let cipher = Aes256Gcm::new(&key);

subject: SMTP command/header injection via taint analysis
truth: Stop passing unsanitized user input directly to smtp.SendMail or smtp.Client.Mail/Rcpt; parse addresses with net/mail first.
quote: SMTP command/header injection via taint analysis

subject: WKWebView loadFileURL allowingReadAccessTo broad scope
truth: Stop granting read access to a broad directory with allowingReadAccessTo; restrict it to the specific file URL being loaded.
quote: - `allowingReadAccessTo` is set to the `demoRoot` directory under the app's caches directory `.../Library/Caches/demoRoot/`.
code_bad: webView.loadFileURL(indexURL, allowingReadAccessTo: demoRoot)
code_good: webView.loadFileURL(indexURL, allowingReadAccessTo: indexURL)

subject: WebView debugging enabled in production
truth: Stop enabling WebView.setWebContentsDebuggingEnabled unconditionally; guard it behind ApplicationInfo.FLAG_DEBUGGABLE check.
quote: - Set `WebView.setWebContentsDebuggingEnabled` to `false` in production, or remove the calls entirely if they are unnecessary.
code_bad: WebView.setWebContentsDebuggingEnabled(true);

subject: Arrays.fill zeroes optimized away by compiler in Android
truth: Do not rely on Arrays.fill with zeroes to clear secrets; the compiler may optimize away the write, leaving data on the heap.
quote: This doesn't, however, guarantee that the content will be overwritten at runtime. To optimize the bytecode, the compiler will analyze and decide not to overwrite data because it will not be used afterwards

subject: Insecure Direct Object Reference in .NET
truth: Stop returning a resource by ID without checking the user's ownership; add an authorization check before returning the view.
quote: When you have a resource (object) which can be accessed by a reference (in the sample below this is the `id`), you need to ensure that the user is intended to have access to that resource.
source: OWASP CS: DotNet Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Raw Query SQL Injection
truth: Stop concatenating user input into raw SQL strings; always use SQL bindings (positional ? or named :param) for request data.
quote: Always remember to use SQL bindings for request data
code_bad: User::whereRaw('email = "'.$request->input('email').'"')->get();
code_good: User::whereRaw('email = ?', [$request->input('email')])->get();
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: unsigned subtraction compared to zero in loop condition
truth: Stop comparing the result of unsigned subtraction to zero; use a direct comparison between the two unsigned values instead.
quote: the value of an unsigned subtraction can never be negative

subject: Exposing system data (PATH) in error messages
truth: Stop sending environment variable values like PATH in error messages to remote users; send a generic error message instead.
quote: Do not expose system configuration information to remote users

subject: Exposing system data (environment variables) in error messages
truth: Stop printing environment variable values (like PATH or APP_KEY) directly in error messages; use a generic message instead.
quote: the value of the PATH environment variable is revealed in full to the user when a particular error occurs

subject: Xerces-C++ XML parser XXE
truth: Stop using XercesDOMParser in its default state; call setDisableDefaultEntityResolution(true) before parsing untrusted XML.
quote: parser is constructed in its default state with setDisableDefaultEntityResolution set to false
code_bad:
XercesDOMParser *parser = new XercesDOMParser();

parser->parse(data); // BAD (parser is not correctly configured, may expand external entity references)

subject: Partial path traversal via getCanonicalPath()
truth: getCanonicalPath() returns a non-slash-terminated string, so startsWith() can match sibling directories, not just children.
quote: users that supply dir may be also allowed to access siblings of parent and not just children of parent

subject: Executing external commands
truth: Execute external commands using an array of strings rather than a single string to avoid command injection vulnerabilities.
quote: Execute external commands using an array of strings rather than a single string

subject: array index validation
truth: Always validate that a user-provided array index is non-negative and less than the array length before accessing the array.
quote: The index used in the array access should be checked against the bounds of the array before being used
code_bad: String productDescription = productDescriptions[productID];

subject: HostnameVerifier always returning true
truth: Stop using a HostnameVerifier that always returns true; verify the certificate hostname against the peer certificate chain.
quote: If a HostnameVerifier always returns true it will not verify the hostname at all

subject: Trust boundary violation — crossing from user to session
truth: Treat the HTTP session as a trusted boundary; validate all data from request parameters before storing them in the session.
quote: A trust boundary violation occurs when a value is passed from a less trusted context to a more trusted context.

subject: Paramiko default host key policy
truth: Stop using AutoAddPolicy or WarningPolicy for Paramiko; the secure default is RejectPolicy which rejects unknown host keys.
quote: The default setting of RejectPolicy is secure because it throws an exception when it encounters an unknown host key.
code_bad: client.set_missing_host_key_policy(AutoAddPolicy)
code_good: client.set_missing_host_key_policy(RejectPolicy)

subject: Agent instructions with user input
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into agent instructions; use a guardrail agent that automatically sanitizes prompts.
quote: user input in agent instruction
code_bad:
badAgent = Agent(
    name="Assistant",
    instructions="This prompt is customized for " + input  # BAD: user input in agent instruction.
)
code_good:
goodAgent = GuardrailAgent(  # GOOD: Agent created with guardrails automatically configured.
    config=Path("guardrails_config.json"),
    name="Assistant",
    instructions="This prompt is customized for " + input)

subject: Ruby format string with interpolated user input
truth: Stop interpolating untrusted data directly into format strings; use %s specifiers and pass the data as an argument instead.
quote: If the format string contains unsanitized input from an untrusted source, then that string may contain unexpected format specifiers that cause garbled output or throw an exception.
code_bad: printf("Unauthorised access attempt by #{params[:user]}: %s", request.ip)
code_good: printf("Unauthorised access attempt by %s: %s", params[:user], request.ip)

subject: typed except with continue in loop
truth: Even typed 'except SomeError: continue' is bad practice unless explicitly configured; prefer logging or handling the error.
quote: This pattern is considered bad practice in general, but also represents a potential security issue

subject: Integer type conversion without overflow check
truth: Stop converting a larger integer type to a smaller type without first checking the value is within the target type's range.
quote: Type conversion which leads to integer overflow
code_bad:
var a uint32 = math.MaxUint32
b := int32(a)
code_good:
var a uint16 = math.MaxUint16
b := int32(a)

subject: SwiftUI SecureField vs UITextField for sensitive input
truth: Stop using UITextField for passwords; use SwiftUI SecureField which masks input by design with no isSecureTextEntry toggle.
quote: SwiftUI's `SecureField` always masks input by design — there is no `isSecureTextEntry` setter involved.
code_bad:
alert.addTextField { tf in
    tf.placeholder = "Password"
    tf.isSecureTextEntry = false
    tf.accessibilityIdentifier = "password_field"
}
code_good:
SecureField("OTP 2", text: $otp2)
    .keyboardType(.numberPad)
    .textFieldStyle(RoundedBorderTextFieldStyle())
    .accessibilityIdentifier("otp_2_field")
    .padding()

subject: Deep link parameter parsing without type validation
truth: Convert numeric deep link parameters with toLongOrNull() or toIntOrNull() and reject nulls instead of using the raw string.
quote: Never use the raw string returned by `getQueryParameter()` directly in an operation that expects a specific type.
code_bad: val amount = uri.getQueryParameter("amount")
code_good:
val amount = uri.getQueryParameter("amount")?.toLongOrNull()
    ?: return  // reject missing or non-numeric values

subject: buffer overflow via gets()
truth: Do not call gets() — it was exploited by the 1988 Internet Worm and is impossible to use safely regardless of buffer size.
quote: one of the vulnerabilities exploited by the Internet Worm of 1988

subject: Wrapper functions that nullify pointers after close
truth: Stop calling fclose directly; use the wrapper that nullifies the pointer after successful close to prevent use-after-free.
quote: The presence of a shell function with additional check indicates the possible risks of the call.
code_bad: fclose(fe); // BAD
code_good: myFclose(fe); // GOOD

subject: XmlWriter.WriteRaw with user input
truth: Stop using WriteRaw with unsanitized user input; use WriteElementString or escape input with SecurityElement.Escape first.
quote: Avoid using the WriteRaw method on System.Xml.XmlWriter with user input
code_bad: writer.WriteRaw("<employee><name>" + employeeName + "</name></employee>");
code_good: writer.WriteRaw("<employee><name>" + SecurityElement.Escape(employeeName) + "</name></employee>");

subject: Android EditText password field
truth: Stop using android:inputType="text" for password fields; use android:inputType="textPassword" to prevent keyboard caching.
quote: BAD: This password field uses the `text` input type, which allows the input to be saved to the keyboard cache.
code_bad:
<EditText
        android:id="@+id/password_bad"
        android:inputType="text"/>
code_good:
<EditText
        android:id="@+id/password_good"
        android:inputType="textPassword"/>

subject: URL forward validation pattern
truth: Do not validate by checking if input starts with or contains a prefix; use exact equality against a server-side whitelist.
quote: you should maintain a list of authorized URLs on the server, then choose from that list based on the user input provided
code_bad: sc.getRequestDispatcher(request.getParameter("target")).forward(request, response);

subject: Plaintext password in configuration file
truth: Stop storing plaintext passwords in configuration files; use a password vault or keystore to encrypt and manage passwords.
quote: Storing a plaintext password in a configuration file allows anyone who can read the file to access the password-protected resources.
code_bad:
<Resource name="jdbc/exampleDS" auth="Container" type="javax.sql.DataSource"
               maxTotal="100" maxIdle="30" maxWaitMillis="10000"
               username="root" password="1234"
               driverClassName="com.mysql.jdbc.Driver"
               url="jdbc:mysql://www.example.com:3306/proj"/>
code_good:
<Resource name="jdbc/exampleDS" auth="Container" type="javax.sql.DataSource"
               maxTotal="100" maxIdle="30" maxWaitMillis="10000"
               username="root" password="${VAULT::exampleDS::password::N2NhZDYzOTMtNWE0OS00ZGQ0LWE4MmEtMWNlMDMyNDdmNmI2TElORV9CUkVBS3ZhdWx0}"
               driverClassName="com.mysql.jdbc.Driver"
               url="jdbc:mysql://www.example.com:3306/proj"/>

subject: Express.js reflected XSS
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into res.send() strings; import escape-html and wrap every interpolated user value.
quote: contextual output encoding/escaping before writing user input to the response
code_bad: res.send("Unknown user: " + req.params.id);
code_good: res.send("Unknown user: " + escape(req.params.id));

subject: SSL/TLS protocol version in Python
truth: Stop using SSLv2, SSLv3, TLSv1.0, or TLSv1.1; use TLSv1.2 or above via ssl.SSLContext with minimum_version or OP_NO flags.
quote: Using a broken or weak cryptographic protocol may make a connection vulnerable to interference from an attacker.
code_bad: ssl.wrap_socket(socket.socket(), ssl_version=ssl.PROTOCOL_SSLv2)
code_good:
context = ssl.create_default_context()
context.minimum_version = ssl.TLSVersion.TLSv1_2

subject: temporary file creation with mktemp
truth: Stop using mktemp (deprecated since Python 2.3); use NamedTemporaryFile or TemporaryFile from the tempfile module instead.
quote: Functions that create temporary file names (such as tempfile.mktemp and os.tempnam) are fundamentally insecure, as they do not ensure exclusive access to a file with the temporary name they return.
code_bad:
from tempfile import mktemp

def write_results(results):
    filename = mktemp()
    with open(filename, "w+") as f:
        f.write(results)
    print("Results written to", filename)
code_good:
from tempfile import NamedTemporaryFile

def write_results(results):
    with NamedTemporaryFile(mode="w+", delete=False) as f:
        f.write(results)
    print("Results written to", f.name)

subject: Prompt injection via string concatenation
truth: Do not build agent instructions by concatenating unsanitized user input; use a guardrail agent with a config file instead.
quote: Prompts can be constructed to bypass the original purposes of an agent
code_bad:
badAgent = Agent(
    name="Assistant",
    instructions="This prompt is customized for " + input  # BAD: user input in agent instruction.
)
code_good:
goodAgent = GuardrailAgent(  # GOOD: Agent created with guardrails automatically configured.
    config=Path("guardrails_config.json"),
    name="Assistant",
    instructions="This prompt is customized for " + input)

subject: httpx.Client with verify=False
truth: Stop passing verify=False to httpx.Client or httpx.AsyncClient; always pass verify=True to enforce certificate validation.
quote: Call to requests with verify=False disabling SSL certificate checks, security issue.
code_bad: httpx.Client(timeout=30, verify=False)
code_good: httpx.Client(timeout=30)

subject: HTTP response header conflict
truth: Never set both Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length headers on the same response; doing so enables HTTP request smuggling.
quote: HTTP request smuggling via conflicting headers or bare LF in body parsing
code_bad:
w.Header().Set("Transfer-Encoding", "chunked")
w.Header().Set("Content-Length", "100")
code_good:
w.Header().Set("Content-Length", "100")
w.Write([]byte("response body"))

subject: WebSocket JSON parsing with eval
truth: Always use JSON.parse() instead of eval() for WebSocket message processing to prevent code execution from untrusted input.
quote: **Always use `JSON.parse()` instead of `eval()`** for JSON processing - `eval()` enables code execution from untrusted input.
code_bad: const message = eval('(' + data + ')');
code_good: const message = JSON.parse(data);
source: OWASP CS: WebSocket Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Storing sensitive data to file in C
truth: Stop writing sensitive information to disk in cleartext; encrypt it using a library like libsodium before calling fwrite.
quote: write password to disk in cleartext

subject: Pointer arithmetic scaling in C/C++
truth: Stop manually multiplying by sizeof(T) in pointer arithmetic; the offset is already automatically scaled by the compiler.
quote: the offset is already automatically scaled by sizeof(int),
code_bad: return *(intPointer + (i * sizeof(int)));
code_good: return *(intPointer + i);

subject: Password reset token leak via Host header injection
truth: Stop embedding the HTTP Host header directly into password reset email bodies; use a trusted base URL from configuration.
quote: If the email contains a password reset link, and the victim clicks the link, the secret reset token will be leaked to the attacker
code_bad:
host := r.Header.Get("Host")
token := backend.getUserSecretResetToken(email)
body := "Click to reset password: " + host + "/" + token
code_good:
host := config.Get("Host")
token := backend.getUserSecretResetToken(email)
body := "Click to reset password: " + host + "/" + token

subject: Hashing credentials before storage
truth: Hash credentials with a salt before storing them; do not concatenate raw username and password into a string for storage.
quote: the credentials are hashed before storing them
code_bad: data = credentials.getUserName() + ":" + new String(credentials.getPassword());

subject: MessageDigest.digest called directly on password bytes
truth: Stop calling md.digest(password.getBytes()) directly; first call md.update(salt) with a random salt of at least 32 bytes.
quote: Use a long random salt of at least 32 bytes then use the combination of password and salt to hash a password or password phrase.
code_bad: byte[] messageDigest = md.digest(password.getBytes());
code_good:
md.update(salt);
byte[] messageDigest = md.digest(password.getBytes());

subject: String.replace with string argument for sanitization
truth: Stop using string.replace with a string argument for escaping; use a regular expression with the global (g) flag instead.
quote: if the first argument to replace is a string literal (as in this case), only the first occurrence of that string is replaced

subject: Rails html_safe method
truth: Never call .html_safe on user-controlled input; only use it on strings that have been manually escaped or are hard-coded.
quote: Take care when using methods such as html_safe or raw
code_bad: Hello <%= params[:user_name].html_safe %>!
code_good: Hello <%= params[:user_name] %>!

subject: hashlib.new with uppercase weak algorithm names
truth: Stop using hashlib.new with uppercase variants like 'MD4', 'SHA', or 'SHA1'; they are still insecure and will be flagged.
quote: Use of weak MD4, MD5, or SHA1 hash for security
code_bad: hashlib.new('MD4', data=b'test')
code_good: hashlib.new('sha256')

subject: Path traversal via taint analysis in Go
truth: Stop using unsanitized user input as file paths; use filepath.Clean, path.Base, or numeric ID mapping to restrict access.
quote: gosec rule G703: Path traversal via taint analysis
code_bad:
filename := r.FormValue("name")
os.WriteFile(filename, []byte("data"), 0644)
code_good:
id := r.FormValue("id")
num, _ := strconv.Atoi(id)
os.Open("/tmp/file" + strconv.Itoa(num))

subject: strncpy without null-termination
truth: Always null-terminate buffers after strncpy by subtracting 1 from the size and manually setting the last element to '\0'.
quote: may cause off-by-one overflow
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: copy user input to fixed buffer

subject: integer overflow when summing shorts into a short
truth: Use a larger integer type (long) for the sum of multiple short values to avoid wraparound before the calculation is used.
quote: integer overflow or wraparound
code_bad: short quarterSold = JanSold + FebSold + MarSold;
code_good: long quarterSold = JanSold + FebSold + MarSold;
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Java LDAP query construction with string concatenation
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into LDAP filter strings; use parameterized filters with {0} placeholders instead.
quote: User input directly concatenated into the filter
source: OWASP CS: LDAP Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Pointer arithmetic past allocation end
truth: Stop computing a pointer one-past-the-end and dereferencing it; subtract one before dereferencing to stay within bounds.
quote: dereferenced, which results in an out-of-bounds write

subject: TOCTOU filesystem race (check-then-use file name)
truth: Stop checking a file by name then operating on it; use functions that operate on file descriptors to avoid TOCTOU races.
quote: the time between the check and the operation, the underlying file referenced by the file name could be changed by an attacker

associative: time of check time of use

subject: new(std::nothrow) with try/catch instead of null check
truth: Stop wrapping 'new(std::nothrow) T' in try/catch for std::bad_alloc; it never throws, so check for null pointer instead.
quote: the allocation won't throw an exception, but

subject: C-style casts and static_cast on polymorphic types
truth: Stop using static_cast (or C-style casts) to downcast polymorphic types; use dynamic_cast and check the result for null.
quote: If possible, use dynamic_cast to safely cast between polymorphic types

subject: SSL_shutdown must be called twice (first returns 0, second returns 1) to complete a bidirectional shutdown
truth: Call SSL_shutdown twice: the first call returns 0 (waiting for peer close-notify), the second call returns 1 (complete).
quote: Incorrect closing of the connection leads to the creation of different states for the server and client, which can be exploited by an attacker.
code_bad:
SSL_shutdown(ssl); 
SSL_shutdown(ssl); // BAD

subject: ASP.NET debug mode in production
truth: Set debug="false" or remove the debug attribute from the <compilation> element in Web.config for production deployments.
quote: Remove the 'debug' flag from the Web.config file if this configuration is likely to be used in production.
code_bad:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <compilation
      defaultLanguage="c#"
      debug="true"
    />
   ...
  </system.web>
</configuration>
code_good:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <compilation
      defaultLanguage="c#"
    />
   ...
  </system.web>
</configuration>

subject: URL scheme validation in Go
truth: Stop only checking for 'javascript' scheme; also check for 'data' and 'vbscript' schemes when sanitizing untrusted URLs.
quote: any validation logic that checks against javascript, but not against data and vbscript, is likely to be insufficient

subject: Email injection via untrusted Host header
truth: Stop using r.Header.Get("Host") to construct email bodies; load the host name from a trusted configuration file instead.
quote: Using untrusted input to construct an email can cause multiple security vulnerabilities
code_bad:
host := r.Header.Get("Host")
token := backend.getUserSecretResetToken(email)
body := "Click to reset password: " + host + "/" + token
smtp.SendMail("test.test", nil, "from@from.com", nil, []byte(body))
code_good:
host := config.Get("Host")
token := backend.getUserSecretResetToken(email)
body := "Click to reset password: " + host + "/" + token
smtp.SendMail("test.test", nil, "from@from.com", nil, []byte(body))

subject: CSV injection via untrusted input
truth: Sanitize CSV output by prefixing entries that start with =, +, -, or @ with a single quote to prevent formula injection.
quote: entries beginning with = as formulas, which may attempt information exfiltration
code_bad:
csv_data = request.args.get('csv')
csvWriter = csv.writer(open("test.csv", "wt"))
csvWriter.writerow(csv_data)
code_good:
csv_data = request.args.get('csv')
csvWriter = csv.writer(open("test.csv", "wt"))
csvWriter.writerow(sanitize_for_csv(csv_data))

subject: SSRF via user-controlled URL in http.Get
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly to http.Get; validate and restrict URLs to an allowlist of known-safe hosts.
quote:
url := r.URL.Query().Get("url")
	http.Get(url)

subject: Open redirect via concatenated user input
truth: Stop concatenating user input into a redirect URL; validate or sanitize the destination before passing to http.Redirect.
quote: Open redirect via taint analysis
code_bad:
dest := r.FormValue("redirect")
	http.Redirect(w, r, "/proxy?to="+dest, http.StatusSeeOther)
code_good: http.Redirect(w, r, fmt.Sprintf("/users/%d", id), http.StatusFound)

subject: HttpOnly cookie flag
truth: Always call setHttpOnly(true) on any cookie containing sensitive data like session IDs before adding it to the response.
quote: Leverage the HttpOnly flag when setting a sensitive cookie in a response
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: generate session id and store in cookie

subject: Verbose Logging Compiled Into iOS Release Builds
truth: Guard print/NSLog statements with #if DEBUG or preprocessor macros so verbose logging is compiled out of release builds.
quote: verbose diagnostics should be compiled out of release builds whenever possible
code_bad: print("Hello world")
code_good:
#if DEBUG
print("Hello world")
#endif

subject: libcurl SSL verification disabled
truth: Stop setting CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER and CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYHOST to 0; set both to 2 to enforce certificate verification.
quote: Disabling verification of the SSL certificate allows man-in-the-middle attacks
code_bad:
curl_easy_setopt(curl.get(), CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER, 0);
	curl_easy_setopt(curl.get(), CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYHOST, 0);
code_good:
curl_easy_setopt(curl.get(), CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER, 2);
	curl_easy_setopt(curl.get(), CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYHOST, 2);

subject: missing comparison operator in if condition
truth: Do not write if(intA+intB) when you mean if(intA+intB>intC); the former is a truthy check on the sum, not a comparison.
quote: possibly no comparison
code_bad: if(intA+intB) return 1;
code_good: if(intA+intB>intC) return 1;

subject: ASP.NET maxRequestLength
truth: Set maxRequestLength to 4096 KB (or lower) instead of a high value like 255000 KB to prevent denial-of-service attacks.
quote: Attackers can use large requests to cause denial-of-service attacks
code_bad:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <httpRuntime maxRequestLength="255000" />
  </system.web>
</configuration>
code_good:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <httpRuntime maxRequestLength="4096" />
  </system.web>
</configuration>

subject: Reflected XSS in Go HTTP handlers
truth: Stop writing user-controlled request parameters directly to HTTP responses; escape them with html.EscapeString() first.
quote: Directly writing user input (for example, an HTTP request parameter) to an HTTP response without properly sanitizing the input first, allows for a cross-site scripting vulnerability.
code_bad: fmt.Fprintf(w, "%q is an unknown user", username)
code_good: fmt.Fprintf(w, "%q is an unknown user", html.EscapeString(username))

subject: Android Intent URI permission reflection via setResult
truth: Never return a user-provided Intent directly via setResult; create a new Intent or sanitize URI permission flags first.
quote: the user-provided Intent is returned as-is

subject: MyBatis XML SQL injection via ${} syntax
truth: Stop using ${xxx} syntax in MyBatis XML mapper files; use #{xxx} parameterized syntax instead to prevent SQL injection.
quote: If the syntax ${param} is used in those statements, and param is under the user's control, attackers can exploit this to tamper with the SQL statements or execute arbitrary SQL commands.
code_bad:
<select id="bad1" parameterType="java.lang.String" resultMap="BaseResultMap">
    -- bad
    select id,name from test where name like '%${name}%'
  </select>
code_good:
<select id="good2" parameterType="java.lang.String" resultMap="BaseResultMap">
    -- good
    select id,name from test where name = #{name}
  </select>

subject: Java ScriptEngine eval with user input
truth: Stop using ScriptEngine.eval() with user-controlled input; use a sandboxed engine or avoid dynamic evaluation entirely.
quote: If an expression is built using attacker-controlled data, and then evaluated in a powerful context, it may allow the attacker to run arbitrary code.
code_good:
// GOOD: enable the safe mode
Scriptable scope = ctx.initSafeStandardObjects();

subject: Missing whitelist validation for method names
truth: Always validate user-controlled method names against a whitelist using hasOwnProperty or a Map check before invocation.
quote: Whitelist the permitted method names or change the type of object the methods are stored on.
code_bad: window[message.name](message.payload);

subject: CORS bypass via startswith on origin
truth: Do not use startswith() to check CORS origins; use exact string comparison (==) to prevent bypass via subdomain tricks.
quote: origin is compared using a startswith call against a list of whitelisted origins. This check can be bypassed easily by origin like domain.com.baddomain.com
code_bad:
if origin.startswith(validCors):
            print("Origin Valid")
code_good:
if origin == validOrigin:
            print("Origin Valid")

subject: Ruby shell command construction with system()
truth: Stop using string interpolation in system() calls; pass arguments as separate parameters to avoid shell interpretation.
quote: Dynamically constructing a shell command with inputs from exported functions may inadvertently change the meaning of the shell command
code_bad: system("wget #{path}")
code_good: system("wget", path)

subject: Rails ERB template output
truth: Stop calling .html_safe on user input in ERB templates; Rails auto-escapes by default, so just output the raw variable.
quote: user-controlled input is emitted without escaping, since it is marked as html_safe
code_bad: Hello <%= params[:user_name].html_safe %>!
code_good: Hello <%= params[:user_name] %>!

subject: path traversal sanitization in Ruby
truth: Stop using a single gsub with /..\// to remove path traversal sequences; use File.sanitize from the facets gem instead.
quote: the remainder of the string then becomes /../, which is another instance of the substring we were trying to remove
code_good:
require 'facets'

def sanitize_path(input)
  File.sanitize(input)
end

subject: Regex with overlapping digit sequences causing polynomial backtracking
truth: Stop using /^0\.\d+E?\d+$/ for number matching; rewrite so the two \d+ sub-expressions do not have overlapping matches.
quote: the second \d+ can start matching digits anywhere after the first match of the first \d+ if there is no E in the input string

subject: Rails SameSite cookie configuration
truth: Stop setting config.action_dispatch.cookies_same_site_protection to :none; set it to :strict or leave the default :lax.
quote: setting the default SameSite attribute sent by the server when setting a cookie to None rather than the default of Lax
code_bad: config.action_dispatch.cookies_same_site_protection = :none
code_good: config.action_dispatch.cookies_same_site_protection = :strict

subject: Rust cookie Secure attribute
truth: Always call .secure(true) on CookieBuilder or set_secure(true) on Cookie to prevent transmission over unencrypted HTTP.
quote: Failing to set the 'Secure' attribute on a cookie allows it to be transmitted over an unencrypted (HTTP) connection

subject: Hardcoded nonce in AES-GCM
truth: Stop using a static nonce with AES-GCM; extract the nonce from the ciphertext or generate it randomly with crypto/rand.
quote: Use of hardcoded IV/nonce for encryption
code_bad: aesGCM.Seal(nil, []byte("ILoveMyNonce"), []byte("My secret message"), nil)
code_good:
nonce, ciphertext := data[:nonceSize], data[nonceSize:]
return gcm.Open(nil, nonce, ciphertext, nil)

subject: Hardcoded byte-slice IV in AES-CTR/OFB
truth: Stop using a literal byte-slice as the IV for cipher.NewCTR or cipher.NewOFB; generate the IV from crypto/rand instead.
quote: Use of hardcoded IV/nonce for encryption
code_good:
iv := make([]byte, 16)
rand.Read(iv)
block, _ := aes.NewCipher([]byte("12345678123456781234567812345678"))
_ = cipher.NewCTR(block, iv)

subject: Array index bounds check
truth: Always validate that an array index is within the valid range (0 <= index < length) before accessing the array element.
quote: does not validate or incorrectly validates the index to ensure the index references a valid position within the array
code_bad:
public String getValue(int index) {
return array[index];}
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: JSON array response for AJAX
truth: Always return JSON with an object on the outside, never a top-level array, to prevent JSON hijacking in older browsers.
quote: Always have the outside primitive be an object for JSON strings
code_bad: [{"object": "inside an array"}]
code_good: {"object": "not inside an array"}
source: OWASP CS: AJAX Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Empty constructor + sensitive fields enables mass assignment
truth: If a domain object has an empty constructor and sensitive fields, an attacker can set those fields via mass assignment.
quote: the object with sensitive fields has an empty constructor
source: OWASP CS: Mass Assignment Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: dead store elimination removing memset
truth: Do not rely on memset to erase secrets; dead store elimination will remove the call if the buffer is never read again.
quote: Because of dead store elimination, the call to memset may be removed by the compiler (since the buffer is not subsequently used)

subject: PAM authorization check
truth: After pam_authenticate, always call pam_acct_mgmt to verify the user is authorized to log in (not just authenticated).
quote: A call to pam_authenticate should be followed by a call to pam_acct_mgmt to check if a user is allowed to login.

subject: Logging user input without sanitization
truth: Stop logging unsanitized user input; replace line breaks using String.Replace(Environment.NewLine, "") before logging.
quote: If unsanitized user input is written to a log entry, a malicious user may be able to forge new log entries
code_bad: logger.Warn(username + " log in requested.");
code_good: logger.Warn(username.Replace(Environment.NewLine, "") + " log in requested");

subject: Integer overflow from user-controlled arithmetic
truth: Always guard arithmetic on user-controlled data with a bounds check against MAX_VALUE before performing the operation.
quote: Performing calculations on user-controlled data can result in integer overflows unless the input is validated.
code_bad: int scaled = data * 10;

subject: Java temp file creation with File.createTempFile
truth: Stop using File.createTempFile() which creates world-readable files; use java.nio.file.Files.createTempFile() instead.
quote: File.createTempFile("random", ".txt"); // BAD: File has permissions `-rw-r--r--`
code_bad: File temp1 = File.createTempFile("random", ".txt"); // BAD: File has permissions `-rw-r--r--`
code_good: Path temp1 = Files.createTempFile("random", ".txt"); // GOOD: File has permissions `-rw-------`

subject: Thread.sleep with user-controlled wait time
truth: Always enforce an upper bound on user-supplied sleep time passed to Thread.sleep to prevent denial-of-service attacks.
quote: no boundary check on wait time
code_bad: Thread.sleep(userSuppliedWaitTime);

subject: Serving entire node_modules directory via express.static
truth: Stop serving the entire node_modules directory; only serve specific subdirectories like jquery/dist or bootstrap/dist.
quote: all the files from the node_modules are served
code_bad: app.use('/node_modules', express.static(path.resolve(__dirname, '../node_modules')));
code_good:
app.use("jquery", express.static('./node_modules/jquery/dist'));
app.use("bootstrap", express.static('./node_modules/bootstrap/dist'));

subject: World-readable temporary files
truth: Stop creating world-readable temp files in os.tmpdir(); use tmp which ensures the file is inaccessible to other users.
quote: The file created above is accessible to other users, and there is no guarantee that the file does not already exist.
code_bad:
const file = path.join(os.tmpdir(), "test-" + (new Date()).getTime() + ".txt");
fs.writeFileSync(file, "content");
code_good:
const file = tmp.fileSync().name;
fs.writeFileSync(file, "content");

subject: node-expat XML parser expands internal entities by default
truth: Stop using node-expat for untrusted XML; use the sax package instead, which only expands standard entities like &.
quote: node-expat expands internal entities by default

subject: Ruby ^ and $ regex anchors
truth: The ^ and $ anchors match line boundaries in multi-line strings, allowing bypass of validation by injecting a newline.
quote: the ^ and $ anchors are used, the regular expression can match a single line of a multi-line string
code_bad:
def bad(input) 
    raise "Bad input" unless input =~ /^[0-9]+$/

    # ....
end
code_good:
def good(input)
    raise "Bad input" unless input =~ /\A[0-9]+\z/

    # ....
end

subject: Jinja2 autoescaping
truth: Stop relying on Jinja2's default autoescape=False; always set autoescape=True or use select_autoescape to prevent XSS.
quote: autoescaping is False by default
code_bad:
Environment(loader=templateLoader,
            load=templateLoader)
code_good:
Environment(loader=templateLoader,
            autoescape=select_autoescape(['html', 'htm', 'xml']))

subject: exec.Command with environment variable in command name
truth: Stop concatenating environment variables into the command name passed to exec.Command; use a hardcoded command string.
quote: // variable is consider as a dangerous user input
code_bad:
run := "sleep" + os.Getenv("SOMETHING")
cmd := exec.Command(run, "5")
code_good:
run := "sleep"
cmd := exec.Command(run, "5")

subject: iOS Network framework (NWConnection) bypasses ATS
truth: Stop using NWConnection with .tcp (cleartext); use NWParameters.tls to create a TLS connection that respects security.
quote: Using Network framework without TLS bypasses ATS
code_bad: let connection = NWConnection(host: host, port: port, using: .tcp)
code_good:
let tlsParameters = NWParameters.tls
let connection = NWConnection(host: host, port: port, using: tlsParameters)

subject: iOS NWEndpoint.Port integerLiteral on port 80
truth: Stop using NWEndpoint.Port with port 80 (cleartext HTTP); use port 443 with NWParameters.tls for secure communication.
quote: The test fails because the app uses `NWEndpoint.Port.integerLiteral` to specify port `80` for a network connection by loading the value `0x50` (which is `80` in decimal) into register `w0` before call
code_bad: let port = NWEndpoint.Port(integerLiteral: 80)
code_good: let port = NWEndpoint.Port(integerLiteral: 443)

subject: Unvalidated redirect via PHP header() with user input
truth: Stop using user-supplied input directly in the Location header; validate the URL against a whitelist or use a mapping.
quote: The following PHP code obtains a URL from the query string (via the parameter named `url`) and then redirects the user to that URL.
code_bad:
$redirect_url = $_GET['url'];
header("Location: " . $redirect_url);
code_good: header("Location: http://www.mysite.com");
source: OWASP CS: Unvalidated Redirects and Forwards Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: chmod with umask arithmetic
truth: Do not subtract the umask mask from permissions; use bitwise AND with complement (~mask) to correctly apply the mask.
quote: fchmod(fileno(fp), 0555 - maskOut); // BAD
code_bad: fchmod(fileno(fp), 0555 - maskOut); // BAD
code_good: fchmod(fileno(fp), 0555 & ~maskOut); // GOOD

subject: SQL query construction via string concatenation
truth: Stop building SQL queries with string concatenation; use a prepared statement with parameters (SqlParameter) instead.
quote: If a SQL query is built using string concatenation, and the components of the concatenation include user input, a user is likely to be able to run malicious database queries.

subject: LDAP search filter with user input
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into LDAP search filters; encode it using Encoder.LdapFilterEncode before use.
quote: User input used in search filter without encoding
code_bad: DirectorySearcher ds = new DirectorySearcher(root, "username=" + userName);
code_good:
string safeUserName = Encoder.LdapFilterEncode(userName);
DirectorySearcher ds = new DirectorySearcher(root, "username=" + safeUserName);

subject: Cookie path scoping
truth: Do not default cookie paths to root; precisely define the path of the web application for which this cookie is valid.
quote: Precisely define the path of the web application for which this cookie is valid
code_bad:
HttpCookie cookie = new HttpCookie("sessionID");
cookie.Path = "/";
code_good:
HttpCookie cookie = new HttpCookie("sessionID");
cookie.Path = "/ebanking";

subject: Azure Storage SDK default client-side encryption
truth: Stop relying on the default client-side encryption version (v1 CBC); explicitly set ClientSideEncryptionVersion.V2_0.
quote: Current release versions of the Azure Storage SDKs use cipher block chaining (CBC mode) for client-side encryption (referred to as v1).

subject: Socket authentication race condition
truth: Always authenticate the user over the new socket channel, not based on prior authentication from a different channel.
quote: no authentication over the socket connection

subject: loop with hidden exit condition masking a bug
truth: Do not rely on a break inside the loop body to compensate for a broken loop condition; fix the loop condition itself.
quote: if this was intended as the only exit condition the loop should be rewritten to make this clear

subject: Unescaped </script> in generated JavaScript
truth: Always escape <, >, / characters when embedding strings into generated JavaScript code to prevent </script> breakout.
quote: However, if key contains </script>, then the generated code will break out of a </script> if inserted into a </script> tag.
code_bad:
const assignment = `obj[${JSON.stringify(key)}]=42`;
return `(function(){${assignment}})`
code_good:
const assignment = `obj[${escapeUnsafeChars(JSON.stringify(key))}]=42`;
return `(function(){${assignment}})`

subject: Using lodash _.escapeRegExp for regex sanitization
truth: Use _.escapeRegExp from lodash to escape regex meta-characters before embedding user input into a regular expression.
quote: use a sanitization function such as lodash's _.escapeRegExp to escape meta-characters
code_bad: var re = new RegExp("\\b" + key + "=(.*)\n");
code_good:
var safeKey = _.escapeRegExp(key);
var re = new RegExp("\\b" + safeKey + "=(.*)\n");

subject: Python code injection via string formatting in exec()
truth: Stop using string formatting (%) to build code strings for exec() or eval(); pass arguments directly to the function.
quote: Avoid including user input in any expression that may be dynamically evaluated
code_bad: exec("setname('%s')" % first_name)
code_good: setname(first_name)

subject: Logging sensitive data with print
truth: Stop logging sensitive data like environment variables, passwords, or system information with print or logging calls.
quote: Sensitive data should not be logged.
code_bad:
import os
print(f"[INFO] Environment: {os.environ}")

subject: SSRF via user-controlled URL in Excon HTTP request
truth: Stop putting user-provided input directly into a request URL; instead validate the path against a known fixed string.
quote: user can control the entire URL of the request
code_bad:
users_service_domain = params[:users_service_domain]
response = Excon.post("#{users_service_domain}/logins", body: {user_id: user}).body
code_good:
path = if params[:users_service_path] == "v1/users"
         "v1/users"
       else 
         "v2/users"
       end
response = Excon.post("users-service/#{path}", body: {user_id: user}).body

subject: Transmitting sensitive data over network without encryption
truth: Always encrypt sensitive information before transmitting it over the network; never send sensitive data in cleartext.
quote: Sensitive information that is transmitted without encryption may be accessible to an attacker.
code_bad: connection.send(content: creditCardNo, completion: .idempotent)
code_good: connection.send(content: encrypt(creditCardNo, encryptionKey), completion: .idempotent)

subject: NSPredicate arguments parameter
