subject: Array subscript vs pointer arithmetic
truth: Use the array subscript operator p[k] instead of manual pointer arithmetic *(p+k) to avoid scaling errors.
quote: use the array subscript operator rather than pointer arithmetic
code_bad: return *(intPointer + (i * sizeof(int)));
code_good: return *(intPointer + i);

subject: fopen file creation without umask
truth: Call umask before fopen to restrict default file permissions, then call chmod to set explicit permissions.
quote: the access rights for the newly created file are not specified as part of the call
code_bad: FILE *fp = fopen(filename,"w"); // BAD

subject: while-do-while leftover after refactoring
truth: After switching from do-while to while, remove the trailing while(); to prevent an infinite loop on break.
quote: when exiting through `break`, it is possible to get into an eternal loop

subject: Buffer access with incorrect length value
truth: Stop passing a hard-coded size larger than the buffer; use sizeof(buf) to pass the actual buffer capacity.
quote: Using a size argument that is larger than the buffer size will result in an out-of-bounds memory access
code_bad:
char buf[256];
X509_NAME_oneline(X509_get_subject_name(peer),buf,1024); // BAD
code_good:
char buf[256];
X509_NAME_oneline(X509_get_subject_name(peer),buf,sizeof(buf)); // GOOD

subject: Netty DefaultHttpRequest with validation disabled
truth: Stop passing false to DefaultHttpRequest; omit the validateHeaders parameter to prevent request splitting.
quote: Disables the internal request splitting verification
code_bad: private final DefaultHttpRequest badRequest = new DefaultHttpRequest(httpVersion, method, uri, false);
code_good: private final DefaultHttpRequest goodResponse = new DefaultHttpRequest(httpVersion, method, uri);

subject: arithmetic with extreme values
truth: Cast to a wider type before performing arithmetic on MAX_VALUE or MIN_VALUE constants to prevent overflow.
quote: Assigning the maximum or minimum value for a type to a variable of that type and then using the variable in calculations may cause overflows.

subject: Timing-safe comparison in Java
truth: Do not use Arrays.equals() for comparing secrets; use MessageDigest.isEqual() which runs in constant time.
quote: the calculation time depends only on the length of input byte arrays, and does not depend on the contents of the arrays
code_bad: return Arrays.equals(signature, actual);
code_good: return MessageDigest.isEqual(signature, actual);

subject: HostnameVerifier.verify() result ignored
truth: Always check the boolean result of HostnameVerifier.verify() and close the connection if it returns false.
quote: the method HostnameVerifier.verify() is called but its result is ignored

subject: Log injection with request_logger.warn
truth: Stop logging unsanitized request parameters with request_logger.warn; sanitize line breaks before logging.
quote: If unsanitized user input is written to a log entry, a malicious user may be able to forge new log entries
code_bad:
name = request.args.get('name')
request_logger.warn('User name: ' + name) # Bad
code_good:
name = request.args.get('name')
name = name.replace('\r\n','').replace('\n','')
logging.info('User name: ' + name) # Good

subject: Ruby regex anchor validation
truth: Use \A and \z anchors instead of ^ and $ to match the start and end of the entire string, not just a line.
quote: Using line anchors can be dangerous, as this can allow malicious input to be hidden using newlines
code_bad:
if params[:url].match? /https?:\/\/www\.example\.com\//
    redirect_to params[:url]
end
code_good:
if params[:url].match? /\Ahttps?:\/\/www\.example\.com\//
    redirect_to params[:url]
end

subject: Rust password hashing with SHA-3
truth: Stop using SHA-3 (including SHA3-256) for password hashing; use Argon2, bcrypt, scrypt, or PBKDF2 instead.
quote: SHA-3 algorithm that is weak to brute force attacks
code_bad: my_hash = sha3::Sha3_256::digest(password); // BAD
code_good:
let argon2_salt = argon2::password_hash::Salt::from_b64(salt)?;
my_hash = argon2::Argon2::default().hash_password(password.as_bytes(), argon2_salt)?.to_string(); // GOOD

subject: Logging sensitive data in Swift
truth: Stop logging sensitive information like passwords in plaintext; encrypt, obfuscate, or omit them entirely.
quote: Attackers could gain access to sensitive information that is logged unencrypted.
code_bad:
let password = "P@ssw0rd"
NSLog("User password changed to \(password)")
code_good:
let password = "P@ssw0rd"
NSLog("User password changed")

subject: hardcoded secrets in global variables in serverless functions
truth: Do not store secrets in global/static variables across invocations; fetch secrets at runtime from a vault.
quote: Avoid storing secrets or temporary sensitive data in global/static variables
code_bad:
# Secret stays in global variable across invocations
SECRET_KEY = "hardcoded-secret"
code_good:
import os
from my_secrets_lib import get_secret

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    secret = get_secret("db-password")  # fetch fresh each time
    ...
source: OWASP CS: Serverless / FaaS Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Operator precedence with logical OR and bitwise XOR
truth: Always parenthesize bitwise operations when combined with logical operators to prevent precedence errors.
quote: confusing use of logical and bitwise operations
code_bad: res = a||b^c; // BAD: possible priority error `res==1`
code_good: res = a||(b^c); // GOOD: `res==1`

subject: XPath injection unchecked error in Go
truth: Stop discarding the error from XPath execution; always check the returned error to avoid silent failures.
quote: unsafeRes, _ := xPath.ExecBool(doc)
code_bad: unsafeRes, _ := xPath.ExecBool(doc)
code_good: safeRes, _ := xPath.ExecBool(doc, opt)

subject: Sensitive data in cleartext SQLite accessible on rooted devices
truth: Do not assume MODE_PRIVATE protects sensitive data in SQLite; encrypt sensitive columns or use SQLCipher.
quote: The information could be accessed by any process or user in rooted devices

subject: Express middleware with case-sensitive regex path
truth: Always add the 'i' flag to regular expression paths in Express middleware to prevent case-bypass attacks.
quote: Using a case-sensitive regular expression path in a middleware route enables an attacker to bypass that middleware when accessing an endpoint with a case-insensitive path.

subject: Build artifact secrets leak via environment variables
truth: Stop serializing process.env into build artifacts; explicitly enumerate only the public variables needed.
quote: Sensitive information included in a build artifact can allow an attacker to access the sensitive information if the artifact is published

subject: Tar archive extraction without path traversal validation
truth: Stop extracting tar entries without checking that the entry name does not contain ".." or absolute paths.
quote: Extracting files from a malicious tar archive without validating that the destination file path is within the destination directory can cause files outside the destination directory to be overwritten
code_bad:
import sys
import tarfile

with tarfile.open(sys.argv[1]) as tar:
    #BAD : This could write any file on the filesystem.
    for entry in tar:
        tar.extract(entry, "/tmp/unpack/")
code_good:
import sys
import tarfile
import os.path

with tarfile.open(sys.argv[1]) as tar:
    for entry in tar:
        #GOOD: Check that entry is safe
        if os.path.isabs(entry.name) or ".." in entry.name:
            raise ValueError("Illegal tar archive entry")
        tar.extract(entry, "/tmp/unpack/")

subject: Timing attack against header value
truth: Stop using == to compare sensitive header values; use hmac.compare_digest() for constant-time comparison.
quote: Unlike == is a fail fast check, If the first byte is not equal, it will return immediately.

subject: Ruby log injection — newline forgery in plain-text logs
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into log strings; sanitize line endings with gsub("\n", "") first.
quote: line breaks should be removed from user input, using String#gsub or similar
code_bad: logger.info "attempting to login user: " + username
code_good: logger.info "attempting to login user: " + sanitized_username.gsub("\n", "")

subject: Rust ctor/dtor using println!
truth: Stop using println! (or any std macro) inside #[ctor]/#[dtor]; use libc_println! from libc-print instead.
quote: the println! macro calls std library functions

subject: UIWebView.loadHTMLString baseURL
truth: Always set baseURL to a URL you control or about:blank when loading HTML into a web view; do not use nil.
quote: Do not use nil, as this does not restrict URLs that can be resolved.
code_bad: webview.loadHTMLString(htmlData, baseURL: nil) // BAD
code_good: webview.loadHTMLString(htmlData, baseURL: URL(string: "about:blank")) // GOOD

subject: Core Data encryption helper
truth: Do not store sensitive attributes directly; apply encryption (e.g. AES.GCM.seal) before calling setValue.
quote: encrypted sensitive information saved
code_bad: databaseObject.setValue(creditCardNo, forKey: "myCreditCardNo")
code_good: databaseObject.setValue(encrypt(creditCardNo, encryptionKey), forKey: "myCreditCardNo")

subject: temporary file creation with tmpnam
truth: Stop using tmpnam() for temporary file names; use mkstemp() which atomically creates and opens the file.
quote: Working with a file, without checking its existence and its rights, as well as working with names that can be predicted, may not be safe.
code_bad:
char filename = tmpnam(NULL);
  fp = fopen(filename,"w"); // BAD

subject: realloc result assignment
truth: Stop assigning realloc directly to the original pointer; store the result in a temporary variable first.
quote: The expression mem = realloc (mem, size) is potentially dangerous, if the call fails, we will lose the pointer to the memory block
code_bad: buffer = (unsigned char *)realloc(buffer, newSize);

subject: C++ exception throwing with comma operator
truth: Do not use parentheses around exception arguments; use throw with a single constructor argument instead.
quote: throw ("my exception!",546); // BAD
code_bad: throw ("my exception!",546); // BAD
code_good: throw errorFunc("my exception!",546); // GOOD

subject: ASP.NET requestValidationMode
truth: Always set requestValidationMode to 4.5, or leave it at its default value; never set it to 4.0 or lower.
quote: The default value of 4.5 is the only recommended value
code_bad:
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <httpRuntime requestValidationMode="4.0"/>
  </system.web>
</configuration>
code_good:
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <httpRuntime requestValidationMode="4.5"/>
  </system.web>
</configuration>

subject: ASP.NET MVC anti-CSRF token validation on POST actions
truth: Add the [ValidateAntiForgeryToken] attribute to every [HttpPost] action method, including login methods.
quote: Although login and authentication methods are not vulnerable to traditional CSRF attacks, they still need to be protected with a token or other mitigation.

subject: Go HTTP cookies without HttpOnly flag
truth: Stop creating http.Cookie without setting HttpOnly: true; always set HttpOnly: true for session cookies.
quote: In the following example, in the case marked BAD, the HttpOnly flag is not set, so the default value of false is used.

subject: Go http.Cookie Secure field default
truth: Stop relying on the zero-value default of the Secure field; explicitly set Secure: true on every cookie.
quote: The Secure flag is set to false by default

subject: Android WebView certificate validation
truth: Do not call handler.proceed() to 'just get past' SSL errors; this enables machine-in-the-middle attacks.
quote: allows an attacker to perform a machine-in-the-middle attack against the application

subject: Credentials in properties files
truth: Stop storing plaintext credentials in properties files; encrypt them using a keystore or password vault.
quote: Storing plaintext credentials in a properties file allows anyone who can read the file access to the protected resource.
code_bad: ldap.password = mysecpass
code_good: ldap.password = eFRZ3Cqo5zDJWMYLiaEupw==

subject: Using crypto.createHash for password storage
truth: Stop using crypto.createHash for password hashing; use a dedicated password hashing library like bcrypt.
quote: A good password hashing scheme requires a computation that cannot be done efficiently.

subject: requests.get default verification
truth: Trust the default: requests.get verifies TLS certificates by default; do not override with verify=False.
quote: Functions in the requests module provide verification by default
code_bad: requests.get('https://semmle.com', verify=False) # UNSAFE
code_good: requests.get('https://semmle.com') # The default is to verify.

subject: Missing sanitization before NoSQL query
truth: Always sanitize user-controlled JSON objects with MongoSanitizer before passing them to MongoDB queries.
quote: using a sanitize library such as MongoSanitizer will ensure that user-supplied sources can not act as a malicious query
code_bad: result = mongo.db.user.find({'name': json_search})
code_good:
safe_search = sanitize(unsanitized_search)
result = client.db.collection.find_one({'data': safe_search})

subject: Log injection via taint analysis (G706)
truth: Stop logging raw os.Args or command-line arguments directly; they are tainted and trigger log injection.
quote: log.Println("Processing:", input)
code_bad: log.Println("Processing:", input)
code_good: log.Println("Application started")

subject: MCP tool configuration with unrestricted shell access
truth: Grant agents the minimum tools required and scope tool permissions with allowlists instead of wildcards.
quote: Dangerous: Agent has unrestricted shell access
source: OWASP CS: AI Agent Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Unvalidated redirect via Rails redirect_to with params
truth: Stop using redirect_to with user-supplied params directly; validate the destination against a whitelist.
quote: redirect_to params[:url]
code_bad: redirect_to params[:url]
code_good: redirect_to login_path
source: OWASP CS: Unvalidated Redirects and Forwards Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Loop exit condition with unmodified loop variable
truth: Always update the loop variable inside the loop body so the exit condition can eventually be satisfied.
quote: condition is never true, so loop will not terminate

subject: wordexp default flags
truth: Stop calling wordexp with 0 or no flags when processing user-controlled strings; always use WRDE_NOCMD.
quote: pass the WRDE_NOCMD flag to prevent command substitution
code_bad: wordexp(filePath, &we, 0);
code_good: wordexp(filePath, &we, WRDE_NOCMD);

subject: ASP.NET requestValidationMode downgrade
truth: Stop setting requestValidationMode to 4.0; set it to 4.5 to enable request validation for all requests.
quote: Downgrading or disabling this configuration is not recommended
code_bad:
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <httpRuntime requestValidationMode="4.0"/>
  </system.web>
</configuration>
code_good:
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <httpRuntime requestValidationMode="4.5"/>
  </system.web>
</configuration>

subject: Go crypto/rand usage pattern
truth: Use crypto/rand.Int with rand.Reader and math/big for secure random integers instead of math/rand.Intn.
quote: crypto/rand provides a cryptographically secure pseudo-random number generator
code_bad: s[i] = charset[rand.Intn(len(charset))]

subject: Logging level control for secrets
truth: Enforce proper logging level control and never write sensitive information to logs even at debug level.
quote: enforce proper logging level control
code_bad: logger.debug("User password is "+password);
code_good: logger.debug("User password changed")

subject: SSLContext TLS version
truth: Stop using SSLContext.getInstance with deprecated protocol strings like "SSLv3"; use "TLSv1.3" instead.
quote: unsafe TLS version
code_bad: SSLContext context = SSLContext.getInstance("SSLv3");
code_good: SSLContext context = SSLContext.getInstance("TLSv1.3");

subject: Default servlet directory listing
truth: Stop setting 'listings' to 'true' in the default servlet; always disable it with 'false' in production.
quote: Always disabling directory listing in the production environment
code_bad: <param-value>true</param-value>
code_good: <param-value>false</param-value>

subject: Using cat for file reads in Node.js
truth: Stop using cat to read files; use fs.readFile or fs.readFileSync which are portable, safe, and simpler.
quote: Using the unix command cat only to read a file is an unnecessarily complex way to achieve something that can be done in a simpler and safer manner

subject: yaml.load() with yaml.Loader (the default loader)
truth: Stop using yaml.load() with Loader=yaml.Loader; use yaml.safe_load() or Loader=yaml.SafeLoader instead.
quote: The yaml.load function provides the ability to construct an arbitrary Python object, which may be dangerous if you receive a YAML document from an untrusted source.
code_bad: yaml.load("{}", Loader=yaml.Loader)
code_good: yaml.load("{}", yaml.SafeLoader)

subject: Prototype pollution via __proto__ and constructor.prototype
truth: Never allow user-controlled path segments to reach __proto__ or constructor.prototype; filter them out.
quote: Ignore attributes which resolve to object prototype
code_bad:
setValueByPath({}, "__proto__.isAdmin", true)

		 setValueByPath({}, "constructor.prototype.isAdmin", true)
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: gRPC reflection enabled in production
truth: Disable gRPC reflection in production to prevent attackers from discovering your service's API surface.
quote: gRPC reflection allows clients to discover service methods and message schemas at runtime
code_bad: reflection.Register(s)
source: OWASP CS: gRPC Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: scanf return value check
truth: Always check the return value of scanf to verify how many arguments were assigned before reading them.
quote: Not checking the return value and reading one of the arguments not assigned a value is undefined behavior
code_bad:
scanf("%i", &i); // BAD
  return i;

subject: truncation from narrowing numeric cast
truth: Casting a user-controlled numeric value to a narrower type silently truncates the value without error.
quote: Casting a user-controlled numeric value to a narrower type can result in truncated values
code_bad: int scaled = (int)data;

subject: innerHTML assignment with string concatenation
truth: Stop concatenating user input into innerHTML; use innerText or textContent to safely set text content.
quote: use safe APIs such as innerText

subject: Host header for password reset tokens
truth: Stop deriving password reset links from the Host header; read the hostname from a trusted config file.
quote: A malicious user can send an HTTP request to the targeted web site, but with a Host header that refers to his own web site.
code_bad: text: `Click to reset password: https://${req.host}/resettoken/${token}`,
code_good: text: `Click to reset password: https://${config.hostname}/resettoken/${token}`,

subject: Ruby string sanitization with String#sub
truth: Stop using String#sub for sanitization; use String#gsub to replace all occurrences of meta-characters.
quote: String#sub will replace only the first occurrence of that string

subject: document.write/document.writeln with untrusted data
truth: HTML-encode then JavaScript-encode untrusted data before passing to document.write or document.writeln
quote: If these methods are provided with untrusted input, then an XSS vulnerability could result
code_bad:
document.write("<HTML> Tags and markup");
 document.writeln("<HTML> Tags and markup");
source: OWASP CS: DOM based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: DQL string concatenation in Doctrine
truth: Stop concatenating user input into DQL strings; use parameterized queries with setParameter() instead.
quote: insecure DQL usage
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: read() into buffer then strcpy()
truth: Always null-terminate the buffer after read() before passing to strcpy() or other C string functions.
quote: the string may not be null terminated, in which case the call to strcpy will perform a buffer overwrite

subject: RSA key generation with insufficient key size
truth: Stop using 1024-bit RSA keys; use at least 2048 bits via EVP_PKEY_CTX_set_rsa_keygen_bits(ctx, 2048).
quote: Using cryptographic algorithms with a small key size can leave data vulnerable to being decrypted
code_bad: EVP_PKEY_CTX_set_rsa_keygen_bits(ctx, 1024);
code_good: EVP_PKEY_CTX_set_rsa_keygen_bits(ctx, 2048);

subject: Azure Storage client-side encryption version
truth: Stop using ClientSideEncryptionVersion.V1_0 (CBC mode); use ClientSideEncryptionVersion.V2_0 instead.
quote: Current release versions of the Azure Storage SDKs use cipher block chaining (CBC mode) for client-side encryption (referred to as v1).

subject: Logging sensitive information
truth: Stop concatenating sensitive data into log messages; never write secrets like passwords to log files.
quote: Do not write secrets into the log files
code_bad: logger.debug("User password is "+password);
code_good: logger.debug("User password changed")

subject: TLS protocol version selection
truth: Stop defaulting to "TLS" or "SSLv3" which resolve to insecure versions; explicitly request "TLSv1.3".
quote: An application should use TLS 1.3
code_bad:
SSLContext context = SSLContext.getInstance("SSLv3");
return (SSLSocket) context.getSocketFactory().createSocket(host, port);
code_good:
SSLContext context = SSLContext.getInstance("TLSv1.3");
return (SSLSocket) context.getSocketFactory().createSocket(host, port);

subject: Java RMI deserialization
truth: Stop accepting complex Object parameters in RMI remote methods; use only strings and primitive types.
quote: Java RMI uses the default Java serialization mechanism (in other words, ObjectInputStream) to pass parameters in remote method invocations. This mechanism is known to be unsafe when deserializing untrusted data.

subject: Webpack DefinePlugin environment variable exposure
truth: Stop dumping all process.env into DefinePlugin; only whitelist specific public environment variables.
quote: inserts all environment variables from the host into the build artifact

subject: libxmljs XML parsing with noent option
truth: Stop passing `{ noent: true }` to libxml.parseXml; the secure default is to omit the option entirely.
quote: the noent option should be omitted or set to false
code_bad:
let xmlSrc = req.body,
    doc = libxml.parseXml(xmlSrc, { noent: true });
code_good:
let xmlSrc = req.body,
    doc = libxml.parseXml(xmlSrc);

subject: Cookie-based auth decision
truth: Stop using req.cookies for authentication decisions; use req.signedCookies to prevent cookie forgery.
quote: cryptographically sign the security critical cookie values
code_bad: if (req.cookies.loggedInUserId !== req.params.userId) {
code_good: if (req.signedCookies.loggedInUserId !== req.params.userId) {

subject: subprocess.call with user input
truth: Stop passing user input directly to subprocess.call; use an allowlist of hard-coded commands instead.
quote: passes it straight to subprocess.call() without examining it first

subject: Log injection with logging.info
truth: Stop passing unsanitized request parameters directly to logging.info; strip newline characters first.
quote: the name provided by the user is recorded using the log output function (logging.info or app.logger.info, etc.)
code_bad:
name = request.args.get('name')
logging.info('User name: ' + name) # Bad
code_good:
name = request.args.get('name')
name = name.replace('\r\n','').replace('\n','')
logging.info('User name: ' + name) # Good

subject: Nokogiri block-style config.noent enables XXE
truth: Stop using config.noent inside a Nokogiri parse block; use config.nonoent or omit the block entirely.
quote: config.noent
code_bad: doc = Nokogiri::XML.parse(xmlSrc) { |config| config.noent } # BAD
code_good: doc = Nokogiri::XML.parse(xmlSrc) { |config| config.nonoent } # GOOD

subject: reqwest TLS certificate verification
truth: Stop setting danger_accept_invalid_certs(true) in production code; always set it to false or omit it.
quote: If this option is set to true, the client will accept any certificate, making it susceptible to man-in-the-middle attacks.

subject: syscall.StartProcess with variable command argument
truth: Stop passing a variable string argument to syscall.StartProcess; use hardcoded command paths instead.
quote: // Calling any function which starts a new process with using
code_bad: _, _, err := syscall.StartProcess(command, []string{}, nil)
code_good: err := syscall.Exec("/bin/cat", []string{"/etc/passwd"}, nil)

subject: umask(0)
truth: Never call umask(0); it disables all permission masking and creates world-writable files by default.
quote: umask(0); // BAD
code_bad: umask(0); // BAD
code_good:
maskOut = S_IRWXG | S_IRWXO;
umask(maskOut); // GOOD

subject: SQL injection via manual escaping
truth: Stop escaping SQL strings manually; use SQL parameters (SqlParameter) instead of string replacement.
quote: the value is insufficiently encoded by only escaping double quotes
code_bad: var query = "select * from Users where Name='" + user.Replace("\"", "\"\"") + "'";

subject: Servlet cookie with user input
truth: Sanitize user input by removing special characters with replaceAll before writing to a cookie value.
quote: remove special characters before putting them in the header
code_bad:
Cookie cookie = new Cookie("name", request.getParameter("name"));
response.addCookie(cookie);

subject: Cleartext HTTP for credential transmission
truth: Use HTTPS (not HTTP) when transmitting any form of authentication credentials, including Basic auth.
quote: Transmitting sensitive information without using HTTPS makes the data vulnerable to packet sniffing
code_bad:
// BAD: basic authentication over HTTP
String urlStr = "http://www.example.com/rest/getuser.do?uid=abcdx";
code_good:
// GOOD: basic authentication over HTTPS
urlStr = "https://www.example.com/rest/getuser.do?uid=abcdx";

subject: nested loop variable in inner loop condition
truth: Stop using the outer loop variable in the inner loop condition; use the inner loop variable instead.
quote: the inner loop condition is constantly true

subject: CORS middleware with allow_credentials=True and allow_origins=["*"]
truth: Stop using wildcard origin '*' when allow_credentials=True; explicitly list trusted origins instead.
quote: dangerous scenario is when Access-Control-Allow-Origin header is set to a value obtained from the request
code_bad:
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware

app = FastAPI()

origins = [
    "*"
]

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=origins,
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)


@app.get("/")
async def main():
    return {"message": "Hello World"}
code_good:
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware

app = FastAPI()

origins = [
    "http://localhost.tiangolo.com",
    "https://localhost.tiangolo.com",
    "http://localhost",
    "http://localhost:8080",
]

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=origins,
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)


@app.get("/")
async def main():
    return {"message": "Hello World"}

subject: bare except with continue in loop
truth: Stop using bare 'except: continue' in loops; always log errors instead of silently suppressing them.
quote: catch an exception and silently ignore it while in a loop

subject: Node.js Docker container running as root
truth: Stop running containers as root; use USER directive with a COPY --chown to drop privileges properly.
quote: **“friends don’t let friends run containers as root!”**
code_bad:
USER node
CMD "npm" "start"
code_good:
COPY --chown=node:node . /usr/src/app
RUN npm ci --omit=dev
USER node
CMD "npm" "start"
source: OWASP CS: Node.js Docker Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SSL certificate verification result conflation
truth: Stop conflating X509_V_OK with other error codes; only accept X509_V_OK from SSL_get_verify_result.
quote: only X509_V_OK is a success code

subject: SQL Server connection encryption not enforced
truth: Stop omitting Encrypt=true; without it the connection silently falls back to unencrypted transport.
quote: not explicitly enabling the Encrypt setting to force encryption

subject: exec with relative path
truth: Stop using relative paths in Runtime.exec(); always use an absolute path to prevent PATH hijacking.
quote: When a command is executed with a relative path, the runtime uses the PATH environment variable to find which executable to run
code_bad: Runtime.getRuntime().exec("make");
code_good: Runtime.getRuntime().exec("/usr/bin/make");

subject: Netty DefaultHttpHeaders validation disabled
truth: Stop passing false to DefaultHttpHeaders; omit the argument or pass true to enable CRLF validation.
quote: Disables the internal response splitting verification
code_bad: private final DefaultHttpHeaders badHeaders = new DefaultHttpHeaders(false);
code_good: private final DefaultHttpHeaders goodHeaders = new DefaultHttpHeaders();

subject: Stapler CSRF protection for state-changing actions
truth: Stop using @GET for state-changing actions in Stapler; use @POST which has default CSRF protection.
quote: a safe HTTP request like GET should not be used for a state-changing action

subject: TOCTOU race condition on synchronized objects
truth: Synchronize on the object itself around the check-and-use sequence, not just on the calling method.
quote: the caller checks the readiness state and then acts, but does not synchronize around the two calls

subject: GET vs POST for sensitive data
truth: Use HTTP POST to send sensitive information as part of the request body; for example, as form data.
quote: Use HTTP POST to send sensitive information as part of the request body

subject: Ruby regex anchors
truth: Stop using ^ and $ for string validation; use \A and \z anchors instead to match the entire string.
quote: Use the \A and \z anchors since these anchors will always match the beginning and end of the string, even if the string contains newlines.
code_bad:
def bad(input) 
    raise "Bad input" unless input =~ /^[0-9]+$/

    # ....
end
code_good:
def good(input)
    raise "Bad input" unless input =~ /\A[0-9]+\z/

    # ....
end

subject: Logging sensitive data in Ruby
truth: Stop logging sensitive information like passwords in cleartext; mask or redact them before logging.
quote: logged sensitive information to be stored as well
code_bad:
require 'Logger'

class UserSession
  @@logger = Logger.new STDOUT

  def login(username, password)
    # ...
    @@logger.info "login with password: #{password})"
  end
end
code_good:
require 'Logger'

class UserSession
  @@logger = Logger.new STDOUT

  def login(username, password)
    # ...
    password_escaped = password.sub(/.*/, "[redacted]")
    @@logger.info "login with password: #{password_escaped})"
  end
end

subject: SHA-512 for data hashing
truth: Use SHA512 (SHA-2 family) instead of MD5 or SHA-1 when hashing sensitive data for integrity checks.
quote: SHA-512 is suitable for hashing sensitive data
code_bad: let digest = Insecure.MD5.hash(data: data) // BAD: MD5 is not suitable for hashing sensitive data.
code_good: let digest = SHA512.hash(data: data) // GOOD: SHA-512 is suitable for hashing sensitive data.

subject: Go HTTP client Cookie header on redirect
truth: Stop adding Cookie header to redirect requests; cookies can be stolen by external redirect targets.
quote: Unsafe redirect policy may propagate sensitive headers

subject: adding to user-controlled integers without overflow check
truth: Stop adding to user-controlled integers without first checking the result will not exceed INT_MAX.
quote: Always guard against overflow in arithmetic operations on user-controlled data

subject: HTML encoding vs URL encoding for web output
truth: Stop using URL encoding for HTML content; use HtmlEncode for HTML entities and UrlEncode for URLs.
quote: Using an inappropriate encoding may yield unintended results

subject: Go TLS MinVersion
truth: Stop setting MinVersion to 0 or insecure versions like SSL3.0; set MinVersion to tls.VersionTLS12.
quote: Setting the MinVersion to 0 equals to choosing the lowest supported version (i.e. SSL3.0)
code_bad:
config := &tls.Config{}
config.MinVersion = 0 // BAD: Setting the MinVersion to 0 equals to choosing the lowest supported version (i.e. SSL3.0)
code_good:
config := &tls.Config{}
config.MinVersion = tls.VersionTLS12

subject: java.util.Random for security-sensitive values
truth: Stop using java.util.Random for security-sensitive values; use java.security.SecureRandom instead.
quote: The java.util.Random random number generator is not cryptographically secure.

subject: Express.js async callback error handling
truth: Wrap async callback handlers in try/catch to prevent uncaught exceptions from crashing the server.
quote: uncaught exceptions can however cause the entire server to terminate abruptly

subject: Shell command construction with os.system
truth: Stop using os.system with string concatenation; use subprocess.run with an argument array instead.
quote: Dynamically constructing a shell command with inputs from library functions may inadvertently change the meaning of the shell command

subject: Custom security provider in Cipher.getInstance
truth: Stop passing a custom provider name to Cipher.getInstance; use the default provider chain instead.
quote: Explicitly specifying a custom provider
code_bad: val cipherCustom = Cipher.getInstance("AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding", "CustomProvider")
code_good: val cipher = Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding")

subject: HTTP URL construction in C++
truth: Stop constructing URLs with the HTTP protocol; use HTTPS URLs instead to enforce SSL connections.
quote: Constructing URLs with the HTTP protocol can lead to unsecured connections.
code_bad: openUrl("http://example.com"); // BAD
code_good: openUrl("https://example.com"); // GOOD: Opening a connection to a URL using HTTPS enforces SSL.

subject: checking pointer before free without NULL assignment
truth: Do not guard free() with a NULL check without first NULL-ing the pointer after the previous free.
quote: the cleanup function does not zero out the pointer
code_bad:
free(buf); 
  if(buf) free(buf); // BAD: the cleanup function does not zero out the pointer

subject: C++ runtime_error not thrown
truth: Do not construct std::runtime_error without throwing it; always use throw to raise the exception.
quote: std::runtime_error("msg error"); // BAD
code_bad: std::runtime_error("msg error"); // BAD
code_good: throw std::runtime_error("msg error"); // GOOD

subject: HTTP response splitting via cookie
truth: Stop writing user input directly into a Cookie value; validate or strip special characters first.
quote: setting a cookie with an unvalidated parameter
code_bad:
Cookie cookie = new Cookie("name", request.getParameter("name"));
response.addCookie(cookie);

subject: Maven dependency repository URL
truth: Stop using HTTP for Maven repository URLs; always use HTTPS to prevent MITM supply-chain attacks.
quote: Using an insecure protocol like HTTP or FTP to download your dependencies leaves your Maven build vulnerable to a Man in the Middle (MITM)
code_bad: <url>http://insecure-repository.example</url>
code_good: <url>https://insecure-repository.example</url>

subject: command execution via user-controlled action string
truth: Stop using user input as a command argument; validate against a predefined allowlist of commands.
quote: use hard-coded string literals to specify the command to run
code_bad: subprocess.call(["application", action])
code_good: subprocess.call(["application", COMMANDS[action]])

subject: Ruby HTTP download with Net::HTTP
truth: Stop using HTTP with Net::HTTP for downloading executables or sensitive files; use HTTPS instead.
quote: Downloading executables or other sensitive files over an unencrypted connection can leave a server open to man-in-the-middle attacks
code_bad:
require "net/http"

script = Net::HTTP.new("http://mydownload.example.org").get("/myscript.sh").body
system(script)
code_good:
require "net/http"

script = Net::HTTP.new("https://mydownload.example.org").get("/myscript.sh").body
system(script)

subject: Rust cookie Secure attribute
truth: Stop building cookies without .secure(true); the default is insecure and sends cookies over HTTP.
quote: Always set the cookie 'Secure' attribute so that the browser only sends the cookie over HTTPS

subject: UIWebView baseURL nil default
truth: Stop passing nil as baseURL to loadHTMLString; set it to about:blank or a controlled URL instead.
quote: a call to UIWebView.loadHTMLString has the baseURL set to nil, which does not restrict URLs that can be resolved from within the web page
code_bad: webview.loadHTMLString(htmlData, baseURL: nil) // BAD
code_good: webview.loadHTMLString(htmlData, baseURL: URL(string: "about:blank")) // GOOD

subject: File permissions via os.Chmod
truth: Stop using overly permissive file permissions like 0777; use restrictive modes like 0400 or 0600.
quote: gosec rule G302
code_bad: err := os.Chmod("/tmp/somefile", 0777)
code_good: err := os.Chmod("/tmp/mydir", 0400)

subject: fscanf without length specifier
truth: Stop using fscanf with %s without a width limit; specify a length one less than the buffer size.
quote: Specify a length within the format string parameter, and make this length one less than the size of the buffer, since the last character should be reserved for the NULL terminator.
code_bad:
char file[10];
fscanf(file, "%s", buf2);

subject: Operator precedence with logical OR and bitwise XOR (alternative grouping)
truth: When the intent is to XOR the result of a logical OR, parenthesize the OR expression explicitly.
quote: confusing use of logical and bitwise operations
code_bad: res = a||b^c; // BAD: possible priority error `res==1`
code_good: res = (a||b)^c; // GOOD: `res==0`

subject: Go HTTP cookies without Secure flag
truth: Always set the Secure flag to true on http.Cookie to prevent transmission over unencrypted HTTP.
quote: The Secure flag is set to false by default

subject: Android allowBackup attribute
truth: Set android:allowBackup to false in the manifest to prevent automatic backups of sensitive data.
quote: Since Android 6.0 (Marshmallow), automatic backups for applications are switched on by default
code_bad:
<manifest ... >
    <!-- BAD: no 'android:allowBackup' set, defaults to 'true' -->
    <application>
        <activity ... >
        </activity>
    </application>
</manifest>
code_good:
<manifest ... >
    <!-- GOOD: 'android:allowBackup' set to 'false' -->
    <application
        android:allowBackup="false">
        <activity ... >
        </activity>
    </application>
</manifest>

subject: Custom Content Security Policy with Helmet
truth: Stop passing false to contentSecurityPolicy; pass an object with directives to set a custom CSP.
quote: You can also enable a custom Content Security Policy by passing an object to the contentSecurityPolicy key.

subject: Log injection with logger.debug
truth: Stop passing unsanitized user input to logger.debug; remove newline characters from input first.
quote: If unsanitized user input is written to a log entry, a malicious user may be able to forge new log entries
code_bad:
name = request.args.get('name')
logtest = logging.getLogger('test')
logtest.debug('User name: ' + name) # Bad
code_good:
name = request.args.get('name')
name = name.replace('\r\n','').replace('\n','')
logging.info('User name: ' + name) # Good

subject: Incomplete hostname regex in Swift
truth: Always escape the . meta-character as \. when constructing hostname regexes for security checks.
quote: the unescaped . allows for any character before example.com
code_bad: let regex = #/^(www|beta).example.com//#  // BAD
code_good: let regex = #/^(www|beta)\.example\.com//#  // GOOD

subject: Core Data cleartext storage
truth: Encrypt sensitive information before storing it in a database; decrypt only at the point of use.
quote: Sensitive information that is stored unencrypted in a database is accessible to an attacker who gains access to that database
code_bad: databaseObject.setValue(creditCardNo, forKey: "myCreditCardNo")
code_good: databaseObject.setValue(encrypt(creditCardNo, encryptionKey), forKey: "myCreditCardNo")

subject: Azure CLI storage account without HTTPS enforcement
truth: Use --https-only true when creating or updating Azure storage accounts to enforce HTTPS traffic.
quote: transmits sensitive or security-critical data in cleartext
code_bad: az storage account show -g {ResourceGroupName} -n {StorageAccountName}
code_good: az storage account update -g {ResourceGroupName} -n {StorageAccountName} --https-only true
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Docker port publishing bypasses UFW firewall
truth: Bind published ports to 127.0.0.1 to prevent Docker from bypassing host firewall rules like UFW.
quote: However, **Docker manages its own `iptables` and `nftables` rules directly and bypasses UFW entirely**.
code_bad: docker run -p 8000:8000 myimage
code_good: docker run -p 127.0.0.1:8000:8000 myimage
source: OWASP CS: Docker Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: wordexp with user input
truth: Pass the WRDE_NOCMD flag to wordexp to prevent command substitution when processing user input.
quote: wordexp performs command substitution
code_bad:
wordexp_t we;
wordexp(filePath, &we, 0);
code_good:
wordexp_t we;
wordexp(filePath, &we, WRDE_NOCMD);

subject: integer multiplication with explicit conversion after operation
truth: Cast one argument to the wider type before multiplication, not the result after multiplication.
quote: the result of the multiplication is subjected to explicit conversion, not the arguments
code_bad: vUnsignedLong = (unsigned long)(vUnsignedInt*vUnsignedInt); // BAD
code_good: vUnsignedLong = ((unsigned long)vUnsignedInt*vUnsignedInt); // GOOD

subject: Cookie path set to root
truth: Stop setting cookie.Path to "/"; restrict it to the specific application path like "/ebanking".
quote: Cookies with an overly broad path, such as the root context path ("/"), can be accessed by all web applications on the same domain name.
code_bad:
HttpCookie cookie = new HttpCookie("sessionID");
cookie.Path = "/";
code_good:
HttpCookie cookie = new HttpCookie("sessionID");
cookie.Path = "/ebanking";

subject: Express rate limiting
truth: Stop serving requests without rate limiting; apply express-rate-limit middleware to all routes.
quote: HTTP request handlers should not perform expensive operations such as accessing the file system, executing an operating system command or interacting with a database without limiting the rate at which requests are accepted.

subject: MD5 for sensitive data hashing
truth: Stop using Insecure.MD5 for hashing sensitive data; use SHA512 (or SHA-2/SHA-3 family) instead.
quote: MD5 is not suitable for hashing sensitive data
code_bad: let digest = Insecure.MD5.hash(data: data) // BAD: MD5 is not suitable for hashing sensitive data.
code_good: let digest = SHA512.hash(data: data) // GOOD: SHA-512 is suitable for hashing sensitive data.

subject: PyCrypto/PyCryptodome RSA/DSA key sizes below 2048 bits
truth: Stop using PyCrypto or PyCryptodome RSA/DSA key sizes below 2048 bits; use bits=2048 or higher.
quote: The recommended key length size for RSA and DSA algorithms is 2048 and higher.
code_bad: pycrypto_rsa.generate(bits=1024)
code_good: pycrypto_rsa.generate(bits=2048)

subject: Go slice bounds out of range
truth: Check that the index is less than the length of the slice before accessing an element by index.
quote: Possible slice bounds out of range
code_bad:
s := make([]byte, 4)

fmt.Println(s[5])
code_good:
s := make([]byte, 4)

fmt.Println(s[3])

subject: std::unique_ptr::get() lifetime
truth: Store the std::unique_ptr in a named variable before calling get() so the pointer stays valid.
quote: the std::unique_ptr object is destroyed as soon as the call to get returns

subject: Session fixation via reused session after login
truth: Stop reusing the previous session after authentication; call Abandon() to force a new session.
quote: Reusing a session could allow an attacker to gain unauthorized access to another account.

subject: raw string literals for hostname regexps
truth: Use raw string literals (backticks) for hostname regexps to avoid double-escaping backslashes.
quote: consider using raw string literals to avoid having to escape backslashes
code_bad: re := "^((www|beta)\\.)?example\\.com/"
code_good: re := `^((www|beta)\.)?example\.com/`

subject: Array size from user input
truth: Stop using >= 0 to validate array size from user input; use > 0 to prevent zero-length arrays.
quote: if the user provides 0 as the numberOfItems parameter, then an empty array is created, and any array access would fail with an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException

subject: Timing-safe comparison in Java
truth: Stop using Arrays.equals() to compare MACs or signatures; use MessageDigest.isEqual() instead.
quote: Use MessageDigest.isEqual() method to check MACs and signatures
code_bad: return Arrays.equals(signature, actual);
code_good: return MessageDigest.isEqual(signature, actual);

subject: Reading passwords from GET params in Rails
truth: Stop reading passwords from params in a GET action; move sensitive input to POST request body.
quote: receives this sensitive information from the query parameters of a GET request, which is transmitted in the URL
code_bad:
def login_get
    password = params[:password]
    authenticate_user(params[:username], password)
  end
code_good:
def login_post
    password = params[:password]
    authenticate_user(params[:username], password)
  end

subject: Ruby script download and execute pattern
truth: Stop using HTTP when downloading scripts that will be executed via system(); always use HTTPS.
quote: The HTTP protocol is vulnerable to MITM, and thus an attacker could potentially replace the downloaded shell script with arbitrary code
code_bad:
require "net/http"

script = Net::HTTP.new("http://mydownload.example.org").get("/myscript.sh").body
system(script)
code_good:
require "net/http"

script = Net::HTTP.new("https://mydownload.example.org").get("/myscript.sh").body
system(script)

subject: cleartext logging of sensitive data in Rust
truth: Stop logging sensitive data like passwords in plaintext; omit them entirely from log messages.
quote: Do not log sensitive data
code_bad:
let password = "P@ssw0rd";
info!("User password changed to {password}");
code_good:
let password = "P@ssw0rd";
info!("User password changed");

subject: NSPredicate with string interpolation
truth: Stop building NSPredicate with string interpolation; use the arguments parameter (%@) instead.
quote: If a predicate is built from user-provided data without sufficient sanitization, an attacker may be able to change the overall meaning of the predicate.
code_bad: let predicate = NSPredicate(format: "SELF LIKE \(remoteString)")
code_good: let predicate = NSPredicate(format: "SELF LIKE %@", remoteString)

subject: File permission bits in ioutil.WriteFile
truth: Stop using permissive file modes like 0744 or os.ModePerm; use 0600 or 0644 for file creation.
quote: gosec rule G306
code_bad: err := ioutil.WriteFile("/tmp/dat1", d1, 0744)
code_good: allowed := ioutil.WriteFile("/tmp/dat1", d1, 0600)

subject: Unbounded message length processing
truth: Validate that the message length does not exceed a defined maximum before processing the body.
quote: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
code_good:
unsigned int length = getMessageLength(message[0]);
if ((length > 0) && (length < MAX_LENGTH)) {...}
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Explicit FLAG_MUTABLE on PendingIntent
truth: Stop using FLAG_MUTABLE unless absolutely necessary for fillIn; use FLAG_IMMUTABLE by default.
quote: Explicit FLAG_MUTABLE used without justification
code_bad:
val explicitMutableIntent = Intent(context, MastgTest::class.java)
val explicitMutablePendingIntent = PendingIntent.getService(
    context,
    1,
    explicitMutableIntent,
    PendingIntent.FLAG_MUTABLE
)

subject: Infinite loop with unsatisfiable exit condition
truth: Ensure every loop exit condition depends on a variable that is modified within the loop body.
quote: If none of the exit conditions can ever be satisfied, then the loop will never terminate

subject: URL encoding vs HTML encoding for redirects
truth: Stop using HTML encoding for URL redirects; use UrlEncode for URL parameters, not HtmlEncode.
quote: redirecting to an HTML encoded URL provided by a remote user can facilitate phishing attacks

subject: Logging credentials in Go HTTP handlers
truth: Do not log password or credential values; log only the username or non-sensitive identifiers.
quote: the credentials should be encrypted, obfuscated, or omitted entirely
code_bad: log.Printf("Registering new user %s with password %s.\n", user, pw)
code_good: log.Printf("Registering new user %s.\n", user)

subject: RSA 1024-bit key as default mental model
truth: Stop defaulting to 1024-bit RSA keys; always specify 2048 or higher as the key size argument.
quote: The following code uses RSA encryption with insufficient key size.
code_bad: pvk, err := rsa.GenerateKey(rand.Reader, 1024)
code_good: pvk, err := rsa.GenerateKey(rand.Reader, 2048)

subject: Apache Commons Email SSL certificate check
truth: Call setSSLCheckServerIdentity(true) when using SSL with credentials in Apache Commons Email.
quote: Don't have setSSLCheckServerIdentity set or set as false

subject: Android Intent Redirection - Origin Validation
truth: Check the calling component's package and class name before launching a user-provided Intent.
quote: In the third example, the component that created the Intent is first checked to make sure it comes from a trusted origin.
code_bad:
Intent forwardIntent = (Intent) getIntent().getParcelableExtra("forward_intent");
startActivity(forwardIntent);

subject: Sending sensitive data via postMessage with wildcard origin
truth: Never use '*' as targetOrigin when the message contains sensitive data like user credentials.
quote: This feature should not be used if the message being sent contains sensitive data such as user credentials: the target window may have been loaded from a malicious site, to which the data would then become available.
code_bad: window.parent.postMessage(userName, '*');
code_good: window.parent.postMessage(userName, 'https://github.com');

subject: Unescaped dot in hostname regex
truth: Stop using unescaped . in hostname regexes; escape every literal dot as \. to prevent bypass.
quote: Escape all meta-characters appropriately when constructing regular expressions for security checks, and pay special attention to the .
code_bad: let regex = #/^(www|beta).example.com//#  // BAD
code_good: let regex = #/^(www|beta)\.example\.com//#  // GOOD

subject: zip file extraction without path traversal validation
truth: Always validate that archive entry paths do not contain '..' before writing extracted files.
quote: Extracting files from a malicious zip file

subject: Android certificate pinning with okhttp
truth: Stop using OkHttpClient without certificate pinning; add a CertificatePinner to the builder.
quote: Certificate pinning implemented via okhttp3.CertificatePinner
code_bad: URLConnection conn = new URL("https://example.com").openConnection();

subject: Android SQLite database stores data in cleartext
truth: Stop storing sensitive data in plain SQLite; use SQLCipher or encrypt data before insertion.
quote: SQLite does not offer any encryption mechanism by default and stores all data in cleartext

subject: window.postMessage with targetOrigin '*'
truth: Specify a target origin instead of '*' when using window.postMessage to send sensitive data.
quote: If the receiver window does not come from this origin, the message is not sent. Alternatively, the sender can specify a target origin of '*', which means that any origin is acceptable and the message is always sent.
code_bad: window.parent.postMessage(userName, '*');
code_good: window.parent.postMessage(userName, 'https://github.com');

subject: Azure storage account HTTPS disabled
truth: Set enableHttpsTrafficOnly to true to enforce HTTPS-only traffic for Azure storage accounts.
quote: transmits sensitive or security-critical data in cleartext
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: innerHTML/outerHTML with untrusted data
truth: HTML-encode then JavaScript-encode untrusted data before assigning to innerHTML or outerHTML
quote: If these methods are provided with untrusted input, then an XSS vulnerability could result
code_bad:
element.innerHTML = "<HTML> Tags and markup";
 element.outerHTML = "<HTML> Tags and markup";
source: OWASP CS: DOM based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Blade Unescaped XSS
truth: Stop using {!! !!} with untrusted data; always use {{ }} which escapes via htmlspecialchars.
quote: This must not be used on any untrusted data, otherwise your application will be subject to an XSS attack
code_bad: {!! request()->input('somedata') !!}
code_good: {{ request()->input('somedata') }}
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: integer overflow before widening conversion
truth: Stop casting the multiplication result; the overflow already happened in the narrower type.
quote: during the multiplication period, you can lose meaningful data
code_bad: vUnsignedLong = (unsigned long)(vUnsignedInt*vUnsignedInt); // BAD
code_good: vUnsignedLong = ((unsigned long)vUnsignedInt*vUnsignedInt); // GOOD

subject: integer overflow in swapped addition check
truth: Stop using if (b+a>c) a=c-b which overflows; restructure to avoid overflow in the addition.
quote: if (b+a>c) a=c-b (swapped terms in addition)
code_bad: if (b+a>c) a=c-b
code_good: if (a>c-b) a=c-b

subject: ASP.NET request validation in web.config
truth: Set validateRequest to true in web.config to enable XSS protection; do not set it to false.
quote: validateRequest flag set to false
code_bad:
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <pages validateRequest="false" />
  </system.web>
</configuration>
code_good:
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <pages validateRequest="true" />
  </system.web>
</configuration>

subject: ASP.NET request validation disabled
truth: Disabling validateRequest leaves the web application vulnerable against common XSS attacks.
quote: disable validation, and leave the web application vulnerable against common XSS attacks
code_bad:
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <pages validateRequest="false" />
  </system.web>
</configuration>
code_good:
<configuration>
  <system.web>
    <pages validateRequest="true" />
  </system.web>
</configuration>

subject: URL sanitization completeness
truth: Treat data: and vbscript: schemes as equally dangerous as javascript: when sanitizing URLs.
quote: the data and vbscript schemes can be used to represent executable code in a very similar way

subject: Go random number generation for security-sensitive values
truth: Stop using math/rand for security-sensitive values like passwords; use crypto/rand instead.
quote: math/rand is not cryptographically secure, and should be avoided in security contexts

subject: build dependency download protocol
truth: Always use a secure protocol, such as HTTPS or SFTP, when downloading artifacts from a URL.
quote: Always use a secure protocol, such as HTTPS or SFTP, when downloading artifacts from an URL

subject: EC curves with key sizes below 224 bits
truth: Stop using EC curves with key sizes below 224 bits; use curves like SECP384R1 or SECP256K1.
quote: EC key length sizes are recommended to be 224 and higher with 160 and below considered breakable.
code_bad:
ec.generate_private_key(curve=ec.SECT163R2,
                        backend=backends.default_backend())
code_good:
ec.generate_private_key(curve=ec.SECP384R1,
                        backend=backends.default_backend())

subject: Authentication error messages
truth: Return a generic error message for all authentication failures to prevent user enumeration.
quote: Using any of the authentication mechanisms (login, password reset, or password recovery), an application must respond with a generic error message regardless of whether:
code_bad:
IF USER_EXISTS(username) THEN
    password_hash=HASH(password)
    IS_VALID=LOOKUP_CREDENTIALS_IN_STORE(username, password_hash)
    IF NOT IS_VALID THEN
        RETURN Error("Invalid Username or Password!")
    ENDIF
ELSE
   RETURN Error("Invalid Username or Password!")
ENDIF
code_good:
password_hash=HASH(password)
IS_VALID=LOOKUP_CREDENTIALS_IN_STORE(username, password_hash)
IF NOT IS_VALID THEN
   RETURN Error("Invalid Username or Password!")
ENDIF
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: XML parsing with SAX2XMLReader without disabling default entity resolution
truth: Set fgXercesDisableDefaultEntityResolution feature to true on SAX2XMLReader to prevent XXE.
quote: Use of SAX2XMLReader, do this to prevent XXE
code_bad: SAX2XMLReader* reader = XMLReaderFactory::createXMLReader();
code_good:
SAX2XMLReader* reader = XMLReaderFactory::createXMLReader();
parser->setFeature(XMLUni::fgXercesDisableDefaultEntityResolution, true);
source: OWASP CS: XML External Entity Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: std::unique_ptr temporary lifetime
truth: Never call get() on a temporary std::unique_ptr; the pointer becomes dangling immediately.
quote: the pointer returned by get is no longer valid

subject: using filepath.Abs on archive entry names
truth: Do not use filepath.Abs on archive entry names without also checking for '..' in the path.
quote: Ensure that output paths constructed from zip archive entries are validated to prevent writing files to unexpected locations

subject: JavaMail SSL certificate validation
truth: Set mail.smtp.ssl.checkserveridentity to true when using SSL with credentials in JavaMail.
quote: Failing to validate the certificate makes the SSL session susceptible to a man-in-the-middle attack

subject: SameSite cookie attribute
truth: Stop setting SameSite=None on authentication cookies; set SameSite=Strict to prevent CSRF.
quote: Set the SameSite attribute to Strict on all sensitive cookies.
code_bad: res.setHeader("Set-Cookie", `authKey=${makeAuthkey()}; secure; httpOnly; SameSite=None`);
code_good: res.setHeader("Set-Cookie", `authKey=${makeAuthkey()}; secure; httpOnly; SameSite=Strict`);

subject: Error.stack in HTTP responses
truth: Do not expose err.stack in HTTP response bodies; it reveals server internals to attackers.
quote: the same information can be useful to an attacker
code_bad: res.end(err.stack); // NOT OK
code_good: res.end("An exception occurred"); // OK

subject: Set-Cookie without secure flag
truth: Always set the 'secure' attribute on cookies that contain sensitive data like auth tokens.
quote: Always transmit sensitive cookies using SSL by setting the secure attribute on the cookie.
code_bad: res.setHeader("Set-Cookie", `authKey=${makeAuthkey()}`);
code_good: res.setHeader("Set-Cookie", `authKey=${makeAuthkey()}; secure; httpOnly`);

subject: Hardcoded credentials in HTTP request headers
truth: Stop hard-coding credentials in source code; read them from environment variables instead.
quote: Including unencrypted hard-coded authentication credentials in source code is dangerous

subject: Prototype pollution via __proto__ key
truth: Block the keys __proto__, constructor, and prototype when using user input as object keys.
quote: prevent the __proto__ property from being used as a key

subject: Ruby log injection via unsanitized user input
truth: Stop logging unsanitized user input; use String#gsub to remove line breaks before logging.
quote: If unsanitized user input is written to a log entry, a malicious user may able to forge new log entries.
code_bad: logger.info "attempting to login user: " + username
code_good: logger.info "attempting to login user: " + sanitized_username.gsub("\n", "")

subject: String length used with NSString
truth: Stop using String.count to create an NSRange for an NSString; use NSString.length instead.
quote: Do not mix values for lengths and offsets between the two types as they are not compatible measures.

subject: inline event handlers blocked by CSP
truth: Replace inline event handlers like onclick with addEventListener calls when CSP is active.
quote: The inline code restriction also applies to `inline event handlers`, so that the following construct will be blocked under CSP:
code_bad: <button id="button1" onclick="doSomething()">
code_good: document.getElementById("button1").addEventListener('click', doSomething);
source: OWASP CS: Content Security Policy Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: TOCTOU filesystem race (chmod after fopen)
truth: Stop using chmod on a file name after opening; use fchmod on the file descriptor instead.
quote: This can be avoided by using fchmod with the file descriptor that was received from opening the file.

subject: Java cookies without HttpOnly flag
truth: Always call setHttpOnly(true) on sensitive cookies created via javax.servlet.http.Cookie.
quote: Cookies without the HttpOnly flag set are accessible to client-side scripts

subject: Binding sockets to empty string interface
truth: Stop binding sockets to '' (empty string); bind only to a dedicated interface IP address.
quote: binds to all interfaces, insecure

subject: Flask cookie SameSite=None
truth: Stop setting samesite='None' on cookies; set samesite='Strict' or samesite='Lax' instead.
quote: Cookies with the SameSite attribute set to 'None' will be sent with cross-origin requests
code_bad: resp.set_cookie("sessionid", value="value", samesite='None') # BAD: the SameSite attribute is set to 'None' and the 'Secure' and 'HttpOnly' attributes are set to False by default.
code_good: resp.set_cookie("sessionid", value="value", secure=True, httponly=True, samesite='Strict') # GOOD: Attributes are securely set

subject: Ignoring error from WriteFile in Go

subject: Backslash-escaping single quotes in Go strings
truth: Replace the fact. Do not teach manual backslash/quote string-escaping as a generic SQL fix for CWE-089: it is DB-dialect dependent and, for PostgreSQL's default (standard_conforming_strings = on since 9.1), it is actively incorrect — backslash is not an escape character there, single quotes must be doubled ('') instead, and applying the backslash-then-quote scheme neither protects the data nor prevents the injection. Even in MySQL, where backslash escaping is the default behavior, this approach has documented bypasses (e.g. charset/encoding-based string-break attacks defeating mysql_real_escape_string). Corrected truth: "Never build SQL via manual string-escaping and concatenation — use parameterized queries / prepared statements (e.g. Go's database/sql placeholder args: db.Query(\"... WHERE x = ?\", val) or $1-style for pq/pgx) so the driver handles quoting correctly for the target dialect. Manual quote-escaping (including 'escape backslash before quote') is a fragile, dialect-specific anti-pattern flagged by tools like CodeQL's go/string-break query, not a recommended mitigation for CWE-089."

subject: JAR signature verification
truth: To actually verify a JAR's signature before loading classes from it: open it with signature checking enabled — `JarFile jf = new JarFile(file, true);` (explicit `true` is recommended even though it's the default for the single-File-arg constructor, to make intent unambiguous and CWE-347-scanner-friendly) — then for each entry obtained via jf.getJarEntry(name) or jf.entries(), fully read its InputStream to EOF via jf.getInputStream(entry) (this is what actually performs the per-entry cryptographic digest check against the signed manifest); only after that read completes, call entry.getCodeSigners() and confirm it is non-null AND that the returned certificate chain(s) belong to a trusted, expected signer — getCodeSigners() returning non-null proves the entry matched its signed digest, but Oracle's own docs warn it does NOT validate the signer's certificate/trust, so the caller must still check that separately before loading classes from the JAR. jf.getManifest().getEntries() and a bare jf.getJarEntry() lookup perform no verification by themselves.
quote: does not verify, or incorrectly verifies, the cryptographic signature for data
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)
_audit_fix: WRONG: JarFile.getManifest().getEntries() and getJarEntry() do not perform or trigger any signature verification — per CWE-347's own Java example and Oracle's JarFile

Stop treating filepath.Clean() as a fix for path traversal from r.URL.Query().Get() values passed to os.Open()/os.OpenFile(): Clean() only collapses redundant separators and resolves ".." lexically against a real preceding segment — it does NOT clamp the result to a base directory, so inputs like "../../etc/passwd" or an absolute path pass through unchanged and the file still opens outside the intended directory (this exact mistake caused a real CVE in GitLab's devfile parser, CVE-2023-49569). Instead: join the input into a fixed base directory (full := filepath.Join(baseDir, filepath.Clean("/"+userInput))) and verify the resulting path still has baseDir as a prefix before opening — or, on Go 1.24+, use the os.Root API (root, _ := os.OpenRoot(baseDir); root.Open(userInput)), which is purpose-built to be traversal-resistant and should be preferred over manual Clean()+prefix-check logic.</corrected_fix>
</invoke>

subject: Prototype pollution via setValueByPath
truth: Filter __proto__, constructor, and prototype from EVERY path segment, including the final one that gets assigned (attributeToSet) — not just the intermediate segments walked in the loop. As published (and as reproduced in code_good), pathArray.pop() removes the last segment into attributeToSet BEFORE the denylist loop runs, so the loop's check never sees it; a path that is exactly "__proto__" (or ends in ".__proto__"/".constructor"/".prototype") reaches `objectToModify[attributeToSet] = value` unchecked and still sets the target's prototype. The fix must validate attributeToSet against the same denylist (or better, use an allowlist of permitted keys) before performing the final assignment, e.g.: `if (["__proto__","constructor","prototype"].includes(attributeToSet)) return object; objectToModify[attributeToSet] = value;`

subject: Key provisioning requires authenticated protected channels
truth: truth (unchanged, still valid): "Use authenticated protected channels (e.g., a mutually authenticated TLS session) or in-person procedures for key provisioning to protect against man-in-the-middle attacks — do not provision keys over unprotected channels." Update metadata: source = "NIST SP 800-63B-4", version = "NIST SP 800-63B-4 (Final, July 2025) — Digital Identity Guidelines: Authentication and Authenticator Management, Sec. 4.1 authenticator binding / key establishment requirements"; drop the withdrawn "NIST SP 800-63B" citation. Re-verify/re-ground the `quote` field against the actual SP 800-63B-4 text (e.g., the key-establishment/authenticator-binding clause requiring a mutually authenticated protected channel) since the original verbatim quote came from the withdrawn document and may not appear verbatim in -4.

subject: Go range loop variable aliasing with struct pointer slice
truth: Only take/avoid-taking the address of a range loop variable based on the module's Go version: for modules whose go.mod `go` directive is 1.21 or lower, the range variable (e.g. `sample`) is reused across iterations, so taking its address inside the loop body (or in a closure/goroutine) captures a single shared memory location and is a bug (gosec G601) — copy it to a local variable first (`sample := sample`) before taking its address. For modules with `go 1.22` or later in go.mod, Go's per-iteration loop-variable semantics (Go 1.22, Feb 2024) already give each iteration its own variable, so this pattern is no longer a bug and gosec v2.20.0+ itself skips/ignores the G601 rule for Go 1.22+ code — do not apply this fix (or flag this pattern) without first checking the go.mod `go` version.
quote: Implicit memory aliasing in RangeStmt

subject: PHP XML parser (libxml2) before PHP 8.0
truth: On current PHP (8.0+, which bundles libxml2 >= 2.9), external entity loading is disabled by default for DOMDocument, SimpleXML, and XMLReader -- libxml_disable_entity_loader() is now a deprecated no-op. XXE risk only reappears if you explicitly pass LIBXML_NOENT, LIBXML_DTDLOAD, LIBXML_DTDATTR, or LIBXML_DTDVALID to the parser (or on PHP < 8.0 / libxml < 2.9, where the old default still applies and libxml_disable_entity_loader(true) or libxml_set_external_entity_loader(null) is required). Avoid those flags; if you must support PHP < 8.0 or want defense-in-depth, call libxml_set_external_entity_loader(null) explicitly rather than relying on the deprecated libxml_disable_entity_loader(). On PHP 8.4+, the new LIBXML_NO_XXE constant/flag gives explicit, flag-proof XXE protection.

source: OWASP CS: XML External Entity Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Log4j JNDI injection via user-controlled log messages
truth: Do not treat setting log4j2.formatMsgNoLookups=true or upgrading to Log4j 2.15.0 alone as sufficient remediation for Log4Shell — the 2.15.0 fix was bypassed within days (CVE-2021-45046, RCE in non-default configs using Pattern Layout with Context Lookup/Thread Context Map). Upgrade to Log4j 2.17.1 or later (2.12.4 for Java 7, 2.3.2 for Java 6) — the first release addressing the full chain of CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-45105 (DoS via uncontrolled recursion, fixed 2.17.0), and CVE-2021-44832 (JDBC Appender RCE, fixed 2.17.1). If upgrading is not immediately possible, remove the JndiLookup class from the classpath as an interim mitigation, but plan to upgrade to 2.17.1+ (or the current maintained 2.x/3.x release) as the durable fix.

subject: DSA (DSS) certificates
truth: Split into an accurate claim: "NIST SP 800-52r2 uses 'DSS' as a historical synonym for DSA in TLS cipher-suite names (quote: 'DSA is referred to as DSS (Digital Signature Standard) for historical reasons') — SP 800-52r2 itself still documents DSA as a supported certificate-signature option (alongside RSA/ECDSA) and does not ban it; a conforming TLS server need only have RSA or ECDSA. Avoid DSA/DSS certificates on separate, current grounds: FIPS 186-5 (effective 2023-02-03) removed DSA as an approved algorithm for generating NEW digital signatures (retaining it only to verify pre-existing legacy signatures), and NIST SP 800-131A Rev. 3 likewise retires DSA for signature generation — use RSA, ECDSA, or EdDSA for new certificates instead."

subject: MD5 in TLS PRF
truth: Do not flag the TLS 1.0/1.1 PRF's use of MD5+SHA-1 as broken crypto in isolation: NIST SP 800-135 (Rev. 1) approves the combined P_MD5 XOR P_SHA-1 construction as a valid KDF even though MD5 alone is disallowed. This is a narrow construction-level approval only — it does NOT mean TLS 1.0/1.1 itself should be used or left enabled. Per NIST SP 800-52 Rev. 2, TLS 1.2 is the required minimum (TLS 1.3 preferred); TLS 1.0/1.1 should be disabled by default and enabled only where required for interoperability with non-government systems. When generating or reviewing TLS config/code: don't "fix" the PRF construction by ripping out MD5 (that breaks a NIST-approved legacy KDF), but do recommend disabling TLS 1.0/1.1 and negotiating TLS 1.2+.
quote: the PRF is specified as acceptable in SP 800-135
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: This conflates a narrow KDF/PRF-construction approval under SP 800-135 with approval of the TLS 1.0/1.1 protocol itself; SP 800-52r2's actual stance is that TLS

subject: Path concatenation with tainted input
truth: Stop concatenating tainted input with a directory prefix. filepath.Join() already calls Clean() internally, so Join()+Clean() together still just lexically normalize the path — they do not enforce that the result stays inside the base directory. filepath.Join(dir, "../../../etc/passwd") cleans straight to a path outside dir. After joining/cleaning, explicitly verify containment before opening the file, e.g.: cleanPath := filepath.Clean(filepath.Join(dir, filePath)); if !strings.HasPrefix(cleanPath, dir+string(os.PathSeparator)) { reject }. (Prefer comparing absolute paths, and note the HasPrefix check itself needs dir to end without a trailing separator mismatch / or use filepath.Rel and check the result doesn't start with "..".)

subject: Prompt Injection via format string to LLM
truth: Input filtering/allowlist regex validation alone does not reliably prevent LLM prompt injection (OWASP LLM01:2025) — prompt injection is a semantic manipulation problem, not a syntactic one, and no fool-proof input-side prevention exists. Use defense-in-depth instead: segregate untrusted/external content from trusted system instructions, apply least-privilege to any tools or functions the LLM can invoke, validate and constrain model outputs before acting on them, and require human-in-the-loop approval for sensitive or irreversible actions. A regex allowlist can still be one weak, supplementary layer (e.g., rejecting obviously malformed input) but must never be relied on as the primary or sole control.

subject: Certificate validity period
truth: Do not treat NIST SP 800-52 Rev. 2's (2019) "3 years or less" figure as the binding ceiling for TLS certificate validity — it reflects general/internal-PKI guidance and is now far more permissive than actual practice. For publicly-trusted TLS certificates, follow the CA/Browser Forum Baseline Requirements instead: capped at 398 days since September 2020, and — per Ballot SC-081v3 (approved April 2025 by all major browser vendors) — phasing down further on a fixed schedule: 200 days maximum from March 15, 2026; 100 days from March 15, 2027; and 47 days from March 15, 2029. Issue/recommend certificates well under the current or scheduled CA/Browser Forum maximum, not the outdated NIST "3 years" ceiling.
quote: 3 years or less
_audit_fix: STALE: NIST SP 800-52r2's '3 years or less' ceiling (from 2019) is far more permissive than the current binding limit for publicly-trusted TLS certificates: the CA/Bro

subject: Js2Py eval_js with untrusted input
truth: js2py.disable_pyimport() is NOT a sufficient mitigation and must not be relied on to make eval_js() safe for untrusted input: CVE-2024-28397 (all js2py versions through v0.74, the final release — unpatched) shows JS can bypass it by walking exposed Python object internals (getOwnPropertyNames -> __getattribute__ -> __base__ -> __subclasses__) to reach subprocess.Popen/os without importing anything new, giving RCE. Never pass untrusted/attacker-controlled input to js2py.eval_js. If evaluating untrusted JS is unavoidable, isolate it in a hard sandbox (separate process/container with no access to sensitive resources) — do not treat js2py, with or without disable_pyimport(), as a safe sandbox.

subject: Ruby regex caching in 3.2+ eliminates worst-case complexity
truth: Ruby 3.2+'s built-in cache-based regex optimization (Onigmo) already eliminates worst-case exponential time for most ReDoS-flagged patterns (~90% in Ruby's own benchmarks) by caching visited position/state pairs during matching — this caching IS the fix, not something to avoid. If you're on Ruby 3.2 or later, don't add redundant length limits or timeouts as your primary ReDoS defense for these patterns; the interpreter already handles it. Still upgrade any pre-3.2 runtime, since it has no such protection. Exception: the cache doesn't cover patterns using look-around, back-references, or subexpression calls — for those, a timeout is still needed as a fallback even on Ruby 3.2+.

truth: Retract or rewrite. The cited OWASP example `Project.delete(:name => 'name')` is NOT vulnerable — ActiveRecord parameterizes hash-based conditions internally, and OWASP lists it as a correct/safe usage alongside create!/where/update_attributes. subject: PHP include with user-controlled file
truth: Never pass user-controllable input to include()/require() (or similar dynamic file-loading constructs) — the included file's PHP is parsed and executed, so this is a code-execution risk, not an output-encoding/XSS issue. Remediate by validating the input against an allowlist of known-good file identifiers (e.g., map a fixed key or numeric ID to a hardcoded filename and reject anything not in the map) or by refactoring to avoid dynamic inclusion entirely. Do not 'fix' this by reading the file's raw contents and escaping them before output — that does not address unauthorized access to arbitrary files on disk and is not a recognized CWE-94/CWE-98 remediation.

subject: LDAP bind authentication
truth: Prevent LDAP injection primarily by escaping/encoding special characters in any user input used to build LDAP distinguished names or search filters (DN specials: \ # + < > , ; " = and leading/trailing spaces; filter specials: * ( ) \ NUL), or by using a framework/parameterized-filter API that does this encoding automatically (e.g. filter placeholders with input passed as separate parameters, not concatenated). Enabling authenticated bind (and disabling anonymous/unauthenticated bind) is an additional hardening measure — not a substitute for input escaping — since anonymous or unauthenticated bind can itself be used to bypass the authentication check.

subject: libxml2 on iOS (up through iOS6)
truth: On iOS, do not rely on library/OS version or "default" behavior to be safe from XXE — always explicitly disable DTD loading and external entity resolution when parsing untrusted XML, regardless of libxml2 version or which parsing API you use (raw libxml2, NSXMLParser, or higher-level wrappers). Defaults have varied across API layers and versions over time (e.g., NSXMLParser's own external-entity resolution remained on by default independent of libxml2's version-level fix, until patched in iOS 8.3/CVE-2015-1092), so version-checking alone is not a reliable safety signal — verify and lock down entity/DTD handling per-parser and per-configuration.

subject: URLSessionDelegate Missing authenticationMethod Check
truth: After confirming challenge.protectionSpace.authenticationMethod == NSURLAuthenticationMethodServerTrust, you must still call SecTrustEvaluateWithError(serverTrust, &error) (or the older SecTrustEvaluate) to actually validate the certificate chain — only create and use a URLCredential(trust: serverTrust) if that evaluation succeeds; otherwise call completionHandler(.cancelAuthenticationChallenge, nil). Checking authenticationMethod alone is a routing precondition, not certificate validation — creating the credential without a prior successful SecTrustEvaluateWithError call blindly accepts any server certificate and is vulnerable to MITM.
