subject: Ruby YAML.load deserialization
truth: Stop using YAML.unsafe_load on untrusted data (or bare YAML.load on Ruby <3.1 / Psych <4, where load is still an alias for unsafe_load). On current Ruby (Psych 4, bundled since Ruby 3.1) YAML.load and YAML.load_file already default to safe_load semantics — no YAML aliases, and only a small whitelist of classes deserialized — so plain YAML.load(untrusted) is safe out of the box on any currently supported Ruby. Only widen that behavior deliberately and never on untrusted input: don't call unsafe_load/unsafe_load_file, and don't pass aliases: true or an expanded permitted_classes: to safe_load unless you trust the source.

subject: lxml.etree.parse
truth: Bandit B320 flags lxml.etree.parse() and lxml.etree.fromstring() equally on untrusted XML — the entry-point function does not matter for XXE, since both accept the same parser= argument. Fix: construct an lxml.etree.XMLParser with resolve_entities=False and no_network=True and pass it to whichever of parse()/fromstring() you call, or use defusedxml's equivalent function instead. (Note: lxml 5.x+ disables external-entity expansion by default, but explicit resolve_entities=False/no_network, or defusedxml, is still the recommended defense-in-depth for untrusted input and for code that may run on older lxml.)

subject: os.Args used directly in file operations

subject: SameSite cookie attribute default
truth: Modern major browsers (Chrome 80+/Feb 2020, Firefox 69+, Safari 13+) already default unspecified cookies to SameSite=Lax — don't assume the old None-by-default behavior. But don't rely on that implicit default for sensitive cookies either: legacy/unsupported clients silently fall back to no SameSite restriction (behaving like None), and Lax alone doesn't block all XS-Leaks vectors (e.g., top-level GET navigation can still carry the cookie). Explicitly set SameSite=Lax or Strict on sensitive cookies, and use SameSite=None only together with Secure when cross-site delivery is genuinely required.

subject: Cookie attributes (Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite)
truth: Always set Secure and HttpOnly on cookies -- both are defined in RFC 6265 (2011), §4.1.2.5 and §4.1.2.6 respectively -- to prevent transmission over unencrypted channels and access via XSS/JavaScript. Additionally set SameSite to mitigate CSRF; note SameSite is NOT defined in RFC 6265 -- it comes from draft-ietf-httpbis-rfc6265bis, an IETF Internet-Draft still unpublished as an RFC as of 2026 (though implemented by all major browsers). Anchor the fact's quote to actual RFC 6265 text defining Secure/HttpOnly (e.g. from §4.1.2.5/§4.1.2.6), not the generic RFC 2119 keywords boilerplate in §2.

subject: Jinja2 select_autoescape with no arguments
truth: select_autoescape() called with no arguments is already safe by default: enabled_extensions defaults to ('html','htm','xml') and default_for_string=True, so autoescaping is enabled for those template extensions (and for string-loaded templates) out of the box. Only pass an explicit extensions list if your templates use different/additional extensions that need autoescaping (e.g. .jinja, .j2). The actually insecure patterns are `autoescape=False` or omitting the autoescape setting entirely when constructing the Environment/Loader without select_autoescape — that is what Bandit B701 flags.

subject: Array subscript vs pointer arithmetic on wrong-type pointer
truth: When a pointer's type doesn't match the underlying element type (e.g., walking a char* through data that is actually an int array), the fix is to use/cast to a pointer of the correct element type for the arithmetic or indexing -- e.g. use intPointer[i] or *(intPointer + i) instead of *(charPointer + i) or charPointer[i] -- so pointer arithmetic scales by the right element size. Switching only the notation between p[k] and *(p+k) does not fix this: the two forms are defined as equivalent in C/C++ and scale identically, so they carry the same bug if the pointer type itself is wrong.

subject: TLS version negotiation fallback
truth: When the supported_versions extension is absent from ClientHello, a server compliant with RFC 8446 that also supports TLS 1.2 or earlier MUST negotiate using the legacy version-negotiation mechanism (ClientHello.legacy_version), per RFC 8446 §4.2.1 — this is normal, expected backward-compatible fallback for pre-TLS-1.3 clients, not a condition to reject. (A server that supports only TLS 1.3 has no legacy version to fall back to and may simply fail to negotiate/reject such a ClientHello, but that is a consequence of not supporting older versions, not a MUST-NOT-fallback rule.)
quote: When it is absent from the ClientHello message, the server shall use the version negotiation specified in TLS 1.2 and earlier.
_audit_fix: WRONG: The truth directly inverts its own cited quote: RFC 8446 / NIST SP 800-52r2 state that when supported_versions is absent, the server SHALL use the legacy TLS 1.

subject: Go range loop variable aliasing
truth: gosec G601 (implicit memory aliasing of range loop variables) applies only to modules whose go.mod declares a Go version below 1.22. In those pre-1.22 modules, don't take the address of (or capture in a closure) the range loop variable 'item' directly inside the loop — assign it to a local variable first or index into the slice/map instead. In go.mod 1.22+ modules this is no longer a bug: Go 1.22 gives range/for loop variables fresh per-iteration scope, and gosec itself (v2.20.0+) skips/ignores the G601 check for 1.22+ modules, so the pattern is safe and won't be flagged.

subject: JWT token storage — localStorage vs sessionStorage
truth: Do not store JWTs/session tokens in localStorage or sessionStorage — both are plain JavaScript-readable Web Storage APIs, so any successful XSS can read either equally; sessionStorage's tab-scoping only limits cross-tab reflected-XSS reach and offers no protection against stored XSS in the same page. Prefer HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict cookies (or a Backend-for-Frontend pattern) instead. If client-side JS access to the token is unavoidable, hold it only in an in-memory variable/closure (never Web Storage), accepting it will be lost on refresh/restart.

subject: node-tar decompression bomb
truth: To defend against decompression-bomb attacks with node-tar, set/limit maxDecompressionRatio (default 1000; do NOT set to Infinity) — it caps the ratio of decompressed to compressed bytes and aborts extraction when exceeded. maxReadSize (default 16 MB) only bounds the buffer size of individual fs.read() calls during extraction; it does not limit total decompressed output and does not mitigate decompression bombs. Correct CWE tag for decompression-bomb/data-amplification issues is CWE-409, not CWE-522 (which is "Insufficiently Protected Credentials").

subject: os.tempnam / os.tmpnam
truth: os.tempnam(), os.tmpnam(), and os.tmpfile() do not exist in Python 3 (all three were removed in Python 3.0) and Bandit removed its B325 check for tempnam/tmpnam as obsolete in release 1.7.5 — it is now a retired, non-firing rule ID. For current Python 3 code, create temporary files via the tempfile module instead: tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(), tempfile.mkstemp(), or tempfile.TemporaryFile(). These use atomic, race-free creation and are the only correct current defense against the symlink-attack class that the old os functions were vulnerable to.

subject: strcat as taint step

subject: RIPEMD160 hash
truth: Stop using RIPEMD-160 for new designs — it's deprecated/legacy (Go moved it out to golang.org/x/crypto) and unnecessary now that SHA-256 is universally available. But unlike MD4, RIPEMD-160 is NOT cryptographically broken: best known attacks reach only semi-free-start collisions on 44 of its 80 rounds, not a practical full collision. Its use in existing protocols where it's already standard (e.g., Bitcoin's HASH160 = RIPEMD160(SHA256(x))) is not itself a vulnerability and does not need to be "fixed."

subject: SQLite secure deletion
truth: Define SQLITE_SECURE_DELETE (not SQLITE_HAS_CODEC) to enable secure deletion that zeroizes memory on destruction — this pragma-default causes deleted content to be overwritten with zeros, which is required for FIPS 140-2 Level 1 (per OWASP's C-Based Toolchain Hardening Cheat Sheet, always define it in US Federal builds). SQLITE_HAS_CODEC is a separate, unrelated (and since SQLite 3.32.0, removed/unsupported) macro that only enables the encryption-codec interface used by extensions like SQLCipher/SEE.
quote: SQLite secure deletion zeroizes memory on destruction. Define as required, and always define in US Federal since zeroization is required for FIPS 140-2, Level 1.

Instead, explicitly bound the copy to the destination's size and always NUL-terminate the result afterward (e.g., pre-zero the buffer or force dest[n-1]='\0'), or use a function that guarantees termination/truncation-safety such as strlcpy or snprintf("%s", src).

subject: buffer copy operations
truth: Stop using strcpy; it is not safely fixed by switching to strncpy alone. strncpy does NOT null-terminate the destination if the source string's length is >= the destination buffer size (CWE-170), which can itself cause buffer overreads in later string operations. Prefer strlcpy (BSD/macOS/musl/glibc >= 2.38) or snprintf(dst, dst_size, "%s", src) for guaranteed-terminated bounded copies. If strncpy must be used, always null-terminate the buffer explicitly afterward (e.g. dst[dst_size-1] = '\0';).

subject: X-Frame-Options vs CSP frame-ancestors priority
truth: Set both the X-Frame-Options and CSP frame-ancestors headers together for clickjacking defense, not one or the other. X-Frame-Options is formally obsolete but costs nothing to send; modern CSP2+ browsers will use frame-ancestors and ignore X-Frame-Options when both are present, but older/legacy browsers that don't support frame-ancestors fall back to honoring X-Frame-Options. Omitting X-Frame-Options in favor of CSP alone leaves those older browsers with no clickjacking protection.

subject: TLS cipher suites with DH or ECDH (static) key exchange
truth: Prefer ephemeral DHE/ECDHE cipher suites over static DH/ECDH for forward secrecy. NIST SP 800-52r2 explicitly states static DH/ECDH keys are "technically acceptable" but recommends ephemeral suites as "encouraged and preferred" -- it does not prohibit static key-exchange cipher suites. (In practice, most modern TLS stacks and browsers have dropped static DH/ECDH suites entirely, so avoiding them is still the right operational default -- just not because the standard forbids them.)

_audit_fix: MISLEADING: NIST SP 800-52r2 recommends/prefers ephemeral DHE/ECDHE over static DH/ECDH for forward secrecy but does not prohibit static key-exchange cipher suites outright

subject: SMS as out-of-band verifier
truth: Per NIST SP 800-63B, SMS/PSTN is a RESTRICTED out-of-band authenticator, not banned outright — it may be used only under specific verifier conditions (confirm the pre-registered number is tied to a specific device/SIM, screen for SIM-swap/number-porting/other abnormal risk signals, and offer subscribers an unrestricted alternative). Prefer a dedicated authenticator app or push-based OOB channel over SMS where possible; do not treat SMS OOB as an absolute forbidden option.

subject: SMS-based MFA
truth: Per NIST SP 800-63B-4 (finalized July 2025), SMS/PSTN-delivered OTP is a "restricted authenticator": it remains permitted, but only with documented risk mitigations (e.g., verifying the number is bound to a specific device, monitoring for SIM swap/number porting), explicit user notification of the risk, an updated risk assessment, and an active migration plan toward stronger methods. For new designs, prefer TOTP, push notifications, or WebAuthn/FIDO2 over SMS/PSTN OTP.

subject: ReDoS via catastrophic backtracking in regex
truth: In JavaScript, native regex syntax has no possessive quantifiers (n++) or atomic groups ((?>...)) — the TC39 proposal for these is still Stage 1 and unshipped, so writing that syntax throws a SyntaxError. truth: Stop using exec() with untrusted input — it enables arbitrary Python code execution (eval/code injection, CWE-95), a distinct vulnerability class from OS command injection (CWE-78, which applies to os.system()/subprocess-style shell command construction). Mitigation is to avoid exec()/eval() on any externally influenced data entirely, not to shell-escape.

truth: Stop assuming other apps won't draw overlays; call View.setFilterTouchesWhenObscured(true) (or set android:filterTouchesWhenObscured="true" in XML) on sensitive UI elements to block touches when obscured, and/or check the MotionEvent.FLAG_WINDOW_IS_OBSCURED / FLAG_WINDOW_IS_PARTIALLY_OBSCURED flags in onTouchEvent to detect overlay/tapjacking attacks.

subject: unary minus on boolean type
truth: Do not apply unary minus to a boolean expecting negation: unary minus on a bool is a no-op (bool promotes to int 0/1, negation of 0 is 0 and negation of 1 converts back to true), so -b always evaluates back to the same value as b (false stays false, true stays true) — it never inverts the value. Use the logical NOT operator (!) to negate a boolean.
quote: variable `b` equal `true`
_audit_fix: WRONG: This misstates the actual bug: for a bool b, unary minus promotes b to int (0 or 1), negates it, and converts back to bool, which yields the SAME value as b (fa

subject: base64.decodebytes as unmodeled taint step
truth: Never pass base64-decoded data from an untrusted source into pickle.loads() (or any other deserializer, e.g. yaml.load, marshal.loads); base64 encoding is a transport/encoding transform only — it does not neutralize or sanitize the underlying unsafe-deserialization risk (CWE-502), so the decoded bytes must still be treated as attacker-controlled.

subject: Runtime.exec command injection
truth: Stop passing user-controlled strings directly to Runtime.exec. Prefer hard-coded string literals for the command/script, selecting among them based on validated user input; only sanitize/whitelist user input directly when hard-coding the command is truly impossible, since ad hoc shell-argument sanitization is notoriously hard to get fully right.

subject: Using ECB cipher mode in Python cryptography
truth: ECB mode is flagged by Bandit rule B305 ("insecure cipher mode", triggered on cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.modes.ECB) — not B304 (which covers insecure cipher algorithms like ARC2/ARC4/Blowfish/DES/XOR/CAST5/IDEA/SEED/TripleDES). Stop using ECB mode; use an authenticated mode such as GCM (or CTR/CBC combined with a MAC) instead.
quote: Use of insecure cipher or cipher mode. Replace with a known secure cipher such as AES.
_audit_fix: WRONG: Mislabeled as Bandit B304: B304 covers insecure cipher algorithms (ARC2/ARC4/Blowfish/DES/XOR/CAST5/IDEA/SEED/TripleDES); ECB is specifically flagged by Bandit

subject: External API calls with untrusted data
truth: Always validate or sanitize untrusted data against the receiving external API's expected format/type before passing it in — do not assume an external analysis tool (e.g. a CodeQL security query) will catch unhandled cases; if no explicit sanitization or allow-list check exists at the call site, treat the data as unsafe to pass.

subject: strcat as a taint propagation step
truth: Never use strcat to copy untrusted/attacker-controlled data into a fixed-size destination buffer — strcat performs no bounds checking, so this is a classic buffer-overflow vector (CWE-120). Use a bounds-checked alternative instead: strncat (with a correctly computed remaining-space limit), snprintf, or strlcat where available.

subject: decommissioning checklist for subdomains
truth: Establish a formal decommissioning checklist that includes identifying all DNS records, pointing to a maintenance page, removing DNS, waiting for TTL propagation, decommissioning the resource, revoking certificates, updating inventory, removing from OAuth/CSP/CORS configs, and running a takeover detection scan.

subject: WebView localStorage cleanup on app stop

subject: Salt size for password hashing in C#
truth: Use a cryptographically random salt of at least 32 bytes generated with RandomNumberGenerator (e.g., RandomNumberGenerator.Fill(Span<byte>) or RandomNumberGenerator.GetBytes(int)), not the obsolete RNGCryptoServiceProvider (marked [Obsolete]/SYSLIB0023 since .NET 6).
quote: Use a long random salt of at least 32 bytes
_audit_fix: STALE: RNGCryptoServiceProvider has been marked Obsolete in .NET since .NET 6 (compiler warning SYSLIB0023) and should no longer be recommended for new code.

subject: Python random module for security purposes
truth: Stop using random.Random, random.random, random.randint, random.choice, random.choices, random.uniform, random.triangular, random.randbytes, random.sample, random.randrange, or random.getrandbits for security/cryptographic purposes; use the secrets module instead.
quote: Standard pseudo-random generators are not suitable for security/cryptographic purposes

subject: iOS file storage in backup-eligible directories
truth: Stop writing sensitive files to .documentDirectory, .cachesDirectory, .libraryDirectory, or .applicationSupportDirectory without excluding them from iCloud backup; use .documentDirectory with NSURLIsExcludedFromBackupKey set to true or store in Keychain instead.
quote: The test fails because the `secret.json` file exists in multiple directories that are eligible for backup:

subject: iOS os_log default privacy is public
truth: In Swift Logger/os_log, interpolated strings and objects are .private (redacted to <private>) by DEFAULT; only numeric scalars (Int/Double/Bool) default to .public, so mark sensitive scalars privacy: .private/.sensitive and never opt strings into .public.
quote: logger.warning: Leaking <private>
_currency_fix: Swift Logger default for strings/objects is .private not public; only scalars public (verified 2026-07-18)

subject: Referer header stripping by intermediaries
truth: Intermediaries SHOULD NOT strip or modify the Referer header when the field value shares the same scheme and host as the target URI; this is a strong recommendation (not an absolute MUST NOT) since doing so can break CSRF protections that rely on Referer.
quote: An intermediary SHOULD NOT modify or delete the Referer header field when the field value shares the same scheme and host as the target URI.
_audit_fix: WRONG: RFC 9110 §10.1.3 (and the fact's own quote field) says an intermediary 'SHOULD NOT modify or delete' the Referer field in this case — a recommendation, not an a

subject: Overlay attack via TYPE_APPLICATION_OVERLAY
truth: Stop using TYPE_APPLICATION_OVERLAY or TYPE_PHONE to draw overlays that intercept user input on other apps; instead, validate that no overlay is covering sensitive UI using View.setOnTouchListener with FLAG_WINDOW_IS_OBSCURED checks on the victim side.
quote: draw a visible overlay over other apps

subject: convergent encryption risk
truth: Do not use convergent encryption for secrets without ensuring the system has sufficient resource challenges during encryption and that secrets are of adequate length, otherwise attackers can derive plaintext secrets by generating matching ciphertexts.
quote: it allows attackers to use the system to generate a set of cryptographic strings that might end up in the same secret, allowing the attacker to derive the plaintext secret
source: OWASP CS: Secrets Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Uncontrolled allocation size from user input
truth: Guard user-controlled allocation sizes against a maximum limit before calling alloc, and make sure the size multiplication itself cannot overflow — either by bounding the inputs first (as in the example) or using checked_mul — before calling alloc.
quote: Allocating memory with a size based on user input may allow arbitrary amounts of memory to be allocated
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: The truth claims the fix uses checked_mul, but code_good never calls checked_mul — it only adds a fixed BUFFER_LIMIT check before an ordinary '*' multiplication

subject: XSS sink identification in external APIs
truth: Stop assuming all external API results are irrelevant; when an external API like HttpResponse.Write receives untrusted data, evaluate whether it is a security-relevant sink (e.g., XSS) and confirm it is handled by the appropriate security query.
quote: If the query were to return the API System.Web.HttpResponse.Write(string) [param 0] then we should first consider whether this a security relevant sink

subject: WebView cleanup only in onStop lifecycle method
truth: Do not rely solely on onStop() for WebView data cleanup; the WebView may retain data if the activity is killed or if cleanup is missed, and deleteAllData() may not clear all storage types (localStorage, sessionStorage, WebSQL, IndexedDB, cache).

subject: SameSite cookie attribute as sole CSRF defense
truth: Do not rely on SameSite alone for CSRF protection unless the app shares no registrable domain, has no state-changing GET endpoints, uses SameSite=Strict or Lax with __Host- prefix, and has Origin/Referer verification on state-changing endpoints.
quote: `SameSite` is useful as a defense-in-depth control but it does not replace a proper CSRF defense in most deployments.
source: OWASP CS: Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: AndroidManifest dangerous permissions
truth: Stop declaring dangerous permissions (WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE, READ_CONTACTS, READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE, ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION) in AndroidManifest unless strictly necessary and runtime-prompted; remove unused dangerous permission declarations.
quote: The test fails because the app declares the following dangerous permissions

subject: SSRF defense via whitelist comparison
truth: Validate a user-supplied URI against a whitelist of known-safe URIs using exact equals() comparison (e.g. VALID_URI.equals(request.getParameter("uri"))) before building the HTTP request, to prevent server-side request forgery (CWE-918).
quote: the request parameter is validated against a known fixed string
_audit_fix: WRONG: The recommendation's polarity is inverted: it tells the model NOT to do exact-match whitelist validation, but the fact's own code_good example does exactly that

subject: BeanShell injection via StaticScriptSource.setScript()
truth: Stop setting untrusted user input via StaticScriptSource.setScript() and then evaluating it; instead, avoid using untrusted input in BeanShell expressions or run them in a sandbox that allows accessing only explicitly allowed classes.
quote: If a BeanShell expression is built using attacker-controlled data, and then evaluated, then it may allow the attacker to run arbitrary code.

subject: Digital signature security strength from hash function collision resistance
truth: Stop using a hash function weaker than the signature algorithm's key strength; the security strength of a digital signature is no greater than the minimum of the algorithm's key strength and the hash function's collision resistance.
quote: The security strength provided by a digital signature generation process is no greater than the minimum of 1) the security strength that the digital signature algorithm can support with a given key length and any domain parameters used , 2) the security strength (with respect to collision resistance) supported by the cryptographic hash function that is used to hash the data to be signed.

subject: LAContext.canEvaluatePolicy for passcode check
truth: Stop using canEvaluatePolicy(.deviceOwnerAuthentication) to check if a passcode is set; use LAContext().canEvaluatePolicy(.deviceOwnerAuthenticationWithBiometrics) for biometric checks and handle the passcodeNotSet error properly.
quote: LAContext().canEvaluatePolicy(.deviceOwnerAuthentication, error: nil)

subject: iOS Keychain biometric protection with SecAccessControl
truth: Stop using LAContext.evaluatePolicy alone to gate access to secrets; use SecAccessControlCreateWithFlags with kSecAccessControlUserPresence and store the secret in the Keychain so the system enforces biometric auth on retrieval.
quote: The test fails because the output only shows calls to biometric verification with LocalAuthentication API and no calls to any Keychain APIs requiring user presence (`SecAccessControlCreateWithFlags`).

subject: Authorization responses over unencrypted connections
truth: Authorization responses MUST NOT be transmitted over unencrypted network connections; authorization servers MUST NOT allow redirection URIs that use the http scheme except for native clients using loopback interface redirection.
quote: Authorization responses MUST NOT be transmitted over unencrypted network connections

subject: IPv6-transition SSRF bypass
truth: Normalize the host before validating it: parse the address with a transition-aware library and unwrap IPv4-mapped, NAT64 and 6to4 forms to their embedded IPv4 address, then apply the private-range check to the normalized value.
quote: When such a guard inspects only the dotted-quad IPv4 form and never unwraps IPv6-transition representations, it can be bypassed

subject: Fallback SCSV for protocol downgrade prevention
truth: If the client does not support TLS 1.3 and is attempting to connect with a TLS version prior to TLS 1.2, the client shall include TLS_FALLBACK_SCSV at the end of the cipher suite list to prevent unintended protocol downgrades.
quote: TLS_FALLBACK_SCSV at the end of the cipher suite list

subject: reauthentication via federation
truth: When requiring reauthentication through a federation protocol, specify the maximum acceptable authentication age to the CSP and the CSP SHALL reauthenticate if the subscriber has not been authenticated within that time period.
quote: specify the maximum acceptable authentication age to the CSP

subject: WKWebView javaScriptEnabled defaults to true
truth: JavaScript is enabled by default in WKWebView; to disable it on iOS 14+ set WKWebpagePreferences.allowsContentJavaScript = false via configuration.defaultWebpagePreferences, not the deprecated WKPreferences.javaScriptEnabled.
quote: To verify that JavaScript was properly disabled search the project for usages of `WKPreferences` and ensure that the `javaScriptEnabled` property is set to `false`:

subject: Over-permissioned AndroidManifest with unnecessary DANGEROUS permissions
truth: Remove all unused permissions from AndroidManifest.xml, especially DANGEROUS and SIGNATURE permissions, and prefer replacement APIs (e.g., SMS Retriever API, ACTION_IMAGE_CAPTURE intent) over declaring sensitive permissions.
quote: For instance, it should be suspicious if a single-player game app requires access to `android.permission.WRITE_SMS`.

subject: Legacy systems with limited logging
truth: Stop relying on legacy systems' own logs for security monitoring; deploy network-based detection tools to monitor traffic patterns, connection attempts, and data flows for systems that don't provide detailed security logs.
quote: Older systems often don't provide the detailed security logs you need for modern threat detection and compliance requirements
source: OWASP CS: Zero Trust Architecture Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Authenticator unique authentication to verifier
truth: Stop using out-of-band authenticators that do not uniquely authenticate to the verifier; the authenticator SHALL uniquely authenticate itself via an authenticated protected channel with approved cryptography or a SIM card.
quote: The out-of-band authenticator SHALL uniquely authenticate itself in one of the following ways when communicating with the verifier

subject: Unanchored regex alternatives
truth: When writing regex with multiple host alternatives, anchor each alternative individually; /^www\.example\.com|beta\.example\.com/ is parsed as /(^www\.example\.com)|(beta\.example\.com)/ and matches evil.beta.example.com.
quote: the regular expression /^www\.example\.com|beta\.example\.com/ will match the host evil.beta.example.com, because the regular expression is parsed as /(^www\.example\.com)|(beta\.example\.com)/

subject: iOS logging APIs leak sensitive data
truth: Stop logging sensitive data with print, debugPrint, dump, NSLog, os_log, Logger, fputs, vfprintf, vdprintf, vsyslog, or FileHandle.standardError; use privacy: .private or .sensitive on interpolated values in Logger calls.
quote: insecurely logs a sensitive token

subject: OpenSSL NULL certificate after X509_V_OK
truth: After OpenSSL verification, always call SSL_get_verify_result AND check that SSL_get_peer_certificate returns non-NULL, because a server that does not supply a certificate will result in X509_V_OK with a NULL certificate.
quote: By design, a server that does not supply a certificate will result in `X509_V_OK` with a **NULL** certificate.
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: nano-id base58 and base62 functions
truth: Stop using nano_id::base58 and nano_id::base62; they use reduced character sets (16 and 32 symbols instead of 58 and 62), severely cutting entropy. Upgrade to nano-id >= 0.4.0 or use nano_id::base64 which is not affected.
quote: Specifically, the `base62` function used a character set of 32 symbols instead of the intended 62 symbols, and the `base58` function used a character set of 16 symbols instead of the intended 58 symbo

subject: Logging sensitive data
truth: Never log sensitive personal data, authentication passwords, session IDs, access tokens, encryption keys, database connection strings, or payment card data directly; instead remove, mask, sanitize, hash, or encrypt them.
quote: Never log data unless it is legally sanctioned
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

quote: Inspect each reported call site using @MASTG-TECH-0076 to confirm whether the JavaScript string assigns to a DOM element property (`innerHTML`, `textContent`, `innerText`, or `value`) and that the val

subject: Verifier impersonation (phishing) resistance

quote: strongly and irreversibly bind a channel identifier that was negotiated in establishing the authenticated protected channel to the authenticator output

subject: Multi-factor authenticator binding requires multi-factor authentication
truth: When binding a new multi-factor authenticator, require multi-factor authentication (or equivalent, such as association with a just-completed identity proofing session) — do not bind using single-factor authentication.
quote: Binding of multi-factor authenticators SHALL require multi-factor authentication or equivalent

subject: destination_is_initially_empty defaults to false, triggering unsafe chmod path
truth: Upgrade gix-worktree-state to >= 0.17.0, which applies the umask correctly on executable-file checkout; do not use destination_is_initially_empty: true as a general fix (safe only when the destination is truly empty).

subject: Logging sensitive data to externally-accessible files
truth: Never log sensitive information such as credit card numbers, passwords, or personal data to files that may be accessible to unauthorized actors; instead, log only non-sensitive identifiers or mask the sensitive data.

subject: AI agent runtime sandboxing

subject: Android Keystore key with setUserAuthenticationRequired(false)
truth: Stop creating Android Keystore keys with setUserAuthenticationRequired(false) for biometric-protected secrets; always set setUserAuthenticationRequired(true) so the key is only usable after biometric authentication.
quote: Key created with setUserAuthenticationRequired(false)

subject: Path traversal and injection in universal links
truth: Use allowlists for the path or parameters that select a resource or action when the set of valid inputs is known; reject any value that does not match rather than attempting to strip or escape individual characters.
quote: Use allowlists for the path or for parameters that select a resource or action when the set of valid inputs is known

subject: Prompt context leakage of secrets
truth: Configure AI tools to exclude sensitive directories from context — AI coding assistants send open files, project structure, and terminal output to the model provider's API, which may contain credentials and secrets.
quote: AI coding assistants send code context (open files, project structure, terminal output) to the model provider's API. This context may contain credentials, personal data, proprietary business logic, and internal architecture details.

subject: WebView JavaScript Bridge exposing SharedPreferences via addJavascriptInterface
truth: Stop exposing native methods that read/write SharedPreferences through addJavascriptInterface without origin restrictions; use WebViewCompat.addWebMessageListener with a narrow allowedOriginRules allowlist instead.
quote: - JavaScript is enabled on the `WebView` and a native bridge (`SupportBridge`) is registered via `addJavascriptInterface()` (line 85).

subject: ATS bypass via Network.framework TLS downgrade
truth: Stop relying on ATS to enforce TLS security; Network.framework operates entirely outside of ATS and lowering TLS versions via sec_protocol_options_set_min_tls_protocol_version bypasses all ATS protections silently.

subject: Python regex engine catastrophic backtracking
truth: Stop assuming Python's re module is safe for user-supplied regex patterns; the backtracking NFA engine has worst-case exponential time complexity, so validate input length or use the regex module with time limits.
quote: The worst-case time complexity of such an automaton can be polynomial or even exponential

subject: setUserAuthenticationRequired(false) for Keystore keys used with biometrics
truth: Stop setting setUserAuthenticationRequired(false) on Keystore keys used with BiometricPrompt.CryptoObject; set setUserAuthenticationRequired(true) so the key cannot be used outside a biometric authentication flow.

subject: ZipSlip symlink extraction
truth: When extracting symbolic links from archives, resolve previously-extracted symlinks using filepath.EvalSymlinks before validating the destination path; a pair of crafted symlinks can bypass syntactic path checks.
quote: a pair of links like subdir/parent -> .. followed by escape -> subdir/parent/.. -> subdir/../.. leaves a link pointing to the parent of the archive root

subject: Spring Framework unsafe service exporters (CVE-2016-1000027)
truth: Do not assume Spring's HttpInvokerServiceExporter or RmiServiceExporter are safe because they are part of the framework; they use ObjectInputStream or Hessian deserialization which allows remote code execution.

subject: WebView addJavascriptInterface with JavaScript enabled
truth: Stop exposing Java objects via addJavascriptInterface without restricting which JavaScript can call them; only allow JavaScript bundled with the APK and verify the URL via WebView.getUrl on each bridged method.
quote: With access to the JavaScript code, via, for example, stored XSS or a MITM attack, an attacker can directly call the exposed Java methods.

subject: email change with MFA
truth: When the user HAS MFA enabled, use MFA for identity verification instead of the current password, and send a notification-only email to the current address plus a confirmation-required email to the new address.
quote: Request the use of [Multifactor Authentication](https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Multifactor_Authentication_Cheat_Sheet.html) for identity verification.
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: rand_core::le::read_u32_into and read_u64_into buffer length check
truth: Stop using incorrect buffer length checks in rand_core::le::read_u32_into and read_u64_into that allow the destination buffer to be under-filled; use the patched versions >= 0.6.2 which enforce correct bounds.
quote: incorrect checks on the source buffer length, allowing the destination buffer to be under-filled

subject: Hard-coded hashed password in Java authentication
truth: Never hard-code credential hashes in source code; store them outside the code in a strongly-protected, encrypted configuration file or database (restrictive file permissions only if encryption is infeasible).
quote: hard-coded credentials

subject: SSLSocket hostname verification
truth: Stop using SSLSocket without a HostnameVerifier; SSLSocket inherently performs no hostname validation, so you must implement and set a HostnameVerifier to verify the server's hostname matches the certificate.
quote: The following sample code demonstrates how to connect to a @MASTG-TOOL-0143 server that delivers a certificate with a **wrong or invalid hostname** using `SSLSocket`, which inherently doesn't perform

subject: Key agreement vs key transport
truth: Do not use key transport when key agreement is required; key agreement ensures neither party can predetermine the shared secret independently, unlike key transport where one party selects the keying material.
quote: Key agreement A (pair-wise) key-establishment procedure where secret keying material is generated from information contributed by two participants so that no party can predetermine the value of the secret keying material independently from the contributions of the other party. Contrast with key-transport.

subject: OTP entropy below 64 bits requires rate-limiting
truth: If the authenticator output or activation secret has less than 64 bits of entropy, the verifier SHALL implement a rate-limiting mechanism that effectively limits the number of failed authentication attempts.
quote: less than 64 bits of entropy, the verifier SHALL implement a rate-limiting mechanism

subject: Android WebView with JavaScript and cross-origin access enabled loading external URLs
truth: Stop enabling both setJavaScriptEnabled(true) and setAllowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs(true) when loading externally controlled URLs in WebView; disable cross-origin file access or only load trusted content.
quote: Have both JavaScript and cross-origin resource access enabled in webview while

subject: Missing path validation rules for user-controlled filenames
truth: Validate user input before using it to construct a file path: do not allow more than a single '.' character, do not allow directory separators such as '/' or '\', and use a whitelist of known good patterns.
quote: Validate user input before using it to construct a file path, either using an off-the-shelf library like ActiveStorage::Filename#sanitized in Rails, or by performing custom validation.

subject: Missing @JavascriptInterface annotation on pre-API-17
truth: Always annotate exposed Java methods with @JavascriptInterface to explicitly allow JavaScript access; without it on API 17+ the methods are inaccessible, but on older devices all public methods are exposed.
quote: In Android 4.2 (API level 17) and above, an annotation `@JavascriptInterface` explicitly allows JavaScript to access a Java method.

subject: jsonpickle.decode
truth: Stop using jsonpickle.decode(), jsonpickle.unpickler.decode(), or jsonpickle.unpickler.Unpickler().restore() on untrusted data; jsonpickle is a wrapper around pickle and inherits its deserialization risks.
quote: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.

subject: iOS Deceptive Location Purpose String
truth: Stop using a deceptive NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription purpose string that claims features the app does not provide; the purpose string must accurately describe the app's actual location-based feature.
quote: // "nearby content" feature that the app does not provide.

subject: Client-side CSRF bypasses token and SameSite defenses
truth: Do not assume CSRF tokens or SameSite cookies alone protect against client-side CSRF; the JavaScript program includes tokens in forged requests and browsers send cookies in same-site JS-initiated requests.
quote: **Note:** These variants of CSRF are particularly important as they can bypass some of the common anti-CSRF countermeasures like [token-based mitigations](https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheet
source: OWASP CS: Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: npm install with lifecycle scripts enabled
truth: Stop running npm install or yarn install without --ignore-scripts; add ignore-scripts=true to .npmrc and use an allowlist like @lavamoat/allow-scripts for packages that legitimately need lifecycle scripts.
quote: - When installing packages make sure to add the `--ignore-scripts` suffix to disable the execution of any scripts by third-party packages.
source: OWASP CS: NPM Security best practices
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Authorization server redirect behavior on error/denial
truth: Do not automatically redirect the user agent to the client's redirect_uri when the user denies consent or when the request is erroneous; authenticate the user first and only redirect if the URI is trusted.
quote: authorization server MUST always authenticate the user first

subject: strconv.Quote limitations for SQL escaping
truth: Do not use strconv.Quote for SQL escaping: it only supports double quotes (not single quotes) and wraps the entire string in quotes, so it cannot be used for substrings within a larger SQL string literal.
quote: It only supports double quotes, not single quotes

subject: Numeric parameter from URL query string
truth: Convert numeric query parameters explicitly with Int.init or Double.init and handle failure gracefully; never use the raw string from URLQueryItem.value directly in operations that expect a specific type.
quote: Never use the raw string from `URLQueryItem.value` directly in operations that expect a specific type.

subject: Using _isDebugAssertConfiguration in Swift for release detection
truth: Stop using _isDebugAssertConfiguration, _isReleaseAssertConfiguration, or _isFastAssertConfiguration; use custom compilation conditions instead as these functions are unsupported in Xcode 8+ and Swift 3+.
quote: Xcode 8 and Swift 3 don't support these functions

subject: Geofencing as MFA factor
truth: Stop relying on geofencing as a standalone MFA factor; it provides no protection if the user's system is compromised, against rogue insiders, or against attackers physically close to the trusted location.
quote: Doesn't provide any protection if the user's system is compromised
source: OWASP CS: Multifactor Authentication Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MX records pointing to deprovisioned mail services
truth: Stop leaving MX records pointing to deprovisioned mail services; remove the MX record when the mail service is decommissioned to prevent email interception and certificate-based domain validation attacks.
quote: - **MX records** pointing to deprovisioned mail services can allow an attacker to receive email for the subdomain.
source: OWASP CS: Subdomain Takeover Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Java String.matches() and Pattern.matches() dot newline bypass
truth: Stop using String.matches(regex) or Pattern.matches(regex, string) for path validation; these methods do not use DOTALL mode and a dot will not match \n or \r, allowing bypass with line break characters.
quote: A string with line return e.g. `/protected/%0dxyz` can bypass the path check

subject: WKScriptMessageHandler returning sensitive data via evaluateJavaScript
truth: Stop returning sensitive native data to JavaScript via evaluateJavaScript in WKScriptMessageHandler without origin validation; use a secure token-based callback or postMessage with origin checks instead.
quote: [MASTG-TEST-0376] Sensitive data (API key) is returned to JavaScript

associative: native bridge return data to JS
send data from native to JavaScript

subject: Behavioral profiling as standalone MFA factor
truth: Stop using behavioral profiling as a standalone MFA factor; early implementations were often inaccurate and caused significant false positives, and it requires large amounts of data and processing power.
quote: Early implementations of behavioral profiling were often inaccurate and caused a significant number of false positives
source: OWASP CS: Multifactor Authentication Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store encryption
truth: Stop using AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store without explicitly enabling encryption; do use AWS Secrets Manager which encrypts by default, or explicitly enable encryption on Parameter Store parameters.
quote: you'll need to make sure you've specified encryption yourself (secrets manager does that by default)
source: OWASP CS: Secrets Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Web service authorization on every request
truth: Authorize clients on every request by checking privileges for the requested method and resource, and add challenge-response authorization to sensitive resources like password changes and contact details.
quote: A web service should authorize its clients whether they have access to the method in question
source: OWASP CS: Web Service Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: DH finite-field parameters (1024, 160)
truth: Stop using DH finite-field parameters with less than 112 bits of security strength, specifically (len(p), len(q)) = (1024, 160) is disallowed; use safe-prime groups or (2048, 224) or (2048, 256) instead.
quote: (len(p), len(q)) = (1024, 160) Disallowed

subject: SqliteConnection::deserialize_readonly_database buffer lifetime
truth: Stop passing a borrowed buffer to SqliteConnection::deserialize_readonly_database without ensuring it outlives the connection; Diesel now stores a copy of the buffer internally to prevent use-after-free.
quote: libsqlite3 requires the buffer to remain alive for as long as the database connection is open and Diesel did not ensure this as part of its safe API

subject: Implicit Intent with startActivityForResult
truth: Stop using implicit intents with startActivityForResult without validating the returned URI; validate the return value of the intent in onActivityResult to prevent arbitrary file read or code execution.
quote: If the return value of the intent isn't properly validated, an attacker may be able to read arbitrary files or execute arbitrary code from the app's internal `/data/data/<appname>' storage.

subject: libcrux_aesgcm AES-GCM authentication tag check
truth: Stop using non-constant-time authentication tag comparisons in AES-GCM decryption; use a best-effort constant-time tag check implementation that has been verified to reliably produce constant-time code.
quote: AES-GCM decryption used an implementation for checking the provided authentication tag against the recomputed authentication tag that was intended to be constant-time, but resulted in non-constant-time code generation in certain circumstances.

subject: WebView with JavaScript enabled and DOM storage
truth: Stop enabling javaScriptEnabled and domStorageEnabled together in WebView without clearing all stored data (localStorage, sessionStorage, WebSQL, IndexedDB, cache) when the WebView is no longer needed.
quote:
javaScriptEnabled = true
domStorageEnabled = true

subject: Root Detection with finishAffinity() in Java/Kotlin
truth: Stop implementing root detection response (finishAffinity) in pure Java/Kotlin where it is trivially reversible; move detection logic to native code with string encryption and control-flow obfuscation.
quote: the reverse-engineered code still makes it straightforward to identify that the app is checking for root manager packages and closing the app when one is found

subject: Unmodeled taint propagation through external APIs
truth: Stop treating external API calls as opaque; if an external API propagates taint from its arguments to its return value or receiver, add explicit taint-step modeling so the analysis can track the flow.
quote: If the result represents a call to an external API which transfers taint, add the appropriate modeling, and re-run the query to determine what new results have appeared due to this additional modeling

subject: Network.framework TLS minimum version lowered via sec_protocol_options_set_min_tls_protocol_version
truth: Stop using sec_protocol_options_set_min_tls_protocol_version to lower the minimum TLS version; omit the call entirely to let the system use the default secure minimum, or explicitly set it to .TLSv13.
quote: Minimum TLS version set to TLS 1.0 for a Network.framework connection

subject: Missing manual deletion of WebView directories after logout
truth: Stop assuming API-level cleanup removes all WebView files; manually delete known WebView directories (app_webview, Cookies, blob_storage, Session Storage, Service Worker) using File.deleteRecursively.
quote: proper data deletion in certain directories might not be that straightforward, some apps use a pragmatic solution which is to _manually_ delete selected directories known to hold user data.

subject: Predefined safe request data list
truth: Store a predefined list of safe endpoint/method combinations in code and use a switch parameter from the URL to select which entry to use, instead of constructing requests directly from URL fragments.
quote: store a list of predefined, safe request data in the JavaScript code (e.g., combinations of endpoints, request methods and other parameters that are safe to be replayed). The program can then use a switch parameter in the URL fragment to decide which entry of the list should each JavaScript function use
source: OWASP CS: Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet

subject: Bundled third-party TLS libraries bypass ATS
truth: When bundling third-party TLS libraries (OpenSSL, BoringSSL, mbedTLS, curl), configure them to require TLS 1.2+, strong cipher suites, and full certificate chain validation — ATS does not cover them.
quote: ATS doesn't apply to calls your app makes to lower-level networking interfaces

subject: Implicit Intent return value not validated
truth: Stop copying the URI from an implicit intent result to an external temp directory without validation; validate the returned data URI and avoid writing attacker-controlled content to external storage.
quote: But if the app stores the URI provided file in an external temp directory e.g by calling `getExternalCacheDir` or `getExternalFilesDir` an attacker can read this file after setting the permission `and

subject: WebView DOM Storage, AppCache, and Database Storage explicitly enabled
truth: Stop enabling WebView DOM storage, app cache, or database storage unless the app actually uses HTML5 local storage; these are disabled by default and enabling them stores sensitive data persistently.
quote: The DOM Storage (for using the HTML5 local storage), Application Caches and Database Storage APIs are disabled by default, but apps might set these settings explicitly to "true".

subject: Server must verify PSK-associated parameters match for 0-RTT
truth: To accept early data, the server MUST verify that TLS version, cipher suite, and ALPN protocol match those associated with the selected PSK; if any check fails, the server MUST NOT accept early data.
quote: MUST verify that the following values are the same as those associated with the selected PSK

subject: CBC mode cipher suites in TLS < 1.3
truth: When supporting TLS versions prior to 1.3 with CBC mode cipher suites, use the bad_record_mac error for padding errors and compute the MAC regardless of padding errors, with constant-time decryption.
quote: compute the MAC regardless of whether padding errors exist

subject: Array bypass of string sanitizers
truth: Stop assuming HTTP request parameters are always strings; when multiple parameters with the same name are sent, the value becomes an array, and array.indexOf behaves differently from string.indexOf.
quote: if an attacker sends multiple request parameters with the same name, then the request parameter is represented as an array instead

subject: JavaScript callback interception in WebView bridge
truth: Stop injecting sensitive data into the page's JavaScript global scope via evaluateJavaScript; use WKScriptMessageHandlerWithReply's replyHandler to return data directly to the calling content world.
quote: The secure alternative is `WKScriptMessageHandlerWithReply`, which returns the reply directly to the calling content world without writing anything into the page context.

subject: callback idempotency
truth: Implement idempotency: only process an order once regardless of how many times the callback is received, and use a unique transaction ID with an expiry timestamp to mitigate callback replay attacks.
quote: Implement idempotency: only process an order once regardless of how many times the callback is received
source: OWASP CS: Secure Integration of Third-Party Payment Gateways Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: XSS via HTTP Parameter Pollution in server-side templates
truth: Stop trusting URL parameters like content_type without encoding or validation; always validate that numeric parameters are actually integers and encode all user input before embedding in JavaScript.
quote: As a result, in this example the main flaw is trusting the content_type in the `Share` page without proper encoding or validation.
source: OWASP CS: XSS Filter Evasion Cheat Sheet

subject: Manual-entry authenticators (OOB, OTP) are NOT verifier impersonation-resistant
truth: Stop treating manually-entered OTP or out-of-band authenticator outputs as resistant to verifier impersonation; they do not bind the output to the specific session and are vulnerable to MitM replay.
quote: SHALL NOT be considered verifier impersonation-resistant because the manual entry does not bind the authenticator output to the specific session

subject: time crate local offset functions
truth: Stop using time::UtcOffset::local_offset_at and related functions in multithreaded programs on Unix; they set environment variables without synchronization, causing segfaults from dangling pointers.
quote: The affected functions set environment variables without synchronization. On Unix-like operating systems, this can crash in multithreaded programs.

subject: rand::rng() / rand::thread_rng() with custom logger
truth: Stop calling rand::rng() (or rand::thread_rng()) inside a custom logger implementation, because reseeding creates an aliased mutable reference (Undefined Behavior) when the logger re-enters the RNG.
quote: aliased mutable reference, violating the Stacked Borrows rules

subject: ReDoS-vulnerable regex with ambiguous repetition
truth: Stop using ambiguous repetitions like r* or r+ where the sub-expression r can match the same string in multiple ways; instead rewrite the regex to remove ambiguity or use a non-backtracking engine.
quote: a repetition of the form r* or r+ where the sub-expression r is ambiguous in the sense that it can match some string in multiple ways

subject: setInvalidatedByBiometricEnrollment(false) in Android Keystore key generation
truth: Stop using setInvalidatedByBiometricEnrollment(false) for biometric-bound keys; use setInvalidatedByBiometricEnrollment(true) (or omit it) so keys are invalidated when biometric enrollment changes.
quote: setInvalidatedByBiometricEnrollment(false)

subject: KeyguardManager.isDeviceSecure and BiometricManager.canAuthenticate runtime detection via Frida
truth: Stop relying solely on KeyguardManager.isDeviceSecure and BiometricManager.canAuthenticate for security decisions; these APIs can be hooked and their return values manipulated at runtime via Frida.
quote: The output reveals the use of `KeyguardManager.isDeviceSecure` and `BiometricManager.canAuthenticate`.

subject: iOS LocalAuthentication evaluatePolicy without Keychain user-presence guard
truth: Stop using LAContext.evaluatePolicy alone for access control; use the Keychain API with SecAccessControlCreateWithFlags and kSecAccessControlUserPresence to cryptographically enforce user presence.
quote: The following sample insecurely accesses sensitive resources, a secret token, **relying solely** on the LocalAuthentication API for access control instead of using the Keychain API and requiring user

subject: External API as implicit taint step
truth: Stop assuming an external API only consumes data; treat every external API that returns a value derived from its arguments as a potential taint propagation step until explicitly modeled otherwise.
quote: this should be reviewed as a possible taint step, because tainted data would flow from the 0th argument to the result of the call.

subject: Root Detection with Plaintext Strings in Java/Kotlin
truth: Stop relying on identifier renaming (R8/minification) alone to obfuscate root detection logic; encrypt all sensitive strings (package names, detection messages) and apply control-flow obfuscation.
quote: the obfuscation applied to the Java/Kotlin layer is not enough to prevent an attacker from reverse engineering the root detection logic with reasonable effort

subject: iOS Siri intents exposing sensitive functionality without authentication
truth: Stop leaving Siri intents accessible without authentication; set requiresUserAuthentication to true and userConfirmationRequired to true on intents exposing sensitive information or functionality.
quote: Configure `requiresUserAuthentication` to `true` on intents that expose
source: OWASP CS: Mobile Application Security Cheat Sheet

subject: Rules files as persistent agent steering
truth: Treat .cursorrules, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and .github/copilot-instructions.md as security-sensitive configuration that can embed persistent instructions; review changes to these files in every PR.
quote: - **Rules files as persistent steering.** Files like `.cursorrules`, `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, and `.windsurfrules` silently steer every future generation.
source: OWASP CS: Secure Coding with AI Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Unmodeled external API taint sinks
truth: Stop treating unmodeled external APIs as safe; if an external API receives untrusted data and is not modeled as a taint step or sink, it may silently propagate taint or introduce vulnerabilities.
quote: External APIs may be from the .NET standard library, third-party dependencies or from internal dependencies.

subject: JavaScript reading password from DOM in WKWebView
truth: Stop reading password values from the DOM via document.getElementById('password').value in a WKWebView; use a native secure text field and pass credentials via the message handler bridge instead.
quote: the plaintext password is present in the DOM and accessible to any script running on the page before and during submission

subject: logging resource exhaustion events
truth: Avoid logging full prompts or tool inputs/outputs when detecting resource exhaustion; log only measured usage (tokens in/out), policy thresholds, request identifiers, and the tool/model involved.
quote: Avoid logging full prompts or tool inputs/outputs. Prefer logging measured usage (tokens in/out), policy thresholds, request identifiers, and the tool/model involved.
source: OWASP CS: Application Logging Vocabulary Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Storing authentication tokens in localStorage/sessionStorage
truth: Do not store authentication tokens, session IDs, JWTs, refresh tokens, or any credential in localStorage or sessionStorage; use HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict cookies or a BFF pattern instead.
quote: > Do not store authentication tokens, session IDs, JWTs, refresh tokens, or any credential in `localStorage` or `sessionStorage`.
source: OWASP CS: Session Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

associative: store auth token in web storage

subject: detecting session ID anomalies
truth: Implement built-in intrusion detection detection points for session anomalies such as cookie modification, new cookie added, session ID reuse from another user, or User-Agent changes mid-session.
quote: establish multiple session related detection points, such as when an existing cookie is modified or deleted, a new cookie is added, the session ID from another user is reused, or when the user location or User-Agent changes

subject: HTTPS does not protect cookies from network attacker injection via HTTP
truth: Do not assume HTTPS-only cookies are safe from injection; an active network attacker can inject Set-Cookie headers by impersonating an HTTP response, and the HTTPS server cannot distinguish them.
quote: An active network attacker can also inject cookies into the Cookie header sent to https://example.com/ by impersonating a response from http://example.com/ and injecting a Set-Cookie header

subject: TLS 1.3 downgrade protection random bytes
truth: When negotiating TLS 1.2 or below, set the last 8 bytes of ServerHello.random to the downgrade sentinel values (44 4F 57 4E 47 52 44 01 for TLS 1.2, 44 4F 57 4E 47 52 44 00 for TLS 1.1 or below).
quote: set the last 8 bytes of their Random value specially

subject: Encryption-then-authentication order
truth: When combining encryption and authentication as separate operations, use well-vetted standardized protocols and constructions; performing operations in the wrong order introduces vulnerabilities.
quote: If care is not taken in performing these operations (e.g., performing the operations in the right order), vulnerabilities can be introduced

subject: Exported Android Service without permission

subject: XSS output encoding via HTTP interceptor/filter
truth: Do not rely on HTTP interceptors or servlet filters for XSS output encoding; perform output encoding as close to where data is rendered as possible, with full knowledge of the rendering context.

subject: Legacy systems on flat trusted networks
truth: Stop assuming legacy systems can have direct network access; isolate them in separate monitored network zones with restricted access and require modern authentication for any access to the zone.

subject: 80-bit security strength is deprecated; 112-bit minimum required

subject: Velocity template injection from HTTP parameter

subject: External script modifying form action attribute

subject: AI-suggested package installation

subject: AI test fabrication and deletion

truth: Do not treat secret keys (symmetric) and private keys (asymmetric) as interchangeable; both need protection against unauthorized disclosure, and all keying material needs integrity protection.

subject: XPathExpression.Compile with concatenated strings
truth: Pre-compile the XPath query with variable placeholders ($varname) and use a custom XsltContext to resolve variables at runtime, instead of concatenating user input into the expression string.
quote: pre-compile the query and use variable references to include the user input

subject: Off-by-one write in HTML entity encoding loop
truth: When expanding a string in-place (e.g., encoding '&' to '&'), pre-calculate the maximum output size and check that dst_index does not exceed the allocated buffer bounds before each write.
quote: writes data past the end, or before the beginning, of the intended buffer

subject: Custom URL Scheme Handler Without Input Validation (iOS)
truth: Stop using URL query parameter values directly without type conversion and bounds checking; validate that the value is numeric and within an expected range before using it in a transfer flow.
quote: The URL handler reads a query parameter value from URLs targeting the `transfer` action and uses it directly as a string without converting it to a numeric type or checking its value against any bound

subject: WKScriptMessageHandler returns sensitive data via evaluateJavaScript without input validation
truth: Stop using evaluateJavaScript to return sensitive native data from WKScriptMessageHandler; validate the message body, check the frame origin, and avoid passing secrets to JavaScript entirely.
quote: No validation is performed on the incoming message content or origin, so any JavaScript in the page can call these actions

subject: iOS Location Capture Without UI Feature
truth: Stop silently collecting GPS coordinates via CLLocationManager when the app has no visible location-based feature; only request location when the user expects it from the app's functionality.
quote: // and records the GPS coordinates to a file while the countdown runs.

subject: Injecting sensitive data into WebView DOM via evaluateJavaScript
truth: Stop using evaluateJavaScript:completionHandler: to write sensitive data into DOM elements; display sensitive information using a native UIKit or SwiftUI view overlaid on the WebView instead.
quote: This test checks whether the app uses `evaluateJavaScript:completionHandler:` to write sensitive data into DOM elements.

subject: CAN bus communication
truth: Stop sending messages on the CAN bus without authentication or encryption; implement message authentication codes (MACs) and use secure CAN (CAN-FD with authentication) or a gateway firewall.
quote: Many vehicles use communication protocols like CAN (Controller Area Network) without adequate security measures

subject: iOS Shortcuts executing sensitive actions while device is locked
truth: Stop allowing Shortcuts to execute sensitive app functions without device unlock; validate secure tokens from Keychain and check isProtectedDataAvailable before executing sensitive shortcuts.
quote: Sensitive app functionalities triggered via Shortcuts should always require device unlock before execution.
source: OWASP CS: Mobile Application Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: RAG document ingestion from untrusted sources
truth: Scan ingested documents for adversarial patterns (prompt injection markers, hidden instructions, invisible Unicode characters, zero-width spaces) and maintain an allowlist of trusted sources.
quote: Scan ingested documents for known adversarial patterns (prompt injection markers, hidden instructions, invisible Unicode characters, zero-width spaces).
source: OWASP CS: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: XMLReader has external entity processing enabled by default
truth: Disable doctype declaration and external entity features on XMLReader by setting disallow-doctype-decl, load-external-dtd, external-general-entities, and external-parameter-entities features.

source: OWASP CS: XML External Entity Prevention Cheat Sheet

subject: Mixed content loading
truth: Stop loading any resource (CSS, SWF, scripts) over insecure connections; a single insecure embedding anywhere on the site compromises the security of the entire site including the login page.
quote: a single insecure embedding anywhere on the overall site compromises the security of their login page

subject: TLS server certificate Extended Key Usage
truth: Require id-kp-serverAuth in the Extended Key Usage extension for TLS server certificates; prohibit anyExtendedKeyUsage and all other EKU values unless consistent with the key usage extension.
quote: Prohibited: anyExtendedKeyUsage; all others unless consistent with key usage extension

subject: Loading remote URLs in WebView with JavaScript bridge
truth: Do not load JavaScript from remote endpoints into a WebView that has a JavaScript bridge; keep page navigation within the app's own domains and open all other domains in the default browser.
quote: No JavaScript should be loaded from remote endpoints, e.g. by keeping page navigation within the app's domains and opening all other domains on the default browser

subject: iOS Secure Text Entry Without Autocorrection Disabled
truth: Stop relying on secureTextEntry alone; also set autocorrectionType = UITextAutocorrectionTypeNo because secureTextEntry does not disable keyboard caching on all UITextViews and UISearchBars.
quote: You can prevent caching by disabling it programmatically, using the `textObject.autocorrectionType = UITextAutocorrectionTypeNo` directive in the desired UITextFields, UITextViews, and UISearchBars.

subject: semantic input validation
truth: Apply semantic validation to enforce correctness of values in the specific business context (e.g., start date before end date, price within expected range), not just syntactic format checks.
quote: - **Semantic** validation should enforce correctness of their *values* in the specific business context (e.g.
source: OWASP CS: Input Validation Cheat Sheet

subject: flat network deployment
truth: Stop deploying services on a single flat network; segment FRONTEND, MIDDLEWARE, and BACKEND behind separate firewalls so that compromising a web server does not grant direct database access.
quote: executing arbitrary commands on a public web server (NginX, Apache, Internet Information Service) prevents an attacker from gaining direct access to the database
source: OWASP CS: Network segmentation Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: AI coding agent processing untrusted repository content
truth: Treat all repository content (issues, PRs, comments, READMEs) as untrusted input when processed by an AI coding agent; do not assume content is safe because it appears in a familiar context.
quote: Treat all repository content (issues, PRs, comments, READMEs) as untrusted input when processed by an AI coding agent.
source: OWASP CS: Secure Coding with AI Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: AI rules files as attack surface
truth: Treat AI rules files (.cursorrules, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, copilot-instructions.md) as security-critical configuration — an attacker who modifies them controls every subsequent AI generation.
quote: These files are a persistence mechanism -- an attacker who can modify them controls every subsequent generation.
source: OWASP CS: Secure Coding with AI Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Outdated algorithm/key size guidance
truth: Stop relying on static algorithm recommendations; check SP 800-131A for current approved algorithms and key sizes, as guidance is updated when algorithms no longer provide adequate security.
quote: SP 800-57, Part 1 is updated whenever the guidance provided therein is no longer valid (e.g., an algorithm no longer provides adequate security)

subject: Verifier-CSP communications must use mutually-authenticated secure channel with approved cryptography
truth: Stop using unauthenticated or weakly-authenticated channels between verifier and CSP; use a mutually-authenticated secure channel (e.g., client-authenticated TLS) with approved cryptography.
quote: mutually-authenticated secure channel (such as a client-authenticated TLS connection) using approved cryptography

associative: secure channel between verifier and CSP

subject: Missing runtime permission request for WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE on Android 6+
truth: Always call requestPermissions and handle the onRequestPermissionsResult callback for every dangerous permission at runtime on Android 6.0+; do not assume the permission is already granted.
quote: If your application has permissions that need to be requested at runtime, the application must call the `requestPermissions` method in order to obtain them.

subject: External Control of System or Configuration Setting
truth: Do not pass user-controlled HTTP parameters directly to connection configuration APIs like setCatalog(); validate and restrict allowed values before setting database connection properties.
quote: Allowing external control of system settings can disrupt service or cause an application to behave in unexpected, and potentially malicious ways.

subject: simultaneous session logons
truth: If simultaneous session logons are not allowed, implicitly terminate the previously available session after each new authentication event, or ask the user which session must remain active.
quote: If the web application does not want to allow simultaneous session logons, it must take effective actions after each new authentication event, implicitly terminating the previously available session
source: OWASP CS: Session Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Early exporter vs regular exporter misuse
truth: Implementations MUST use the exporter_master_secret unless explicitly specified by the application; a separate interface for the early exporter is RECOMMENDED to prevent accidental misuse.
quote: Implementations MUST use the exporter_master_secret unless explicitly specified by the application

subject: Off-by-one in memcpy size calculation
truth: When computing the size argument for memcpy, ensure the calculation does not underflow to a negative value that wraps to a huge unsigned size; validate the return value before arithmetic.
quote: writes data past the end, or before the beginning, of the intended buffer
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: BiometricManager.canAuthenticate with BIOMETRIC_STRONG called without authentication callback
truth: Stop calling BiometricManager.canAuthenticate as a standalone security gate; it only checks availability/enrollment, not actual user authentication, and can be bypassed via Frida hooking.
quote: - `BiometricManager.canAuthenticate` is called from the file `MastgTest.kt`, class `MastgTest`, method `checkStrongBiometricStatus` at line 38.

subject: Emulator detection via TelephonyManager identifiers
truth: Stop checking TelephonyManager values like line1Number, networkOperatorName, voiceMailNumber against known emulator defaults; use Play Integrity API with server-side verification instead.
quote: - The returned values (e.g., `+15551234567` for `getLine1Number`, `T-Mobile` for `getNetworkOperatorName`) are typical emulator defaults.

subject: Native ptrace self-check anti-debugging
truth: Stop using ptrace(PTRACE_SEIZE) or ptrace(PTRACE_ATTACH) self-checks for anti-debugging; they are easily bypassed by LD_PRELOAD hooks or kernel modules and give a false sense of security.
quote: Note that this demo uses `ptrace(PTRACE_SEIZE)` for its self-check, but you should also check for `ptrace(PTRACE_ATTACH)`, which is an alternative approach used in real-world apps.

subject: Android Backup (allowBackup)
truth: Stop leaving android:allowBackup=true (the default for API 23+) which sends app data including SharedPreferences to Google Cloud; set android:allowBackup=false or exclude sensitive files.
quote: Auto Backup is enabled by default for applications that target Android 6.0 (API level 23)

subject: Storing secrets in Web Workers
truth: For secrets that frontend JavaScript must access, store them in a Web Worker and never transmit the secret to the main window context; this provides HttpOnly-cookie-level confidentiality.
quote: Storing secrets within the memory of a Web Worker offers the same security guarantees as an HttpOnly cookie

subject: Referer header from secure to cross-origin
truth: A user agent SHOULD NOT send a Referer header field if the referring resource was accessed with a secure protocol and the request target has a different origin, unless explicitly allowed.
quote: A user agent SHOULD NOT send a Referer header field if the referring resource was accessed with a secure protocol and the request target has an origin differing from that of the referring resource

associative: Referer header HTTPS to different origin

subject: Silent authentication (prompt=none) open redirect
truth: Do not automatically redirect the user agent to the attacker-controlled redirect_uri during silent authentication (prompt=none) unless the authorization server trusts the redirection URI.
quote: authorization server SHOULD only automatically redirect the user agent if it trusts the redirection URI

subject: Token validation failure handling
truth: Stop returning true for all paths in token validation delegates; throw SecurityTokenInvalidAudienceException or SecurityTokenInvalidLifetimeException to fail validation when appropriate.
quote: throw SecurityTokenInvalidAudienceException or SecurityTokenInvalidLifetimeException in failure cases when you want to fail validation and have other cases pass by returning true

subject: Allowlist validation before outbound HTTP requests
truth: Do not validate user-supplied URLs by checking against a blocklist or prefix; instead maintain an allowlist of authorized URLs on the server and choose from that list based on the input.
quote: maintain a list of authorized URLs on the server; then choose from that list based on the input provided

subject: Extended biometric key validity duration in Android Keystore
truth: Stop using extended validity durations like 86400 seconds in setUserAuthenticationParameters; set the duration to 0 to require biometric authentication for every cryptographic operation.
quote: When a key is configured with `setUserAuthenticationParameters(86400, type)` (86400 seconds = 24 hours), it remains unlocked for 24 hours after successful authentication.

subject: WebView allowContentAccess default
truth: Stop relying on the default 'true' value of WebView's allowContentAccess; explicitly set allowContentAccess = false when the WebView does not need to load content from content providers.
quote: /* `allowContentAccess` is intentionally not set to false to showcase the default behavior.

subject: WKWebView native bridge exposes sensitive data to JavaScript without origin validation
truth: Stop exposing sensitive native data via WKScriptMessageHandler without validating the message origin; validate the message origin and sanitize inputs before returning data to JavaScript.

subject: Password length and composition rules
truth: Enforce a minimum password length (8 chars with MFA, 15 without) and a maximum of at least 64 characters; allow all characters including unicode and whitespace with no composition rules.
quote: There should be no password composition rules limiting the type of characters permitted.
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Prototype-based data skimming in extension main-world scripts
truth: Stop executing scripts with sensitive user information in the web page's main-world context where prototypes can be polluted; use only non-sensitive data for cross-context communication.
quote: When an extension uses sensitive user information in any scripts executed on the web page's context, the data becomes accessible to the page's scripts.
source: OWASP CS: Browser Extension Security Vulnerabilities Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Client-side CSRF input validation gap
truth: Validate that async request endpoints and methods derived from attacker-controllable inputs only allow non-state-changing operations (e.g., GET only, endpoints with a predefined prefix).
quote: input validation checks has to be implemented. These checks should strictly assess the format and choice of the values of the request parameters and decide whether they can only be used in non-state-changing operations
source: OWASP CS: Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet

subject: Azure App Service deletion
truth: Delete the Azure App Service AND remove the DNS CNAME record pointing to *.azurewebsites.net; otherwise any Azure tenant can create a new app with the same name and claim your subdomain.
quote: App names are globally unique. After deletion, any Azure tenant can create a new app with the same name.
