subject: Trailer field merging
truth: Do not merge trailer fields into the header section unless the recipient knows the corresponding field definition permits and defines how to merge; otherwise store separately or discard.
quote: implementations are encouraged either to store the trailer fields separately or to discard them instead of merging

subject: 80-bit protected data processing carries accepted risk
truth: Processing already-protected data at the 80-bit security strength is allowed but a certain amount of risk must be accepted; do not apply new cryptographic protections at 80-bit strength.
quote: processing of this already-protected data at the lower security strength is allowed, but a certain amount of risk must be accepted

subject: EdDSA deterministic signatures vs ECDSA/DSA random nonces
truth: Prefer EdDSA (Ed25519/Ed448) over ECDSA/DSA because EdDSA signatures are deterministic and do not require a random bit generator for each signature, avoiding nonce reuse vulnerabilities.
quote: EdDSA signatures are deterministic: the unique value is computed using the private key and the message to be signed

subject: LDAPS endpoint identification disabled via system property
truth: Stop disabling LDAPS endpoint identification via System.setProperty("com.sun.jndi.ldap.object.disableEndpointIdentification", "true"); instead, leave the default enabled check in place.
quote: frequently developers simply disable the LDAPS endpoint check

subject: Spring HttpInvokerServiceExporter
truth: Stop using HttpInvokerServiceExporter, SimpleHttpInvokerServiceExporter, RmiServiceExporter, or HessianServiceExporter; they deserialize untrusted data and enable remote code execution.
quote: If a remote attacker can reach endpoints created by the exporters, it results in remote code execution in the worst case.

subject: URL allowlist bypass via substring
truth: Stop using 'in' to check if a host appears anywhere in a URL string; an attacker can embed the allowed host anywhere (e.g., 'evil-example.net/example.com') to bypass the check silently.
quote: the attacker can simply add example.com anywhere in the url

subject: XML parsing in Python
truth: Stop using xml.etree.cElementTree, xml.etree.ElementTree, xml.sax, xml.dom.expatbuilder, xml.dom.minidom, and xml.dom.pulldom to parse untrusted XML; use defusedxml equivalents instead.
quote: Using various XLM methods to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable

subject: Logging exception with user object in Android
truth: Never log the full User object or its toString() representation in exception handlers, as it may contain sensitive fields; log only the exception message and a non-sensitive identifier.
quote: Do not write secrets into the log files

subject: Missing WebStorage.deleteAllData and CookieManager.removeAllCookies on logout
truth: Stop relying only on clearCache; call WebStorage.getInstance().deleteAllData() and CookieManager.getInstance().removeAllCookies(null) to fully clear JavaScript storage APIs and cookies.
quote: `WebStorage.deleteAllData` can be also used to clear all storage currently being used by the JavaScript storage APIs, including the Web SQL Database and the HTML5 Web Storage APIs.

subject: runner segregation
truth: Use runner groups and labels to separate high-privilege runners from low-privilege runners, giving granular control over which repositories and workflows can access sensitive resources.
quote: Use [runner groups](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/concepts/runners/runner-groups) and [labels](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/manage-runners/self-hosted-runners/apply-labels) to separ
source: OWASP CS: GitHub Actions Security Cheat Sheet

subject: Microservices security documentation
truth: Stop skipping structured security documentation of microservice building blocks; collect and document service names, API definitions, data storages, message queues, and their relations.
quote: Collect information on the building blocks
source: OWASP CS: Microservices based Security Arch Doc Cheat Sheet

subject: MFA factor independence
truth: Stop requiring multiple instances of the same factor (e.g., password + PIN); require factors from different categories that are independent and cannot be compromised by the same attack.
quote: It should be noted that requiring multiple instances of the same authentication factor (such as needing both a password and a PIN) **does not constitute MFA** and offers minimal additional security.
source: OWASP CS: Multifactor Authentication Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: TLS 1.3 client downgrade check
truth: TLS 1.3 clients receiving a ServerHello indicating TLS 1.2 or below MUST check the last 8 random bytes are not the downgrade sentinel values and abort with illegal_parameter if matched.
quote: MUST check that the last 8 bytes are not equal to either of these values

subject: TLS server cipher suite configuration
truth: Configure the server to only use NIST-recommended cipher suites by disabling all non-approved cipher suites, especially when the server does not allow administrator preference ordering.
quote: the only way to ensure that a server uses NIST-approved algorithms is to disable cipher suites that use other algorithms

subject: ProcessName-to-hash flow as backdoor indicator
truth: Stop flowing ProcessName into a hash function; this pattern matches Solorigate-style backdoors that compare hashed process names against an obfuscated blocklist of antivirus processes.
quote: code flow from ProcessName property on the Process class into a hash function

subject: WKNavigationDelegate server trust evaluation
truth: Always call SecTrustEvaluateWithError on serverTrust before calling completionHandler(.useCredential, ...) in WKNavigationDelegate; never accept a certificate without evaluating trust.
quote: // for WebView connections by accepting any certificate without calling SecTrustEvaluateWithError.

subject: Verbose/Debug Logging in Production iOS Apps
truth: Stop using print, NSLog, or ad hoc logging in production; use Apple's Unified Logging system (Logger/os_log) with privacy modifiers and compile out verbose logging from release builds.
quote: Avoid ad hoc logging through `print`, `NSLog`, or third-party SDKs that do not support structured logging and privacy controls.

subject: Missing Bounds Checking on URL Scheme Parameters
truth: After type conversion, verify that the value falls within acceptable bounds before acting on it; enforce both minimum and maximum limits for financial or resource-sensitive operations.
quote: verify that the value falls within acceptable bounds before acting on it

subject: SameSite=Lax allowing state-changing GET requests
truth: Stop using GET endpoints for state-changing operations when relying on SameSite=Lax; the default Lax behavior still allows cookies on top-level navigations using safe methods like GET.
quote: If any state-changing operation in the application is reachable via a `GET` request, `SameSite=Lax` will not stop it.

subject: Decompression bomb (gzip/any compression)
truth: Attackers can create a huge file by just repeating a single byte and compress it to a small file — always enforce a decompressed size threshold when extracting user-provided archives.
quote: Attackers can create a huge file by just repeating a single byte and compress it to a small file

subject: Express.js POST handler authentication assumption
truth: Do not assume cookie-based session authentication alone protects POST endpoints from CSRF; an attacker can forge requests on behalf of an authenticated user visiting a malicious site.
quote: An attacker can submit a POST changeEmail request on behalf of a user who visited a malicious website

subject: urlparse misparsing mistyped URLs
truth: Mistyped URLs like https:/example.com or https:///example.com have empty netloc in urlparse but browsers still redirect to the external host; strip backslashes and validate carefully.
quote: Mistyped URLs such as https:/example.com or https:///example.com are parsed as having an empty netloc attribute, while browsers will still redirect to the correct site.

subject: Logging sensitive data in Java
truth: Never log personally identifiable information (PII), credentials, or financial data like credit card numbers; log only non-sensitive identifiers or omit the sensitive fields entirely.
quote: Do not write secrets into the log files

subject: Logging SQL query with sensitive data in exception handler
truth: Never log the full SQL query string in exception handlers when the query contains user-controlled or sensitive data; log only a generic error message and a non-sensitive reference ID.
quote: Do not write secrets into the log files
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: ContentProvider DISPLAY_NAME path traversal
truth: Never trust the DISPLAY_NAME returned by an external ContentProvider; always sanitize or strip path separators from provider-controlled filenames before using them in file operations.
quote: The vulnerability lies in the victim app trusting and using the filename returned by an external `ContentProvider` without sanitization.

subject: SharedPreferences storing secrets unencrypted
truth: Stop storing sensitive data in SharedPreferences without encryption; use EncryptedSharedPreferences or encrypt values with a KeyStore-backed key before calling putString/putStringSet.

subject: iOS Simulator vs device WebView file access enforcement
truth: Do not rely on iOS Simulator for WebView file-access testing; the Simulator is more permissive and allows JavaScript to reach local files even when allowingReadAccessTo is restricted.
quote: The iOS Simulator is **more permissive** than a physical device when it comes to `file://` access in WebViews.

subject: Global callback functions in WKWebView JavaScript bridge
truth: Stop using global JavaScript callback functions (e.g., window.receiveSecret) for native bridge responses; any JavaScript in the page can override them before the native handler fires.
quote: any JavaScript running on the page can override them before the native handler fires, intercepting the sensitive values on their way back from native code

subject: CI/CD poisoned pipeline execution (PPE)
truth: Do not allow untrusted code or user-controlled input to define pipeline execution logic; implement strict pipeline-based access controls (PBAC) to prevent poisoned pipeline execution.
quote: Poisoned Pipeline Execution (PPE)

subject: SameSite cookie attribute set to domain-specific cookie
truth: Do NOT set a cookie specifically for a domain, as all subdomains will share the cookie and a subdomain with a CNAME to domains not in your control introduces a security vulnerability.
quote: be careful to NOT set a cookie specifically for a domain. This action introduces a security vulnerability because all subdomains of that domain will share the cookie, and this is particularly an issue if a subdomain has a CNAME to domains not in your control.

subject: CNAME records pointing to third-party services
truth: Stop creating CNAME records to third-party services without a process to remove them when the service is decommissioned; implement automated monitoring to detect dangling DNS targets.
quote: - **CNAME records** are the most common vector.

subject: NS records delegating to third-party DNS providers
truth: Stop leaving NS records that delegate subdomains to third-party DNS providers after closing the provider account; remove the NS delegation before deactivating the DNS service account.
quote: - **NS records** delegating a subdomain to a third-party DNS provider are particularly dangerous.

subject: virtual patching
truth: Use a positive security model (allowlist) that validates parameter values against an expected pattern rather than a negative security model (denylist) that blocks specific characters.

subject: TLS server handling missing client certificate
truth: Configure the TLS server to terminate the connection with a fatal 'handshake failure' alert when a client certificate is requested but the client does not have a suitable certificate.

subject: narrow integer overflow in loop condition
truth: Stop using a narrow integer type as a loop counter when the termination bound exceeds the narrow type's maximum; the counter will overflow silently and the loop will never terminate.
quote: the narrower value may overflow. This can lead to an infinite loop

subject: LDAP injection via unencoded user input in DN and filter
truth: Always encode user input for both DN (encodeForDN) and search filter (encodeForLDAP) using OWASP ESAPI, or use framework builders that auto-escape, to prevent LDAP injection attacks.
quote: If user input must be included in an LDAP query, it should be escaped to avoid a malicious user providing special characters that change the meaning of the query.

subject: Android WebView with JavaScript enabled and setAllowFileAccessFromFileURLs(true) loading untrusted URLs
truth: Stop calling setAllowFileAccessFromFileURLs(true) when JavaScript is enabled and WebView loads untrusted content; this allows file:// context scripts to access arbitrary local files.

subject: ObjectInputStream.readObject() from Intent extra
truth: Stop deserializing untrusted data from Intent extras with ObjectInputStream.readObject() without class filtering; use a safe deserialization library or validate against an allowlist.

subject: EncryptedSharedPreferences deprecated library
truth: Stop using the deprecated Jetpack security crypto library (EncryptedSharedPreferences/EncryptedFile) without a migration plan; use it only until an official replacement is available.

subject: GitHub Actions workflow static analysis
truth: Enable CodeQL with 'language: actions' and use Zizmor to scan GitHub Actions workflows; configure them to run on every pull request and block merges on high/critical severity issues.
quote: - If available, enable [CodeQL](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/reference/code-scanning/codeql/codeql-queries/actions-built-in-queries) `actions` scanning in your repositories to detect vulne
source: OWASP CS: GitHub Actions Security Cheat Sheet

subject: Removing '../' sequences from user input

quote: removing "../" sequences is not sufficient, since the input could still contain a path separator followed by "..". For example, the input "

subject: Sensitive patient data stored in world-readable ContentProvider database
truth: Stop storing sensitive patient medical records (diagnosis, notes) in an exported ContentProvider without access restrictions; use permission-protected providers or internal storage.
quote: exposes sensitive patient medical records through an exported database-backed content provider without enforcing appropriate access restrictions

subject: iOS CCCrypt encryption without runtime hook detection
truth: Stop using CCCrypt for sensitive data without runtime integrity verification; implement runtime hook detection (e.g., via @MASTG-DEMO-0118) or use the Keychain for storing API keys.
quote: The app calls CCCrypt with sensitive data without detecting runtime hooks.

subject: iOS App Permissions - Missing Authorization Check
truth: Always check authorization status via dedicated APIs (e.g. CLLocationManager.locationServicesEnabled) before accessing protected resources; users can revoke permissions at any time.

subject: iOS Keyboard Cache Leaks Sensitive Data
truth: Stop using default UITextField/UITextView/UISearchBar for sensitive input; set autocorrectionType = UITextAutocorrectionTypeNo and secureTextEntry = YES to prevent keyboard caching.
quote: The application must prevent the caching of sensitive information entered into text fields.

subject: baseline vs diff-based review
truth: Perform baseline reviews for new applications, major releases, legacy onboarding, compliance, and post-incident analysis; use diff-based reviews for pull requests and daily changes.
quote: **Baseline Reviews** examine the entire codebase comprehensively.
source: OWASP CS: Secure Code Review Cheat Sheet

subject: positive security model virtual patching
truth: Use a positive security model (allowlist) that specifies characteristics of valid input and denies anything that does not conform, rather than trying to block known attack patterns.
quote: Positive security model (allowlist) is a comprehensive security mechanism that provides an independent input validation envelope to an application.
source: OWASP CS: Virtual Patching Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: External API calls with untrusted data
truth: Stop passing unsanitized untrusted data directly to external API calls; validate and sanitize all untrusted input before passing it to any function not defined in your source code.
quote: Using unsanitized untrusted data in an external API can cause a variety of security issues

subject: Ignoring external API sink results
truth: Stop dismissing external API results as uninteresting; first verify whether the highlighted API is a known security sink (XSS, injection, etc.) before classifying it as irrelevant.
quote: If the result highlights a known sink, no action is required. If the result highlights an unknown sink for a problem, then add modeling for the sink to the relevant query.

subject: lxml.etree.GlobalParserTLS
truth: Stop using lxml.etree.GlobalParserTLS or lxml.etree.getDefaultParser without configuring a parser that disables external entities; they return parsers vulnerable to XXE by default.
quote: xml_bad_etree

subject: Relative Path Traversal in file upload
truth: Canonicalize the upload path and verify it starts with the intended upload directory before writing the file; do not concatenate user-supplied filename directly into the file path.

subject: BiometricManager.canAuthenticate check without actual authentication
truth: Stop treating canAuthenticate(BiometricManager.Authenticators.BIOMETRIC_STRONG) as proof of biometric authentication; it only checks availability/enrollment, not user verification.
quote: This sample checks if the device has a secure lock screen via `KeyguardManager.isDeviceSecure` and if the device supports strong biometric authentication using `BiometricManager.canAuthenticate`.

subject: iOS deserialization with NSCoding and requiresSecureCoding=false
truth: Stop using NSCoding with requiresSecureCoding=false for deserializing attacker-controlled data; use NSSecureCoding with unarchivedObject(ofClass:from:) to restrict decoded classes.
quote: requiresSecureCoding = false

subject: AI coding agent running without sandboxing
truth: Run AI coding agents in sandboxed environments (dev containers, restricted shells, VMs, ephemeral workspaces); do not run agents with full developer credentials without sandboxing.
quote: Run AI coding agents in sandboxed environments: dev containers, restricted shells, virtual machines, or ephemeral cloud workspaces.
source: OWASP CS: Secure Coding with AI Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Key management as security foundation
truth: Do not assume strong algorithms alone provide security; the protection of data reduces to the protection of key information, requiring a Cryptographic Key Management System (CKMS).
quote: the security of information protected by cryptography directly depends on the protection afforded the key information

subject: XPath query built via string concatenation
truth: Stop building XPath expressions by concatenating user input; use a variable-binding mechanism (e.g., XPathExpression with variable resolver) or pre-compiled parameterized queries.
quote: does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements

subject: Exported BroadcastReceiver without permission
truth: Stop declaring exported BroadcastReceivers with android:exported="true" and no android:permission; enforce access control with a custom permission or set android:exported="false".

subject: isSecureTextEntry alone insufficient for keyboard caching
truth: For non-password UITextField inputs handling sensitive data (names, emails, etc.), explicitly set both autocorrectionType and spellCheckingType to .no to prevent keyboard caching.
quote: this input remains eligible for keyboard caching

subject: OS command input validation with allow-list regex
truth: When OS commands must accept user-supplied arguments, validate them against an allow-list regular expression that excludes metacharacters like & | ; $ > < \ ` ! and limits length.
quote: - Allow-list Regular Expression - where is explicitly defined a list of good characters allowed and the maximum length of the string.

subject: iOS deep links bypassing authentication
truth: Stop allowing deep links to bypass authentication; implement authentication checks on view controllers accessed via deep links and redirect unauthorized users to the login screen.

subject: domain verification for cloud services
truth: Retain domain verification TXT records (e.g., Azure asuid, Google Search Console) even after decommissioning the cloud resource to prevent another tenant from claiming the domain.
quote: Keep this TXT record in place even after decommissioning the App Service to prevent another tenant from claiming the domain.
source: OWASP CS: Subdomain Takeover Prevention Cheat Sheet

subject: TLS 1.3 record layer version field
truth: Set legacy_record_version to 0x0303 for all TLS 1.3 records except initial ClientHello; do not use 0x0300 (SSL 3.0) or 0x0301 (TLS 1.0) except for backward-compatible ClientHello.
quote: MUST be set to 0x0303 for all records generated by a TLS 1.3 implementation

subject: Deprecated symmetric-key algorithms for encryption
truth: Stop using deprecated symmetric-key algorithms for applying cryptographic protection (encryption); they may only be used for processing already-protected information (decryption).
quote: cryptographic protection (e.g., encryption) but may continue to be used for processing

subject: UserIdentity::is_verified in matrix-sdk-crypto
truth: Stop using UserIdentity::is_verified() without separately checking the verification status of the user's own identity; the method may return a value contrary to its documentation.
quote: doesn't take into account the verification status of the user's own identity while performing the check and may as a result return a value contrary to what is implied by its name and documentation

subject: Trojan Source attack via invisible Unicode reordering
truth: Stop relying on visual code review to catch logic-altering Unicode characters; run Bandit rule B613 to detect bidirectional control characters that silently change program logic.

subject: HTTPSConnection on old Python

quote: Use of HTTPSConnection on older versions of Python prior to 2.7.9 and 3.4.3 do not provide security

subject: Loading third-party JavaScript from external domains
truth: Do not load executable scripts (JavaScript) from untrusted external domains; host all scripts on your own controlled server or use Subresource Integrity (SRI) with a trusted CDN.
quote: Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere

subject: Android app trusts user-installed CA certificates by default
truth: Stop referencing a Network Security Configuration that includes user-added CAs; remove the android:networkSecurityConfig attribute or ensure only system certificates are trusted.
quote: Network Security Configuration that permits user-added CA certificates

subject: FileProvider URI grant with arbitrary filename from exported activity
truth: Stop accepting arbitrary file_name extras from intents in exported activities that return FileProvider URIs; validate the filename against an allowlist to prevent path traversal.
quote: Any app that receives a URI grant from the victim's `ShareReportActivity` can request any filename — including sensitive files such as `session_token.txt`.

subject: WKWebView allowFileAccessFromFileURLs enabled via KVC
truth: Stop setting allowFileAccessFromFileURLs to true via KVC; this grants JavaScript access to local file:// URLs and enables reading arbitrary files within the read-access boundary.
quote: This sample demonstrates a WKWebView with the undocumented `allowFileAccessFromFileURLs` property enabled, allowing JavaScript to read other local `file://` URLs even when `loadFileURL:allowingReadAcc

subject: Exported BroadcastReceiver leaks decrypted password via SMS
truth: Stop exporting BroadcastReceivers that handle sensitive data without a permission; use LocalBroadcastManager or set android:permission and never send decrypted secrets in extras.
quote: BroadcastReceivers should use the `android:permission` attribute;

subject: email change without MFA
truth: When the user does NOT have MFA enabled, require the current password for identity verification AND send confirmation-required emails to BOTH the current and new email addresses.
quote: Request the user's current password for identity verification.

subject: encryption algorithm selection for secrets
truth: Select an algorithm that provides both encryption and authentication simultaneously, such as AES-256-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305, rather than using unauthenticated encryption modes.
quote: Please note that in all cases, we need to preferably select an algorithm that provides encryption and confidentiality at the same time, such as AES-256 using GCM [(Galois Counter Mode)](https://en.wik

subject: binding session ID to IP address for security
truth: Do not rely on client IP address binding to defend against session hijacking; a skilled attacker can bypass this by sharing the same network or using the same outbound web proxy.
quote: a skilled attacker can bypass these controls by reusing the same IP address assigned to the victim user by sharing the same network

subject: Domain cookie scoping allows sibling-domain overwrite
truth: Do not assume cookies set by one subdomain are isolated from sibling subdomains; a server on foo.example.com can overwrite cookies for example.com that bar.example.com relies on.
quote: Cookies do not provide integrity guarantees for sibling domains

subject: legacy_compression_methods must be null-only in TLS 1.3
truth: Set legacy_compression_methods to exactly one byte of value zero (null compression) in TLS 1.3 ClientHello; any other value must cause the server to abort with illegal_parameter.
quote: this vector MUST contain exactly one byte, set to zero

subject: DSA signature certificates for TLS
truth: At minimum, configure TLS servers with an RSA signature certificate or an ECDSA signature certificate; DSA, DH, and ECDH certificates are not commonly used and should be avoided.
quote: TLS servers conforming to this specification shall be configured with an RSA signature certificate or an ECDSA signature certificate.

subject: dangerous native function calls (P/Invoke)
truth: Stop calling dangerous native functions like NtCreateProcess, NtCreateThreadEx, or NtWriteVirtualMemory via P/Invoke; they are commonly used for backdoors and process injection.
quote: finds native calls to external functions that are often used in creating backdoors or are generally attributed to unsafe code practices

subject: Android biometric authentication callback pattern

subject: JMX-RMI server initialization with null/empty environment
truth: Always pass an environment with a deserialization filter to JMXConnectorServerFactory.newJMXConnectorServer() or RMIConnectorServer constructor; never pass null or an empty map.
quote: the JMX-RMI service is vulnerable in the default configuration. For this reason an initialization with a null environment is also vulnerable.

subject: SSRF via internal network boundary bypass
truth: Do not allow user-controlled hostnames in outgoing requests from a server connected to an internal network, as this can bypass the network boundary and target internal services.
quote: If the server performing the request is connected to an internal network, this can give an attacker the means to bypass the network boundary and make requests against internal services.

subject: Firebase Analytics silently exfiltrates sensitive data
truth: Do not pass personally identifiable information (PII) or health data as event parameters to Firebase Analytics; the SDK sends this data to Google servers without user awareness.
quote: sensitive user data and sends it to Firebase Analytics using the `logEvent` method

subject: Android cleartext HTTP traffic via usesCleartextTraffic
truth: Stop setting android:usesCleartextTraffic to true in AndroidManifest.xml; remove the attribute or set it to false and use a Network Security Configuration that blocks cleartext.
quote: Setting the `android:usesCleartextTraffic` attribute of the `<application>` tag.

subject: server-side cart validation
truth: Validate all cart details (product IDs, prices, discounts) on the backend and recalculate totals server-side using trusted data before order creation to prevent price tampering.

subject: XSS via event handlers in HTML tags

subject: TLS 1.3 HelloRetryRequest key_share validation
truth: Upon receipt of key_share in HelloRetryRequest, the client MUST verify selected_group was in supported_groups and NOT in the original key_share, or abort with illegal_parameter.

subject: clean_text output in unquoted HTML attributes

subject: String.utf8.count / String.utf16.count / String.unicodeScalars.count used in place of String.count
truth: Stop using String.utf8.count, String.utf16.count, or String.unicodeScalars.count as a substitute for String.count; they measure different units and can cause buffer overwrites.

subject: XSS sanitization via naive string replacement
truth: Stop using naive string replacement (e.g., replaceAll) to sanitize XSS; use a dedicated HTML sanitization library or output encoding that handles all alternate syntax variants.

truth: Stop using kSecAccessControlBiometryAny which accepts newly enrolled biometrics; use kSecAccessControlBiometryCurrentSet to bind access to the biometric set at enrollment time.

truth: Stop logging sensitive data with Kotlin string interpolation in Log calls; the compiled bytecode leaks the constructed string into memory even if ProGuard removes the Log call.

subject: Origin/Referer header verification for CSRF
truth: Verify the Origin header matches the target origin; if absent, verify the Referer header hostname matches the target origin, and block requests where neither header is present.
quote: If the Origin header is not present, verify that the hostname in the Referer header matches the target origin.

subject: File upload storage location
truth: Store uploaded files on a different server or outside the webroot; if public access is needed, use a handler that maps an internal ID to the file instead of direct file access.
quote: Store the files on a different server. If that's not possible, store them outside of the webroot

subject: input validation as primary defense
truth: Do not use input validation as the primary method of preventing XSS, SQL Injection, or other injection attacks; use dedicated output encoding and parameterized queries instead.

subject: Node.js X-XSS-Protection header default
truth: Set X-XSS-Protection to 0 to disable the XSS Auditor instead of enabling it, as modern browsers deprecated the header and enabling it can introduce client-side security issues.

subject: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy (COOP) for frame counting
truth: Set the Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy header to 'same-origin' or 'same-origin-allow-popups' to prevent cross-origin documents from accessing the window object and counting frames.
quote: Setting this header will prevent cross-origin documents from opening in the same browsing context group.

subject: Legacy systems with weak authentication
truth: Stop relying on basic username/password authentication for legacy systems; deploy an identity-aware proxy that handles MFA and session management in front of the legacy system.

subject: cookies lack confidentiality/integrity by default even with HTTPS
truth: Do not rely on cookies alone for confidentiality or integrity; cookies do not provide confidentiality or integrity from network attackers by default, even when used with HTTPS.
quote: by default, cookies do not provide confidentiality or integrity from network attackers, even when used in conjunction with HTTPS

subject: Hybrid symmetric-asymmetric approach
truth: Use asymmetric-key algorithms only for digital signatures and initial key establishment; use symmetric-key algorithms for all other encryption, especially bulk data protection.
quote: a hybrid approach is often used whereby asymmetric-key algorithms are used for the generation and verification of digital signatures and for initial key establishment, while symmetric-key algorithms are used for all other purposes

subject: Client-side URL redirect from user-controlled input
truth: Stop redirecting to URLs constructed from untrusted DOM input; instead, maintain a server-side allowlist of authorized redirects and choose from that list based on user input.
quote: avoid putting user input directly into a redirect URL

subject: SharedPreferences XML readable on rooted/compromised devices
truth: Do not assume app sandbox isolation protects SharedPreferences files; encrypt all sensitive values because the XML file is readable on rooted devices or via backup extraction.
quote: if the app data directory becomes accessible, for example on a rooted or compromised device or through backup extraction, any unencrypted values stored in these XML files can be read directly.

subject: iOS deserialization gadget via CachedDocument file write
truth: Stop allowing deserialization of arbitrary classes from untrusted archives; a gadget class like CachedDocument can write attacker-controlled files to disk during init(coder:).

subject: Silent Background Location Capture Without UI Justification
truth: Stop collecting GPS coordinates in the background during a countdown without any location-based UI feature; the location access must be justified by visible app functionality.
quote: // and records the GPS coordinates to a file while the countdown runs.

subject: iOS iCloud backup exclusion for sensitive files
truth: Stop writing sensitive data to backup-eligible directories without setting NSURLIsExcludedFromBackupKey; always exclude sensitive files from iCloud backup or use the Keychain.
quote: eligible for backup

subject: storing encryption keys in the same location as encrypted data
truth: Store encryption keys in a separate location from encrypted data (e.g., keys in filesystem, data in database) so an attacker cannot access both through a single vulnerability.
quote: encryption keys should be stored in a separate location from encrypted data

subject: PKCE downgrade attack mitigation
truth: Authorization servers must ensure a token request containing a code_verifier parameter is accepted only if a code_challenge parameter was present in the authorization request.
quote: Authorization Servers are mitigating PKCE Downgrade Attacks by ensuring a token request containing a "code_verifier" parameter is accepted only if a "code_challenge" parameter is present in the authorization request.
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Password length > algorithm block size gets hashed first
truth: Stop assuming longer passwords always increase security; passwords larger than an algorithm-specific size are first hashed, reducing effective search space to the hash length.
quote: passwords larger than a certain algorithm-specific size are first hashed, which reduces an attacker's effective search space to the length of the hash algorithm

subject: auditing for key compromise prevention
truth: Stop relying on key management without auditing; perform compliance audits, cryptographic mechanism audits, and human-action audits to detect and recover from key compromises.
quote: Auditing is a mechanism used for the prevention, detection, and recovery from key compromises

subject: Verifier compromise resistant public keys require approved crypto at 112-bit minimum
truth: Stop storing verifier compromise resistant secrets without approved hash algorithms; public keys must use approved crypto at minimum 112-bit security strength per SP 800-131A.
quote: public keys stored by the verifier SHALL be associated with the use of approved cryptographic algorithms and SHALL provide at least the minimum security strength specified in the latest revision of SP 800-131A

subject: HTTP URL used with HttpsURLConnection
truth: Stop constructing URLs with the HTTP protocol when you intend to use HttpsURLConnection; use HTTPS URLs instead to ensure secure SSL connections and avoid ClassCastException.
quote: Constructing URLs with the HTTP protocol can lead to unsecured connections

subject: Runtime.exec with user-controlled script argument
truth: Stop passing user-controlled strings directly as script arguments to Runtime.exec; instead validate the input against a whitelist of hard-coded script names before execution.
quote: The script to be executed by /bin/sh is controlled by the user.

subject: Tool description injection via user-controlled topic
truth: Stop concatenating user input into tool descriptions exposed to AI agents; keep tool descriptions as fixed trusted strings and pass user input via user-role messages instead.
quote: user input is used in the description of a tool exposed to the agent

subject: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in Python
truth: Stop passing user input directly to requests.get() or similar HTTP request functions; validate and sanitize the input against an allowlist of permitted hosts or URL patterns.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into an HTTP request without validating the input can facilitate server-side request forgery (SSRF) attacks.

subject: ReDoS-vulnerable regex with ambiguous repetition
truth: Stop using regex patterns with ambiguous repetitions (r* or r+) that can match the same input in multiple ways; rewrite the pattern to remove ambiguity or limit input length.
quote: contains a repetition of the form r* or r+ where the sub-expression r is ambiguous

subject: os.chmod with group-writable or group-executable permissions
truth: Stop using os.chmod with stat.S_IWGRP, stat.S_IXGRP, or masks like 0o664, 0o770, 0o776, 0o760 that grant group write/execute; restrict to owner-only or owner+group read-only.
quote: a file is set to group write or executable and a HIGH warning is reported if a

subject: signed integer subtraction without overflow check
truth: Always validate that the subtrahend is not greater than the minuend before performing subtraction on signed integers, or use a safe arithmetic library that detects underflow.
quote: subtracts one value from another, such that the result is less than the minimum allowable integer value

subject: Java ObjectInputStream.readObject() on attacker-controlled input
truth: Stop calling ObjectInputStream.readObject() on attacker-controlled input from Intent extras; use a safe parsing approach like JSON with kotlinx.serialization or Gson instead.
quote: The app deserializes objects from an untrusted source without any type filtering or validation.

subject: SSLSocket bypassing Network Security Configuration
truth: Stop assuming Network Security Configuration (NSC) protects SSLSocket connections; NSC does not affect SSLSocket, so hostname verification must be handled explicitly in code.
quote: **Note:** The connection succeeds even if the app has a fully secure Network Security Configuration (NSC) in place because `SSLSocket` is not affected by it.

subject: ECDSA Private Key Exposed via rawRepresentation
truth: Stop calling rawRepresentation on P256.Signing.PrivateKey to extract the key material into memory; instead use the Secure Enclave-backed keys that never expose raw key bytes.

subject: iOS virtual device detection via sysctlbyname
truth: Stop relying on sysctlbyname('hw.machine') alone for jailbreak/virtual-device detection; it returns 'unknown' on sandboxed or restricted environments and is trivially hooked.
quote: The app queries hw.machine through sysctlbyname as a virtual-device indicator.

subject: Implicit intents resolved by Android matching
truth: Stop using implicit intents for internal communication; Android resolves them by matching installed apps, so any app with a compatible intent-filter can intercept the extras.
quote: Android resolves implicit intents by matching installed apps that declare compatible `<intent-filter>` elements, so any matching app can become the selected recipient and receive the intent extras.

subject: Implicit Intent in PendingIntent
truth: The base Intent wrapped in a PendingIntent must specify its target component explicitly (package, class, or both), so that only the intended app and component can receive it.
quote: The base `Intent` wrapped in a `PendingIntent` must specify its target component explicitly (package, class, or both), so that only the intended app and component can receive it.

subject: iOS App Permissions - Purpose Strings Mismatch
truth: Stop requesting permissions without matching purpose strings to actual feature usage; verify each Info.plist purpose string corresponds to a real programmatic implementation.
quote: - whether the _purpose strings_ in the `Info.plist` file match the programmatic implementations.

subject: encrypting sensitive data at the wrong layer
truth: Choose the encryption layer (application, database, filesystem, or hardware) based on your threat model; hardware-level encryption does not protect against remote compromise.
quote: hardware level encryption is effective at protecting against the physical theft of the server, but will provide no protection if an attacker is able to compromise the server remotely.

subject: GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN permissions
truth: Restrict default GITHUB_TOKEN permissions to 'Read repository contents and packages permissions' and explicitly grant additional permissions in the workflow file if required.
quote: - Restrict default `GITHUB_TOKEN` permissions to `Read repository contents and packages permissions` in the repository settings.
source: OWASP CS: GitHub Actions Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Cloud provider API quotas and self-DoS
truth: Stop ignoring cloud provider API rate limits on secret management services; do spread workloads across accounts/projects and use data key caching to avoid hitting API quotas.
quote: You could potentially (D)DoS yourself when you run into these limits.

subject: Session ID binding to authentication
truth: Stop treating session IDs as equivalent to authentication without proper binding; ensure the session ID is bound to the user's authentication credentials and access controls.
quote: the session ID (or token) is temporarily equivalent to the strongest authentication method used by the application
source: OWASP CS: Session Management Cheat Sheet

subject: transaction authorization methods
truth: Implement transaction authorization using a dedicated second factor such as TOTP, SMS OTP, digital signature, or challenge-response token, not by reusing the primary session.
quote: - A time-based one-time password (OTP) token, such as an [OATH TOTP (Time-based One-Time Password)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Time-based_One-time_Password_Algorithm)
source: OWASP CS: Transaction Authorization Cheat Sheet

subject: TLS server certificate Authority Information Access
truth: Require both id-ad-caIssuers (HTTP URL for issuing CA certificates) and id-ad-ocsp (HTTP URL for the issuing CA OCSP responder) in the Authority Information Access extension.
quote: id-ad-caIssuers Required. Access method entry contains HTTP URL for certificates issued to issuing CA

subject: Perfect forward secrecy in TLS cipher suites
truth: Prefer cipher suites using ephemeral DH or ephemeral ECDH (DHE or ECDHE) to provide perfect forward secrecy; each ephemeral key-pair shall have at least 112 bits of security.
quote: Cipher suites using ephemeral DH and ephemeral ECDH (i.e., those with DHE or ECDHE in the second mnemonic) provide perfect forward secrecy

subject: preprocessor defines for buffer sizes
truth: Stop hardcoding buffer sizes in multiple places; use a preprocessor define so the size is specified in one location and reused for both the declaration and the bounds check.
quote: use a preprocessor define so that the size is only specified in one place

subject: null origin in CORS
truth: Never use null as the value of Access-Control-Allow-Origin when Access-Control-Allow-Credentials is true, because an attacker can set a null origin using a sandboxed iframe.
quote: it is never safe to use null as the value of the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header when the Access-Control-Allow-Credentials header value is true

subject: External API calls with untrusted data
truth: Stop passing untrusted data directly to external APIs without treating each call as a potential sink, taint step, or uninteresting result that must be explicitly classified.
quote: Using unsanitized untrusted data in an external API can cause a variety of security issues.

subject: unsafe package usage
truth: Stop using unsafe.Pointer, uintptr, unsafe.String, unsafe.Slice, and unsafe.StringData/unsafe.SliceData without explicit audit and safety review; use safe Go idioms instead.
quote: Audit the use of unsafe block

subject: Android implicit intent result file path construction
truth: Stop constructing File paths from untrusted intent result data without canonical path validation; normalize and verify the resolved path stays within the intended directory.
quote: Writing to internal storage using the unsanitized filename

subject: Automatic implicit intent resolution without user prompt
truth: Stop automatically resolving implicit intents by iterating queryIntentActivities and calling setClassName; always show a chooser dialog to let the user select the recipient.
quote: developers may choose to suppress this dialog and automatically determine a recipient for the intent. This would allow the attack to occur without any additional user interaction.

subject: Android Backup (adb backup)
truth: Stop assuming adb backup is blocked; even with allowBackup=false, verify by running 'adb backup -apk -nosystem <package-name>' and inspecting the archive for sensitive data.
quote: After executing all available app functions, attempt to back up via `adb`.

subject: LLM prompt structure — instructions vs data separation
truth: Do NOT concatenate user input directly into a prompt; use a structured format that clearly separates SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS from USER_DATA_TO_PROCESS with explicit demarcation.
quote: Use structured formats that clearly separate instructions from user data.
source: OWASP CS: LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Mandatory TLS extensions for clients
truth: Clients shall be configured to use Renegotiation Indication, Server Name Indication, Extended Master Secret, Signature Algorithms, and Certificate Status Request extensions.
quote: The client shall be configured to use the following extensions

subject: CSP must offer non-RESTRICTED authenticator alternative
truth: When accepting a RESTRICTED authenticator, offer subscribers at least one alternate authenticator that is not RESTRICTED and can be used to authenticate at the required AAL.
quote: Offer subscribers at least one alternate authenticator that is not RESTRICTED

subject: password special character restrictions
truth: Stop rejecting passwords with spaces and special characters; properly hashed passwords are not sent intact to databases, so SQL-injection-based restrictions are unnecessary.
quote: Many services reject passwords with spaces and various special characters

subject: gix_path::env::installation_config and installation_config_prefix
truth: Stop treating the local repository's git config as installation-scoped when higher scopes are absent; instead, only trust config from system, global, or installation scopes.
quote: mistakenly treats the local repository's configuration as system-wide if no higher scoped configuration is found

subject: libcrux_chacha20poly1305 encrypt buffer overflow panic
truth: Stop passing a ciphertext buffer longer than ptxt.len() + TAG_LEN to encrypt; the fix silently truncates output to the first ptxt.len() + TAG_LEN bytes instead of panicking.
quote: panic in this case, but instead write out the ciphertext and tag into

subject: LDAPS certificate mismatch handled by disabling validation instead of fixing certificate
truth: Replace non-conforming LDAP server certificates to include a DNS name in the subjectAltName field matching the FQDN of the service, instead of disabling the endpoint check.
quote: Replace any non-conforming LDAP server certificates to include a DNS name in the subjectAltName field of the certificate that matches the FQDN of the service

subject: RMIConnectorServer instantiation without credentials filter pattern
truth: Set the key RMIConnectorServer.CREDENTIALS_FILTER_PATTERN ("jmx.remote.rmi.server.credentials.filter.pattern") to "java.lang.String;!*" when creating an RMIConnectorServer.
quote: The filter can be configured by setting the key jmx.remote.rmi.server.credentials.filter.pattern (given by the constant RMIConnectorServer.CREDENTIALS_FILTER_PATTERN).

subject: using child_process.execFile with forwarded user input
truth: Do not assume child_process.execFile is safe when the wrapper script internally forwards arguments to child_process.exec; use execFile throughout or parse with shell-quote.
quote: If another program uses child_process.execFile to invoke the above wrapper script with input from a remote user, then there may be a command-line injection vulnerability. This may be surprising, since a command-line invocation with child_process.execFile is generally considered safe.

associative: remote user input to child process

subject: Missing output filtering for prompt injection
truth: Always apply output filtering using a separate LLM call to detect if the model's response discloses system prompts or internal instructions before returning it to the user.
quote: Output filtering uses a separate LLM call to inspect the model's response and blocks it if it has leaked the system prompt or other internal instructions, complementing the input guardrail.

subject: os.chmod with numeric octal masks that are overly permissive
truth: Stop using os.chmod with numeric masks like 0o227, 0o7, 511 (0o777), or 0x1ff (0o777); use restrictive masks like 0o400, 0o600, or 0o644 for owner-only or owner+group read.
quote: This plugin test looks for the use of ``chmod`` and will alert when it is used

subject: Relative Path Traversal via string concatenation
truth: Canonicalize and validate the constructed path against an allowlist of allowed directories before opening the file; do not concatenate user input directly into a file path.
quote: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize sequences such as ".." that can resolve to a location that is

associative: get user parameter and read file

subject: Signal Handler Race Condition
truth: Design signal handlers to only set flags, rather than perform complex functionality like syslog, free, or exit; check and act upon those flags within the main program loop.
quote: Design signal handlers to only set flags, rather than perform complex functionality
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: WebView onReceivedSslError calling handler.proceed() unconditionally
truth: Stop calling handler.proceed() unconditionally in onReceivedSslError; validate the SslError and only call proceed() after user confirmation or when the error is acceptable.
quote: calls `proceed()` in its `onReceivedSslError(

subject: Unprotected service writes to SharedPreferences from intent extra
truth: Stop accepting and writing sensitive data from intent extras in an exported service without verifying the caller; enforce caller permission or set android:exported="false".
quote: // External callers, like other apps including adb, can start it directly and reset the password.

subject: iOS Keychain biometric enrollment change detection
truth: Stop using kSecAccessControlBiometryAny which silently allows new biometrics to unlock Keychain items; use kSecAccessControlBiometryCurrentSet to detect enrollment changes.
quote: does not ensure that the associated keychain item becomes inaccessible when changes are made to the biometric database

subject: Using file:// URIs from external components without validation
truth: Prefer content:// URIs over file:// URIs when processing externally supplied data; a malicious app can return a file:// URI pointing at any path the calling app can access.
quote: This means a malicious responding app can return a `file://` URI pointing at any path the calling app can access, which, depending on the permissions the app holds, may go well beyond its own private

subject: Digital Asset Links file requirements
truth: Host the Digital Asset Links file at https://<domain>/.well-known/assetlinks.json served over HTTPS without redirects, and present on every host declared in intent filters.
quote: - Served over HTTPS, without redirects (a redirect from `http` to `https` or `example.com` to `www.example.com` causes verification to fail).

subject: UIActivityViewController init activityItems inspection
truth: Stop sharing sensitive data without inspecting what items are passed to UIActivityViewController; verify the activityItems array does not contain unintended sensitive data.
quote: the data (items) being shared

subject: Software cryptographic key storage
truth: The key SHALL be stored in suitably secure storage (e.g., keychain storage, TPM, or TEE) and SHALL be strongly protected against unauthorized disclosure by access controls.
quote: stored in suitably secure storage available to the authenticator application

subject: Java temp directory creation with mkdir() in shared temp dir
truth: Stop using File.mkdir() or Files.createDirectory() on paths under java.io.tmpdir without explicit owner-only POSIX permissions; the default permissions are world-readable.
quote: new File(System.getProperty("java.io.tmpdir"), "/child").mkdir(); // BAD: Directory has permissions `-rw-r--r--`

subject: OGNL sandbox via system property
truth: Enable OGNL security manager by setting system property 'ognl.security.manager' or adding -Dognl.security.manager to JVM arguments before evaluating untrusted expressions.
quote: You can add a sandbox by setting a system property

subject: Log4j JNDI injection mitigation via system property
truth: Set the system property log4j2.formatMsgNoLookups to true on all client- and server-side components to disable JNDI lookups in log messages when upgrading is not possible.
quote: In previous releases (>=2.10) this behavior can be mitigated by setting system property log4j2.formatMsgNoLookups to true or by removing the JndiLookup class from the classpath (example:

subject: Unsafe Reflection (Class.forName without allowlist)
truth: Stop using Class.forName with user-controlled input without an allowlist; validate the class name against a whitelist of permitted classes before reflective instantiation.
quote: Allowing users to freely choose the name of a class to instantiate could provide means to attack a vulnerable application.

subject: User-controlled HTTP header names overwriting security headers
truth: Stop using user input directly as HTTP header names; sanitize to prevent overwriting security-critical headers like Access-Control-Allow-Origin or Content-Security-Policy.
quote: if the name of an HTTP header is user-controlled, an attacker may exploit this to overwrite security-critical headers such as Access-Control-Allow-Origin or Content-Security-Policy

subject: FileProvider URI grant with overly broad file_paths.xml
truth: Stop declaring path="." in file_paths.xml; restrict the files-path element to only the specific subdirectory (e.g. path="reports/") that should be shared via FileProvider.
quote: Because `file_paths.xml` declares `path="."`, the `FileProvider` will accept any file under `filesDir` — not just the intended `reports/` subdirectory.

subject: iOS custom URL scheme deserialization
truth: Stop deserializing payloads from custom URL schemes with NSCoding; use NSSecureCoding with class-restricted unarchiving to reject substituted classes before instantiation.
quote: [MASTG-TEST-0386] The app accepts a session from a deep link and deserializes

subject: Location Written to Plaintext File in Documents Directory
truth: Stop writing captured GPS coordinates to a plaintext file in the app's Documents directory; location data is sensitive and must be stored securely or not persisted at all.
quote: When the countdown ends, the coordinates are written to `location_capture.txt` in the app's Documents directory.

subject: iOS Metal GPU availability check for device detection
truth: Stop using MTLCreateSystemDefaultDevice() nil check as a virtual-device indicator; simulators and some virtual environments now expose Metal, making this check unreliable.
quote: The app checks whether a Metal GPU is available.

subject: iOS file backup exclusion with sensitive data
truth: Stop writing sensitive data to a file that is only marked isExcludedFromBackup; use the Data Protection API (NSFileProtectionComplete) to encrypt the file at rest instead.
quote: The test fails because `secret.txt` might be restored from the backup and it contains sensitive data.

subject: Xamarin ServicePointManager Certificate Pinning
truth: Stop using ServicePointManager.ServerCertificateValidationCallback with hardcoded public keys; pinning the intermediate CA alone can silently break when the chain changes.
quote: Applications developed in Xamarin will typically use `ServicePointManager` to implement pinning.

subject: Exported Service allows remote password change without authentication
truth: Stop exporting services that mutate sensitive state (password/PIN) without a permission or caller verification; set android:exported="false" or enforce android:permission.
quote: Because this service is exported, you can use the module `app.service.send` to communicate with the service and change the password stored in the target application:

subject: Exported ContentProvider exposes all database records without permission
truth: Stop exporting ContentProviders that serve sensitive data without a permission; set android:exported="false" or enforce android:permission and use per-URI access controls.
quote: Use the following commands to call the content provider using the identified URIs

subject: Fetch Metadata CSRF protection without fallback
truth: Always implement a fallback to standard origin verification headers when using Fetch Metadata for CSRF protection, because legacy browsers do not send Sec-Fetch-* headers.
quote: Because some legacy browsers do not send `Sec-Fetch-*` headers, a fallback to [standard origin verification](#using-standard-headers-to-verify-origin) headers **is a mandatory requirement** for any Fe
source: OWASP CS: Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Browser caching of PII and credentials in input fields
truth: Stop letting browsers cache PII and credentials in input fields; add spellcheck='false' autocomplete='off' autocorrect='off' autocapitalize='off' to every sensitive input.
quote: tell the input fields not to assist in any way
source: OWASP CS: HTML5 Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: wildcard DNS records
truth: Avoid wildcard DNS records; if required, scope them narrowly (e.g., *.staging.example.com) and combine with a reverse proxy that maintains an allowlist of valid hostnames.
quote: Wildcard DNS records (`*.example.com`) are especially dangerous because they resolve for any subdomain, including those matching services that no longer exist.
source: OWASP CS: Subdomain Takeover Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Cookie Path attribute does not provide integrity protection
truth: Do not rely on the Path attribute for integrity; mutually distrusting services on different paths of the same host must not use cookies for security-sensitive information.
quote: the Path attribute does not provide any integrity protection because the user agent will accept an arbitrary Path attribute in a Set-Cookie header

subject: Non-compliant DH/MQV finite field schemes after 2020
truth: Stop using any finite field DH or MQV scheme that does not conform to SP 800-56A; all non-compliant finite field DH and MQV schemes are disallowed after December 31, 2020.
quote: After December 31, 2020, the use of these schemes is disallowed

subject: CMAC with TDEA (two-key and three-key)
truth: Stop using two-key TDEA for CMAC generation (disallowed) and three-key TDEA for CMAC generation (deprecated through 2023, disallowed after 2023); use AES for CMAC instead.
quote: Two-key TDEA Disallowed

subject: Unapproved cryptographic algorithms
truth: Only use cryptographic algorithms approved by inclusion in a FIPS or NIST Special Publication; unapproved algorithms must not be used for protecting sensitive information.
quote: an approved algorithm must be used; approval is indicated by inclusion in a FIPS or SP

subject: resource descriptor construction
truth: Stop building resource descriptors (connection strings, file paths, URIs) via string concatenation with user input; use the dedicated builder class for the resource type.
quote: If a resource descriptor is built using string concatenation, and the components of the concatenation include user input, a user may be able to hijack the resource which is loaded.

subject: buffer expansion without reallocation check
truth: When expanding a buffer character-by-character (e.g., encoding HTML entities), check that the destination buffer has enough remaining space before writing each character.
quote: dst_buf[dst_index++] = '&';
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: External Control of System or Configuration Setting
truth: Do not pass user-controlled input directly to system configuration APIs like sethostid(); validate and sanitize all external input before using it to set system settings.
quote: Allowing external control of system settings can disrupt service or cause an application to behave in unexpected, and potentially malicious ways.
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Password hashing without adaptive work factor
truth: Stop using raw MD5/SHA even with salt; use an adaptive hash function (bcrypt, scrypt, Argon2, PBKDF2) that supports configurable cost factors for iteration and/or memory.
quote: Use an adaptive hash function that can be configured to change the amount of computational effort needed to compute the hash
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: iOS file integrity (HMAC)
truth: Stop writing sensitive data to disk without computing and verifying an HMAC or signature; always append an HMAC-SHA256 tag and verify it before trusting the data on read.
quote: stores data it later trusts without verifying its integrity

subject: iOS secure text entry for non-password fields
truth: Stop assuming only password fields need keyboard cache protection; set isSecureTextEntry = true OR disable autocorrection and spell checking on name and email fields too.
quote: The password input is protected against keyboard caching because `isSecureTextEntry` is set to `true`.

subject: iOS CommonCryptor deprecated algorithm usage
truth: Stop using deprecated algorithms like DES, 3DES, RC2, RC4, or MD5 with CommonCryptor; use only recommended algorithms like AES-256 (kCCAlgorithmAES) with GCM or CBC mode.
quote: Note that functions which are noted in chapter "Cryptography for Mobile Apps" as deprecated, are still programmatically supported.

subject: dangerous contexts for variables
truth: Stop placing variables into dangerous contexts (inside script tags, HTML comments, CSS directly, attribute names, tag names) — output encoding will NOT prevent XSS there.
quote: Don't place variables into dangerous contexts as even with output encoding, it will not prevent an XSS attack fully.
source: OWASP CS: Cross Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: secrets detection lifecycle
truth: Treat secrets like authorization tokens: rotate them often, automate rotation, enforce least privilege, make them revocable with logging, and never log them in plaintext.
quote: Never be logged
source: OWASP CS: Secrets Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: XSS via javascript: in href/src attributes
truth: Stop allowing user-controlled data to populate href/src attributes with javascript: URIs; validate and sanitize URLs against a whitelist of allowed schemes (http, https).
quote: This attack will bypass most SRC domain filters
source: OWASP CS: XSS Filter Evasion Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SHA-1 in TLS 1.3 CertificateVerify
truth: Stop using SHA-1 in TLS 1.3 CertificateVerify signatures; use only algorithms from the signature_algorithms extension that are compatible with the end-entity certificate.
quote: The SHA-1 algorithm MUST NOT be used in any signatures of CertificateVerify messages.

subject: TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 cipher suite
truth: Stop using TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256; it uses RSA key exchange (static RSA) which provides no forward secrecy and is deprecated per SP 800-131A transition guidance.
quote: TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256

subject: transmute_vec_as_bytes with non-Pod types
truth: Stop using transmute_vec_as_bytes with generic T that may have padding bytes; require T: Pod (plain old data) instead of T: Copy to prevent uninitialized memory exposure.
quote: The library provides a public safe API `transmute_vec_as_bytes`, which incorrectly assumes that any generic type `T` could have stable layout, causing to uninitialized memory exposure if the users pas

subject: pprof::report::ReportBuilder::build and pprof::validate
truth: Stop using std::slice::from_raw_parts with a generic type without first constraining the type's alignment and validity; perform type checks before unsafe type conversion.
quote: The library breaks the safety assumptions when using unsafe API `std::slice::from_raw_parts`.

subject: Bounds check for pointer arithmetic
truth: When validating a virtual method result for pointer arithmetic, check that the value is both non-negative AND less than the buffer length, not just less than the length.
quote: it is not sufficient to check that it is smaller than the length. We must also ensure that it's greater than zero, to prevent writes to locations before the buffer

subject: Spring CSRF protection disabled
truth: Stop disabling CSRF protection via csrf.disable() in Spring Security configuration; remove the .csrf(csrf -> csrf.disable()) call to keep the default enabled protection.
quote: When you use Spring, Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) protection is enabled by default.

subject: Log4j version detection gap in security queries
truth: Do not assume a security query can detect which version of Log4j is in use; manually verify the Log4j version in pom.xml or build files and ensure it is 2.15.0 or later.
quote: this query will not try to determine which version of Log4j is used

subject: ApolloServer CORS with user-controlled origin
truth: Stop passing user-controlled data directly as the CORS origin without validation; validate user-controlled origin against a whitelist of allowed origins before using it.
quote: If the origin value is user-controlled, ensure that the data is properly sanitized and validated against a whitelist of allowed origins.

subject: Flask string interpolation with user input
truth: Stop using string concatenation or f-strings with user input in Flask responses; always wrap user-controlled values with escape() or use Flask's auto-escaping templates.
quote: The first view is unsafe as first_name is not escaped, leaving the page vulnerable to cross-site scripting attacks.

subject: PAM expired credentials bypass
truth: Stop treating pam_authenticate success as sufficient for login; pam_authenticate only verifies credentials, not whether the account is expired or otherwise unauthorized.
quote: A pam_authenticate only verifies the credentials of a user. It does not check if a user has an appropriate authorization to actually login.

subject: Internal implicit intent for component communication
truth: Stop using implicit intents for internal component communication; use explicit intents with setComponent/setClass or setPackage to prevent interception by attacker apps.
quote: the victim app using an implicit intent for internal component communication

subject: iOS file storage without integrity verification
truth: Stop trusting data read from disk without verifying its integrity; an attacker on a jailbroken device can tamper with files stored in the Documents directory undetected.
quote: - An **insecure** flow writes a sensitive value to `user_profile.json` in the Documents directory and later reads it back, trusting it without computing or verifying any HMAC or signature.

subject: Cordova SSL Certificate Checker Plugin
truth: Stop relying on PhoneGap SSL Certificate Checker's successCallback alone; always verify the errorCallback handles CONNECTION_NOT_SECURE and CONNECTION_FAILED distinctly.
quote: PhoneGap SSL Certificate Checker

subject: UIWebView deprecated API still in use
truth: Stop using deprecated UIWebView which always enables file:// scheme and universal file access; migrate to WKWebView which disables file access from file URLs by default.
quote: This only applies to `WKWebView`s (`UIWebVIew`s always allow file access).

subject: Dynamic SQL in stored procedures without bind variables
truth: Stop constructing SQL strings by concatenation inside stored procedures; use bind variables with EXECUTE IMMEDIATE ... USING or sp_executesql with parameter definitions.
quote: Bind variables are used to tell the database that the inputs to this dynamic SQL are 'data' and not possibly code
source: OWASP CS: Query Parameterization Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SWF and CSS mixed content scripting
truth: Do not embed SWF movies or CSS over insecure connections because both can script the embedding page, and some browsers do not issue mixed content warnings for SWF files.
quote: both cascading style sheets and SWF movies can script the embedding page

subject: TLS 1.3 forbids renegotiation
truth: Do not implement TLS renegotiation when using TLS 1.3; terminate the connection with an unexpected_message alert if a ClientHello is received after handshake completion.
quote: TLS 1.3 forbids renegotiation

subject: theshit loads Python rules from user-writable paths without ownership checks when running as root
truth: Always validate that configuration and rule files are owned by the effective user (root) and not writable by non-root users before loading them with elevated privileges.
quote: continues to trust configuration files originating from the unprivileged user's environment

subject: Path traversal sanitization via string replacement
truth: Stop trying to sanitize '../' with string replacement; it is bypassable (e.g. '.../...//' becomes '../'). Use path.resolve + fs.realpathSync + startsWith check instead.
quote: it is not sufficient to only remove "../" sequences: for example, applying this filter to "

subject: Rails CSRF default behavior change in v5+
truth: Stop relying on the default protect_from_forgery behavior; in Rails 5+ the default is `with: :exception` but calling it bare downgrades to null-session which is weaker.
quote: Rails versions 5 and later automatically run protect_from_forgery with: :exception by default, but manually calling protect_from_forgery with no with argument will downgrade protection

subject: Go HTTP server without timeouts
truth: Stop using http.ListenAndServe, http.ListenAndServeTLS, http.Serve, and http.ServeTLS without configuring ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, and IdleTimeout on the http.Server.
quote: Use of net/http serve function that has no support for setting timeouts

subject: Android Keystore key alias reused for conflicting crypto purposes
truth: Stop reusing a single key alias for both encryption and signing; create separate aliases (e.g. 'EncryptionKey' and 'SigningKey') each with only their required purposes.
quote: This key is used for multiple, conflicting purposes: encryption/decryption and sign/verify

subject: WebView loadDataWithBaseURL with file:// scheme
truth: Stop using file:// as the base URL in loadDataWithBaseURL; it gives the page a non-opaque origin that can access content:// URIs when combined with permissive settings.
quote: Using a base URL with the file:// scheme gives the page a non‑opaque origin (using content:/// works the same way)

subject: Unprotected URL Delivery Path in Scene Lifecycle
truth: Validate sourceApplication in both scene(_:willConnectTo:options:) for cold launches and scene(_:openURLContexts:) for warm opens to avoid leaving one path unprotected.
quote: Validate `sourceApplication` in both handlers to avoid leaving one path unprotected.

subject: WebView on old Android WebKit
truth: Devices running Android older than 4.4 (API level 19) use a WebKit version with several security issues; confirm WebViews display only trusted content on these devices.
quote: Devices running platforms older than Android 4.4 (API level 19) use a version of WebKit that has several security issues

subject: WebView file scheme JavaScript access enabled on old Android
truth: Disable setAllowFileAccessFromFileURLs and setAllowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs to prevent JavaScript in file:// URLs from accessing other files or cross-origin content.
quote: The default value is `true` for Android 4.0.3 - 4.0.4 (API level 15) and below and `false` for Android 4.1 (API level 16) and above.

subject: application:openURL:options: file handling
truth: Stop trusting incoming file URLs without validation; verify the received data and sanitize file paths in application:openURL:options: before moving or processing files.
quote: - if the app properly _verifies the received data_ by looking into the implementation of `application:openURL:options:` (or its deprecated version `UIApplicationDelegate application:openURL:sourceAppl

subject: iOS App Extension unrestricted NSExtensionActivationRule
truth: Stop using a wildcard or overly permissive NSExtensionActivationRule that accepts any data type; restrict activation to only the required UTIs with explicit max counts.
quote: Only the data types present here and not having `0` as `MaxCount` will be supported.

subject: signed/unsigned comparison
truth: Stop comparing signed integers against unsigned integers without understanding C/C++ promotion rules; enable -Wsign-compare or -Wconversion to catch the -1 > 1 pitfall.
quote: As a concrete example, (and for those not familiar with C/C++ promotion rules), a warning will be issued if a signed integer is promoted to an unsigned integer and then compared because a side effect
source: OWASP CS: C-Based Toolchain Hardening Cheat Sheet

subject: CI/CD artifact integrity validation
truth: Do not deploy artifacts without verifying their integrity; implement cryptographic signing and integrity validation for all build artifacts moving through the pipeline.
quote: Improper Artifact Integrity Validation
source: OWASP CS: CI/CD Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Incomplete tenant data deletion on offboarding
truth: Stop leaving residual tenant data after deletion; implement verified data purging across all storage layers (database, cache, file storage, backups) during offboarding.
quote: Incomplete provisioning or data retention after deletion
source: OWASP CS: Multi-Tenant Application Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: secrets management developer onboarding
truth: Provide clear documentation, SDKs, CLI tools, and self-service workflows so developers adopt the secrets management solution instead of resorting to insecure practices.
quote: If the process is too complex, developers may resort to insecure practices
source: OWASP CS: Secrets Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: TLS cipher suites
truth: Disable null ciphers, anonymous ciphers, EXPORT ciphers, RSA transport, and non-forward-secrecy Diffie-Hellman ciphers; use AEAD ciphers (AES-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305).
quote: - RSA transport (`TLS_RSA_*`) and ephemeral/static Diffie-Hellman key agreement (`TLS_DH_*`, `TLS_ECDH_*`) which do not provide forward secrecy.
source: OWASP CS: Transport Layer Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: API authentication
truth: Stop relying on network perimeter security for APIs; require authentication and authorization for every API call with request schema validation and behavioral analysis.
quote: requires authentication and authorization for every API call, validates request schemas, and uses behavioral analysis to detect abuse patterns
source: OWASP CS: Zero Trust Architecture Cheat Sheet
