subject: TLS exporter context handling
truth: In TLS 1.3, providing no context and providing an empty context compute the same exporter value — unlike in previous TLS versions where they produced different outputs.
quote: providing no context computes the same value as providing an empty context

subject: Pre-shared key establishment
truth: Establish pre-shared keys only via a secure method (e.g., secure manual distribution or automated key-establishment scheme), never by transmitting the key in the clear.
quote: A secret key that has previously been established between the parties who are authorized to use it by means of some secure method

subject: gix_worktree_state::checkout sets executable files world-writable when destination_is_initially_empty is false
truth: When setting executable permissions on checked-out files, use OpenOptionsEx::mode (which respects umask) instead of PermissionsExt::set_mode with 0777 (which does not).
quote: A mode of 0777 is not safe here, because the umask is not applied.

subject: Runtime.exec command injection via environment variable
truth: Stop reading a script name from an environment variable and passing it to Runtime.exec; instead use hard-coded string literals to specify the command or script to run.
quote: If possible, use hard-coded string literals to specify the command or script to run

subject: WebViewAssetLoader as safe alternative
truth: Use WebViewAssetLoader instead of manually constructing WebResourceResponse from user input; it safely processes data from resources, assets or a predefined directory.
quote: the Android class WebViewAssetLoader can be used, which safely processes data from resources, assets or a predefined directory

subject: HTTP-to-file access (backdoor download)
truth: Stop writing user-controlled or remote data directly to the local filesystem without validation; treat all remote content as untrusted even after it is stored locally.
quote: Storing user-controlled data on the local file system without further validation allows arbitrary file upload, and may be an indication of malicious backdoor code

associative: fetch remote content save to disk

subject: Android ContentProvider SQL injection via URI path segment
truth: Stop appending untrusted URI path segments directly into SQL WHERE clauses via SQLiteQueryBuilder.appendWhere(); use parameterized queries with ? placeholders instead.
quote: * Arbitrary caller controlled URI path input is appended directly

subject: Android Network Security Configuration allows user-added CA certificates
truth: Stop including <certificates src="user" /> in your Network Security Configuration; use only system-trusted certificates to prevent MITM attacks via user-installed CAs.
quote: allows user-added CA certificates

subject: KeyguardManager.isDeviceSecure check without authentication
truth: Stop treating isDeviceSecure/canAuthenticate as proof of user presence; always pair device-secure checks with a cryptographic BiometricPrompt authentication challenge.
quote: checks if the device has a secure lock screen

subject: Root Manager Package Names in AndroidManifest Queries
truth: Stop declaring root manager package names in <queries> blocks in the manifest; they are visible in the decompiled AndroidManifest.xml and reveal the detection targets.
quote: the package visibility queries for the monitored root manager packages

subject: Missing Pinning for App's Own Backend in URLSession
truth: When using URLSession for first-party backend domains, ensure each domain has a corresponding NSPinnedDomains entry; omitting it leaves connections vulnerable to MITM.
quote: connections to the app-controlled backend rely only on the system CA trust store and are not protected by ATS certificate pinning

subject: iOS API key stored in plaintext variable then encrypted with CCCrypt
truth: Stop hardcoding API keys in source code and encrypting them with CCCrypt without runtime hook detection; use the iOS Keychain (SecItemAdd/SecItemCopyMatching) instead.
quote: The app does not implement any runtime hook detection mechanisms.

subject: ContentProvider returning arbitrary file paths via implicit intent
truth: Stop trusting content:// or file:// URIs returned from implicit intents; validate the URI scheme, authority, and path before copying any data from the returned intent.
quote: An improperly handled return value of an implicit intent can lead to arbitrary code execution if the victim app allows `content://` and `file://` URLs.

subject: LLM prompt injection input filtering
truth: Stop using simple anagram-based similarity checks; use established string metric libraries (Levenshtein, Damerau-Levenshtein, Jaro-Winkler) for typoglycemia detection.
quote: The `_is_similar_word` helper above is intentionally minimal and only catches anagram-style scrambles.
source: OWASP CS: LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Auto-accept mode on AI coding agents
truth: Do not enable auto-accept mode on untrusted or unfamiliar codebases; understand the risk of flags like --dangerously-skip-permissions that bypass confirmation prompts.
quote: Understand and evaluate the risk of flags like `--dangerously-skip-permissions` or auto-accept modes. These bypass confirmation prompts and give the agent unrestricted execution.
source: OWASP CS: Secure Coding with AI Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: WebSocket authentication
truth: Do not rely on WebSocket's lack of built-in authentication; implement explicit authentication (e.g., tokens in the initial handshake) before allowing message exchange.
quote: No built-in authentication makes access control easy to forget
source: OWASP CS: WebSocket Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: ECDSA signature validation must check exact signature length
truth: When validating ECDSA signatures in JWS, first check that the JWS Signature value is exactly the expected length (64, 96, or 132 octets) before splitting into R and S.
quote: MUST be a 64-octet sequence. If it is not a 64-octet sequence, the validation has failed.

subject: JWT 'none' algorithm
truth: Do not accept or generate JWTs with algorithm 'none' unless the JWT is cryptographically protected by a separate transport layer and the caller explicitly requests it.
quote: using "none" unless explicitly requested to do so by the caller.

subject: Algorithm selection when none is specified
truth: When developing specifications, cryptographic algorithms specified in FIPS and SPs must be used when available; do not default to unapproved or proprietary algorithms.
quote: cryptographic algorithms specified in FIPS and SPs must be used, when available

subject: Digital signature assurance requirements
truth: Do not accept digital signatures without verifying domain parameter validity, public key validity, proof of private key possession, and identity of the key-pair owner.
quote: assurance of domain parameter validity, assurance of public key validity, assurance that the key-pair owner actually possesses the private key, and assurance of the identity of the key-pair owner

subject: Digital signature minimum security strength for attestation
truth: Stop using digital signatures weaker than 112 bits of security strength for attestation; use algorithms meeting the minimum security strength specified in SP 800-131A.
quote: signed using a digital signature that provides at least the minimum security strength specified in the latest revision of SP 800-131A (112 bits as of the date of this publication)

subject: WebView addJavascriptInterface
truth: Do not expose SQLiteOpenHelper or database-access objects to JavaScript via addJavascriptInterface; any SQL injection in exposed methods becomes remotely exploitable.
quote: Objects exposed to JavaScript are available in all frames of the WebView.

subject: Mako templates lack auto-escaping
truth: Stop using Mako templates without explicit escaping; use the 'h' (HTML escape), 'n' (no-op), or 'x' (XML) filter flags on every variable expression like ${ data |h }.
quote: Mako has no environment wide variable escaping mechanism

subject: Implicit intent for file request resolved to attacker provider
truth: Do not use implicit intents to request files from external providers; use explicit intents targeting a known component or validate the responding provider's identity.
quote: When Android resolves it as a handler, its `AttackerActivity` returns a `content://` URI pointing to its own `AttackerContentProvider`, which supplies a path-traversal string (`../private/secret.txt`)

subject: iOS Logger (Unified Logging) with .debug level exposes internal validation logic
truth: Stop using Logger.debug() to log internal validation logic, password hashing algorithms, or database operations; these strings persist in the binary as oslog strings.
quote: os_log with .debug level exposes validation logic

subject: LLM prompt concatenation
truth: Stop directly concatenating user input with system prompts; use input separation, delimiters, or structured API calls that treat user input as data, not instructions.
quote: Vulnerable: Direct concatenation without separation
source: OWASP CS: LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Unicode injection via bidi and zero-width characters in code
truth: Detect and flag bidi override characters (U+202A–U+202E, U+2066–U+2069) and zero-width characters (U+200B, U+200C, U+200D, U+FEFF) in code, commits, and agent output.
quote: - Detect and flag bidi override characters (U+202A through U+202E, U+2066 through U+2069) and zero-width characters (U+200B, U+200C, U+200D, U+FEFF) in code, commits, and agent output.
source: OWASP CS: Secure Coding with AI Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: DNS decommissioning order
truth: Do NOT delete the cloud resource before removing the DNS record; redirect or serve a maintenance page first, then update DNS, wait for propagation, then decommission.
quote: The common mistake is doing these steps in reverse: deleting the cloud resource first, which creates an immediate window for takeover
source: OWASP CS: Subdomain Takeover Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Server must validate ticket age for 0-RTT PSK
truth: When accepting 0-RTT via a PSK from a NewSessionTicket, the server MUST validate the ticket age against a small tolerance and reject 0-RTT if the age is out of range.
quote: MUST validate that the ticket age for the selected PSK identity

subject: Web.config authorization configuration
truth: Stop leaving sensitive paths unprotected in Web.config; use the <authorization> tag inside a <location> element to deny access to anonymous users for specific paths.
quote: deny users="?"

subject: Missing regex timeout for user-supplied patterns
truth: Stop using user-supplied regex patterns without a timeout; pass a TimeSpan timeout to the Regex constructor or set REGEX_DEFAULT_MATCH_TIMEOUT via AppDomain.SetData.
quote: a timeout should be used to avoid Denial of Service attacks

subject: Removing '../' sequences from user input
truth: Removing '../' sequences is not sufficient for path traversal prevention, since input can contain patterns like '.../...//' that still result in '../' after removal.
quote: removing "../" sequences is not sufficient, since the input could still contain a path separator followed by ".."

subject: Android WebView loading externally controlled URLs with JavaScript enabled
truth: Stop loading externally controlled URLs into WebViews with JavaScript enabled; only allow trusted web content to be displayed in WebViews when JavaScript is enabled.
quote: Only allow trusted web content to be displayed in WebViews when JavaScript is enabled

subject: Spring deserialization exporters
truth: Apply global deserialization filters (JEP 290) if vulnerable exporters cannot be replaced, as the vulnerability is a design limitation that cannot be fixed outright.
quote: If the vulnerable exporters can not be replaced, consider using global deserialization filters introduced in JEP 290.

subject: Spring Framework unsafe service exporters deserialization filter
truth: If you cannot replace vulnerable Spring exporters, apply global deserialization filters (JEP 290) to mitigate remote code execution via ObjectInputStream or Hessian.
quote: consider using global deserialization filters introduced in JEP 290

subject: heap buffer overflow via insufficient length check during encoding
truth: Stop using strlen() for bounds checking without accounting for output expansion; calculate the maximum possible output size before allocating and check against that.
quote: if ( MAX_SIZE <= strlen(user_supplied_string) )
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Loading remote classes via URLClassLoader without integrity verification
truth: Always verify the integrity (e.g., via checksum or signature) of remotely loaded classes before using URLClassLoader; never load classes from unverified remote URLs.
quote: downloads source code or an executable from a remote location and executes the code without sufficiently verifying the origin and integrity of the code
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: PHP direct echo of GET parameter
truth: Always escape or encode user input with htmlspecialchars() before echoing into HTML context; never concatenate raw $_GET/$_POST/$_COOKIE values directly into output.
quote: improper neutralization of dangerous input
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Static Code Injection via fwrite into include file
truth: Never write unsanitized user input directly into a file that will be included/executed as code; escape or validate all output before writing to executable resources.
quote: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes code syntax before inserting the input into an executable resource, such as a library, conf
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: write user input to log file

subject: SQL injection in ContentProvider query method
truth: Stop concatenating user input into SQL queries in ContentProvider methods; use parameterized query methods (query, update, delete) with properly sanitized arguments.
quote: > To avoid SQL injection attacks within the app, use parameterized query methods, such as `query`, `update`, and `delete`.

subject: Client-Side CSRF via URL hash fragment
truth: Stop using attacker-controllable URL fragments to construct async request endpoints and methods; validate inputs against a predefined allowlist of safe request data.
quote: the JavaScript program uses URL fragments to obtain the server-side endpoint for the asynchronous HTTP request
source: OWASP CS: Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: HTML encoding lost on DOM value retrieval
truth: Do not rely on server-side HTML encoding alone when values are retrieved via DOM value attributes; the encoding is reversed on retrieval and code becomes executable.
quote: encodings are lost when you retrieve them using the value attribute of a DOM element
source: OWASP CS: DOM based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: trusting backend feeds without validation
truth: Validate data from all potentially untrusted sources including backend feeds from suppliers, partners, vendors, and regulators, not just Internet-facing web clients.
quote: Data from all potentially untrusted sources should be subject to input validation, including not only Internet-facing web clients but also backend feeds
source: OWASP CS: Input Validation Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MCP server tool descriptions
truth: Stop embedding natural-language instructions or commands in tool descriptions; the LLM treats all description text as behavioral directives, enabling tool poisoning.
quote: Malicious instructions hidden in tool descriptions
source: OWASP CS: MCP (Model Context Protocol) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Insecure Direct Object References (IDOR) across tenants
truth: Stop trusting user-supplied resource IDs to enforce tenant boundaries; always verify that the authenticated tenant owns the requested resource before returning data.
quote: Accessing resources by manipulating tenant/resource IDs
source: OWASP CS: Multi-Tenant Application Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: weak key derivation in encryption library README
truth: Stop copying README examples that derive AES keys from passphrases using MD5 with a single iteration; use the library's PBKDF2 module with stronger defaults instead.
quote: A widely-used encryption library's README examples derive AES keys from passphrases using MD5 with a single iteration
source: OWASP CS: NPM Security best practices
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: reauthentication after risk events
truth: Require reauthentication (primary credentials or MFA) when high-risk events are detected such as password changes, new device logins, or account recovery completion.
quote: applications should require reauthentication when specific high-risk events are detected
source: OWASP CS: Session Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Cryptographic key naming for user recognition
truth: Give cryptographic keys appropriately descriptive names that are meaningful to users so they can recognize and recall which key to use for which authentication task.
quote: Give cryptographic keys appropriately descriptive names that are meaningful to users

subject: X25519 all-zero shared secret check in HPKE
truth: Stop computing X25519 shared secrets without checking that the result is non-zero; validate that the shared secret is not all-zero after Diffie-Hellman key exchange.
quote: does not include the check that the key exchange was contributory, i.e. does not ensure on its own that the resulting shared secret is non-zero

subject: libflate MultiDecoder::read drops uninitialized memory on panic
truth: Abort immediately instead of unwinding when a panic occurs in caller-supplied Read implementation within MultiDecoder::read, to prevent drop of uninitialized memory.
quote: This would cause `drop()` to be called on uninitialized memory of a generic type implementing `Read`.

subject: Leaking system configuration to remote users
truth: Stop revealing technologies, operating systems, or product versions in error messages sent to remote users; use a generic message that does not aid attack planning.
quote: Exposing system data or debugging information may help a malicious user learn about the system and form an attack plan.

subject: Environment variable injection from user input
truth: Stop assigning user-controllable data directly to process.env; validate and restrict environment variable writes to privileged users or non-critical variables only.
quote: Assigning Value to environment variables from user-controllable data is not safe

subject: Django CSRF middleware disabled
truth: Stop disabling CSRF protection by commenting out 'django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware' in MIDDLEWARE; always include it to prevent cross-site request forgery.
quote: CSRF protection is disabled by overriding the default middleware stack and not including the one protecting against CSRF

subject: Path traversal via string interpolation in file paths
truth: Stop interpolating user-controlled params[:path] into a file path string; validate the input to reject directory separators and '../' sequences, or use a whitelist.
quote: a malicious user could enter a file name containing special characters. For example, the string "../../etc/passwd"

subject: Go struct serialization leaks sensitive fields via default JSON marshaling
truth: Stop serializing structs with sensitive fields using json.Marshal/json.MarshalIndent/json.NewEncoder.Encode without tagging the field with `json:"-"` to exclude it.
quote: gosec rule G117

subject: Storing credentials in plaintext properties files
truth: Never store passwords, secrets, or credentials in cleartext in properties files, config files, or on disk; use a secure vault or encrypted credential store instead.
quote: stores sensitive information in cleartext in a file
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Storing database connection strings with plaintext passwords
truth: Never store database passwords in cleartext in connection strings in config files; use integrated security, encrypted config sections, or a secrets manager instead.
quote: stores sensitive information in cleartext in a file
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: store db password in config file
put uid pwd in connection string

subject: OS Command Injection via string concatenation in PHP
truth: Never pass user input directly to system() or exec(); use escapeshellarg() or escapeshellcmd() to neutralize shell metacharacters, or avoid shell commands entirely.
quote: does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended OS command
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: run a shell command with user input

subject: HostnameVerifier returning true for all hostnames (Java)
truth: Stop using HostnameVerifier whose verify() unconditionally returns true; implement proper hostname verification against the certificate's subject alternative names.
quote: Use of the HostnameVerifier that Allows Any Hostname

subject: Android App Link domain verification (android:autoVerify)
truth: Add android:autoVerify="true" to the intent-filter's data element for http/https schemes so Android verifies domain ownership before routing deep links to your app.
quote: The weakness is that the App Link is declared **without** `android:autoVerify="true"`, so Android never verifies that the app owns the `deeplink.example.com` domain.

subject: Inline syscall anti-debugging via raw syscalls
truth: Stop using inline syscalls (raw_syscall3/raw_syscall1) to read /proc/self/status for anti-debugging; seccomp filters or kernel-level bypasses defeat this trivially.
quote: Inline-syscall native TracerPid check

subject: Unobfuscated Java/Kotlin layer exposing native security logic correlation
truth: Obfuscate both the Java/Kotlin layer and the native library together so decompiled code does not reveal the security logic or its correlation with native functions.
quote: the Java/Kotlin layer isn't obfuscated, and the code helps correlate the native code with the security-relevant logic

subject: WKWebView file access via KVC
truth: Stop enabling allowFileAccessFromFileURLs and allowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs via KVC on WKWebView; they are not part of the public API and should remain disabled.
quote: should remain disabled unless there is a specific, well justified need

subject: Exported Activity bypasses login authentication
truth: Stop exporting activities that contain sensitive data or functionality; set android:exported="false" on all non-launcher activities that handle protected resources.
quote: Since the activity `.PWList` is called directly in this example, you can use it to bypass the login form protecting the password manager, and access the data contained within the password manager.

subject: unauthenticated access to public resources
truth: Do not require authentication for public resources; authorization is separate from authentication and unauthenticated users may be authorized for certain resources.
quote: authentication is not always required for accessing resources; an unauthenticated user may be authorized to access certain public resources, such as an image or login page
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Using user-supplied filenames instead of generating random filenames
truth: Generate a random string (UUID/GUID) as the filename instead of using the user-supplied filename to prevent path traversal, special file overwrite, and XSS attacks.
quote: In order to avoid the above mentioned threat, creating a **random string** as a filename, such as generating a UUID/GUID, is essential.
source: OWASP CS: File Upload Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: AI-suggested dependency versions with CVEs
truth: Stop accepting AI-suggested dependency versions without checking for known CVEs; run dependency auditing tools on every AI-generated dependency list before merging.
quote: Accept AI-suggested dependency versions without checking for known CVEs.
source: OWASP CS: Secure Coding with AI Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: TLS 1.3 multiple ticket independence
truth: Use distinct keys for each ticket established on the same connection; compromise of one ticket's PSK must not compromise connections established with other tickets.
quote: if multiple tickets are established on the same connection, they are associated with different keys, so compromise of the PSK associated with one ticket does not lead to the compromise of connections established with PSKs associated with other tickets

subject: 415 response must include Accept-Encoding for coding failures
truth: When failing a request due to unsupported content coding, respond with 415 and include Accept-Encoding; for non-coding 415 errors, MUST NOT include Accept-Encoding.
quote: Servers that fail a request due to an unsupported content coding ought to respond with a 415 (Unsupported Media Type) status and include an Accept-Encoding header field

subject: NIST SP 800-175Br1 references approved crypto standards
truth: Use only NIST-approved cryptographic algorithms (AES, SHA-2/3, ECDSA, RSA with OAEP) and avoid deprecated algorithms not listed in FIPS and SP 800-series standards.
quote: The cryptographic algorithms and schemes in FIPS and SPs are usually included in ISO/IEC standards

subject: URL construction from user input
truth: When you construct a URL using java.net.URL from a remote source, don't call openStream on it. Instead, use an HTTP Client to fetch the URL and access its content.
quote: When you construct a URL using java.net.URL from a remote source, don't call openStream on it

subject: Custom HTML entity encoding/decoding
truth: Use a well-tested sanitization library instead of writing custom HTML encoding/decoding functions, which are likely to mishandle corner cases like double-escaping.
quote: Use a (well-tested) sanitization library if at all possible

subject: Mako vs Jinja2 auto-escaping
truth: Stop assuming Mako auto-escapes like Jinja2; Mako has no environment-wide auto-escaping, so every variable must be explicitly filtered with 'h', 'n', or 'x' flags.
quote: Unlike Jinja2 (an alternative templating system), Mako has no environment wide variable escaping mechanism

subject: Buffer underwrite via pointer arithmetic and strcpy
truth: Validate that the calculated index (result of pointer subtraction) is within the bounds of the destination buffer before using it in strcpy or any write operation.
quote: All calculated values that are used as index or for pointer arithmetic should be validated to ensure that they are within an expected range.
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: strncpy() does not null-terminate when source >= n
truth: Do not use strncpy() for safe string copying; use snprintf() or strlcpy() instead, or manually ensure null termination after strncpy() by setting dest[n-1] = '\0'.
quote: strncpy() function call incorrectly, which prevents a null terminator from being added at all
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Open Redirect in PHP
truth: Never pass user-controlled input directly to the Location header; validate the redirect URL against a whitelist of allowed destinations or use an indirect mapping.
quote: Use an "accept known good" input validation strategy, i.e., use a list of acceptable inputs that strictly conform to specifications.
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: LDAP search filter built via string concatenation
truth: Stop building LDAP search filters by concatenating user input; use a proper API that supports safe parameter substitution or escape special LDAP filter characters.
quote: does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: External Storage File Write via FileOutputStream
truth: Stop writing sensitive data to external storage via FileOutputStream; use internal storage (Context.getFilesDir()) or EncryptedFile from AndroidX Security instead.
quote: files are not encrypted and contain sensitive data

subject: AndroidManifest.xml allowBackup attribute
truth: Stop setting android:allowBackup="true" without explicitly excluding sensitive files; set android:allowBackup="false" or use exclude rules for all sensitive paths.
quote: - The `android:allowBackup="true"` attribute in the AndroidManifest.xml file.

subject: Android App Links autoVerify
truth: Set android:autoVerify="true" on every <intent-filter> that declares an http/https deep link so Android verifies domain ownership before routing links to your app.
quote: Without verification, any other app can register the same intent filter and intercept the links

subject: Android Backup (exclude sensitive files)
truth: Stop relying on allowBackup=false alone; if backup is required, exclude sensitive files (SharedPreferences, databases) from Auto Backup using the backup rules XML.
quote: If you don't want to share files with Google Cloud, you can exclude them from Auto Backup. Sensitive information stored at rest on the device should be encrypted before being sent to the cloud.

subject: Universal Links path exclusion bypass
truth: Do not rely on AASA path exclusion (NOT prefix) as a security boundary; iOS does not open unverified links by default and the exclusion mechanism is only a filter.
quote: This path exclusion mechanism is not to be seen as a security feature but rather as a filter

subject: iOS custom keyboard app extension restriction
truth: Stop allowing custom keyboard app extensions in sensitive apps (e.g. banking) without implementing application:shouldAllowExtensionPointIdentifier: to reject them.
quote: It is possible to reject a specific type of app extension by using the following method:

subject: UIWebView JSContext Access via valueForKeyPath
truth: Stop using undocumented key paths like 'documentView.webView.mainFrame.javaScriptContext' to access JSContext; this is fragile and bypasses Apple's security model.
quote: [webView valueForKeyPath:@"documentView.webView.mainFrame.javaScriptContext"]

subject: telematics API security
truth: Stop exposing telematics APIs without strong authentication and authorization; implement OAuth 2.0, API keys with least privilege, and encrypt all data in transit.
quote: Telematics units that connect vehicles to cloud services may have insufficient security controls
source: OWASP CS: Top 10 Automotive Security Vulnerabilities
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Human-in-the-loop for high-risk LLM operations
truth: Implement human oversight (HITL) for inputs containing high-risk keywords like password, api_key, admin, system, bypass, override combined with injection patterns.
quote: Implement human oversight for high-risk operations.
source: OWASP CS: LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Laravel missing security headers
truth: Add X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, and Content-Security-Policy headers to your Laravel middleware or web server configuration.
quote: You should consider adding the following security headers to your web server or Laravel application middleware
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: custom authorization solution in microservices
truth: Base the authorization solution on widely-used solutions instead of building a custom one to avoid maintenance burden, missing SDKs, and lack of community support.
quote: The authorization solution should be based on widely-used solutions because implementing a custom solution has the following cons
source: OWASP CS: Microservices Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SAML Destination attribute validation
truth: Validate the Destination attribute on samlp:Response exactly matches the SP's expected ACS URL and reject responses missing Destination or where it does not match.
quote: Validate the `Destination` attribute on `<samlp:Response>` exactly matches the SP's expected Assertion Consumer Service (ACS) URL
source: OWASP CS: SAML Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: callback trust model
truth: Only server-to-server callbacks should be trusted for payment verification and order fulfillment; user redirection parameters are untrusted and can be manipulated.
quote: Only server-to-server callbacks should be trusted for payment verification and order fulfillment
source: OWASP CS: Secure Integration of Third-Party Payment Gateways Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Deeply nested XML without closing tags for DoS
truth: Stop accepting deeply nested XML elements without closing tags; enforce limits on nesting depth and require well-formed closing tags to prevent stack-overflow DoS.
quote: One popular coercive attack in XML involves parsing deeply nested XML documents without their corresponding ending tags. The idea is to make the victim use up -and eventually deplete- the machine's resources and cause a denial of service on the target.
source: OWASP CS: XML Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: dynamic policy for access decisions
truth: Stop using static rules or permanent permissions; evaluate multiple risk factors (identity, device, location, time, behavior) dynamically for every access request.
quote: Access decisions consider multiple factors
source: OWASP CS: Zero Trust Architecture Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 signature algorithms in TLS 1.3 handshake
truth: Do not use RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 signature algorithms for TLS 1.3 handshake signatures; they are only defined for certificates and backward compatibility with TLS 1.2.
quote: not defined for use in signed TLS handshake messages

subject: Sensitive data exposure via HttpResponse.Write
truth: Stop writing sensitive data (passwords, exception details) directly to the HTTP response; instead log technical errors and return a generic user-friendly message.
quote: Transmitting sensitive data to the user is a potential security risk

subject: os.chmod with world-writable or world-executable permissions
truth: Stop using os.chmod with 0o777, 0o770, 0o776, 0o760, 0x1ff, or any mask granting world write/execute; restrict permissions to owner-only or owner+group read-only.
quote: Test for setting permissive file permissions

subject: Paramiko exec_command with string concatenation
truth: Stop building exec_command commands via string concatenation or f-strings with user input; use shlex.quote() or pass a list of arguments to avoid shell injection.
quote: These commands are run within a shell on the target and are thus vulnerable to various shell injection attacks.

subject: C rand()/srand() for security contexts
truth: Use a cryptographically secure PRNG like arc4random(), getrandom(), or /dev/urandom instead of rand()/srand() for any security-sensitive random number generation.
quote: When a non-cryptographic PRNG is used in a cryptographic context, it can expose the cryptography to certain types of attacks.
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Hardcoded credentials in config files
truth: Stop storing plaintext credentials in configuration or properties files; use a secure credential store, keystore, or environment-specific secrets manager instead.
quote: transmits or stores authentication credentials, but it uses an insecure method
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Android ContentProvider SQL injection via selection parameter
truth: Stop passing caller-controlled selection strings directly into SQLiteQueryBuilder.query(); always use selectionArgs with ? placeholders to bind user input safely.
quote: * The caller controlled selection argument is passed directly

subject: Android ContentProvider UriMatcher wildcard route for SQL injection
truth: Stop using UriMatcher wildcard (*) routes that accept arbitrary path input and feed it into SQL queries; use # for numeric IDs or validate/parameterize the input.
quote: The second finding uses the `students/filter/*` route, which accepts arbitrary path input and is exploitable as path based SQL injection.

subject: MediaStore API writes sensitive data to world-readable external storage
truth: Stop writing sensitive data to shared external storage via MediaStore; use internal app-specific storage (Context.getFilesDir() or Context.getCacheDir()) instead.
quote: The snippet below shows sample code that uses the `MediaStore` API to write a file to shared storage in a path like `/storage/emulated/0/Download/` which does not require any permissions to access and

subject: WKWebView loadFileURL with overly broad read access
truth: Stop granting WKWebView read access to the entire Documents directory; restrict allowingReadAccessTo to only the specific subdirectory containing the web content.
quote: grants the WebView read access to the entire `Documents` directory by calling `loadFileURL(_:allowingReadAccessTo:)`

subject: Custom permission without protectionLevel defaults to normal
truth: Stop declaring custom permissions without an explicit protectionLevel; set android:protectionLevel="signature" or "dangerous" to avoid automatic grant to any app.
quote: This means any app can request and typically receive it automatically, so it is a weak security boundary.

subject: iOS Keychain accessibility for HMAC key
truth: Store the HMAC key in the iOS Keychain with kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly so it cannot be extracted from a backup or transferred to another device.
quote: Use a key that is stored in the iOS Keychain with a strict accessibility setting (e.g., `kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly`) so it cannot be extracted from a backup or transferred to anothe

subject: Kubernetes vulnerability prioritization
truth: Prioritize remediation of vulnerabilities by combining severity score with deployment context (privileged containers, internet exposure, environment criticality).
quote: For example, a deployment containing a vulnerability with severity score of 7 or greater should be moved up in remediation priority if that deployment contains privileged containers and is open to the
source: OWASP CS: Kubernetes Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: logging prompt injection events
truth: Avoid logging the full prompt/tool I/O when detecting prompt injection; log only a detection category or rule ID, the target tool/server, and request identifiers.
quote: Avoid logging the full prompt/tool I/O. Prefer logging a detection category or rule ID, the target tool/server, and request identifiers
source: OWASP CS: Application Logging Vocabulary Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: typosquatting npm packages
truth: Do not install packages with names that visually resemble popular packages; verify package authenticity through download counts, GitHub repo, and release history.
quote: bad actors publish malicious modules to the npm registry with names that look much like existing popular modules
source: OWASP CS: NPM Security best practices
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: iOS CA public key pinning via Info.plist
truth: On iOS, pin a CA public key by specifying it in Info.plist under App Transport Security Settings, or use TrustKit, rather than implementing custom SSL validation.
quote: Apple suggests pinning a CA public key by specifying it in `Info.plist` file under [App Transport Security Settings](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/security/preventing_insecure_network_conn
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Model output execution without validation
truth: Validate all model outputs before returning them to users or downstream systems; never execute model outputs directly, especially in agent or automation contexts.
quote: Execute model outputs directly, especially in agent or automation contexts. Model output is untrusted until validated.
source: OWASP CS: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: gRPC input validation with Protocol Buffers
truth: Protocol Buffers provide type safety but not business logic validation; always perform thorough server-side validation using protoc-gen-validate or custom checks.
quote: Protocol Buffers provide type safety but not business logic validation
source: OWASP CS: gRPC Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: ClientHello legacy_version must be 0x0303 (TLS 1.2)
truth: Set the legacy_version field in ClientHello to 0x0303 (TLS 1.2), not to the highest supported version; version preferences go in the supported_versions extension.
quote: legacy_version field MUST be set to 0x0303

subject: Max-Forwards header not checked/decremented by intermediaries
truth: Each intermediary that receives a TRACE or OPTIONS request containing a Max-Forwards header field MUST check and update its value prior to forwarding the request.
quote: MUST check and update its value prior to forwarding the request

subject: Key usage restriction
truth: Stop using a key for purposes other than its designated use; a key designated for key transport must not be used for digital signature generation or verification.
quote: a key designated for key transport must not be used for the generation or verification of digital signatures

subject: exportable cryptographic keys
truth: Stop allowing secret keys to be exportable from cryptographic devices; secret keys SHALL NOT be exportable from single-factor cryptographic device authenticators.
quote: SHALL NOT be exportable (i.e., cannot be removed from the device)

subject: Casting HTTP URLConnection to HttpsURLConnection
truth: Stop casting the result of url.openConnection() to HttpsURLConnection when the URL uses HTTP; use an HTTPS URL so the cast succeeds and the connection is secure.
quote: This is impossible if the URL that has been constructed uses HTTP rather than HTTPS, and results in a run-time ClassCastException

subject: Including third-party JavaScript from an untrusted domain
truth: Do not include script tags from untrusted external domains; instead, self-host all JavaScript dependencies or use Subresource Integrity (SRI) with a trusted CDN.
quote: Inclusion of Web Functionality from an Untrusted Source
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: load a JavaScript library from a CDN
insert a script tag from another domain

subject: JDWP debugger detection via Debug.isDebuggerConnected()
truth: Stop relying solely on Debug.isDebuggerConnected() for anti-debugging; it can be bypassed by hooking the return value or using Frida to spoof the debugger state.
quote: Debug.isDebuggerConnected reports an attached JDWP debugger.

subject: iOS app binary retains literal log strings exposing network security configuration
truth: Stop logging SSL pinning state, certificate validation mode, or network configuration details; these strings are recoverable from the binary via static analysis.
quote: Verbose logging exposes network configuration

subject: Exported receiver with permission
truth: When registering an exported receiver that should only accept broadcasts from specific apps, pass an appropriate permission in the broadcastPermission parameter.
quote: For context-registered receivers, when registering an exported receiver that should only accept broadcasts from specific apps, pass an appropriate permission in the `broadcastPermission` parameter.

subject: Unsanitized String Parameters in Custom URL Schemes
truth: Sanitize string parameters from URL schemes using allowlists for expected values; reject non-matching values instead of attempting to strip or escape characters.
quote: Use allowlists for expected values when the set of valid inputs is known. Reject any value that does not match rather than attempting to strip or escape individual characters.

subject: Node.js csurf CSRF package
truth: Do not use the csurf package for CSRF protection; it is deprecated with an unfixed security hole and the team recommends using any other CSRF protection package.
quote: a security hole in this package has been recently discovered. The team behind the package has not fixed the discovered vulnerability and they have marked the package as deprecated
source: OWASP CS: NodeJS Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Expiring inactive user passwords during hash upgrade
truth: Do not expire and delete password hashes of inactive users; instead use the existing hashes as inputs for a more secure algorithm and replace them on next login.
quote: Expiring the passwords of many users may cause issues for support staff or may be interpreted by users as an indication of a breach.
source: OWASP CS: Password Storage Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Accept header to Content-Type reflection
truth: Do NOT copy the Accept header to the Content-Type header of the response; reject with 406 Not Acceptable if the Accept header does not contain an allowable type.
quote: - **Do NOT** simply copy the `Accept` header to the `Content-type` header of the response.
source: OWASP CS: REST Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: CDATA section normalization by XML parsers
truth: Do not rely on CDATA sections preserving special characters; parsers in recovery mode may normalize CDATA content into escaped entities, altering data integrity.
quote: This means that they will update the special characters contained in the `CDATA` section to contain the safe versions of these characters even though is not required:
source: OWASP CS: XML Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Digital signature without hashing
truth: Always hash the data using an approved hash function before generating or verifying a digital signature; the signature is computed on the hash, not the raw data.
quote: obtain a hash value, which is a condensed version of the data to be signed (i.e.,

subject: using untrusted input in security-critical if-conditions
truth: Stop using untrusted external inputs directly in if-statements that make security decisions; add or strengthen integrity checks on the user-supplied data first.
quote: untrusted inputs are used in an if statement, and the body of that statement makes a security decision

subject: Missing path normalization before prefix check
truth: Always normalize the path with os.path.normpath or os.path.realpath before checking it starts with a safe root folder, otherwise '..' segments bypass the check.
quote: the normalization step is important, since otherwise even a path that starts with the root folder could be used to access files outside the root folder

subject: Hardcoded ECDSA Private Key in CryptoKit
truth: Stop embedding hardcoded ECDSA private keys as byte arrays in source code; use the Secure Enclave or the Keychain to store and generate private keys at runtime.
quote: hardcoded ECDSA private key within the binary's DATA section

subject: URLSession TLS minimum version lowered to TLS 1.0
truth: Stop setting tlsMinimumSupportedProtocolVersion to .TLSv10 or any value below .TLSv12; use the default ATS-enforced minimum or explicitly set .TLSv12 or higher.
quote: Although ATS would block the connection at runtime unless a matching `Info.plist` exception is also present, explicitly setting `tlsMinimumSupportedProtocolVersion` to a deprecated TLS version is a ba

subject: iOS logging APIs (NSLog, print, debugPrint, dump, Logger) expose sensitive implementation details
truth: Stop logging sensitive implementation details via NSLog, print, debugPrint, dump, or Logger; use conditional compilation (#if DEBUG) to guard all debug logging.
quote: Verbose logging exposes internal API endpoint and request details

subject: Exported FileProvider without grantUriPermissions
truth: Stop exporting FileProvider or omitting grantUriPermissions; keep android:exported="false" and set android:grantUriPermissions="true" with temporary URI grants.
quote: Keep the provider non-exported and enable per-URI grants.

subject: Deep link data passed directly to WebView without validation
truth: Stop passing unvalidated deep link query parameters directly to WebView.loadUrl(); validate and sanitize all externally controlled input before loading any URL.
quote: the attacker has full control of the URL being loaded to the WebView

subject: encrypting stored keys with envelope encryption
truth: Encrypt stored keys using envelope encryption: a Data Encryption Key (DEK) encrypts the data, and a separately-stored Key Encryption Key (KEK) encrypts the DEK.
quote: encryption keys should themselves be stored in an encrypted form
source: OWASP CS: Cryptographic Storage Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: LLM prompt injection testing
truth: Test defenses against prompt injection using known attack patterns including direct injection, typoglycemia, best-of-N variations, and remote injection vectors.
quote: Test with known attack patterns to verify defenses
source: OWASP CS: LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MCP server authorization granularity
truth: Stop using broad OAuth scopes (e.g., full Gmail access) for MCP servers; enforce least-privilege scopes to prevent aggregation risk and confused deputy attacks.
quote: Excessive Permissions / Over-Scoped Tokens
source: OWASP CS: MCP (Model Context Protocol) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Vector database authentication
truth: Enable authentication and use strong credentials on vector databases before production use; they often ship with authentication disabled or optional by default.
quote: Some vector databases or deployment modes may ship with authentication disabled or optional by default.
source: OWASP CS: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: initial login timeout via JavaScript
truth: Use JavaScript in the login page to measure elapsed time since page load and force session ID renewal if login exceeds the timeout, preventing session fixation.
quote: use JavaScript code in the login page to evaluate and measure the amount of time since the page was loaded and a session ID was granted
source: OWASP CS: Session Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: change of authorization method
truth: Stop allowing users to change their authorization method without confirming the current method; require authorization using the current method before switching.
quote: Change of authorization method should be authorized using the current authorization method
source: OWASP CS: Transaction Authorization Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: OAuth client redirect validation
truth: Do not blindly redirect to any URL passed as a parameter; only redirect if the target URLs are allowed or the request origin and integrity can be authenticated.
quote: clients should only redirect if the target URLs are allowed or if the origin and integrity of a request can be authenticated

subject: source authentication for automated key establishment
truth: When using automated key establishment methods, require source authentication for participating entities via a PKI or a manually distributed authentication key.
quote: source authentication is required for the participating entities

subject: Untrusted data in external API calls
truth: Stop passing unsanitized request parameters directly to external API methods like HttpServletResponse.sendError; validate and encode untrusted data before use.
quote: a request parameter is written directly to an error response page

subject: Empty checkServerTrusted body
truth: Stop leaving checkServerTrusted empty or with only a comment; it must validate the certificate chain and throw CertificateException for untrusted certificates.
quote: The checkServerTrusted method of your TrustManager does not throw a CertificateException.

subject: Storing sensitive data in cookies
truth: Avoid placing sensitive information in cookies altogether; instead store a key in the cookie that can be used to look up the sensitive information server-side.
quote: avoid placing sensitive information in cookies altogether. Instead, prefer storing, in the cookie, a key that can be used to look up the sensitive information

subject: unsigned integer subtraction used for length calculation
truth: Never subtract unsigned integers where the result could be negative; cast to a signed type and check for underflow before using the result as a size or length.
quote: size_t len = a - b; char buf[len];
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Unrestricted File Upload - No Extension Check
truth: Check the file extension against a whitelist of allowed types and reject any file that does not match, instead of blindly accepting the user-supplied filename.
quote: dangerous file types that are automatically processed
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Exported ContentProvider without permission enforcement
truth: Stop exporting ContentProviders without android:readPermission or android:writePermission; declare a signature-level permission and enforce it in the manifest.
quote: AppointmentProvider is exported without android:readPermission or android:writePermission, allowing any app on the device to query patient appointment records

subject: iOS Keychain biometric enrollment sensitivity
truth: Stop using .biometryAny in SecAccessControlCreateWithFlags for sensitive tokens; use .biometryCurrentSet to invalidate access when new biometrics are enrolled.
quote: When it is required that the associated keychain item become inaccessible when changes are made to the biometric database (e.g., when a new fingerprint or face is added), the app must use the`kSecAcce

subject: Jailbreak Detection Bypass via File Existence Checks
truth: Stop relying on FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath:) for jailbreak detection; attackers can trivially bypass these checks by hooking or patching the binary.
quote: Check 1: Common jailbreak files and directories

subject: WAF unreliability for XSS prevention
truth: Do not rely on Web Application Firewalls for XSS prevention; WAFs are unreliable, miss client-side DOM XSS, and new bypass techniques are discovered regularly.
quote: WAF’s are unreliable and new bypass techniques are being discovered regularly.
source: OWASP CS: Cross Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Tenant context injection via request parameters
truth: Stop deriving tenant identity from user-controllable request parameters; derive tenant context from a cryptographically signed token or authentication context.
quote: Manipulating tenant identifiers in requests, tokens, or headers
source: OWASP CS: Multi-Tenant Application Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: CI/CD pipeline storing secrets instead of letting consumers retrieve them
truth: Secrets do not necessarily need to be brought to a consumer by a CI/CD pipeline; it is even better when the consumer of the secret retrieves the secret itself.
quote: Secrets do not necessarily need to be brought to a consumer of the secret by a CI/CD pipeline. It is even better when the consumer of the secret retrieves the secret.
source: OWASP CS: Secrets Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: automatic client logout on idle timeout
truth: Use JavaScript to automatically logout client sessions after idle timeout expires, redirecting to the logout page, and optionally warn the user before timeout.
quote: JavaScript code can be used by the web application in all (or critical) pages to automatically logout client sessions after the idle timeout expires
source: OWASP CS: Session Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: standard CLF HTTP logging for incident response
truth: Stop relying on standard Common Log Format (CLF) for incident response; enable full HTTP audit logging including request URI, headers, body, and response data.
quote: The standard Common Log Format (CLF) utilized by most web servers does not provide adequate data for conducting proper incident response
source: OWASP CS: Virtual Patching Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Java XML parsers (DocumentBuilderFactory, SAXParserFactory, DOM4J) have XXE enabled by default
truth: Disable DTDs entirely by setting 'http://apache.org/xml/features/disallow-doctype-decl' to true on DocumentBuilderFactory, SAXParserFactory, and DOM4J parsers.
quote: Since most Java XML parsers have XXE enabled by default, this language is especially vulnerable to XXE attack, so you must explicitly disable XXE to use these parsers safely.
source: OWASP CS: XML External Entity Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: ASP.NET httpRuntime targetFramework
truth: Set httpRuntime targetFramework to ≥4.5.2 in Web.config; omitting it or setting it lower makes ASP.NET unsafe-by-default regardless of the actual .NET version.
quote: Omitting this tag will also result in unsafe-by-default behavior.
source: OWASP CS: XML External Entity Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: ECDSA signature R/S octet encoding must not strip leading zero octets
truth: When encoding ECDSA signature values R and S as octet sequences, do NOT strip leading zero octets; each must be exactly the full length (32, 48, or 66 octets).
quote: MUST NOT be shortened to omit any leading zero octets

subject: memmap2 range validation
truth: Always validate that offset + len does not exceed the mapped file length before calling advise_range, flush_range, or their unchecked variants on Mmap/MmapMut.
quote: Affected versionf of `memmap2` did not perform enough validation on the `offset` and `len` parameters of

subject: Virtual method result in pointer arithmetic
truth: Always validate the result of virtual method calls before performing pointer arithmetic to avoid reading or writing outside the bounds of an allocated buffer.
quote: Unvalidated use of virtual method call result in pointer arithmetic
_audit_fix: WRONG: code_good computes the bounds-checked pointer into `newCharPointer2` but then dereferences the original, unvalidated `newCharPointer` (`*newCharPointer = 'A'`)

subject: Storing sensitive data in heap data structures
truth: Stop storing sensitive information in cleartext in heap data structures like java.util.Properties that may be written to disk later; encrypt before insertion.
quote: encrypt information before it is put into a heap data structure (such as Java.util.Properties) that may be written to disk later

subject: Relative URL check for redirect safety
truth: Stop assuming a relative URL is safe without checking; verify the URL is not absolute before redirecting, and also check the host against a known good domain.
quote: check that the target URL does not redirect to a different host by checking that the URL is either relative or on a known good host

subject: TOCTOU symlink resolution race
truth: Do not check file ownership or symlink status before reading; instead, open the file directly and verify properties on the file descriptor, or use O_NOFOLLOW.
quote: - Implementation The most basic advice for TOCTOU vulnerabilities is to not perform a check before the use.
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)
