subject: Storing sensitive data in cookies in cleartext
truth: Never store sensitive information like account IDs or passwords in cookies without encryption; use server-side sessions or encrypted cookies instead.
quote: stores sensitive information in cleartext within a resource that might be accessible to another control sphere
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: set a cookie with user id

subject: PRNG seeding with hardcoded constant
truth: Stop using a hardcoded constant as the PRNG seed; use a cryptographically secure random seed from SecureRandom (Java) or os.urandom (Python) instead.
quote: protecting seeds as cryptographic material
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: use a fixed seed for reproducibility

subject: WebView URL loading handlers returning false without host inspection
truth: Stop returning false from shouldOverrideUrlLoading without inspecting the URL host, scheme, and path; this allows navigation to any untrusted domain.
quote: the handler returned without ever reading the URL's host, scheme, or path

subject: JavascriptInterface method reading JWT and PII from SharedPreferences
truth: Stop exposing getUserProfileJson() via @JavascriptInterface which returns JWT tokens and PII from SharedPreferences to any JavaScript in the WebView.
quote: - `getUserProfileJson()` (line 37) reads the user's name, email, and a JWT from `SharedPreferences` and returns them to JavaScript as a JSON string, exposing PII and a credential.

subject: Root detection via PackageManager.getPackageInfo for root packages
truth: Stop using PackageManager.getPackageInfo to detect root management apps; Frida can hook getPackageInfo to throw NameNotFoundException or return null.
quote: - **`PackageManager.getPackageInfo` calls for root packages:**

subject: SwiftUI SecureField vs TextField for sensitive input
truth: Use SwiftUI SecureField instead of TextField for passwords, OTPs, and other secrets; SecureField always masks input and blocks third-party keyboards.
quote: - The OTP 2 input (`OTP 2`) is a SwiftUI `SecureField` which always masks input.

subject: ContentProvider SQL injection via raw SQL in insert/update/delete
truth: Stop using dynamically constructed SQL for insert, update, or delete operations; use SQLiteStatement or SQLiteDatabase methods with argument binding.
quote: When performing insert, update, or delete operations, use SQLite prepared statements (for example, `SQLiteStatement` or `SQLiteDatabase` methods that support argument binding) instead of dynamically c

subject: NWConnection TLS minimum version
truth: When using NWConnection with TLS, explicitly set the minimum TLS version to at least TLS 1.2 using sec_protocol_options_set_min_tls_protocol_version.
quote: Apple's documentation states that "ATS doesn't apply to calls your app makes to lower-level networking interfaces like the `Network` framework or `CFNetwork`.

subject: iOS server trust evaluation ignored in URLSession
truth: Always verify the challenge type, host name, and credentials when performing server trust evaluation in URLSession delegate; never ignore TLS errors.
quote: verifies the challenge type and the host name and credentials when performing server trust evaluation

subject: Mass Assignment via forceFill
truth: Stop using forceFill with unsanitized request input; qualify allowed parameters using $request->only or $request->validated instead of $request->all.
quote: - Avoid using methods such as `forceFill` or `forceCreate` that bypass the protection mechanism.

subject: MCP message signature verification failure handling
truth: Fail closed when signature verification fails — reject the message entirely; never silently fall back to unsigned processing when signing is enabled.
quote: Fail closed when verification fails. If a signature is missing, invalid, or the nonce has been seen before, reject the message entirely. Never silently fall back to unsigned processing when signing is enabled.
source: OWASP CS: MCP (Model Context Protocol) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Geolocation as MFA factor
truth: Stop using geolocation as a standalone MFA factor; it is easy for an attacker to bypass by obtaining IP addresses in the trusted country or location.
quote: Easy for an attacker to bypass by obtaining IP addresses in the trusted country or location
source: OWASP CS: Multifactor Authentication Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: dependency confusion
truth: Always use scoped package names (@yourorg/package-name) for internal packages and configure .npmrc to point scoped packages to your private registry.
quote: - Always use **scoped package names** for internal packages (e.g., `@yourorg/package-name` instead of `package-name`)
source: OWASP CS: NPM Security best practices
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: npm install in Docker image
truth: Stop using 'RUN npm ci' without --omit=dev in production Docker images; install only production dependencies to reduce attack surface and image size.
quote: In the case of building a Docker image for production we want to ensure that we only install production dependencies in a deterministic way, and this brings us to the following recommendation for the
source: OWASP CS: Node.js Docker Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Access control metadata stripped during chunking
truth: Store access control metadata (classification, owner, permitted roles, permitted tenants) alongside every vector chunk, not just the source document.
quote: Strip access control metadata during chunking or embedding
source: OWASP CS: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: model deserialization
truth: Stop using pickle or torch.load on untrusted model files without integrity verification; use safetensors or verify signatures before deserialization.
quote: A `.pt` or `.pkl` file embedded with malware is uploaded to a pipeline and executed during deserialization.
source: OWASP CS: Secure AI/ML Model Ops Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: session ID in URL
truth: Do not include the session ID in the URL; URL-based session IDs are disclosed in web links, logs, browser history, bookmarks, and the Referer header.
quote: The usage of specific session ID exchange mechanisms, such as those where the ID is included in the URL, might disclose the session ID (in web links and logs, web browser history and bookmarks, the Referer header or search engines)
source: OWASP CS: Session Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: HSTS strict security policy enforcement
truth: UAs must rewrite all insecure 'http' URI loads to use 'https' for HSTS-enabled sites, and must prevent users from clicking through security warnings.
quote: UAs need to rewrite all insecure UA "http" URI loads to use the

subject: Weak vs Strong Validators (ETags)
truth: Do not use weak ETags for If-Match or If-Unmodified-Since preconditions that require strong comparison; use strong ETags for 'lost update' avoidance.
quote: Strong validators are usable for all conditional requests, including

subject: key and certificate inventory management
truth: Stop operating without a key/certificate inventory; establish and maintain an inventory that tracks owners, status, expiration, and compromise state.
quote: establishing and maintaining an inventory of keys and/or certificates; assigning and tracking their owners

subject: Usability vs. security tradeoff in authentication
truth: Do not assume higher AAL authenticators are less usable; allow higher-AAL authenticators for lower-AAL applications when they offer better usability.
quote: Authenticators with a higher AAL sometimes offer better usability and should be allowed for use for lower AAL applications.

subject: path containment validation
truth: After resolving the full path with realpath(), verify that the resolved path starts with the intended base directory to prevent directory traversal.
quote: Check if the resolved path starts with the base directory

subject: DataSet/DataTable type limitations bypass
truth: Do not rely on the default type limitations of DataSet/DataTable as a security boundary; attackers can bypass them to achieve remote code execution.
quote: DataSet and DataTable do impose default limitations on the types that are allowed to be present while deserializing XML payloads, DataSet and DataTable are in general not safe when populated with untrusted input

subject: Hard-coded cryptographic values in Rust
truth: Stop using hard-coded cryptographic values that attackers can recover from source code or compiled executables; use randomly generated key material.
quote: Attackers can easily recover hard-coded values if they have access to the source code or compiled executable

subject: io.Copy zip extraction unbounded
truth: Stop using io.Copy when extracting zip file entries from untrusted archives; use io.CopyN with a size limit to prevent zip bomb decompression bombs.
quote: Detect io.Copy instead of io.CopyN when decompressing

subject: Missing authorization check on database query function
truth: Add an authorization check before executing the query to verify the authenticated user has permission to access the specified database and resource.
quote: does not perform an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action

subject: Hardcoded HMAC key bypasses storage integrity
truth: Stop hardcoding HMAC keys in the app; generate and store the key in the Android Keystore so it cannot be extracted from the app package or a backup.
quote: Storage integrity checks are bypassable if the attacker can extract the HMAC key (for example, if it is hardcoded in the app or recoverable on a rooted device)

subject: iOS secure text entry (isSecureTextEntry)
truth: Stop leaving sensitive text fields unmasked; set isSecureTextEntry = true on any UITextField that accepts passwords, PINs, or other sensitive input.
quote: If the text field is defined in the source code, make sure that the option `isSecureTextEntry` is set to "true".

subject: WKWebView JavaScript Bridge Exposing Native Methods
truth: Stop exposing native methods that return sensitive data through WKScriptMessageHandler; only expose minimal, safe functions and validate all inputs.
quote: exposes a sensitive function

subject: CORS with Access-Control-Allow-Origin wildcard and credentials
truth: Stop setting Access-Control-Allow-Origin=* when Access-Control-Allow-Credentials=true; the browser will reject the response, silently breaking CORS.
quote: The browser will reject any response that includes `Access-Control-Allow-Origin=*` if credentials are allowed.
source: OWASP CS: Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: database account permissions
truth: Stop using root/sa/SYS accounts or granting administrative rights; create dedicated accounts with only SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE on specific databases.
quote: - Do not use the built-in `root`, `sa` or `SYS` accounts.
source: OWASP CS: Database Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Reverse Tabnabbing via window.open
truth: Stop calling window.open without noopener,noreferrer in the windowFeatures parameter; always include 'noopener,noreferrer' and reset opener to null.
quote: - For the JavaScript `window.open` function, add the values `noopener,noreferrer` in the [windowFeatures](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/open) parameter of the `window.open` f

subject: LLM prompt injection — guardrail model susceptibility
truth: Do not rely on a guardrail LLM as a single defense; it is itself susceptible to prompt injection and must be one layer in a defense-in-depth design.
quote: A guardrail LLM is itself an LLM and is itself susceptible to prompt injection.
source: OWASP CS: LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MCP server sandboxing
truth: Stop running local MCP servers with full host access; sandbox them to prevent file system traversal, credential theft, and arbitrary code execution.
quote: Local MCP servers running with full host access, enabling file system traversal, credential theft, or arbitrary code execution
source: OWASP CS: MCP (Model Context Protocol) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Service-to-storage access documentation
truth: Document every service-to-storage relation with access type (Read or Read/Write) to enforce least privilege and prevent over-privileged data access.
quote: #### Identify "service-to-storage" relations
source: OWASP CS: Microservices based Security Arch Doc Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: npm install without frozen lockfile
truth: Stop using npm install or yarn install in CI/build; use npm ci or yarn install --frozen-lockfile to enforce the lockfile and abort on inconsistency.
quote: When they detect an inconsistency between the project’s `package.json` and the lockfile, they compensate for such change based on the `package.json` manifest by installing different versions than thos
source: OWASP CS: NPM Security best practices
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: npm publish without dry-run
truth: Stop publishing without first reviewing the tarball; add --dry-run to npm publish to inspect what files will be included before actually publishing.
quote: To be extra careful, add a `--dry-run` command-line argument to your publish command in order to first review how the tarball is created without actually publishing it to the registry.
source: OWASP CS: NPM Security best practices
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: documentation examples copied into production
truth: Never copy README examples into production without a security review; treat documentation code as a starting point, not a production-ready solution.
quote: Never copy README examples into production without a security review.
source: OWASP CS: NPM Security best practices
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Session ID entropy and randomness
truth: Generate session IDs with sufficient entropy using a cryptographically secure random number generator to prevent prediction and brute force attacks.
quote: prediction, brute force, or fixation of the session ID will lead to session hijacking
source: OWASP CS: Session Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Set-Cookie header folding
truth: Do not fold multiple Set-Cookie header fields into a single header field; the comma character used by Set-Cookie conflicts with HTTP header folding.
quote: SHOULD NOT fold multiple Set-Cookie header fields into a single header field

subject: TLS 1.3 resumption PSK forward secrecy between connections
truth: Ensure the resumption master secret from connection N is separate from traffic keys of connection N to maintain forward secrecy between connections.
quote: the resumption master secret computed by connection N and needed to form connection N+1 is separate from the traffic keys used by connection N, thus providing forward secrecy between the connections

subject: TLS 1.3 PSK without psk_key_exchange_modes extension
truth: Clients MUST provide a psk_key_exchange_modes extension if they offer a pre_shared_key extension; servers MUST abort the handshake if it is missing.
quote: A client MUST provide a "psk_key_exchange_modes" extension if it offers a "pre_shared_key" extension.

subject: TLS server certificate Subject Alternative Name
truth: Require the Subject Alternative Name (SAN) extension with DNS host name (or IP address) for TLS server certificates; do not rely on the Common Name.
quote: Required. Multiple SANs are permitted

subject: Trust anchor configuration
truth: Only trust certificates issued by a CA that the client has been explicitly configured to trust; do not accept arbitrary or self-signed certificates.
quote: configure the clients to trust that CA

subject: Open redirect via unvalidated parameter
truth: Stop using request parameters directly in sendRedirect without a whitelist or host validation; maintain a server-side list of authorized redirects.
quote: maintain a list of authorized redirects on the server; then choose from that list based on the user input provided

subject: Regex matching HTML end tags in Ruby
truth: Do not assume </script> is the only valid script end tag; browsers accept </script foo="bar"> as a parser error that still closes the script block.
quote: Browsers will not only accept </script> as script end tags, but also tags such as </script foo="bar"> even though it is a parser error

subject: output buffer too small for HTML entity encoding expansion
truth: When encoding input for HTML entity expansion (e.g., & to &), allocate output buffer at least 5x the input size to handle worst-case expansion.
quote: allocate enough memory to handle the largest possible encoding

subject: SSL certificate expiration validation
truth: Always verify that the current date is within the certificate's validity period; do not accept certificates that have expired or are not yet valid.

subject: PRNG seeding with insufficient entropy bytes
truth: Stop seeding Python's random module with only 2 bytes from os.urandom; use secrets module or at minimum 32 bytes of entropy for cryptographic keys.
quote: proper seeding is critical to the secure operation of the PRNG

subject: Returning local stack array from function
truth: Never return a pointer to a stack-allocated local variable; allocate memory on the heap with malloc/calloc or use a caller-provided buffer instead.
quote: returns the address of a stack variable

subject: Command Injection via Chatbot Prompt Injection
truth: Always validate and sanitize user input against a strict allowlist before interpolating it into any command or prompt, even for AI/chatbot systems.

read remote file from user input

subject: Exported activity with intent-filter for custom action
truth: Stop exporting activities that handle internal implicit intents; set android:exported="false" on activities not meant to be launched by other apps.
quote: any app can register for a custom action and be presented to the user as a valid handler

truth: Always validate the host, scheme, and path using request.getUrl() in both shouldOverrideUrlLoading and shouldInterceptRequest against an allowlist.
quote: Because the handlers inspect only the full URL string for logging and make no host-, scheme-, or path-based decision, they impose no restriction on navigation, and the WebView can load content from an

subject: Activity-level overlay protection via setHideOverlayWindows
truth: Call activity.window.setHideOverlayWindows(true) and declare HIDE_OVERLAY_WINDOWS permission to prevent overlay attacks on all UI elements at once.
quote: The sample shows an app that uses `setHideOverlayWindows(true)` at the activity level (available from Android 12, API level 31) and declares the required `HIDE_OVERLAY_WINDOWS` permission in the manif

subject: Exported deep link activity without domain verification
truth: Stop exporting deep link handling activities without android:autoVerify="true"; a malicious app can hijack the link or invoke the handler directly.
quote: the handler is also exported, any app on the device can invoke it directly to trigger the sensitive action

subject: isDeviceSecure() used as gate without actual authentication
truth: Stop using isDeviceSecure() as a security gate; it only checks if a lock screen is configured, not that the user has authenticated in this session.
quote: Checks if the device has a secure lock screen

subject: iOS ATS Configuration (Info.plist)
truth: Stop disabling App Transport Security globally with NSAllowsArbitraryLoads; configure NSExceptionDomains only for necessary cleartext destinations.
quote: NSAllowsArbitraryLoads = true

subject: Password field in WKWebView DOM
truth: Stop rendering <input type='password'> directly in a WKWebView DOM; use a native UITextField overlay or secure text entry on a native view instead.
quote: The password field lives in the DOM, so any JavaScript running on the page can read the user's typed value at any time

subject: WKUserScript without WKContentWorld
truth: Stop creating WKUserScript without specifying a WKContentWorld; always use the content world parameter to isolate your script from page JavaScript.

subject: Certificate Pinning in OkHttp
truth: Stop using certificate pinning without a backup plan; use OkHttp's CertificatePinner with proper SHA-256 hashes and handle pin failures gracefully.

subject: iOS Custom URL Scheme handler without source application validation
truth: Stop opening URLs from any source app without validating the sourceApplication; validate the source application identity before processing the URL.
quote: The source application is not being verified in any of those methods.

subject: CSP hash-based script allowlisting
truth: Using hashes for CSP is risky because any change to whitespace or formatting inside the script tag changes the hash and silently breaks the script.
quote: If you change *anything* inside the script tag (even whitespace) by, e.g., formatting your code, the hash will be different, and the script won't render.

subject: writing custom cryptographic functions
truth: Never write your own cryptographic functions; always use a trusted, well-known implementation library like Google Tink instead of JCA/JCE directly.

subject: password storage with fast hashing algorithms
truth: Stop using fast hashing algorithms like SHA-256 for password storage; use slow, memory-hard algorithms such as Argon2id, bcrypt, or PBKDF2 instead.
quote: Fast hashing algorithms such as SHA‑256 are not suitable for password storage because they allow attackers to perform large numbers of guesses quickly.

subject: Missing X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on API responses
truth: Include X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff in API responses to prevent browsers from MIME sniffing and inappropriately interpreting responses as HTML.
quote: This header with a `nosniff` value prevents browsers from performing MIME sniffing, and inappropriately interpreting responses as HTML.

subject: CI/CD credentials without rotation and expiry
truth: Credentials used by CI/CD tooling to authenticate against the secret management system must be rotated frequently and expire after a job completes.

subject: Downgrade protection with multiple connection attempts
truth: Do not retry connections with older versions to work around buggy servers; this practice is vulnerable to downgrade attacks and is NOT RECOMMENDED.
quote: this practice is vulnerable to downgrade attacks and is NOT RECOMMENDED

subject: Derivation of a key from a password
truth: Stop deriving cryptographic keys directly from passwords; use an approved key derivation function (KDF) with a salt and sufficient iteration count.
quote: Derivation of a Key from a Password

subject: Deprecated and disallowed algorithms per SP 800-131A
truth: Consult SP 800-131A to determine whether each algorithm and service is acceptable, deprecated, allowed only for legacy applications, or disallowed.
quote: 800-131A, indicating whether its use is acceptable,71 deprecated,72 allowed only for legacy

subject: Passing immutable reference to out-argument pointer in glib::VariantStrIter
truth: Pass a mutable reference (&mut p) instead of an immutable reference (&p) when passing a pointer as an out-argument to a C function that mutates it.
quote: This was fixed by passing the out-argument pointer explitly as `&mut p` instead of `&p`.

subject: strcat with tainted data as taint step
truth: Stop treating strcat as a safe string operation; tainted data flows from the 1st argument to the 0th argument and must be modeled as a taint step.
quote: tainted data would flow from the 1st argument to the 0th argument of the call

subject: Quoting user input in shell commands is insufficient defense
truth: Do not rely on shell quoting (e.g., double quotes around %s) to prevent command injection; use a dedicated shell-encoding library routine instead.
quote: Use a library routine to escape characters in the user-supplied string before passing it to a command shell

subject: Hard-coded credentials in source code
truth: Remove hard-coded credentials (user names, passwords, certificates) from source code and place them in configuration files with restricted access.
quote: Including unencrypted hard-coded inbound or outbound authentication credentials within source code or configuration files is dangerous because the credentials may be easily discovered.

subject: Environment variable injection via user-controlled key and value
truth: Stop assigning user-controlled keys and values to process.env; validate both the key and value against an allowlist of safe environment variables.
quote: Controlling the value of arbitrary environment variables from user-controllable data is not safe.

subject: SQLAlchemy TextClause literal SQL fragments
truth: Stop using SQLAlchemy TextClause to represent literal SQL fragments from user input; it inserts text directly into the final SQL without escaping.

subject: SSRF without IP verification or allowlist
truth: If you must use user-controlled URLs, verify the resolved IP address is not private and maintain an allowlist of authorized domains on the server.

subject: Flask endpoint passing request args to SSH without authorization
truth: Stop accepting user-controlled commands from request.args and passing them to SSH exec_command without validating authorization (e.g., JWT token).

subject: ZipInfo.file_size decompression bomb check
truth: Do not rely on ZipInfo.file_size to detect decompression bombs; attackers can forge this header; use ZipFile.open() and read byte-by-byte instead.
quote: attackers can forge this decompressed size header too. So can't rely on file_size attribute of ZipInfo class

subject: Perl HTML encoding insufficient for shell command
truth: HTML encoding does not prevent shell injection; use taint checking, parameterized APIs, or strict whitelisting when passing user data to system().

subject: malloc with unchecked multiplication from network data
truth: Validate that the multiplication of count and element size does not overflow before calling malloc, especially when count comes from network data.

subject: Rate limiting on login endpoint
truth: Implement account lockout, timeout, or disconnection after a small number of failed authentication attempts instead of allowing unlimited retries.

subject: os.Create with default permissions
truth: Always set explicit restrictive permissions on created files using os.Chmod immediately after creation, or use a more secure file creation method.

subject: Authorization via client-controlled cookie

subject: allowlist virtual patch for integer parameters
truth: For parameters expected to contain only integers, create an allowlist virtual patch that verifies the payload contains only integer characters.
quote: In this case, the `reqID` parameter is supposed to only hold integer characters so we can use this virtual patch:
source: OWASP CS: Virtual Patching Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Algorithm and key length risk acceptance
truth: Do not use algorithms and key lengths that require the user to accept some risk; only use approved, adequate-strength cryptographic mechanisms.
quote: The use of the algorithm and key length is allowed, but the user must accept some risk.

subject: Deterministic random bit generators (DRBGs)
truth: Do not use DRBGs without initializing them from a randomness source that provides sufficient entropy for the security strength to be supported.
quote: DRBGs must be initialized from a randomness source that provides sufficient entropy for the security strength to be supported by the DRBG

subject: Certificates with less than 112 bits of security
truth: Stop using certificates with less than 112 bits of security; both the public key and the signature shall provide at least 112 bits of security.
quote: both the public key contained in the certificate and the signature shall provide at least 112 bits of security

subject: age plugin name parsing does not validate path separators
truth: Stop using plugin names from untrusted input without validating they contain no path separators; use a allowlist of known plugin names instead.
quote: A plugin name containing a path separator may allow an attacker to execute an arbitrary binary.

subject: grep_cli::DecompressionReader::new resolves binary names via Windows API, checking CWD before PATH
truth: Stop relying on Windows API to resolve binary names; use grep_cli::resolve_binary to resolve decompression programs to absolute paths via PATH.
quote: using `PATH`, instead of relying on the Windows API to do it.

subject: i_tree Tree::node and Tree::mut_node
truth: Always validate index bounds before calling Vec::get_unchecked; do not expose safe public methods that pass unchecked indices to get_unchecked.
quote: These methods accepted an arbitrary `u32` index and passed it directly to `Vec::get_unchecked` / `get_unchecked_mut` on the internal node buffer, without validating that the index was in bounds.

subject: ASP.NET WebForms action methods
truth: Stop writing sensitive action handlers without authorization; use User.IsInRole to check the current user's role before performing the action.
quote: No authorization is used

subject: Array size validation boundary
truth: Stop using >= 0 as the validation guard for array sizes; use > 0 because zero is a valid integer but an invalid array size for indexed access.
quote: The first array construction is protected by a condition that checks if the user input is zero or more. However, if the user provides 0 as the numberOfItems parameter, then an empty array is created

subject: Missing validation on redirect target
truth: Stop redirecting to arbitrary URLs derived from query parameters without validation; maintain a server-side allowlist of authorized redirects.
quote: maintain a list of authorized redirects on the server; then choose from that list based on the user input provided

subject: Rust path traversal separator validation
truth: Check for both '/' and '\' path separators and '..' sequences; removing '../' alone is insufficient because inputs like '.../...//' bypass it.
quote: removing "../" sequences is not sufficient, since the input could still contain a path separator followed by ".."

subject: Rust HTML escaping with html_escape crate
truth: Do not assume Rust's type safety prevents XSS; explicitly call html_escape::encode_text() on any untrusted string before embedding it in HTML.
quote: the user input should be HTML-encoded before being included in the response

subject: Paramiko exec_command shell injection
truth: Stop passing unsanitized user input to Paramiko's exec_command; use shlex.quote() or pass commands as argument lists to avoid shell injection.
quote: Possible shell injection via Paramiko call, check inputs are properly sanitized.

subject: ssh.InsecureIgnoreHostKey
truth: Stop using ssh.InsecureIgnoreHostKey which disables host key verification; use ssh.FixedHostKey or implement a proper HostKeyCallback instead.
quote: Audit the use of ssh.InsecureIgnoreHostKey function

subject: JSP output encoding
truth: Always escape or encode user-controlled data when outputting in JSP; use JSTL c:out or fn:escapeXml instead of directly embedding with <%= %>.
quote: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: heap buffer overflow via strcpy with unchecked input
truth: Stop using strcpy() with unchecked input; use strncpy() or snprintf() with explicit length limits, or use automatic bounds-checking functions.
quote: strcpy(buf, argv[1])
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: copy a string into a buffer
copy user input to malloc buffer

subject: Dynamic file inclusion from user-controlled content
truth: Never include or execute files based on user-controlled content; validate and sanitize all input before writing to files that may be included.
quote: does not properly restrict reading from or writing to dynamically-managed code resources
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: write user input to a file

subject: Android SDK version check using Build.VERSION.SDK_INT
truth: Stop using Build.VERSION.SDK_INT alone for security decisions; use PackageManager.hasSystemFeature or check specific API availability instead.
quote: Uses of Build.VERSION.SDK_INT with semgrep

subject: Process.killProcess() as anti-tamper response
truth: Stop using Process.killProcess() as the sole response to hooking detection; attackers can trivially bypass it by hooking the detection method.
quote: The code includes a runtime hook detection mechanism that scans `/proc/self/maps` for Frida-related libraries and terminates the process via `Process.killProcess()` if any are found.

subject: Internal Storage File with Sensitive Data
truth: Stop writing unencrypted sensitive data to internal storage files; use EncryptedSharedPreferences or Android Keystore to encrypt data at rest.
quote: the file is not encrypted and contains sensitive data (a password)

subject: Android missing input validation on IPC/URL scheme data
truth: Stop trusting data from IPC or URL schemes without validation; always validate input against an expected pattern or convert to expected types.
quote: input from IPC or URL schemes, a validation function should be created

subject: WebView JavaScript execution
truth: Stop enabling JavaScript in WebViews unless absolutely necessary; setJavaScriptEnabled(true) expands the attack surface for XSS and injection.
quote: JavaScript is disabled by default for WebViews and must be explicitly enabled

subject: iOS immutable let for key material prevents secure wiping
truth: Stop using 'let' for key-holding data structures; use 'var' inside a temporary struct so the key material can be explicitly wiped from memory.
quote: If the keyword `let` is used, then you create an immutable structure which is harder to wipe from memory.

subject: iOS rolling own crypto instead of using Apple CryptoKit or CommonCrypto
truth: Stop implementing custom cryptographic algorithms or rolling your own crypto; use Apple's CryptoKit or CommonCrypto which have been validated.
quote: We recommend not to roll your own crypto, nor to implement known cryptographic functions yourself.

subject: Error handling - generic error responses for REST APIs
truth: Use RFC 7807 Problem Details for HTTP APIs to return generic 5xx errors without implementation details, and log all error details server-side.
quote: use 5xx errors only to indicate responses to requests that it cannot fulfill, but not provide any content as part of the response that would reveal implementation details
source: OWASP CS: Error Handling Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Microservice API definition with security schemes
truth: Define security schemes (scopes, API keys) in every microservice API definition (e.g., OpenAPI spec) instead of leaving endpoints unprotected.
quote: It is advisable to define used security scheme, e.g. define scopes or API keys needed to invoke dedicated endpoint
source: OWASP CS: Microservices based Security Arch Doc Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: RAG source attribution failure
truth: If source attribution cannot be generated, block the response — an unattributed response in a regulated environment is a compliance violation.
quote: An unattributed response in a regulated environment is a compliance violation.
source: OWASP CS: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Missing Cache-Control: no-store on API responses
truth: Include Cache-Control: no-store in all API responses consumed by browser clients to prevent sensitive information from being cached or stored.
quote: Providing `no-store` indicates that any caches of any kind (private or shared) should not store the response that contains the header.
source: OWASP CS: REST Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Rails security scanning
truth: Use static analysis tools like Brakeman or Bearer to identify security issues in Rails applications instead of relying on manual review alone.
quote: Use [brakeman](https://brakemanscanner.org/), an open source code analysis tool for Rails applications, to identify many potential issues.
source: OWASP CS: Ruby on Rails Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MCP server tool descriptions as injection surface
truth: Review tool descriptions for hidden instructions; tool descriptions are part of the agent's context and can contain prompt injection payloads.
quote: Review tool descriptions for hidden instructions. Tool descriptions are part of the agent's context and can contain prompt injection payloads.
source: OWASP CS: Secure Coding with AI Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: CSS files accessible via forced browsing across roles
truth: Ensure CSS files are access-controlled per role; if a Student can access AdministratorStyling.CSS via forced browsing, log an intrusion alert.
quote: If an authenticated user with the **Student** Role tries to access `AdministratorStyling.CSS` through forced browsing, an alert that an intrusion is occurring should be recorded.
source: OWASP CS: Securing Cascading Style Sheets Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Lucee XML external entities before 5.4.2
truth: Set this.xmlFeatures with externalGeneralEntities:false, secure:true, and disallowDoctypeDecl:true in Application.cfc to disable XXE in Lucee.
quote: you can disable support for XML external entities by adding the following to your Application.cfc
source: OWASP CS: XML External Entity Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Separate CSPRNGs for public vs private values
truth: Use separate CSPRNGs to generate public values (ClientHello/ServerHello random) and private keying material to prevent state recovery attacks.
quote: to generate public and private values.

subject: Casting char* to wchar_t* yields incorrect termination
truth: Stop casting byte strings to wide-character strings; the cast yields strings that are not correctly terminated and can cause buffer overruns.
quote: This cast might yield strings that are not correctly terminated; including potential buffer overruns

subject: BinaryFormatter deserialization of delegates
truth: Stop deserializing delegate objects with BinaryFormatter; use a safe serialization approach that does not allow arbitrary type instantiation.
quote: Deserializing a delegate object may result in remote code execution, when an attacker can control the serialized data.

subject: DataSet/DataTable deserialization
truth: Stop using DataSet and DataTable to deserialize untrusted XML payloads; use a safer serialization library or apply strict type allow-listing.
quote: DataSet and DataTable are in general not safe when populated with untrusted input

subject: SQL query construction in Rust
truth: Do not use format!() to embed user input into SQL strings; use prepared statements with ? placeholders and .bind() for parameterized queries.
quote: You should use these features to build queries, rather than string concatenation or similar methods.

subject: readlink() without null termination
truth: Always null-terminate the buffer after readlink() by setting buf[return_value] = '\0', or ensure the buffer size accounts for the terminator.
quote: does not null terminate

subject: Absolute Path Traversal in Java
truth: Always validate that the resolved path stays within a restricted base directory; do not directly use external input to construct a File path.
quote: does not properly neutralize absolute path sequences

subject: Command Injection via strcat into system() call
truth: Never concatenate user input directly into a command string passed to system(); use execve() with separate arguments or library APIs instead.

subject: WebViewClient URL loading handlers without validation
truth: Stop overriding shouldOverrideUrlLoading and shouldInterceptRequest without validating the URL's host, scheme, and path against an allowlist.
quote: neither handler validates the requested URL

subject: Custom overlay protection via onFilterTouchEventForSecurity
truth: Stop ignoring the FLAG_WINDOW_IS_OBSCURED flag on touch events; override onFilterTouchEventForSecurity and return false when the flag is set.
quote: // and checks FLAG_WINDOW_IS_OBSCURED to implement a custom security policy.

subject: iOS Keychain biometric ACL flag
truth: Stop using kSecAccessControlUserPresence for sensitive data; use kSecAccessControlBiometryCurrentSet or kSecAccessControlBiometryAny instead.
quote: The test fails because the output shows the runtime use of `SecAccessControlCreateWithFlags(..., kSecAccessControlUserPresence)` which allows for a fallback to passcode authentication.

subject: exit(0) on hook detection before crypto
truth: Do not call exit(0) when hooking is detected; attackers bypass the detection and the exit is never reached, giving a false sense of security.

subject: iOS error logging with dump() and Thread.callStackSymbols exposes internal error structure and stack traces
truth: Stop using dump() on error objects and logging Thread.callStackSymbols; these expose internal error structure and stack traces in the binary.

subject: Swift debug logging without conditional compilation guards
truth: Guard all debug print statements behind a custom compilation condition like DEBUG_LOGGING or MSTG_DEBUG set in Active Compilation Conditions.
quote: Swift 3 uses conditional compilation blocks based on the defined conditions

subject: CSRF token expiration with timestamps
truth: Stop including timestamps in CSRF tokens for expiration; CSRF tokens are not access tokens and should be tied to the session, not time-based.

subject: global variable declarations via id/name attributes
truth: Do not rely on id or name attributes of HTML elements to define global variables; use explicit variable declarations with let, const, or var.

subject: WCF security modes
truth: Use at least two security modes for WCF bindings — combine message security with transport security (SSL) via TransportWithMessageCredential.
quote: Use at least two security modes for your binding. Message security includes security provisions in the headers. Transport security means use of SSL.

subject: Reverse Tabnabbing via target=_blank
truth: Stop opening links with target=_blank without rel='noopener noreferrer'; add rel='noopener noreferrer' to every link that opens a new window.

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: IaC resource tagging
truth: Tag all cloud assets during IaC provisioning; untagged assets become ghost resources that cause drift, billing waste, and observability loss.
quote: untagged assets are most likely to result in ghost resources that make it difficult to detect, visualize, and gain observability within the cloud environment

subject: session tokens removed from requests during testing

subject: rogue CA certificate from trusted CA

quote: An attacker is able to acquire a rogue digital certificate from a trusted certificate authority (CA) in the name of the victim site
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SAML KeyInfo from untrusted document elements
truth: Ignore any KeyInfo elements in the SAML document; obtain signing keys directly from the identity provider via StaticKeySelector or local JKS.

subject: PR content passed unsanitized to CI agents

subject: SHA-1 signature algorithms in TLS 1.3
truth: Do not include rsa_pkcs1_sha1 or ecdsa_sha1 in supported signature algorithms except for backward compatibility; these are legacy algorithms.

subject: CertificateVerify signature verification failure handling
truth: Do not silently ignore CertificateVerify signature failures; MUST terminate the handshake with a decrypt_error alert on verification failure.
quote: If the verification fails, the receiver MUST terminate the handshake with a "decrypt_error" alert.

subject: Password-based key encryption for JWTs
truth: Passwords should only be used to perform key encryption, not content encryption, and even then they are still subject to brute-force attacks.
quote: passwords should only be used to perform key encryption, rather than content encryption

subject: Referer header sent from secure to insecure
truth: A user agent MUST NOT send a Referer header field in an unsecured HTTP request if the referring resource was accessed with a secure protocol.
quote: A user agent MUST NOT send a Referer header field in an unsecured HTTP request if the referring resource was accessed with a secure protocol.

subject: Trusted Platform Module (TPM) 2.0
truth: Use TPM 2.0 (ISO/IEC 11889:2015) for cryptographic key storage and device identity; TPM 1.2 is legacy and should not be used for new systems.
quote: TPM 2.0 Library Specification

subject: MAC with multiple receivers does not authenticate source
truth: When a MAC key is shared among more than two entities, the receiver cannot cryptographically prove which specific entity originated the data.
quote: if the data and MAC are sent to more than one entity

subject: Symmetric key wrapping without integrity protection
truth: Use approved key wrapping methods that provide both confidentiality and integrity protection for keys; do not encrypt keys without integrity.
quote: Key wrapping is a method used to provide confidentiality and integrity protection for keys

subject: Password-derived keys
truth: Do not use password-derived keys for most applications; they are suitable only for electronic storage (e.g., disk encryption) per SP 800-132.

subject: Database exception exposure via HttpResponse.Write

subject: Client-supplied IP in security check

subject: Dot as literal vs wildcard in regex

subject: Re-authentication for sensitive features

subject: authorization vs authentication distinction
truth: Do NOT assume authentication equals authorization; always verify that an authenticated user is approved for the specific action or resource.

subject: ignoring snprintf return value

subject: Django safe filter and mark_safe

subject: Session fixation prevention

subject: open redirect via user-supplied URL

subject: HS256 with human-memorizable passwords

subject: Certificate key usage separation for signature vs. key establishment
truth: Do not reuse the same certificate for both digital signatures and key establishment; use separate certificates with distinct key-usage bits.
quote: usage bits in a certificate indicate the purpose for which the public key is intended to be

subject: Certificate revocation warning ignored
truth: Do not ignore certificate revocation warnings; ignoring them means accepting the risk that the certificate's private key may be compromised.
quote: This warning must not be taken lightly

subject: Combined algorithm security strength is the weakest link
truth: When algorithms of different strengths are used together, the security provided is the strength of the weakest algorithm in the combination.
quote: the security provided by the combination of algorithms is the strength associated with the algorithm with the lowest security strength

subject: Out-of-band verifier identifying key storage
truth: Do not store the authenticator's identifying key itself; use a verification method such as an approved hash function or proof of possession.
quote: SHALL NOT store the identifying key itself

subject: Diesel SQLite string deserialization with from_utf8_unchecked
truth: Stop using str::from_utf8_unchecked on sqlite3_value_text output; always validate UTF-8 with str::from_utf8 before constructing a Rust &str.
quote: Based on that we used `str::from_utf8_unchecked` to construct a Rust string slice without any additional UTF-8 checks in place.

subject: snprintf buffer size mismatch
truth: Stop passing a size argument to snprintf that is larger than the actual buffer; always match the size argument to the true buffer capacity.
quote: the size argument to snprintf is larger than the actual size of the buffer

subject: strcpy into undersized buffer
truth: Stop using strcpy into a buffer allocated with malloc(strlen(x)) — it writes past the end because there is no room for the null terminator.
quote: buffer overflow attack, which could lead to anything from program crashes to malicious code execution

subject: world-writable file creation with open()
truth: Stop using open() with O_CREAT and default permissions; pass S_IWUSR | S_IRUSR as the mode argument to restrict access to the current user.
quote: uses more restrictive permissions: a combination of the standard Unix constants S_IWUSR and S_IRUSR

subject: ASP.NET X-Frame-Options header
truth: Set X-Frame-Options HTTP header to DENY or SAMEORIGIN in Web.config or unconditionally in Application_BeginRequest to prevent clickjacking.
quote: Web sites that do not specify the X-Frame-Options HTTP header may be vulnerable to UI redress attacks

subject: StringBuilder with untrusted data
truth: Stop appending unsanitized request parameters into StringBuilder for SQL queries; use parameterized queries or prepared statements instead.
quote: a request parameter is concatenated directly into a SQL query

subject: Java ReDoS from exponential backtracking regex
truth: Do not use regex patterns with nested repetitions and ambiguous alternatives; they cause exponential backtracking and enable ReDoS attacks.
quote: a string consisting of an odd number of underscores followed by some other character will cause the regular expression engine to run for an exponential amount of time before rejecting the input

subject: Modifying sanitized HTML before rendering
truth: Do not modify sanitized HTML values further before treating them as HTML; use a well-tested sanitization library and avoid post-processing.
quote: may in fact cause cross-site scripting vulnerabilities

subject: Using unsafe raw pointers when safe Rust types suffice
truth: Stop using unsafe blocks and raw pointers for returning values; rewrite using safe Rust types like Box to avoid lifetime issues altogether.
quote: If possible, rewrite the code using safe Rust types to avoid this kind of problem altogether

subject: Storing LDAP credentials in cleartext in properties files
truth: Never store passwords or secrets in cleartext in properties files or configuration files; use a secrets manager or encrypted configuration.
quote: stores sensitive information in cleartext within a resource that might be accessible to another control sphere
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Race condition in balance transfer without atomic transaction
truth: Wrap the entire read-check-update sequence on shared financial data in a single atomic transaction or critical section with proper locking.
quote: a timing window exists in which the shared resource can be modified by another code sequence operating concurrently
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Signal Handler Race Condition - Non-reentrant functions
truth: Only use reentrant functions within signal handlers; never call non-reentrant functions like syslog, free, or malloc from a signal handler.
quote: Only use reentrant functions within signal handlers
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: clean up resources in signal handler

subject: TOCTOU stat then use race
truth: Do not stat a file and then act on the cached stat data; instead, open the file and use fstat on the file descriptor to ensure consistency.
quote: - Implementation The most basic advice for TOCTOU vulnerabilities is to not perform a check before the use.
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: stat a file then check timestamp
check file modification time then update
stat file then act on metadata
get file stats then use them

subject: Firebase Analytics logEvent with sensitive user data
truth: Stop logging sensitive user data (PII, health info) via Firebase Analytics logEvent; use anonymized or non-identifiable parameters instead.
quote: sensitive data (`blood_type` parameter) is being sent to Firebase Analytics via the `logEvent` method

subject: Root detection via File.exists() on su binary paths
truth: Stop relying on File.exists() checks for su binary paths; root detection can be trivially bypassed by hooking File.<init> or File.exists().
quote: - **`java.io.File.<init>` calls for `su` path checks:**

subject: loadHTMLString with nil baseURL
truth: Set baseURL to nil when loading local HTML with loadHTMLString to give the document an opaque origin and prevent access to local resources.
quote: - For `WKWebView`, setting `baseURL` to `nil` gives the document an opaque origin.

subject: Deep link data rendered in WebView enabling XSS
truth: Stop rendering attacker-controlled deep link parameters in WebView without sanitization; validate all input from deep links to prevent XSS.
quote: the following deep link payload would trigger Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) within the context of the WebView

subject: iOS backup exclusion not set on sensitive files
truth: Stop storing sensitive data in files that are backed up by default; set NSURLIsExcludedFromBackupKey on any file containing sensitive data.
quote: avoid storing sensitive data in plaintext within any of the files or folders that are in the app's private directory or subdirectories

subject: Client-Side Authorization Checks
truth: Never rely on client-side access control checks; authorization must be enforced server-side, at the gateway, or using serverless functions.
quote: Developers must never rely on client-side access control checks
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: PIE and DEP flags dismissed by project Makefile
truth: Stop using project Makefiles that dismiss user-supplied CFLAGS/LDFLAGS; ensure PIE (-fPIE -pie) and DEP (-z noexecstack) flags are honored.
quote: Dismissing user settings combined with insecure out of the box settings (and not picking them up during auto-setup or auto-configure) means a program built with the following will likely have neither defense
source: OWASP CS: C-Based Toolchain Hardening Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: C/C++ assert calls abort() instead of breaking into debugger
truth: Replace standard assert() with a custom ASSERT that raises SIGTRAP so the debugger snaps at the point of first failure instead of aborting.
quote: Your assert will exchange auto-aborting behavior for auto-debugging behavior. The auto-debugging facility will ensure the debugger snaps when a problem is detected
source: OWASP CS: C-Based Toolchain Hardening Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: CSP as sole XSS defense
truth: Do not rely on CSP as the only defense against XSS; always follow proper output encoding and input validation first, then layer CSP on top.
quote: You must still follow good development practices such as the ones described in [Cross-Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet](Cross_Site_Scripting_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.md), and then deploy CSP on top
source: OWASP CS: Content Security Policy Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Java ObjectInputStream subclassing with resolveClass()
truth: Override ObjectInputStream.resolveClass() to restrict which classes are allowed to be deserialized instead of using a generic readObject().
quote: The general idea is to override [`ObjectInputStream.html#resolveClass()`](http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/io/ObjectInputStream.html#resolveClass(java.io.ObjectStreamClass)) in order to r
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: ASP.NET Web API error handler returning exception details
truth: Stop leaking exception details in error responses; log the exception server-side and return a generic JSON response with a generic message.
quote: Exception exception = context.Exception;
source: OWASP CS: Error Handling Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: GitHub Actions workflow approval for external contributors
truth: Enable 'Require approval for all external contributors' to ensure workflows triggered by pull requests from forks do not run automatically.
quote: This ensures that workflows triggered by pull requests from forks (i.e., users who are not members of the repository or organization) do not run automatically and therefore prevents untrusted code exe
source: OWASP CS: GitHub Actions Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: JWT token sidejacking — fingerprint context
truth: Bind the JWT to a hardened cookie containing a random fingerprint; store a SHA-256 hash of the fingerprint in the token, not the raw value.
quote: One way to prevent this is by adding a "user context" to the token.
source: OWASP CS: JSON Web Token Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: logging security events without standard vocabulary
truth: Use the standardized event vocabulary (AUTHN, AUTHZ, etc.) consistently across all applications so monitoring teams can key on these terms.
quote: specific keywords which, when applied consistently across software, would allow groups to simply monitor for these events terms across all applications
source: OWASP CS: Application Logging Vocabulary Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Tenant ID from request header
truth: Stop trusting client-supplied tenant IDs from request headers; derive tenant from the authenticated session or verified JWT claims instead.
quote: Never trust client-supplied tenant IDs without validation
source: OWASP CS: Multi-Tenant Application Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MFA on sensitive actions
truth: Require MFA not only at login but also for sensitive actions like changing passwords, changing email, disabling MFA, and session elevation.
quote: it may also be appropriate to require MFA for performing sensitive actions
source: OWASP CS: Multifactor Authentication Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: redirect URI scheme
truth: Authorization Servers must not allow redirect URIs that use the http scheme except for native clients using Loopback Interface Redirection.
quote: Authorization Servers must not allow redirect URIs that use the "http" scheme except for native clients that use Loopback Interface Redirection.
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: API keys in URL query parameters
truth: Stop putting API keys, passwords, or security tokens in URL query parameters; transfer them in request headers or the request body instead.
quote: Passwords, security tokens, and API keys should not appear in the URL, as this can be captured in web server logs
source: OWASP CS: REST Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: DNS records pointing to deprovisioned cloud resources
truth: Stop leaving dangling DNS records when deprovisioning cloud resources; always remove the DNS record as part of the decommissioning process.
quote: Cloud resources are temporary, DNS records are persistent
source: OWASP CS: Subdomain Takeover Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: unvalidated redirects and forwards
truth: Do not allow the URL as user input for the destination; use server-side mapping of short names, IDs, or tokens to full target URLs instead.
quote: do not allow the URL as user input for the destination
source: OWASP CS: Unvalidated Redirects and Forwards Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: HSTS no user recourse for security warnings
truth: UAs must prevent users from 'clicking through' security warnings; halting connection attempts on secure transport exceptions is acceptable.
quote: UAs need to prevent users from "clicking through" security

subject: mix-up attack defense
truth: Clients interacting with multiple authorization servers MUST use the iss parameter per RFC 9207 or an alternative iss-based countermeasure.
quote: use the iss parameter as a countermeasure according to [RFC9207]

subject: OAuth authorization server redirect after credential submission
truth: Authorization servers MUST NOT use HTTP 307 redirect after credential submission; use 303 (See Other) instead to force POST-to-GET rewrite.
quote: MUST NOT use the HTTP 307 status code for redirection

subject: OAuth authorization server redirect after credential submission
truth: Only HTTP 303 unambiguously enforces rewriting POST to GET; 302 and other status codes may leak credentials in the POST body to the client.
quote: only the status code 303 unambiguously enforces rewriting the HTTP POST request to an HTTP GET request

subject: TLS client certificate validation
truth: When requesting client certificates, validate the certification path per Section 6 of RFC 5280 and check revocation status via OCSP or CRL.
quote: The client certificate shall be validated in accordance with the certification path validation rules specified in Section 6 of [19].

subject: Displaying authentication secret on locked device
truth: Stop displaying authentication secrets on locked out-of-band devices; the device SHOULD NOT display the authentication secret while locked.
quote: SHOULD NOT display the authentication secret while it is locked by the owner

subject: aes-gcm decrypt_in_place_detached exposes plaintext on tag failure
truth: After decrypt_in_place_detached returns an error, zeroize or re-encrypt the buffer to prevent plaintext exposure on authentication failure.
quote: the decrypted ciphertext (i.e. the correct plaintext) is exposed even if tag verification fails

subject: gix-path hard-coded fallback paths on Windows
truth: Stop using hard-coded fallback paths for finding git.exe on Windows; instead only search PATH or use a safe, validated discovery mechanism.
quote: When looking for Git for Windows so it can run it to report its paths, `gix-path` can be tricked into running another `git.exe` placed in an untrusted location by a limited user account.

subject: strcat with untrusted input
truth: Stop using strcat to concatenate untrusted user input into a buffer; use bounds-checked string functions or a safe string library instead.
quote: Using unsanitized untrusted data in an external API can cause a variety of security issues

subject: untrusted data to external API (strcat into SQL query)
truth: Stop concatenating untrusted data read via fgets directly into SQL query strings via strcat; use parameterized queries or proper escaping.
quote: buffer will be executed as an SQL query, potentially leading to an SQL injection vulnerability

subject: Undefined behavior from calling mutating functions in the same expression
truth: Stop calling functions that modify the same resource as arguments in a single expression; assign each result to a separate variable first.
quote: undefined behavior when calling functions to change the `buff` variable

subject: LDAP encoding with AntiXSS library
truth: Use Encoder.LdapDistinguishedNameEncode for DN components and Encoder.LdapFilterEncode for search filter values to prevent LDAP injection.
quote: Organization name is encoded before being used in DN

subject: ThreadDeath exception in Java
truth: Do not rely on catching ThreadDeath to clean up after Thread.stop(); the exception can be thrown from too many locations to handle safely.
quote: it is impractical to catch all such cases

subject: Jakarta Expression Language (EL) injection via JUEL
truth: Stop concatenating untrusted user input directly into EL expressions; validate input against a strict allowlist pattern before evaluation.
quote: If an expression is built using attacker-controlled data, and then evaluated, it may allow the attacker to run arbitrary code.

subject: main method in Java EE web component
truth: Having a main method in the Java EE application increases the attack surface that an attacker can exploit to attack the application logic.
quote: Having a main method in the Java EE application increases the attack surface that an attacker can exploit to attack the application logic

subject: HTML tag name case sensitivity
truth: Stop writing case-sensitive regex for HTML tag names; HTML tag names can contain upper case characters and regex must be case-insensitive.
quote: HTML tag names can contain upper case characters

subject: Ruby backslash escaping with String#sub
truth: Stop using String#sub to backslash-escape meta-characters; it fails to escape the backslash itself and only replaces the first occurrence.
quote: backslash-escaping various meta-characters but not the backslash itself

subject: Rails CSRF null session vs exception
truth: Stop using protect_from_forgery without `with: :exception`; null-session is silent and may still allow CSRF if session parts are memoized.
quote: The default behaviour of this method is to null the session when an invalid CSRF token is provided. This may not be sufficient to avoid a CSRF vulnerability - for example if parts of the session are memoized

subject: strconv.Atoi result cast to int32/int16
truth: Stop casting strconv.Atoi results to int32 or int16 without overflow checking; use strconv.ParseInt with the appropriate bit size instead.
quote: Converting strconv.Atoi result to int32/int16
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: The code_good example is just `fmt.Println(bigValue)` — it never demonstrates strconv.ParseInt with a bit size as the truth prescribes, so a model shown this pa

subject: Login failure messages revealing whether username or password is incorrect
truth: Return a generic 'Login Failed' message for all authentication failures; do not distinguish between invalid username and invalid password.
quote: Login Failed - incorrect password

subject: CSRF protection missing on form submission
truth: Include a unique, unpredictable anti-CSRF token as a hidden field in every form and validate it server-side before processing the request.
quote: Generate a unique nonce for each form, place the nonce into the form, and verify the nonce upon receipt of the form.
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: WebView loadDataWithBaseURL with file:// scheme
truth: Stop using file:// as the base URL in loadDataWithBaseURL; it gives the page a non-opaque origin allowing file:// XHR requests to succeed.
quote: Using a base URL with the file:// scheme gives the page a non‑opaque origin (using content:/// works the same way).

subject: Android debuggable flag in production
truth: Stop shipping release builds with android:debuggable="true"; it exposes debug interfaces and allows arbitrary code injection via debugger.
quote: The test case fails because the `android:debuggable` attribute is explicitly set to `true`.

subject: Universal Link Handler Input Validation
truth: Always validate and sanitize query parameters from universal links by converting them to the expected type and checking bounds before use.
quote: The handler reads the `amount` query parameter from the universal link's `webpageURL` for the `/transfer` path and uses it directly as a string, without converting it to a numeric type or checking its

subject: committing .npmrc and lockfiles to source control
truth: Commit package manager configuration files (e.g., .npmrc) and lockfiles to source control so CI/CD enforces pinned, verified dependencies.
quote: Finally, regardless of the platform used, ensure the file(s) responsible for controlling these settings (such as `.npmrc` in node environments), is committed to source control and accessible in the CI
source: OWASP CS: CI/CD Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: RAG document ingestion without integrity verification
truth: Hash every document at ingestion time (SHA-256 minimum) and verify the hash before retrieval; reject documents that fail integrity checks.
quote: Hash every document at ingestion time (SHA-256 minimum) and store the hash alongside the document metadata.
source: OWASP CS: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Access control checked only at ingestion, not retrieval
truth: Enforce access control checks at retrieval time, not just at ingestion time. Permissions may have changed since the document was ingested.
quote: Document-level permissions are checked at ingestion but not at retrieval time, allowing permission changes to be ignored.
source: OWASP CS: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: separate DB users per application
truth: Stop using the same owner/admin account across web applications; use different designated database user accounts for each web application.
quote: avoid using the same owner/admin account in the web applications to connect to the database
source: OWASP CS: SQL Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

associative: single db user for all apps

subject: storing secrets in immutable String objects
truth: Do not use immutable structures such as Strings to store secrets; use byte arrays or char arrays where memory can be directly overwritten.
quote: do not use immutable structures such as Strings to store secrets, since it is impossible to force them to be garbage collected
source: OWASP CS: Secrets Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: XML content validation against bombs and XXE
truth: Validate XML input against malformed entities, XML Bomb attacks, external entity attacks (XXE), and use a strong allowlist for all inputs.
quote: Like any web application, web services need to validate input before consuming it
source: OWASP CS: Web Service Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: implicit trust inside network perimeter
truth: Stop trusting users based on location, role, or tenure inside the network; verify every action regardless of where the request originates.
quote: no implicit trust
source: OWASP CS: Zero Trust Architecture Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SSL 3.0 is not approved for TLS
truth: Stop using SSL 3.0; it is not approved for protecting Federal information because it relies on non-NIST-approved cryptographic algorithms.
quote: is not approved for use in the protection of Federal information

subject: OpenSSL X509 extension builders parse input as mini-language allowing arbitrary file reads
truth: Stop passing user-controlled strings to SubjectAlternativeName::new or ExtendedKeyUsage::other; use the type-safe builder methods instead.
quote: parses all input using an OpenSSL mini-language which can perform arbitrary file reads

subject: Quoting executable paths in lpCommandLine
truth: When passing NULL for lpApplicationName, always wrap the executable path in lpCommandLine with quotation marks to prevent path injection.
quote: use quotation marks around the executable path in lpCommandLine

subject: relying on umask for file permissions
truth: Do not assume the user has set a restrictive umask; always pass explicit restrictive permissions (S_IWUSR | S_IRUSR) when creating files.
quote: a program should not assume that the user will set an umask, and should still set restrictive permissions by default

subject: Input validation for code injection
truth: If dynamic code generation is unavoidable, validate user input against a whitelist to prevent arbitrary code from appearing in the input.
quote: the user input should be validated to prevent arbitrary code from appearing in the input. For example, a whitelist may be used to ensure that the input is limited to an acceptable range of values.

subject: DataSet/DataTable default type limitations
truth: Do not rely on DataSet/DataTable default type limitations as a security boundary; they are not sufficient against untrusted XML payloads.
quote: DataSet and DataTable do impose default limitations on the types that are allowed to be present while deserializing XML payloads

subject: Using prepared statements even without user input in Java
truth: Always use prepared statements or query parameters for supplying parameter values, whether or not parameters are traceable to user input.
quote: It is good practice to use prepared statements (in SQL) or query parameters (in the Java Persistence Query Language) for supplying parameter values to a query, whether or not any of the parameters are directly traceable to user input

subject: Storing credentials in java.util.Properties that may be persisted
truth: Encrypt sensitive information before putting it into a java.util.Properties or any heap data structure that may be written to disk later.
quote: encrypt information before it is put into a heap data structure (such as Java.util.Properties) that may be written to disk later

subject: Android BroadcastReceiver accepting system intents from third-party apps
truth: A BroadcastReceiver registered for system intents must verify the intent action because third-party apps can send explicit intents to it.
quote: a BroadcastReceiver that is registered to receive system intents is still able to receive intents from a third-party application, so it should check that the intent received has the expected action

subject: Rust path traversal canonicalization check
truth: Do canonicalize the resolved path and verify it stays within the intended directory; do not rely on simple prefix checks on the raw path.
quote: ensure that the path stays within the public folder

subject: Range to NSRange conversion using incompatible lengths
truth: Stop manually converting between Range and NSRange using incompatible length values; use the appropriate initializer to convert directly.
quote: If you need to convert between Range and NSRange, do so directly using the appropriate initializer. Do not attempt to use incompatible length and offset values to accomplish conversion.

subject: DatagramPacket checksum validation
truth: Always validate the integrity check value of received DatagramPackets according to the protocol specification before processing the data.
quote: Improper validation of checksums before use results in an unnecessary risk

subject: Hardcoded credentials in connection strings
truth: Stop embedding plaintext passwords in connection strings; use integrated security, encrypted config sections, or a secrets vault instead.

subject: JSP expression tag with request parameter
truth: Always escape request parameters with a context-aware encoder like ESAPI or JSTL fn:escapeXml() before outputting in JSP expression tags.
quote: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

subject: Hard-coded database credentials in JDBC connection
truth: Never hard-code database credentials in source code; store them in a strongly-protected, encrypted configuration file or secrets manager.
quote: hard-coded credentials
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Frida runtime detection of multi-purpose asymmetric key misuse
truth: Stop ignoring WARNING messages about keys used for multiple conflicting purposes; enforce single-purpose key separation at the API level.
quote: WARNING: This key is used for multiple, conflicting purposes: encryption/decryption and sign/verify

subject: WebView javaScriptEnabled with file:// base URL
truth: Stop enabling javaScriptEnabled when loading content with a file:// base URL; it allows XSS to exfiltrate local files via XMLHttpRequest.
quote: /* `javaScriptEnabled` is required for the attacker's script to even execute.

subject: HMAC integrity check silently returns empty string on exception
truth: Stop returning empty string when HMAC computation fails; propagate the error or handle it securely so integrity is not silently bypassed.
quote: catch (e: NoSuchAlgorithmException) {

subject: Jailbreak Detection Bypass via Environment Variable Checks
truth: Stop checking DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES and similar environment variables for jailbreak detection; these are trivially bypassed by attackers.
quote: Check 3: Suspicious environment variables

subject: Ignoring canEvaluatePolicy return value
truth: Stop ignoring the return value of canEvaluatePolicy; check the Boolean result and handle the error to avoid silent authentication bypass.
quote: return LAContext().canEvaluatePolicy(.deviceOwnerAuthentication, error: nil)

subject: Unprotected /Passwords path in ContentProvider
truth: Stop exposing sensitive data paths in content providers without permission; protect all paths with read/write permissions, not just some.
quote: there are actually two paths, "/Keys" and "/Passwords", and the latter is not being protected in the manifest and is therefore vulnerable

subject: Forgotten identities in CI/CD environments
truth: Maintain an accurate, comprehensive, and up-to-date inventory of identities to prevent forgotten identities from becoming attack vectors.
quote: the "forgotten" identity can be the vector an attacker users to compromise a CI/CD system.

subject: unencrypted database connections
truth: Stop using unencrypted database connections; configure the database to only allow encrypted connections with TLSv1.2+ and modern ciphers.
quote: Configure the database to only allow encrypted connections
source: OWASP CS: Database Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SBOM format choice
truth: Use standard formats (SPDX or CycloneDX) and publish at least one machine-readable SBOM per release; avoid ad-hoc or proprietary formats.
quote: Use standard formats (SPDX or CycloneDX) and publish at least one machine-readable SBOM per release.
source: OWASP CS: Dependency Graph & SBOM Best Practices Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Legacy code injection flaws are easy to discover in code review
truth: Stop relying on black-box testing alone; examine source code to find injection flaws since they are easy to discover when reviewing code.
quote: Injection flaws are easy to discover when examining code, but more difficult via testing.
