subject: logging PII without considering data privacy
truth: Treat all optional fields as subject to data privacy review; consider the value of logged fields versus responsibility as a data steward.
quote: All fields logged after event type should be considered optional. Businesses implementing this logging method should consider value of the fields being logged vs. responsibility as a data steward for the data logged.
source: OWASP CS: Application Logging Vocabulary Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Missing Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none' on API responses
truth: Include Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none' in API responses consumed by browser clients to prevent clickjacking via framing.
quote: Providing `frame-ancestors 'none'` prevents any domain from framing the response returned by the API call.

subject: certificate domain name attribute
truth: Place the primary FQDN in the subjectAlternativeName (SAN) attribute, not only in the commonName (CN), because modern browsers ignore CN.

source: OWASP CS: Transport Layer Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: algorithm transition security
truth: Data encrypted with a once-secure algorithm may have been captured and later decrypted when the algorithm is no longer considered secure.
quote: considered secure, it may have been captured (by an ad versary) at that time and later decrypted by that

subject: DSA legacy verification with sub-2048-bit L
truth: DSA parameters with L < 2048 bits are disallowed for signature generation but may continue in legacy use for signature verification only.
quote: key lengths of 512 and 1024 bits, may continue to be used for signature verification but not for signature generation

subject: 80-bit security strength
truth: Stop using algorithms and keys providing 80 bits of security strength for applying cryptographic protection; they are no longer approved.
quote: a security strength of 80 bits was previously approved as well. Since it is no longer considered to provide adequate protection, the use of algorithms and keys providing a security strength of 80 bits are no longer approved for applying cryptographic protection

subject: TLS 1.0, 1.1, 1.2 certificate transparency
truth: Do not assume Certificate Transparency (Signed Certificate Timestamps) applies only to TLS 1.3; it applies to TLS 1.0, 1.1, 1.2, and 1.3.
quote: Applies to TLS versions: 1.0, 1.1, 1.2, 1.3

subject: Client public-key size checking
truth: The server shall examine the public key and algorithm in the client certificate to verify acceptable key size and cryptographic strength.
quote: The only direct mechanism for a server to check whether the key size and algorithms presented in a client public-key certificate are acceptable is for the server to examine the public key and algorithm

subject: Signature algorithm for TLS client certificates
truth: Stop using SHA-1 or MD5 for certificate signatures; use sha256WithRSAEncryption, id-RSASSA-PSS, ecdsa-with-SHA256, or id-dsa-with-sha256.
quote: sha256WithRSAEncryption {1 2 840 113549 1 1 11}, or stronger

subject: ECB cipher mode
truth: Stop using ECB mode; encrypted messages are vulnerable to replay attacks because the same plaintext always produces the same ciphertext.
quote: Data is encrypted the same way every time meaning the same plaintext input will always produce the same ciphertext

subject: JPA named query with positional parameter in Java
truth: Use @NamedQuery with positional parameters (?1) and setParameter(position, value) instead of concatenating user input into JPQL strings.
quote: These versions are immune to injection attacks, because any special characters in the environment variable or user-supplied value are not given any special treatment.

subject: Client IP from X-Forwarded-For header
truth: Stop trusting the first entry of X-Forwarded-For; use the last entry if behind a reverse proxy, or use request.getRemoteAddr() directly.
quote: Attackers can forge the value of these identifiers to bypass a ban-list

subject: Paramiko SSH host key verification
truth: Stop using AutoAddPolicy or WarningPolicy; set_missing_host_key_policy must use RejectPolicy or a custom policy that verifies host keys.
quote: Paramiko call with policy set to automatically trust the unknown host key

subject: Using path.Join instead of filepath.Join for archive extraction
truth: Stop using path.Join for archive extraction paths; use filepath.Join and then validate the resolved path is within the target directory.
quote: filePath := path.Join(destPath, f.Name)

subject: Truncation of floating-point to integer in Java
truth: Do not silently truncate floating-point values to integers; use Math.round(), Math.floor(), or Math.ceil() and validate the range first.
quote: data can be omitted or translated in a way that produces unexpected values
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Returning negative int as unsigned int in C
truth: Do not return a negative int value from a function declared to return unsigned int; validate the value is non-negative before returning.
quote: data can be omitted or translated in a way that produces unexpected values
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: OS Command Injection via string concatenation
truth: Never concatenate user input directly into a shell command string; use parameterized APIs or escape/validate input against an allowlist.
quote: does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: run a shell command with user input
build a command from form data
run ls with a filename from user

subject: LDAP query string concatenation in Java
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into LDAP search filters; use a whitelist of acceptable inputs or encode special LDAP characters.
quote: does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: query Active Directory with user input
look up a user in LDAP

subject: Implicit Intent with Sensitive Extras
truth: Stop using implicit intents for internal communication that carries sensitive data; use explicit intents that name the target component.
quote: The test case fails because the app attaches sensitive extras (`user_id` and `session_token`) to an implicit intent and dispatches it without constraining the recipient.

subject: FileProvider oversharing via path="." exposes all filesDir files
truth: Stop using path="." in FileProvider file_paths.xml; this grants access to every file under filesDir, not just the intended subdirectory.
quote: Because `file_paths.xml` declares `path="."`, the `FileProvider` will accept any file under `filesDir` — not just the intended `reports/` subdirectory.

subject: Logging sensitive PII in Android Logcat
truth: Stop logging sensitive PII (email, phone, credit card) to Android Logcat via Log.d/Log.e where it can be read by any app with READ_LOGS.
quote: Log.d("HTTP_SUCCESS", "Successfully authenticated.")

subject: Android decryption via Cipher.doFinal without hook detection
truth: Stop using Cipher.doFinal for decryption without runtime integrity checks; Frida can hook the return value to extract plaintext secrets.
quote: The first `doFinal` call reveals the sensitive API key in plaintext as the input parameter during encryption and the second call reveals it as the return value during decryption.

subject: Logging secrets with Android Log API
truth: Stop logging sensitive data (passwords, keys, IVs) via Log.v/i/w/d/e/wtf; use a redaction layer or remove log statements before release.
quote: The snippet contains many calls to logging APIs which are used to print out secrets such as passwords or IVs.

subject: iOS app logs session tokens via debugPrint
truth: Stop logging authentication tokens and session secrets with debugPrint in production builds; use conditional compilation or remove them.
quote: debugPrint("✅ [DEBUG] Authentication successful - Session token generated: \(generateMockToken())")

subject: Android ContentProvider SQL Injection via string concatenation in query()
truth: Stop concatenating URI path segments directly into SQL queries; use parameterized queries with ? placeholders and selectionArgs instead.
quote: SQL Injection when providing an ID

subject: Sensitive data in notification content
truth: Stop passing sensitive data to setContentTitle or setContentText; notifications are visible on the lock screen and in notification logs.
quote: `setContentTitle` or `setContentText` from `NotificationCompat.Builder`.

subject: iOS CCCryptorCreate missing return status check
truth: Stop ignoring the CCCryptorStatus return value; always check that CCCryptorCreate returns kCCSuccess before using the cryptor reference.
quote: You can then compare all the `enum` types to determine which algorithm, padding, and key material is used.

subject: iOS ATS disabled globally via NSAllowsArbitraryLoads
truth: Stop disabling App Transport Security globally; set NSAllowsArbitraryLoads to false and use specific ATS exceptions only when necessary.
quote: Disabling ATS means that unsecured HTTP connections are allowed

subject: iOS URL scheme hijacking via CFBundleURLSchemes
truth: Stop assuming your custom URL scheme is unique; if more than one app registers the same scheme, the system may route to a malicious app.
quote: If more than one third-party app registers to handle the same URL scheme, there is currently no process for determining which app will be given that scheme.

subject: CI/CD pipeline security risk awareness
truth: Do not treat CI/CD pipelines as low-risk automation; treat them as high-privilege attack surfaces requiring dedicated security controls.
quote: CI/CD pipelines are also an appealing target for malicious hackers, and their security cannot be ignored
source: OWASP CS: CI/CD Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: storing encryption keys in environment variables
truth: Avoid storing keys in environment variables, as they can be accidentally exposed through functions like phpinfo() or /proc/self/environ.
quote: - Avoid storing keys in environment variables, as these can be accidentally exposed through functions such as [phpinfo()](https://www.php.net/manual/en/function.phpinfo.php) or through the `/proc/self
source: OWASP CS: Cryptographic Storage Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Storing uploaded files inside the webroot with read access
truth: Store uploaded files on a different host or outside the webroot; if inside webroot, set write-only permissions and restrict read access.
quote: Store the files on a **different host**, which allows for complete segregation of duties between the application serving the user, and the host handling file uploads and their storage.
source: OWASP CS: File Upload Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: LLM security logging and monitoring
truth: Configure comprehensive logging for all LLM interactions and set up monitoring and alerting for suspicious patterns and usage anomalies.
quote: Configure comprehensive logging for all LLM interactions
source: OWASP CS: LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: caching authorization decisions at microservice level
truth: Do not cache authorization decisions at the microservice level; cache the authorization policy instead to avoid applying outdated rules.
quote: caching authorization decisions may lead to applying outdated authorization rules and access control violations
source: OWASP CS: Microservices Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: runtime integrity checks for mobile app
truth: Implement runtime anti-tampering controls that verify app signatures, detect debugging/hooking, and check for rooted/jailbroken devices.
quote: Implement runtime anti-tampering controls
source: OWASP CS: Mobile Application Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: proprietary TOTP tokens
truth: Stop using proprietary/vendor-specific TOTP variants (e.g., Symantec); use standardized TOTP so users can install any authenticator app.
quote: This should be avoided in favour of a standards-based approach.
source: OWASP CS: Multifactor Authentication Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SQL Injection prevention via parameterized queries
truth: Stop building SQL queries with string concatenation or interpolation; use parameterized queries (prepared statements) with placeholders.
quote: SQL Injection is best prevented through the use of [*parameterized queries*](SQL_Injection_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.md).
source: OWASP CS: Query Parameterization Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

associative: building SQL string with user input

subject: Session ID Name Fingerprinting
truth: Change the default session ID name of the web development framework to a generic name such as 'id' to prevent technology fingerprinting.
quote: It is recommended to change the default session ID name of the web development framework to a generic name, such as `id`.
source: OWASP CS: Session Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: DTD entity expansion (Billion Laughs / XXE)
truth: Disable DTD processing entirely in XML parsers to prevent entity expansion attacks (Billion Laughs) and external entity injection (XXE).
quote: When an XML parser tries to resolve the external entities included within the following code, it will cause the application to start consuming all of the available memory until the process crashes.
source: OWASP CS: XML Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: permanent admin rights
truth: Stop granting permanent admin rights with periodic reviews; use just-in-time access with automatic expiration and continuous monitoring.
quote: Just-in-time access with automatic expiration and continuous monitoring
source: OWASP CS: Zero Trust Architecture Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Zero Trust implementation pace
truth: Do not rush Zero Trust implementation in a few months; treat it as a multi-year journey with careful planning and phased implementation.
quote: Moving too fast
source: OWASP CS: Zero Trust Architecture Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Deterministic Random Bit Generators (DRBG) without proper entropy
truth: Use NIST-approved Random Bit Generators (SP 800-90A) with proper entropy input; do not use weak or predictable random number generators.
quote: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators

subject: Same symmetric key for multiple purposes
truth: Do not reuse the same symmetric key for different cryptographic purposes (e.g., encryption and HMAC); generate a unique key per purpose.
quote: the same key can be used for multiple purposes when the same algorithm is used, but this is usually ill-advised

associative: same key for encrypt and mac
derive encryption and auth key from same

subject: SSL 3.0 CBC padding (POODLE)
truth: TLS implementations shall correctly decode CBC padding bytes to prevent POODLE-style attacks; do not reuse SSL 3.0 decoder code for TLS.
quote: TLS implementations shall correctly decode the CBC padding bytes

subject: OCSP stapling
truth: When the client requests certificate status, the server shall include OCSP-stapled certificate status via the CertificateStatus message.
quote: the client includes the Certificate Status Request (status_request) extension in the ClientHello message

subject: Out-of-band authenticator channel encryption
truth: Stop using unencrypted secondary channels for out-of-band authentication; the secondary channel SHALL be encrypted unless sent via PSTN.
quote: communication over the secondary channel SHALL be encrypted unless sent via the public switched telephone network (PSTN)

subject: Multi-factor cryptographic device post-authentication handling
truth: Do not require users to keep multi-factor cryptographic devices connected following authentication; users may forget to disconnect them.
quote: Do not require users to keep multi-factor cryptographic devices connected following authentication

subject: lettre sendmail transport argument injection
truth: Stop passing user-controlled email addresses directly as arguments to the sendmail command; use '--' to separate options from addresses.
quote: It was possible, using forged `to` addresses,

subject: fgets into fixed-size buffer without validation
truth: Stop using fgets to read untrusted data into a fixed buffer without validating or sanitizing the input before passing it to other APIs.
quote: input is read from fgets and then ultimately used in a call to the fputs external API

subject: Backslash handling in URL redirects
truth: Replace all backslashes with forward slashes before parsing a redirect URL, because some browsers treat backslashes as forward slashes.
quote: some browsers treat backslashes in URLs as forward slashes. To account for this, we replace all backslashes with forward slashes before parsing the URL and checking its hostname.

subject: Serializable objects containing cleartext sensitive data
truth: Always encrypt sensitive information inside serializable or marshallable objects, unless you are certain they will never be serialized.
quote: Objects that are serializable or marshallable should also always contain encrypted information unless you are certain that they are not ever going to be serialized

subject: JHipster password reset token generation using predictable PRNG
truth: Stop using RandomStringUtils for security-sensitive tokens; initialize a static SecureRandom and pass it to RandomStringUtils.random().
quote: attackers can request a password reset token and use this to predict the value of future reset tokens

subject: No input validation on reflective class loading
truth: Stop reflective class loading without validation; add an allowlist check before Class.forName to prevent arbitrary class instantiation.
quote: strictly verify the input to ensure that users can only instantiate classes or execute methods that ought to be allowed.

subject: Hardcoded hex-decoded import path
truth: Stop decoding hardcoded hex string literals and using them as import paths; use a normal require() with a plain string literal instead.
quote: hexadecimal constant decoded and interpreted as import path

subject: Django environ default constant secret
truth: Stop using a constant string as the default value in django-environ's Env() for SECRET_KEY; the default fallback is a hardcoded secret.
quote: following is not safe if there is default value in Env(..)

subject: File content loaded with .html_safe in ERB
truth: Stop using .html_safe when loading file content into script tags; ensure the file is trusted and cannot be modified by untrusted users.
quote: Care should taken in these cases to ensure both that the loaded files are trusted, and that the file cannot be modified by untrusted users.

subject: SSRF via user-controlled URL in reqwest::get
truth: Stop passing user input directly to reqwest::get; validate the input against an allowlist of known-safe URLs before making the request.
quote: Directly incorporating user input into an HTTP request without validating the input can facilitate server-side request forgery (SSRF) attacks

subject: Hard-coded LDAP credentials in properties file
truth: Stop hard-coding passwords in configuration files; store credentials in a strongly-protected, encrypted configuration file or database.
quote: The product contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or for outbound communication to external components.
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: PRNG seeding with current time
truth: Stop using System.currentTimeMillis() as a PRNG seed; use SecureRandom which self-seeds from a cryptographically secure entropy source.
quote: using a uniquely generated seed from a cryptographically secure source
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Returning int from unsigned int function in C
truth: Do not return a signed int value from a function declared to return unsigned int; ensure the return type matches or validate the range.
quote: data can be omitted or translated in a way that produces unexpected values
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: WebView allowFileAccess
truth: Stop enabling allowFileAccess; it allows the WebView to load local files from internal or external storage, enabling file exfiltration.
quote: * allows the WebView to load local files from the app's internal or external storage.

subject: Process.killProcess for anti-tampering response
truth: Stop using Process.killProcess() as an anti-tampering response; attackers can trivially hook killProcess itself to prevent termination.
quote: android.os.Process.killProcess(android.os.Process.myPid())

subject: Jailbreak Detection Bypass via Write-Access Test
truth: Stop using write-access tests to system paths for jailbreak detection; these are trivially bypassed by hooking or sandbox manipulation.
quote: Check 4: Write access to system paths

subject: iOS CCCrypt decryption without runtime hook detection
truth: Stop decrypting sensitive data with CCCrypt without runtime integrity checks; a Frida hook on CCCrypt extracts plaintext keys silently.
quote: The app decrypts sensitive data without detecting runtime hooks.

subject: UITextField spell checking default on non-secure fields
truth: Disable both autocorrection AND spell checking on UITextField for sensitive inputs; setting only autocorrection to .no is insufficient.
quote: Since `spellCheckingType` remains at its default value, this input is still eligible for keyboard caching and therefore fails the test.

subject: secure random number generation
truth: Use cryptographically secure PRNGs (CSPRNGs) for security-critical randomness; never use PRNGs like rand(), Math.random(), or Random().
quote: Pseudo-Random Number Generators (PRNG) provide low-quality randomness
source: OWASP CS: Cryptographic Storage Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: hard-coded encryption keys in source code
truth: Do not hard-code keys into the application source code; use secure storage mechanisms like HSMs, key vaults, or OS secure storage APIs.
quote: Do not hard-code keys into the application source code.
source: OWASP CS: Cryptographic Storage Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Kubernetes security context
truth: Set securityContext with readOnlyRootFilesystem and runAsNonRoot on every pod; do not run containers as root with writable filesystems.
quote: any attack that depends on installing software or writing to the file system will be stopped if you specify read-only root file systems
source: OWASP CS: Kubernetes Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MCP tool output fed back into LLM context unsanitized
truth: Treat every tool response as untrusted user input and sanitize it before feeding back into the LLM context to prevent prompt injection.
quote: - Treat every tool response as **untrusted user input** — sanitize before feeding back into the LLM context.
source: OWASP CS: MCP (Model Context Protocol) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MFA factor changes
truth: Require reauthentication with an existing enrolled factor before allowing MFA factor changes; do not rely solely on the active session.
quote: Require reauthentication with an existing enrolled factor before allowing changes
source: OWASP CS: Multifactor Authentication Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Framing protection against XS-Leaks via element ID focus
truth: Set Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors or X-Frame-Options to prevent your application from being embedded in cross-origin iframes.
quote: Setting up framing protection efficiently blocks the ability to embed your application in a frame on the attacker-controlled origin
source: OWASP CS: Cross-site leaks Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: per-session access granting
truth: Stop granting permanent access; grant access to resources on a per-session basis with short-lived sessions requiring re-authentication.
quote: Don't give permanent access to anything
source: OWASP CS: Zero Trust Architecture Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: atoi without overflow validation before arithmetic
truth: Validate user-controlled integer input against INT_MAX minus the operation magnitude before performing arithmetic to prevent overflow.
quote: Always guard against overflow in arithmetic operations on user-controlled data

subject: Persistent cookies with sensitive data
truth: Stop setting sensitive information in persistent cookies; use session cookies (no expiration) or short-lived cookies (≤5 min) instead.
quote: Do not put sensitive information in persistent cookies.

subject: DataSet/DataTable default type limitations
truth: Do not assume default type limitations in DataSet/DataTable make XML deserialization safe; they are still unsafe with untrusted input.
quote: DataSet and DataTable do impose default limitations on the types that are allowed to be present while deserializing XML payloads, DataSet and DataTable are in general not safe when populated with untrusted input

subject: Client-side request forgery via open redirect chaining
truth: Avoid user input in the hostname of the URL; pick the hostname from an allow-list instead of constructing it directly from user input.
quote: Avoid user input in the hostname of the URL. Pick the hostname from an allow-list instead of constructing it directly from user input.

subject: Rust SHA-2 family for passwords
truth: Stop using SHA-2 (SHA-224, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512) for password hashing; all SHA-2 variants are weak against offline brute forcing.
quote: All of MD5, SHA-1, SHA-2 and SHA-3 are weak against offline brute forcing, so they are not suitable for hashing passwords

subject: Mako template variable escaping
truth: Stop rendering Mako template variables without a filter; apply the 'h' filter for HTML context like ${ user_input |h } to prevent XSS.
quote: all input variables must be carefully escaped before use to prevent possible vulnerabilities to Cross Site Scripting (XSS) attacks

subject: Python 2 vs Python 3 input()
truth: Stop assuming input() is safe; in Python 2 it evaluates input as code — use raw_input() for Python 2 and only use input() in Python 3.
quote: On Python 2, use raw_input instead, input is safe in Python 3.

subject: text/template with user input
truth: Stop using text/template with user-controlled template strings or data; use html/template or escape user input with html.EscapeString.
quote: Server-side template injection via text/template

subject: Buffer underwrite via unchecked index decrement in while loop
truth: Validate that the index stays within the bounds of the buffer (>= 0) before accessing or writing to the buffer in a decrementing loop.
quote: All calculated values that are used as index or for pointer arithmetic should be validated to ensure that they are within an expected range.
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: remove spaces from end of string

subject: Unvalidated dimensions in malloc size calculation leading to integer overflow
truth: Validate that each dimension is positive and within bounds, and check that the multiplication does not overflow before calling malloc.
quote: does not validate or incorrectly validates that the quantity has the required properties
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: unvalidated numeric input from user attribute
truth: Validate that numeric input from user attributes is non-negative and within expected bounds before using it in financial calculations.
quote: does not validate or incorrectly validates that the input has the properties that are required to process the data safely
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Hard-coded cryptographic key in C
truth: Stop embedding hard-coded cryptographic keys in source code; store keys in a secure key management system or hardware security module.
quote: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Authorization check before file read in PHP
truth: Check authorization before performing the expensive file read operation, not after; move the isOwnerOf check before file_get_contents.
quote: consume more resources than their access level permits
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: WebView storing sensitive data in localStorage
truth: Stop storing sensitive tokens and PII in WebView localStorage via setItem; use Android Keystore or EncryptedSharedPreferences instead.
quote: localStorage.setItem('sensitive_token', 'SECRET_TOKEN_123456')

subject: Hardcoded RSA Private Key in iOS App
truth: Stop embedding hardcoded RSA private keys in source code; use the iOS Keychain to store and retrieve private keys securely at runtime.
quote: hardcoded RSA private key was found in the code

subject: File-based Content Provider vulnerable to directory traversal
truth: Stop exposing file-based content providers without path validation; restrict access to specific files and prevent directory traversal.
quote: Content providers can provide access to the underlying filesystem

subject: UIActivityViewController excludedActivityTypes
truth: Stop relying on default exclusion; explicitly exclude all inappropriate activity types when sharing data via UIActivityViewController.
quote: The developers have to be aware of this and **explicitly exclude** the ones that are not appropriate for the app data.

subject: session cookie theft detection false negatives
truth: Do not rely solely on IP-Geo or single-attribute checks to detect cookie theft; they produce both false positives and false negatives.
quote: At the same time, even if the IP-Geo does not change, there is also the possibility that the attacker is attacking from within the same country.
source: OWASP CS: Cookie Theft Mitigation Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Naive Double-Submit Cookie pattern
truth: Stop using the Naive Double-Submit Cookie pattern; use the Signed Double-Submit Cookie pattern with session-bound HMAC tokens instead.
quote: The Naive Double-Submit Cookie pattern is bypassable by an attacker who can write cookies on the target domain
source: OWASP CS: Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Angular bypassSecurityTrustAs*
truth: Stop using Angular's bypassSecurityTrustAs* functions; they disable Angular's built-in XSS protection and require manual sanitization.
quote: - Angular’s `bypassSecurityTrustAs*` functions
source: OWASP CS: Cross Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MAVLink heartbeat messages
truth: Secure heartbeat messages to avoid command injection vulnerabilities; do not send unsigned heartbeat bytes at predictable frequencies.
quote: - You must secure **heartbeat messages** to avoid [command injection vulnerabilities](https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/Command_Injection).
source: OWASP CS: Drone Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: logging email addresses and tokens
truth: Never log verification, reset, or authentication tokens or full verification/reset URLs; mask or pseudonymize email addresses in logs.
quote: Never log verification, reset, or authentication tokens or full verification/reset URLs
source: OWASP CS: Email Validation and Verification in Identity Systems Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Mounting .npmrc as Docker secret instead of COPY
truth: Mount .npmrc as a Docker build secret using --mount=type=secret instead of copying it into the image, and add .npmrc to .dockerignore.
quote: - The `.npmrc` file is mounted as a secret and is never copied into the Docker image.
source: OWASP CS: Node.js Docker Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: trusting system CA store for mobile pinning
truth: Do not rely solely on the system's built-in CA trust store for pinning; an attacker who can update the trust store can defeat pinning.
quote: an attacker with the access to update a trust store will have the access to change the workings of the mobile application, potentially defeating pinning
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SQL injection prevention in Ruby ActiveRecord
truth: Stop interpolating user input into SQL strings; use ActiveRecord's parameterized conditions with ? placeholders or symbol hash syntax.
quote: Parameterized Query Examples
source: OWASP CS: Query Parameterization Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: XML Signature Wrapping via getElementsByTagName
truth: Never use getElementsByTagName to select security-related XML elements; always use absolute XPath expressions after schema validation.
quote: - Never use `getElementsByTagName` to select security related elements in an XML document without prior validation.
source: OWASP CS: SAML Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Azure Key Vault permissions model
truth: Stop granting permissions at the vault level for separate workloads; do create separate Key Vaults per workload and sensitivity level.
quote: This means secrets for separate workloads and separate sensitivity levels should be in separated Key Vaults accordingly.
source: OWASP CS: Secrets Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Reflected XSS via user-controlled timer in JavaScript
truth: Stop passing unsanitized user input directly into setTimeout/setInterval; validate and constrain the delay value to a numeric integer.
quote: Reflected XSS in JavaScript
source: OWASP CS: XSS Filter Evasion Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SameSite cookie default Lax in Chromium
truth: Do not assume cookies without SameSite attribute are sent cross-site; Chromium defaults to Lax, which blocks most cross-site requests.
quote: cookies without SameSite attribute set by default are treated as Lax
source: OWASP CS: Cross-site leaks Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: JWS algorithm 'none' (unsecured JWS)
truth: Reject JWS with alg=none unless the application explicitly accepts a specific unsecured object; never accept unsecured JWS by default.
quote: Implementations MUST NOT accept Unsecured JWSs by default

subject: HTTP trailer field merging
truth: Do not merge trailer fields into the header section unless you understand the field definition and it explicitly permits safe merging.
quote: A recipient MUST NOT merge a trailer field into a header section unless the recipient understands the corresponding header field definition and that definition explicitly permits and defines how trailer field values can be safely merged.

subject: HMAC key lengths below 112 bits
truth: Stop using HMAC keys shorter than 112 bits for generation (disallowed) and verification (legacy use only); use key lengths ≥ 112 bits.
quote: Key lengths < 112 bits Disallowed

subject: Approved security strengths
truth: Only use cryptographic algorithms and key lengths providing security strengths of 112, 128, 192, or 256 bits for federal applications.
quote: The approved security strengths for federal applications are 112, 128, 192, and 256 bits

subject: Compromise-recovery plan
truth: Establish a compromise-recovery plan before the system becomes operational to handle compromised keys, signatures, and encrypted data.
quote: A compromise -recovery plan should be established before the system

subject: password hashing with fast hash (e.g., plain SHA-256)
truth: Stop using fast general-purpose hash functions for passwords; use a key derivation function like PBKDF2 or Balloon with a cost factor.
quote: SHALL be salted and hashed using a suitable one-way key derivation function

subject: CSharpCodeProvider with user input
truth: Do not use CSharpCodeProvider to compile source code that contains any unvalidated user input; this enables arbitrary code execution.
quote: If the application dynamically compiles and runs source code constructed from user input, a malicious user may be able to run arbitrary code.

subject: time-bomb backdoor using file modification timestamp offset
truth: Stop using file modification timestamp offsets as conditional triggers; this pattern matches known backdoor implants like Solorigate.
quote: an offset to a last file modification time is used to conditionally execute a particular block of code

associative: scheduled trigger based on file time

subject: External API usage with untrusted data
truth: Stop calling external APIs with unsanitized untrusted data; validate and sanitize all inputs before passing them to external methods.
quote: Using unsanitized untrusted data in an external API can cause a variety of security issues

subject: Environment variable as command
truth: Stop using environment variables or other user-controlled strings as command names; validate against a whitelist of allowed commands.
quote: takes a shell script that can be changed maliciously by a user, and passes it straight to Runtime.exec without examining it first

subject: Path traversal via path.join with unsanitized query parameter
truth: Stop passing unsanitized req.query data to path.join; validate and sanitize the file path input before constructing filesystem paths.
quote: the result of the path.join will be used as a file path, leading to a path traversal vulnerability

subject: URL encoding is not a defense against SSRF
truth: Stop relying on URL encoding to prevent SSRF; it does not block path traversal or internal endpoint access behind proxies like NGINX.
quote: URL encoding is not a solution in certain scenarios, such as, an architecture build over NGINX proxies.

subject: Flask Jinja2 XSS
truth: Rendering user input through Jinja2 templates without autoescape silently produces unescaped HTML, enabling stored and reflected XSS.
quote: The first view is unsafe as first_name is not escaped, leaving the page vulnerable to cross-site scripting attacks

subject: Regex catastrophic backtracking in Ruby < 3.2
truth: Do not rely on Ruby's regex engine being safe; Ruby < 3.2 has no caching and ambiguous repetitions cause exponential time complexity.
quote: Ruby 3.2 and later have implemented a caching mechanism that completely eliminates the worst-case time complexity for the regular expressions flagged by this query

subject: Password hashing without computational hardness
truth: Use a password hashing algorithm that is slow and/or memory intensive (Argon2, scrypt, bcrypt, PBKDF2) to resist brute force attacks.
quote: Password hashing algorithms should be slow and/or memory intensive to compute, to make brute force attacks more difficult

subject: HTTP header injection via cookie value
truth: Always sanitize or reject CR (\r) and LF (\n) characters from user-controlled input before using it in HTTP headers or cookie values.
quote: does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes CR and LF characters before the data is included in outgoing HTTP headers
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: set a cookie with user input

subject: Exception messages exposing internal configuration details
truth: Log exception details server-side and return a generic error message to the user; never echo exception messages or config file paths.
quote: echo 'Caught exception: ', $e->getMessage()
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Hard-coded password in C authentication function
truth: Stop hard-coding passwords in source code; store password hashes in a configuration file or database with appropriate access control.
quote: hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: TOCTOU file access check then open
truth: Do not check access permissions before opening a file; instead, attempt the open operation directly and handle errors after the fact.
quote: - Implementation The most basic advice for TOCTOU vulnerabilities is to not perform a check before the use.
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: check if file is writable before opening

subject: Uninitialized variable in switch statement (C)
truth: Initialize all variables at declaration and ensure every switch case (including default) assigns all variables used after the switch.
quote: stack variables are not initialized by default
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Logger API defaults to public interpolation
truth: Stop using default Logger interpolation which leaks data; always pass sensitive values with privacy: .private or privacy: .sensitive.
quote: logger.debug: Leaking \(token)

subject: Bean Validation @SafeHtml deprecated
truth: Stop using @SafeHtml; it is deprecated and removed. Use a dedicated HTML sanitization library like OWASP Java HTML Sanitizer instead.
quote: Note that `@SafeHtml`, a previously valid constraint, has been deprecated according to the [Hibernate Validator 6.1.0.Final and 6.0.18.Final release blogpost](https://in.relation.to/2019/11/20/hiberna
source: OWASP CS: Bean Validation Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Bean Validation cascading with @Valid
truth: Apply @Valid on nested bean fields to trigger cascading validation of the object graph; without it, nested objects are not validated.
quote: To validate that graph in one go, apply cascading validation with [@Valid](https://docs.jboss.org/hibernate/validator/9.0/reference/en-US/html_single/#_cascaded_validation)
source: OWASP CS: Bean Validation Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Bean Validation custom error messages
truth: Always specify a message ID in validation annotations and externalize error messages via MessageSource instead of hardcoding strings.
quote: specify a message ID with the validation annotation, so that error messages are customized
source: OWASP CS: Bean Validation Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Least privilege for CI/CD pipeline identities
truth: Adopt a deny-by-default mindset and assign only the minimum permissions necessary for each pipeline identity to perform its function.
quote: One should adopt a "deny by default" mindset.
source: OWASP CS: CI/CD Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: safe HTML attributes list
truth: Only use attributes from the safe HTML attributes list for user-controlled values; for others, ensure JavaScript code cannot execute.
quote: **Safe HTML Attributes include:** `align`, `alink`, `alt`, `bgcolor`, `border`, `cellpadding`, `cellspacing`, `class`, `color`, `cols`, `colspan`, `coords`, `dir`, `face`, `height`, `hspace`, `ismap`,
source: OWASP CS: Cross Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: IaC immutable infrastructure
truth: Do not mutate running infrastructure; provision a new set of infrastructure from updated specifications and decommission the old set.
quote: If a change to a specification is required, then a whole new set of infrastructure is provisioned based on the updated requirements, and the previous infrastructure is taken out of service as obsolete.
source: OWASP CS: Infrastructure as Code Security Cheatsheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: JWT token revocation — denylist keyed on raw token or its hash
truth: Key the token denylist on the 'jti' and 'iss' claims, not on a hash of the raw token, because JWT byte representations are malleable.
quote: Do not use the raw JWT or a hash of it as the denylist key
source: OWASP CS: JSON Web Token Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MCP server tool definitions after approval
truth: Stop allowing MCP servers to change tool definitions after user approval; a rug pull attack swaps a trusted tool for a malicious one.
quote: A server changes its tool definitions after initial user approval, turning a trusted tool malicious
source: OWASP CS: MCP (Model Context Protocol) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SAML schema validation with external schemas
truth: Always use local, trusted copies of schemas for XML validation; never allow automatic download of schemas from third-party locations.
quote: - Never allow automatic download of schemas from third party locations.
source: OWASP CS: SAML Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SAML signature algorithm SHA-1 rejection
truth: Explicitly verify the signature algorithm is at least RSA-SHA-256 and reject SHA-1-based algorithms including rsa-sha1 and hmac-sha1.
quote: Explicitly verify the signature algorithm is at least RSA-SHA-256 (or stronger). Reject SHA-1-based algorithms
source: OWASP CS: SAML Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MLFlow authentication
truth: Enable authentication and access controls on MLFlow and similar model registries to prevent exposure of all models and training logs.
quote: Open MLFlow Instance: No authentication on MLFlow or similar tool exposes all models and training logs.
source: OWASP CS: Secure AI/ML Model Ops Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Treating output from one agent as trusted input to another agent
truth: Treat output from one agent as untrusted input when passed to another agent — prompt injection can propagate across agent boundaries.
quote: Treat output from one agent as untrusted input when passed to another agent
source: OWASP CS: Secure Coding with AI Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Session ID Content Must Be Meaningless
truth: The session ID value must be meaningless and must never include sensitive information or PII; store associated data server-side only.
quote: The session ID content (or value) must be meaningless to prevent information disclosure attacks
source: OWASP CS: Session Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Unvalidated forward via request dispatcher with user input
truth: Stop forwarding to user-supplied paths via getRequestDispatcher; validate the forward target against a whitelist of authorized pages.
quote: The following code is a Java servlet that will receive a `GET` request with a URL parameter named `fwd` in the request to forward to the address specified in the URL parameter.
source: OWASP CS: Unvalidated Redirects and Forwards Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: JWE compression before encryption
truth: Do not compress data before encrypting it in JWE; compression before encryption leaks plaintext information via side-channel attacks.
quote: Compression of data SHOULD NOT be done before encryption

subject: DSA with insufficient key lengths
truth: Stop using DSA with weak key lengths; the security strength depends on the lengths of p and q and proper domain parameter generation.
quote: The security strength that can be provided by the algorithm depends on the length of p (L), the length of q (N), and the proper generation of the domain parameters used.

subject: RSA key transport with modulus n < 2048
truth: Stop using RSA keys with modulus length n < 2048 for key establishment; n = 2048 is the minimum to support 112-bit security strength.
quote: n = 2048, which is estimated to support a security strength of 112 bits

subject: Approved cryptographic algorithms and key lengths
truth: Stop using algorithms and key lengths that are no longer approved by NIST; transition to current approved algorithms and key lengths.
quote: When Algorithms are No Longer Approved

subject: key pair generation per purpose
truth: Generate a separate key pair for each cryptographic purpose (digital signatures, key establishment) rather than reusing one key pair.
quote: Key pairs for asymmetric-key algorithms should be generated for each purpose

associative: separate keys for signing and encryption

subject: TLS versions prior to 1.2
truth: Clients supporting TLS versions prior to 1.2 that do not support TLS 1.3 must support the Fallback SCSV to prevent downgrade attacks.
quote: The Fallback Signaling Cipher Suite Value (SCSV) shall be supported if the client supports versions of TLS prior to TLS 1.2 and does not support TLS 1.3

subject: Password paste and display functionality
truth: Permit paste functionality for password entry and offer an option to display the secret in plaintext to facilitate password managers.
quote: Verifiers SHOULD permit claimants to use “paste” functionality when entering a memorized

subject: VOIP and email for out-of-band authentication
truth: Stop using VOIP or email for out-of-band authentication; methods that do not prove possession of a specific device SHALL NOT be used.
quote: Methods that do not prove possession of a specific device, such as voice-over-IP (VOIP) or email, SHALL NOT be used for out-of-band authentication

subject: Decrypting sensitive data only at the point of use
truth: Decrypt sensitive information only at the point where it is necessary for it to be used in cleartext, minimizing exposure in memory.
quote: decrypt sensitive information only at the point where it is necessary for it to be used in cleartext

subject: OpenSSL Heartbleed missing bounds check
truth: Stop trusting attacker-supplied size values in network protocols without validating they are within the bounds of the source buffer.
quote: payload is supplied as part of the remote request, and there is no code that checks the size of it

subject: reverse DNS lookup for authentication
truth: Stop using reverse DNS lookup results as a security decision; use numeric IP addresses or a cryptographic session mechanism instead.
quote: An attacker can return an incorrect reverse DNS entry for the requesting IP and thus gain the same access as a legitimate user

subject: DataSet/DataTable XML deserialization
truth: Stop using DataSet or DataTable to deserialize untrusted XML input; use a safer serializer or validate input before deserialization.
quote: DataSet and DataTable are in general not safe when populated with untrusted input

subject: dangerous native function calls (P/Invoke)
truth: Stop calling NtWriteVirtualMemory via P/Invoke to write to remote processes; this is a common code injection and backdoor technique.
quote: finds native calls to external functions that are often used in creating backdoors

subject: Stack trace details in user-facing error messages
truth: Do not include server-side file names, SQL code, or other internal details from stack traces in error messages sent to remote users.
quote: The error message at the top of a stack trace can include information such as server-side file names and SQL code

subject: TOCTOU race condition on synchronized objects
truth: Synchronized methods on the resource class do not prevent state changes between separate method calls from the caller's perspective.
quote: it does not prevent a state change in between separate method invocations

subject: Java regex library choice for linear-time matching
truth: Stop relying on Java's default backtracking regex engine for untrusted input; use RE2J or another linear-time regex library instead.
quote: an alternate regex library that guarantees linear time execution, such as Google's RE2J, may be used

subject: NashornScriptEngine with -scripting flag
truth: Stop using NashornScriptEngineFactory.getScriptEngine() with the -scripting flag on user input; it enables arbitrary code execution.

subject: Regex-based HTML tag expansion breaking attribute boundaries
truth: Never use regex to transform HTML tags; the regex can match inside attribute values, breaking attribute boundaries and enabling XSS.
quote: the function is not safe

subject: Hex-decoding function for obfuscated strings
truth: Stop writing functions that hex-decode hardcoded string literals to use as code or import paths; use plain string literals directly.
quote: takes a hard-coded string literal, hex-decodes it and then uses it as an import path

subject: SSRF via path traversal in request URL pathname
truth: Restrict user input in the pathname of the URL so that path traversal ('../') cannot redirect the request to an unintended endpoint.
quote: Restrict the input so that path traversal ("../") cannot be used to redirect the request to an unintended endpoint.

subject: Dynamic code evaluation via Worker with user-controlled URL
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly to the Worker constructor; validate and restrict the URL to a safe allowlist of scripts.
quote: Directly evaluating user input (for example, an HTTP request parameter) as code without properly sanitizing the input first allows an attacker arbitrary code execution.

subject: Werkzeug debugger arbitrary code execution
truth: Stop exposing the Werkzeug debugger in production; the interactive debugger allows arbitrary code execution via the /crash endpoint.
quote: By visiting /crash, it is possible to gain access to the debugger, and run arbitrary code through the interactive debugger

subject: Storing sensitive data in cookies
truth: Stop storing sensitive information in cookies in plain text; encrypt it using the cryptography module or store a lookup key instead.
quote: stores user credentials (in this case, their password) in a cookie in plain text

subject: XSLT injection via user-controlled stylesheet
truth: Stop passing untrusted user input directly as an XSL stylesheet to etree.XML; validate and sanitize all XSL input before processing.
quote: Processing an unvalidated XSL stylesheet can allow an attacker to change the structure and contents of the resultant XML, include arbitrary files from the file system, or execute arbitrary code.

subject: Flask config file default fallback constant
truth: Stop using a constant string as the default fallback in os.getenv() for SECRET_KEY; check and raise if the default is still present.
quote: # if we are loading SECRET_KEY from config files then

subject: Django mark_safe with user-controlled input
truth: Stop passing user-controlled or dynamically-constructed strings to mark_safe(); only mark_safe() hardcoded, trusted string literals.
quote: Use of mark_safe() may expose cross-site scripting vulnerabilities

subject: ssl._create_unverified_context
truth: Stop using ssl._create_unverified_context; use ssl.create_default_context instead to get certificate validation and hostname checks.
quote: behavior that does not validate certificates or perform hostname checks.

subject: malloc with integer multiplication overflow
truth: Check for integer overflow when multiplying to compute buffer size before calling malloc; use calloc or safe multiplication helpers.

subject: Numeric truncation from int to short in C
truth: Never assign a larger integer type to a smaller integer type without explicit range checking; the high-order bits are silently lost.

subject: CBC IV initialization with memset to zero
truth: Always generate IVs randomly using a cryptographically secure random number generator; never use a fixed or zeroed IV with CBC mode.

subject: Session ID generation with predictable seed
truth: Stop seeding Random with user-controlled or low-entropy values like userID; use a cryptographically secure random generator instead.

subject: PRNG seed from time in C
truth: Stop seeding PRNGs with time(); use a cryptographically secure random generator like arc4random() or read from /dev/urandom instead.

subject: XML bomb (billion laughs) entity expansion
truth: Disable XML entity expansion or limit entity expansion depth and count; never parse untrusted XML with DTD entity expansion enabled.

subject: Android insecure random for password generation
truth: Stop using java.util.Random for generating passwords; use java.security.SecureRandom to generate cryptographically strong passwords.

truth: Stop relying on the default (false) for isSecureTextEntry; you must explicitly set isSecureTextEntry = true to mask sensitive input.

subject: iOS logging functions left in release builds
truth: Strip all NSLog, print, dump, debugPrint, and println calls from release builds using preprocessor macros or compilation conditions.

subject: Client-side security logic
truth: Never rely on client-side logic for security or business rules — the user controls the client and can bypass any client-side checks.

subject: Treating extension message senders as trusted by default
truth: Treat all incoming messages as untrusted input and validate sender origin even when the message comes from your own content scripts.

subject: GCC overflow removal
truth: Always add -fno-strict-overflow and -fwrapv to prevent the compiler from removing overflow or wrap checks that your code depends on.

subject: Multi-step login processes enabling user enumeration
truth: Ensure multi-step login processes do not facilitate user enumeration, which can make credential stuffing attacks harder to identify.

subject: window/document named property access for script src
truth: Stop reading script source URLs from window or document named properties; use a hardcoded or validated configuration object instead.

subject: CAPTCHA for DoS defense
truth: Do not rely on CAPTCHAs or input puzzles to defend against DoS attacks; they only prevent functionality abuse like mailbox flooding.

subject: Security questions as sole password reset mechanism
truth: Do not use security questions as the sole mechanism for password reset; answers are frequently guessable or obtainable by attackers.

subject: self-hosted runners on public repositories
truth: Never use self-hosted runners with public repositories; anyone who can fork and open a pull request can execute code on your runner.

subject: application code reading cryptographic keys
truth: Stop having standard application-level code read or use cryptographic keys; all key operations must be done inside the sealed vault.

subject: Shell-form CMD signal forwarding

subject: SAML Unsolicited Response (IdP-Initiated SSO)
truth: Stop using IdP-initiated SSO without CSRF protection; prefer SP-initiated SSO or apply replay detection and RelayState allowlisting.

subject: multiple cookie verification

subject: unpinned third-party dependencies
truth: Stop using unpinned version ranges for third-party dependencies; pin to exact versions or hashes to prevent supply chain compromise.

subject: Diffie-Hellman parameter generation
truth: Do not set custom Diffie-Hellman parameters for TLS 1.2 or earlier; use the supported_groups extension with standard groups instead.

subject: Weak symmetric keys for HMAC JWT signing
truth: Stop using weak symmetric keys (e.g., human-memorable passwords) with HMAC algorithms like HS256; use a key with sufficient entropy.

subject: 112-bit security strength insufficient past 2030
truth: Do not rely on 112-bit security strength for data with a security life extending past 2030; transition to 128-bit security strength.
quote: if the data to be encrypted has a security life of 15 years, then protection at a security strength of 112 bits will not be sufficient, since the 15-year period extends beyond 2030

subject: Certificate chain verification required
truth: Always verify the certificate chain using the CA's public key before trusting a certificate's public key for signature verification.

subject: TLS version 1.3 client must send supported_versions extension
truth: A TLS 1.3 client shall send the supported_versions extension in the ClientHello message; omitting it causes protocol downgrade risk.

subject: Cast before pointer arithmetic on char*
truth: When a char* points to an array of a different type, cast to the correct type before using pointer arithmetic, e.g. (double*)p + k.

subject: PRNG seed from system time

subject: session hijacking response
truth: When session hijacking is suspected, re-authenticate the user or present a CAPTCHA rather than blindly trusting the session cookie.

subject: Secrets in source-controlled config files
truth: Do not store secrets in source-controlled config files; use User Secrets for development and a managed secret store for production.

subject: allow-listing interception proxies

subject: CSRF protection via custom request headers
truth: Stop relying on tokens for AJAX/API endpoints; use a custom request header instead, which browsers will preflight automatically.

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: JavaScript context variable placement
truth: Stop placing variables in arbitrary JavaScript contexts; only place them inside quoted data values and encode using \xHH format.
quote: However, the only ‘safe’ location for placing variables in JavaScript is inside a “quoted data value”.

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: database credentials in source code
truth: Stop storing database credentials in application source code; store them in a protected configuration file outside the web root.
quote: Database credentials should never be stored in the application source code

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: WCF RESTful service HTTP method
truth: Only use HTTP POST to pass requests in RESTful WCF services with TLS enabled; do not use HTTP GET which exposes data in the URL.

source: OWASP CS: DotNet Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MCP tool URL fetching without allowlist validation

subject: RAG document storage without integrity verification
truth: Store document hashes at ingestion and verify them before retrieval; without integrity verification you cannot detect tampering.

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: RAG access control failure
truth: When an access control check fails, return nothing — not a filtered subset — because a failed check may indicate a system error.

subject: SQL injection in WordPress Shopping Cart reqID parameter

subject: RSA signature algorithm selection in TLS 1.3

subject: thick client direct database access
truth: Never connect directly from a thick client to the backend database; always route through an API that enforces access control.

subject: password hashing without a unique salt

subject: exec() with unsanitized user input

subject: session fixation via cookies
truth: Do not store session identifiers in cookies without regeneration on login; doing so creates session fixation vulnerabilities.

subject: Cookies lack scheme isolation

subject: HSTS URI loading must upgrade http to https

subject: PBES2 password length bounds
truth: Use passwords no shorter than 16 octets (PBES2-HS256+A128KW) or 32 octets (PBES2-HS512+A256KW) and no longer than 128 octets.
quote: no shorter than 16 octets and no longer than 128 octets

subject: Asymmetric encryption uses public key, not private key
truth: For asymmetric encryption, encrypt with the public key and decrypt with the private key; do not encrypt with the private key.
quote: the encryption is performed using the public key, and the decryption is performed using the private key

subject: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are only conditionally approved
truth: Stop using TLS 1.0 and 1.1 by default; they are approved only when required for interoperability with non-government systems.
quote: TLS versions 1.1 and 1.0 are approved only when they are required for interoperability with non-government systems

subject: CBC cipher suites require Encrypt-then-MAC extension
truth: When negotiating CBC cipher suites, servers must support the Encrypt-then-MAC extension to mitigate MAC-then-encrypt attacks.
quote: Servers that are configured to negotiate CBC cipher suites shall support this extension

subject: OTP zeroization after use
truth: Stop retaining unencrypted OTP keys, activation secrets, or biometric samples after OTP generation; zeroize them immediately.
quote: SHALL be zeroized immediately after an OTP has been generated

subject: Single-factor software authenticator cloning prohibition
truth: Single-factor cryptographic software authenticators SHALL NOT facilitate the cloning of the secret key onto multiple devices.

subject: Session Management
truth: Embed and verify a session identifier into web requests to prevent CSRF; do not rely on same-origin or referer headers alone.

subject: Pointer type mismatch in C/C++
truth: Cast to the correct type before using pointer arithmetic when the pointer type does not match the actual array element type.

subject: URL redirect host validation
truth: Stop checking only that the URL is absolute; instead, verify the URL is relative or on a known good host before redirecting.

subject: integer overflow in pointer array allocation

subject: PHP file inclusion with user input
