subject: Race condition on static field read from one thread while another thread writes
truth: Synchronize access to shared static fields or use volatile/AtomicInteger to ensure visibility and prevent race conditions.
quote: If two threads of execution use a resource simultaneously, there exists the possibility that resources may be used while invalid
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: start a thread then modify a shared variable
print a static field from a thread
main thread writes while child thread reads

subject: SQL injection via dynamic SQL in PL/SQL
truth: Use bind variables in dynamic SQL (EXECUTE IMMEDIATE ... USING) instead of concatenating user input into the query string.
quote: The product constructs all or part of an SQL command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could mod
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: build query with user supplied values
create stored procedure with sql concatenation
return cursor from procedure with user input

subject: Root detection via PackageManager.getPackageInfo is trivially bypassable
truth: Stop using PackageManager.getPackageInfo to detect root management apps; Magisk and other tools hide from package queries.
quote: context.packageManager.getPackageInfo(packageName, 0)

subject: iOS app logs password hashing algorithm via os_log Logger.debug
truth: Stop logging password hashing algorithms or credential validation internals with os_log Logger.debug in production builds.
quote: logger.debug("Checking password hash: SHA256 algorithm")

subject: Autotools ignoring user CFLAGS/CXXFLAGS/LDFLAGS
truth: Do not rely on Autotools to honor security flags passed via configure; verify and harden the generated makefiles manually.
quote: they often ignore user requests
source: OWASP CS: C-Based Toolchain Hardening Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SameSite cookie attribute for clickjacking defense
truth: Set SameSite=Strict or SameSite=Lax on session cookies to prevent them from being sent when the page is loaded in a frame.
quote: - Preventing session cookies from being included when the page is loaded in a frame using the [SameSite](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Set-Cookie/SameSite) cookie attribute
source: OWASP CS: Clickjacking Defense Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Missing Vary header with Fetch Metadata
truth: Include Vary: Sec-Fetch-Site, Origin to prevent CDNs and proxies from reusing responses across different request contexts.
quote: - If the server responds differently based on HTTP headers (e.g., `Sec-Fetch-Site`, `Origin`), caches must vary on those headers.
source: OWASP CS: Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Error handling - stack trace disclosure
truth: Stop returning stack traces or exception details to the user; return a generic 5xx response and log the error server-side.
quote: Unhandled errors can assist an attacker in this initial phase
source: OWASP CS: Error Handling Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Web Worker eval from user input
truth: Do not create Web Worker scripts from user-supplied input or exchange JavaScript for evaluation via eval() in Web Workers.
quote: Don't allow creating Web Worker scripts from user supplied input.
source: OWASP CS: HTML5 Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: LLM prompt injection — guardrail model choice
truth: Use a purpose-trained classifier as a guardrail, not a general-purpose chat model from the same family as the primary LLM.
quote: A purpose-trained classifier is preferable to a general-purpose chat model from the same family, because the same jailbreak that defeats the primary model is more likely to defeat a guardrail that shares its training and prompt format.
source: OWASP CS: LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: external entity identity propagation via access token reuse
truth: Stop reusing the external access token for internal propagation; issue a signed internal representation structure instead.
quote: this approach is highly insecure due to possible external access token leakage
source: OWASP CS: Microservices Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

associative: propagate access token to internal services
send the same token to backend services

subject: AI-suggested npm packages
truth: Stop blindly installing AI-suggested packages; run npm view <package-name> to verify the package exists before installing.
quote: - Never blindly run `npm install` on packages suggested by AI tools without independent verification.
source: OWASP CS: NPM Security best practices
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: RAG fail-closed design
truth: When retrieval fails, do not answer from model memory alone; return an error indicating the knowledge base is unavailable.
quote: - **Retrieval fails** -- do not answer from model memory alone.
source: OWASP CS: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: REST API access control per endpoint
truth: Stop relying on global or gateway-only access control; perform access control checks at each individual REST API endpoint.
quote: Non-public REST services must perform access control at each API endpoint.
source: OWASP CS: REST Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: CSS file isolation by access control level
truth: Always keep CSS isolated by access control level; each role gets its own CSS file accessible only to users with that role.
quote: always keep the CSS isolated by access control level
source: OWASP CS: Securing Cascading Style Sheets Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: PHP session strict mode
truth: Stop using permissive session management; enable strict mode so the application only accepts session IDs it has generated.
quote: PHP defaults to permissive
source: OWASP CS: Session Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: transaction state transition ordering
truth: Enforce sequential order of transaction authorization steps; prevent users or attackers from skipping or reordering steps.
quote: developers must ensure that the business logic flow for a transaction authorization occurs in sequential order
source: OWASP CS: Transaction Authorization Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: gRPC error responses exposing internals
truth: Return generic error messages to clients while logging detailed information server-side to prevent information disclosure.
quote: Detailed error messages can reveal system internals to attackers. Return generic error messages while logging detailed information server-side.
source: OWASP CS: gRPC Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: JWE compression side-channel
truth: Stop compressing JWE payloads before encryption; compressed ciphertexts reveal plaintext content through size differences.
quote: such compressed data often reveals information about the plaintext

subject: OAuth response type selection
truth: Use the authorization code response type instead of response types that issue access tokens at the authorization endpoint.
quote: authorization code response type instead of response types that cause access token issuance at the authorization endpoint

subject: CBC mode without approved authentication
truth: Do not use CBC mode alone; use an authenticated encryption mode (e.g., GCM, CCM) or pair CBC with a separate approved MAC.
quote: CBC Cipher Block Chaining mode; specified in SP 800-38A

subject: ECB (Electronic Codebook) mode
truth: Do not use ECB mode for encryption; it is insecure because identical plaintext blocks produce identical ciphertext blocks.
quote: Electronic Codebook mode; specified in SP 800-38A

subject: Minimum 112-bit security for all TLS cryptography
truth: All TLS cryptography — certificates, keys, signatures, symmetric algorithms — shall provide at least 112 bits of security.
quote: provide at least 112 bits of security.

subject: shlex::quote/join with control characters for interactive shells
truth: Do not pass shlex::quote output to an interactive shell's stdin; control characters can cause arbitrary command injection.
quote: The `quote` family of functions does not and cannot escape control characters.

subject: OpenSSL Heartbleed buffer overflow
truth: Always validate that the attacker-controlled payload size does not exceed the actual buffer length before calling memcpy.
quote: there is no code that checks the size of it

subject: ASP.NET global error handler
truth: Provide an Application_Error method in global.asax.cs instead of relying on default error pages when customErrors is Off.
quote: provide an implementation of the Application_Error method in the global.asax.cs page

subject: Path traversal validation using normalization
truth: Normalize the resolved path and verify it stays within the intended directory using startsWith with a trailing separator.
quote: check that the resolved path is still contained within that directory

subject: XSLT TransformerFactory
truth: Stop processing untrusted XSLT stylesheets with TransformerFactory.newInstance() without enabling secure processing mode.
quote: Processing unvalidated XSLT stylesheets can allow attackers to read arbitrary files from the filesystem or to execute arbitrary code.

subject: XML parser default configuration
truth: Stop relying on default XML parser settings; explicitly disable DTDs or external entity processing for every parser type.
quote: their default settings enable parsing of external entities

subject: Pug template injection via string concatenation
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into template strings. Pass user data as template interpolation variables instead.
quote: Pug template being constructed from user input, allowing attackers to run arbitrary code via a payload such as #{global.process.exit(1)}

subject: YAML deserialization with arbitrary functions
truth: Avoid deserialization of untrusted data if at all possible; use formats like JSON or XML that cannot represent functions.
quote: Avoid deserialization of untrusted data if at all possible. If the architecture permits it, then use formats like JSON or XML that cannot represent functions.

subject: Flask reflected XSS
truth: Stop directly concatenating user input into HTTP responses; use escape() to sanitize input before writing it to the page.
quote: Directly writing user input (for example, an HTTP request parameter) to a webpage without properly sanitizing the input first, allows for a cross-site scripting vulnerability.

subject: Flask debug mode in production
truth: Stop running Flask applications with debug=True in production; set debug=False or use environment variable FLASK_DEBUG=0.
quote: Running a Flask application with debug mode enabled may allow an attacker to gain access through the Werkzeug debugger

subject: unvalidated integer multiplication for malloc size
truth: Validate that integer dimensions are positive and check for overflow before using them in memory allocation calculations.
quote: does not validate or incorrectly validates that the input has the properties that are required to process the data safely
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: NULL pointer dereference from deferred response.Body.Close before error check
truth: Check the error from client.Do() for nil before deferring response.Body.Close(), or move the defer after the error check.
quote: Check the results of all functions that return a value and verify that the value is non-null before acting upon it
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: ContentProvider SQL Injection via URI path segments
truth: Stop concatenating URI path segments into raw SQL strings; use selection and selectionArgs parameterized queries instead.
quote: Instead of building SQL using string concatenation, use `selection` and `selectionArgs` parameters.

subject: FileProvider with root-path or path="." exposes entire directory
truth: Stop using <root-path> or path="." in FileProvider declarations; declare only the narrowest specific subdirectory needed.
quote: !!! warning "Avoid `<root-path>` and `path=\".\"` declarations"

subject: Encrypt-then-MAC pattern for encrypted stored data
truth: If encrypting stored data, follow the Encrypt-then-MAC pattern: encrypt first, then compute the HMAC over the ciphertext.
quote: If you also encrypt the data, follow the Encrypt-then-MAC pattern: encrypt first, then compute the HMAC over the ciphertext.

subject: horizontal privilege elevation
truth: Stop trusting that an authenticated user can only access their own resources; verify resource ownership on every request.
quote: Horizontal privilege elevation (i.e. being able to access another user's resources) is an especially common weakness that an authenticated user may be able to take advantage of.
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

associative: fetch user by id from params
user can access their own data

subject: assert() in production
truth: Define NDEBUG in release builds so assert() does not abort production programs; auto-abortion is not acceptable behavior.
quote: Live hosts running production code should always define `NDEBUG` (i.e., release configuration), which means they do not assert or auto-abort.
source: OWASP CS: C-Based Toolchain Hardening Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: React dangerouslySetInnerHTML
truth: Stop using dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitizing the HTML first; always sanitize untrusted HTML before injecting it.
quote: - React’s `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` without sanitising the HTML
source: OWASP CS: Cross Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: HTML attribute context without quoting
truth: Stop placing variables in HTML attributes without quoting; always surround with quotes and apply HTML attribute encoding.
quote: **It’s critical to use quotation marks like `"` or `'` to surround your variables.** Quoting makes it difficult to change the context a variable operates in, which helps prevent XSS.
source: OWASP CS: Cross Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: ASP.NET request validation (validateRequest)
truth: Do not disable validateRequest in web.config or page setup; it provides partial XSS protection and should remain enabled.
quote: - Do not disable [validateRequest](http://www.asp.net/whitepapers/request-validation) in the `web.config` or the page setup.
source: OWASP CS: DotNet Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: GraphQL broken authorization
truth: Ensure the API has proper access control checks to prevent broken object-level authorization and excessive data exposure.
quote: - Ensure that the API has proper [access control](Access_Control_Cheat_Sheet.md) checks.
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: IaC artifact signing
truth: Digitally sign artifacts at build time and validate signatures before use to prevent tampering between build and runtime.
quote: Digital signing of artifacts at build time and validation of the signed data before use protects artifacts from tampering between build and runtime
source: OWASP CS: Infrastructure as Code Security Cheatsheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: encrypting passwords instead of hashing
truth: Stop encrypting passwords; hash them using one-way, adaptive hashing algorithms like Argon2id, bcrypt, or PBKDF2 instead.
quote: Passwords should be securely hashed using modern, adaptive hashing algorithms (e.g., Argon2id, bcrypt, or PBKDF2), rather than encrypted or stored in plaintext.
source: OWASP CS: Password Storage Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: REST services lack formal documentation unlike SOAP
truth: Do not expect WSDL/WADL; seek developer guides, source code, or configuration files to determine the REST attack surface.
quote: Formal service description - While for other types of web services such as SOAP a formal description, usually in WSDL is often available, this is seldom the case for REST.
source: OWASP CS: REST Assessment Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Certificate Transparency log monitoring for subdomains
truth: Monitor Certificate Transparency logs for unexpected certificate issuance on your subdomains as an indicator of takeover.
quote: - **Monitor Certificate Transparency logs.** Use services like [crt.sh](https://crt.sh) or [certspotter](https://sslmate.com/certspotter/) to alert on any certificate issuance for your subdomains.
source: OWASP CS: Subdomain Takeover Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: HTTP trailer field generation
truth: Do not generate trailer fields that are necessary for the user agent to receive, because intermediaries may discard them.
quote: a server SHOULD NOT generate trailer fields that it believes are necessary for the user agent to receive

subject: Non-validated cryptographic implementations
truth: Only use implementations of approved algorithms that have been validated and included in validated cryptographic modules.
quote: federal agencies are required to use only implementations of these algorithms that have been validated

subject: RSA key transport and static DH key exchanges
truth: Stop using cipher suites with RSA key transport or static Diffie-Hellman key exchanges; use forward-secret key exchanges.
quote: (HKDF) [37], and the removal of cipher suites that use RSA key transport or static Diffie-

subject: OCSP revocation checking for TLS certificates
truth: TLS servers shall be configured with certificates issued by a CA that publishes revocation information in OCSP responses.
quote: TLS servers shall be configured with certificates issued by a CA that publishes revocation

subject: Certificate validation
truth: Validate the server certificate chain, including revocation status via CRL or OCSP, before establishing a TLS connection.
quote: deny the connection or accept a potentially revoked or compromised certificate

subject: Multi-factor cryptographic software authenticator cloning prevention
truth: Do not facilitate cloning of secret keys onto multiple devices; discourage it through secure storage and access controls.
quote: SHALL NOT facilitate the cloning of the secret key onto multiple devices

subject: Android REQUEST_INSTALL_PACKAGES permission
truth: Do not add REQUEST_INSTALL_PACKAGES permission in the manifest unless your app absolutely requires package installation.
quote: When your application does not require package installations, do not add the REQUEST_INSTALL_PACKAGES permission in the manifest file.

subject: Disabling hostname verification to fix TLS config issues
truth: Do not use an open HostnameVerifier to work around TLS configuration problems; fix the underlying configuration instead.
quote: If you have a configuration problem with TLS/HTTPS, you should always solve the configuration problem instead of using an open verifier

subject: Java regex with ambiguous alternation inside repetition
truth: Stop using ambiguous alternations like (__|.)+ inside repetitions; use ([^_])+ or another non-ambiguous pattern instead.
quote: sub-expression "(__|.)+?" can match the string "__" either by the first alternative "__" to the left of the "|" operator, or by two repetitions of the second alternative "." to the right

subject: splitting shell argument strings on whitespace
truth: Stop splitting shell argument strings on whitespace; arguments may contain quoted whitespace and will split incorrectly.
quote: If given arguments as a single string, avoid simply splitting the string on whitespace. Arguments may contain quoted whitespace, causing them to split into multiple arguments.

subject: Missing array type check before iterating user input
truth: Always check that a user-controlled value is an Array (using instanceof Array) before using its .length as a loop bound.
quote: Either check that the object is indeed an array or limit the size of the .length property

subject: UUID version selection
truth: Stop defaulting to uuid.uuid1() for tokens; uuid.uuid4() is the only version that generates unpredictable random tokens.
quote: all other versions should be avoided

subject: SQL injection in Ruby on Rails ActiveRecord
truth: Passing user input as a hash to ActiveRecord's find_by automatically parameterizes the query and prevents SQL injection.
quote: Using a hash to parameterize arguments

subject: XXE attacks work even when parse results are not returned
truth: Do not assume XXE is safe because parsed data is not returned; DoS and out-of-band data exfiltration are still possible.
quote: Even when the result of parsing is not returned to the user, DoS attacks are still possible and out-of-band data retrieval techniques may allow attackers to steal sensitive data.

subject: Python assert removed under -O flag
truth: Stop using assert for runtime security checks; they are silently removed when Python runs with -O (optimized byte code).
quote: assert is removed with compiling to optimised byte code

subject: Paramiko AutoAddPolicy and WarningPolicy
truth: Stop using AutoAddPolicy and WarningPolicy; they silently accept unknown host keys, defeating SSH identity verification.
quote: automatically trust the unknown host key

subject: SSL certificate verification skipped
truth: Always call SSL_get_verify_result() and check for X509_V_OK before trusting a peer certificate; never skip verification.
quote: does not validate, or incorrectly validates, a certificate
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: PHP cookie value echoed without encoding
truth: Always escape cookie values with htmlspecialchars() before embedding in HTML output; cookie data is attacker-controlled.
quote: improper neutralization of dangerous input
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Internal Activity Communication via Implicit Intent (Java)
truth: Stop using implicit intents for internal activity communication; use explicit intents that specify the target component.
quote: The app uses an implicit intent to start an internal activity

subject: Android cleartext traffic default
truth: Stop setting android:usesCleartextTraffic="true" globally; it allows HTTP exfiltration of data from WebView XSS attacks.
quote: Note that for the exfiltration to work we include the `android:usesCleartextTraffic="true"` attribute in the `AndroidManifest.xml` file to allow cleartext traffic.

subject: WebView javaScriptEnabled
truth: Stop enabling javaScriptEnabled on WebViews that load untrusted content; it allows attacker-injected scripts to execute.
quote: * This is very common in WebViews, unless they only load static content.

subject: NotificationCompat.Builder also leaks PII
truth: Stop using NotificationCompat.Builder with PII in content text; use a non-sensitive message like 'New message received'.
quote: the notification contains sensitive data

subject: iOS ATS Exception Domain Disabling Forward Secrecy
truth: Stop disabling forward secrecy with NSExceptionRequiresForwardSecrecy = false; this weakens TLS security for the domain.
quote:
NSExceptionRequiresForwardSecrecy</key>
					<false />

subject: Auto-approving high-risk agent actions without human-in-the-loop
truth: Require explicit human approval for high-impact or irreversible agent actions instead of auto-approving all risk levels.
quote: Auto-approve low-risk actions
source: OWASP CS: AI Agent Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: OpenSSL build without disabling weak protocols and compression
truth: Stop building OpenSSL with default options; pass -no-ssl2 -no-ssl3 -no-comp to disable broken protocols and compression.
quote: If you configure without the switches, then you will likely have vulnerable code/libraries and risk failing an audit
source: OWASP CS: C-Based Toolchain Hardening Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: CSRF token storage in cookies or browser local storage
truth: Do not store CSRF tokens in cookies or browser local storage; store them in a <meta> tag or JavaScript variable instead.
quote: it is not recommended to store the CSRF token in cookies or browser local storage
source: OWASP CS: Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: document.write with untrusted hash data
truth: Stop using document.write with untrusted data from location.hash; it creates DOM XSS. Use safe DOM manipulation instead.
quote: Vulnerable code
source: OWASP CS: DOM based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Input-based resource allocation
truth: Prevent input-based resource allocation and input-based function/threading interaction to avoid resource exhaustion DoS.
quote: Prevent input based resource allocation
source: OWASP CS: Denial of Service Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: DRF ModelSerializer exclude leads to excessive data exposure
truth: Use Meta.fields (allowlist) instead of Meta.exclude (denylist) in ModelSerializer to prevent exposing unintended fields.
quote: DO NOT use the exclude Meta property
source: OWASP CS: Django REST Framework (DRF) Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: GitHub Actions workflow approval settings
truth: Do NOT use 'Require approval for first-time contributors'; use 'Require approval for all external contributors' instead.
quote: > Please note that the `Require approval for first-time contributors` setting presents a security risk because an attacker can submit an initially legitimate-looking pull request
source: OWASP CS: GitHub Actions Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Laravel cookie encryption middleware disabled
truth: Enable the EncryptCookies middleware in the web middleware group to prevent client-side reading or tampering of cookies.
quote: - Enable the cookie encryption middleware if you use the `cookie` session store or if you store any kind of data that should not be readable or tampered with by clients.
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Source documents deleted but chunks remain searchable
truth: Ensure deleted or de-permissioned source documents are removed from vector stores, response caches, and derived indexes.
quote: Delete source documents while leaving their chunks searchable in the vector store.
source: OWASP CS: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: hardcoded API keys in notebooks
truth: Stop hardcoding API keys and tokens in source code or notebooks; use environment variables or a secrets manager instead.
quote: Hardcoded Secrets – The inclusion of sensitive credentials (e.g., API keys, tokens) in source code or notebooks.
source: OWASP CS: Secure AI/ML Model Ops Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: missing AWS_SESSION_TOKEN check in Lambda Secrets Extension
truth: Fail fast if AWS_SESSION_TOKEN is missing when using the Parameters and Secrets Extension to avoid confusing 4xx errors.
quote: Fail fast if the token is missing to avoid confusing 4xx errors
source: OWASP CS: Serverless / FaaS Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: User IP address leakage via third-party content
truth: Give users the option to block third-party external content (images, avatars, signatures) to prevent IP address leakage.
quote: Preventing leakage of user IP addresses is of great significance when user protection is in scope
source: OWASP CS: User Privacy Protection Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Weak key sizes in certificate paths
truth: Enforce minimum key sizes: reject certification paths with keys or signatures weaker than 2048-bit RSA or 224-bit ECDSA.
quote: certification paths containing keys or signatures weaker than 2048-bit RSA or 224-bit ECDSA are not appropriate

subject: 0-RTT early data replay risks
truth: Do not assume 0-RTT early data is fresh; it is vulnerable to replay and must be treated as idempotent or safe-to-replay.
quote: 0-RTT messages sent in the first flight have the same (encrypted) content types

subject: SHA-1 digital signature generation disallowed
truth: SHA-1 is disallowed for digital signature generation except where NIST protocol-specific guidance explicitly permits it.
quote: SHA-1 may only be used for digital signature generation where specifically allowed by NIST protocol-specific guidance.

subject: Key copy accountability
truth: Stop making uncontrolled copies of keys; every extra copy must be accounted for and destroyed if the key is compromised.
quote: these extra copies need to be accounted for

subject: TLS 1.2 is the minimum approved version
truth: Use TLS 1.2 as the minimum protocol version; it is approved for protecting Federal information when properly configured.
quote: TLS 1.2 is approved for the protection of Federal information when properly configured

subject: Triton VM sponge_absorb_mem instruction
truth: Always include constraints that verify hashed values come from the claimed memory location when using sponge_absorb_mem.
quote: fails to verify that hashed values come from the claimed memory location

subject: String.Format with untrusted format string
truth: Stop passing untrusted input as the format string to String.Format; use a string literal for the format string instead.
quote: Passing untrusted format strings to String.Format can throw exceptions and cause a denial of service

subject: Slash-terminated prefix check for directory containment
truth: When using getCanonicalPath() for directory containment, always slash-terminate the parent path before checking prefix.
quote: ensure that DIR is slash-terminated before checking that DIR is a prefix

subject: SecureASTCustomizer as sole sandbox for Groovy
truth: Stop relying on SecureASTCustomizer alone; it is statically bypassed — add runtime checks and a block-list ClassLoader.
quote: Groovy SecureASTCustomizer is harmful

subject: Suppressing vs logging exceptions server-side
truth: Log the exception message server-side for developers, but send only a generic non-revealing message to the remote user.
quote: log it only on the server

subject: Exception XSS via AJV validation error response
truth: Stop sending AJV validation error messages directly in HTTP responses; they contain user input and cause reflected XSS.
quote: the error message can contain parts of the input

subject: Hard-coded password change
truth: Stop changing passwords to hard-coded values in source code; attackers with source access can observe the new password.
quote: a password is changed to a new, hard-coded value. If an attacker has access to the source code, they will be able to observe the new password.

subject: Ruby on Rails library function documentation for XSS
truth: Document all library functions that construct HTML dynamically as potentially unsafe when they accept untrusted inputs.
quote: document to clients of the library that the function should only be used with trusted inputs

subject: Encrypt before transmission
truth: Encrypt sensitive information before including it in any URL or network transmission; decrypt only at the point of use.
quote: Ensure that sensitive information is always encrypted before being transmitted over the network

subject: snprintf with user-controlled format string
truth: Never pass a user-controlled string as the format argument to snprintf; use snprintf(buf, size, "%s", argv[1]) instead.
quote: Ensure that all format string functions are passed a static string which cannot be controlled by the user
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Privilege elevation without error handling in user directory creation
truth: Always wrap privilege elevation/drop in try/finally blocks to ensure privileges are dropped even when exceptions occur.
quote: raisePrivileges()
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Missing permission check in ContentProvider query method
truth: Add checkCallingPermission() or enforce android:readPermission in the manifest to prevent unauthorized database access.
quote: A secure implementation would either keep the provider non-exported or guard it with a signature-level permission

subject: Exported Deep Link Activity
truth: Stop exporting activities that handle custom URL schemes without validating all query parameters from the incoming URI.
quote: Any app on the device can open it, for example with `mastestapp://transfer?amount=9999999`.

subject: Sensitive data in external storage via MediaStore API
truth: Stop writing sensitive data to MediaStore external storage; use EncryptedSharedPreferences or internal storage instead.
quote: files are not encrypted and contain sensitive data (a password and an API key)

subject: Using unsanitized filenames from ContentProvider
truth: Sanitize filenames from ContentProvider using File(name).name to strip directory components before using as a filename.
quote: A malicious provider can return a path-traversal sequence (such as `../lib/native.so`) that redirects writes outside the intended directory.

subject: Missing backend enforcement of minimum app version
truth: Block API calls from outdated/vulnerable app versions on the backend; do not rely solely on client-side update prompts.
quote: This includes validating whether the backend will stop calls to vulnerable backends and/or whether the vulnerable app-version itself is blocked by the backend.

subject: Debugger detection bypass via Frida/Xposed hooking
truth: Stop relying on single API calls like isDebuggerConnected for anti-debugging; hooking frameworks trivially bypass them.
quote: manipulate the return values of functions such as `isDebuggable` and `isDebuggerConnected` to hide the debugger.

subject: CSRF token in URL or GET request
truth: Do not transmit CSRF tokens in URLs or GET requests; they can leak via browser history, log files, and Referer headers.
quote: A CSRF token must not be leaked in the server logs or in the URL.

subject: checkout with mutable references

subject: LDAP DN and search filter escaping in Java

subject: pinning root CA certificates

subject: HTTP status codes in REST APIs

subject: crypt_decrypt_fail logging

subject: mixing HTTP and HTTPS for session

subject: ASP.NET Core global cookie policy missing HttpOnly

quote: CookiePolicyOptions can be used to set a default policy for cookies

subject: DataSet/DataTable deserialization
truth: Stop using DataSet and DataTable for deserializing untrusted input; use a safer serialization framework instead.
quote: DataSet and DataTable are in general not safe when populated with untrusted input

subject: Dynamic permission string construction from user input
truth: Use fixed permissions for particular actions, rather than generating the permission to check for from user data.
quote: avoid using computation to decide which permissions to check for

subject: Plaintext password in configuration file
truth: Stop storing plaintext passwords in configuration files; always encrypt passwords stored in configuration files.
quote: Storing a plaintext password in a configuration file allows anyone who can read the file to access the password-protected resources.

subject: Authorization check using user-controlled data
truth: Stop using user-controlled data in authorization checks; validate input against a fixed list of expected values.
quote: do not use data that is entirely controlled by that user in the permissions check

subject: LDAP cleartext credential transmission
truth: Stop creating LDAP connections without SSL; the entire communication including credentials is sent in cleartext.
quote: the entire communication to be sent in cleartext making it easier for an attacker to intercept it

subject: SSL certificate verification result commented out
truth: Never comment out or skip the SSL_get_verify_result() check; always validate the return value against X509_V_OK.
quote: does not validate, or incorrectly validates, a certificate
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Java PRNG with hardcoded seed
truth: Never use a hardcoded or constant seed for java.util.Random; use the no-arg constructor or SecureRandom instead.
quote: using the same seed for each initialization will lead to the same output in the same order
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Active Debug Code in HTML Forms
truth: Remove all debug parameters like 'debug=1' from production forms and authentication endpoints before deployment.
quote: Remove debug code before deploying the application
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: add a debug parameter to a form
leave debug flag in login form

subject: WebView shouldInterceptRequest without URL allowlist
truth: Stop overriding shouldInterceptRequest without validating the request URL against an allowlist of trusted hosts.
quote: Every intercepted request falls through to the default loading behavior, including requests to third-party hosts

subject: Notification with PII in setContentTitle/setContentText
truth: Stop putting PII (names, emails, tokens) in notification title or text; use generic placeholders or IDs instead.
quote: the notification contains sensitive data, specifically a first and a last name (PII)

subject: EncryptedSharedPreferences hardcoded secrets
truth: Stop hardcoding secrets in Kotlin source; store them in the Android Keystore or a remote config service instead.
quote: these keys should NOT be hardcoded. They should be stored securely, for instance, in the Android Keystore.

subject: CryptoKit Insecure hashing (SHA1, MD5)
truth: Stop using Insecure.SHA1 and Insecure.MD5 for cryptographic hashing; use SHA256 (SHA256.hash) or SHA512 instead.
quote: Uses of Broken Hashing Algorithms in CryptoKit

subject: Obfuscation detection via APKiD
truth: Stop assuming obfuscation is present; use APKiD to detect obfuscator, packer, and anti-VM indicators in the DEX.
quote: APKiD: Fast Identification of AppShielding Products

subject: SafeBrowsing disabled in Android WebView
truth: Stop disabling SafeBrowsing in WebView; remove the manifest meta-data or the setSafeBrowsingEnabled(false) call.
quote: SafeBrowsing Disabled

subject: SIGTRAP handler for debug assertions
truth: Install a SIGTRAP handler at program startup to safely handle debug assertions without crashing production code.
quote: At program startup, a `SIGTRAP` handler will be installed if one is not provided by another component:
source: OWASP CS: C-Based Toolchain Hardening Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Secrets hardcoded in code repositories or CI/CD config files
truth: Never hardcode secrets in code repositories or CI/CD configuration files; use a secrets management tool instead.
quote: Secrets should **never** be hardcoded in code repositories or CI/CD configuration files.
source: OWASP CS: CI/CD Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: storing sensitive information unnecessarily
truth: Minimise the storage of sensitive information; the best way to protect it is to not store it in the first place.
quote: The best way to protect sensitive information is to not store it in the first place.
source: OWASP CS: Cryptographic Storage Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Unvalidated redirect in .NET
truth: Stop redirecting to a returnUrl without validation; use Url.IsLocalUrl() to ensure the redirect target is local.

source: OWASP CS: DotNet Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: file upload stored filename reference
truth: When storing uploaded files, never reference the original filename in the download; always move/rename the file.
quote: never reference the original filename in the download
source: OWASP CS: Application Logging Vocabulary Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: application logging vocabulary
truth: Log sequence_fail events at CRITICAL level when a user reaches a part of the application out of the normal flow.
quote: When a user reaches a part of the application out of sequence it may indicate intentional abuse of the business logic and should be tracked.
source: OWASP CS: Application Logging Vocabulary Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Object.create(null) for null-prototype objects
truth: When objects are required, create them with Object.create(null) so they don't inherit from the Object prototype.
quote: If objects have to be used then they should be created using the `Object.create(null)` API to ensure they don't inherit from the Object prototype:
source: OWASP CS: Prototype Pollution Prevention Cheat Sheet

subject: RAG document ingestion trusting file extensions
truth: Do not trust document content based solely on file extension or MIME type; scan and validate the actual content.

source: OWASP CS: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SAML Response missing InResponseTo attribute
truth: Always validate that the SAML Response contains an InResponseTo attribute matching the original AuthnRequest ID.

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SAML Response Lifetime and Replay Protection
truth: Prefer short lifetimes and OneTimeUse on the SAML Response to counter replay and browser state exposure attacks.
quote: Prefer short lifetimes on the SAML Response
source: OWASP CS: SAML Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Java SQL query with string concatenation
truth: Stop building SQL queries by concatenating user input; use PreparedStatement with parameterized queries instead.
quote: parameterized queries force the developer to define all SQL code first and pass in each parameter to the query later
source: OWASP CS: SQL Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: unsafe function detection
truth: Stop using eval(), exec(), innerHTML, and document.write; use grep to find and eliminate these unsafe functions.
quote: Find unsafe functions

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: unverified build artifacts
truth: Verify build artifacts against signed checksums before deployment to prevent deployment of compromised binaries.
quote: deployment of compromised binaries

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Authorization code invalidation after first use
truth: Authorization codes MUST be invalidated by the authorization server after their first use at the token endpoint.
quote: authorization codes MUST be invalidated by the authorization server after their first use at the token endpoint

subject: Non-SP 800-56A-compliant key-agreement schemes
truth: Non-SP 800-56A-compliant key-agreement schemes have a termination date; use only DH groups listed in SP 800-56A.
quote: provided a termination date for non-SP 800-56A-compliant key-agreement schemes

subject: Time-based OTP replay resistance
truth: Verifiers SHALL accept a given time-based OTP only once during the validity period to provide replay resistance.
quote: accept a given time-based OTP only once during the validity period

subject: futures_task::waker missing 'static bound
truth: Always ensure the data passed to futures_task::waker satisfies 'static lifetime bound to prevent use-after-free.
quote: did not properly implement a `'static` lifetime bound on the `waker` function

subject: all relational operators in overflow-prone addition check
truth: Stop using any relational operator (>, >=, <, <=) with a+b on one side; restructure to a>c-b to avoid overflow.
quote: >=, <, <= instead of > (all operators)

subject: Division by zero using return value
truth: Stop dividing by a return value without first checking it is not zero; guard with a zero check before division.
quote: Possible cases of division by zero when using the return value from functions

subject: ASP.NET Core cookies without HttpOnly flag
truth: Set HttpOnly = true on CookieOptions when creating authentication cookies to prevent client-side script access.
quote: Cookies without the HttpOnly flag set are accessible to client-side scripts

subject: domain subdomain regex
truth: Anchor domain checks with (^|\.) at the start and $ at the end to correctly match the domain or its subdomains.
quote: When checking for a domain name that may have subdomains, it is important to anchor the regular expression or ensure that the domain name is prefixed with a dot.

subject: Go command injection via sh -c
truth: Stop using exec.Command with sh -c and string interpolation; use exec.Command directly with separate arguments.
quote: It's better to avoid shell commands by using the exec.Command function directly

subject: Go TLS insecure cipher suites
truth: Stop using insecure cipher suites like TLS_RSA_WITH_RC4_128_SHA; only use secure cipher suites from crypto/tls.
quote: TLS_RSA_WITH_RC4_128_SHA is one of the non-secure cipher suites; it's not safe to be used.

subject: Android notifications exposing sensitive data
truth: Stop putting sensitive data like passwords or 2FA codes in notification content; use a generic message instead.
quote: Sensitive information such as passwords or two-factor authentication (2FA) codes should not be exposed in a system notification.

subject: MyBatis XML SQL injection in INSERT with trim
truth: Stop using ${xxx} inside <trim> elements in MyBatis INSERT statements; use #{xxx} parameterized syntax instead.

subject: HTTP response splitting via header injection
truth: Always sanitize user input by removing \n, \r, and : characters before using it in HTTP header names or values.

subject: arbitrary class instantiation during deserialization
truth: Avoid deserialization of untrusted data entirely; use JSON or other safe formats instead of serialized objects.

subject: Creating tempfile using a predictable path
truth: Stop using predictable paths like /tmp/demo2 for temporary files; use ioutil.TempFile or os.CreateTemp instead.

truth: Use java.security.SecureRandom instead of java.util.Random for any security-sensitive random number generation.

source: OWASP CS: Forgot Password Cheat Sheet

subject: buffer overread from fixed-size copy
truth: Copying a fixed large size from a small source buffer causes overread, leaking adjacent memory like passwords.

subject: session secret entropy
truth: Session secrets MUST contain at least 64 bits of entropy and be generated by an approved random bit generator.
quote: contain at least 64 bits of entropy

subject: federation protocol session correlation
truth: There SHALL NOT be any assumption of correlation between CSP and RP sessions when using a federation protocol.
quote: there SHALL NOT be any assumption of correlation between these sessions

associative: CSP session maps to RP session
assume RP session valid if CSP valid

subject: libgit2-sys (git_index_add heap corruption)
truth: Stop using git_index_add on untrusted index data; upgrade to libgit2-sys >= 0.16.2 (bundles libgit2 >= 1.7.2).
quote: * The `git_index_add` function may cause heap corruption and possibly lead to arbitrary code execution.

subject: missing size limit on sprintf write
truth: Replace sprintf with snprintf and pass sizeof(buffer) as the maximum write length to prevent buffer overflow.
quote: Preferably, replace the call to sprintf with snprintf, using the defined constant size of the buffer or `sizeof(buffer)` as maximum length to write.

subject: HttpResponse.Write with untrusted input
truth: Stop passing unsanitized HttpRequest data directly to HttpResponse.Write; encode or validate the input first.
quote: A query string parameter is read from HttpRequest and then ultimately used in a call to the HttpResponse.Write external API

subject: Hard-coded credentials in Go constants
truth: Stop using Go constants (const) to store credentials; use environment variables or a secrets manager instead.
quote: Remove hard-coded credentials, such as user names, passwords and certificates, from source code.

subject: Maven POM Bintray/JCenter repository
truth: Stop using Bintray or JCenter as a Maven repository; use the canonical repository like Maven Central instead.
quote: Bintray and JCenter are shutting down on February 1st, 2022

subject: SecureRandom self-seeding
truth: Do not call setSeed on SecureRandom at all; SecureRandom implementations seed themselves securely by default.
quote: SecureRandom implementations seed themselves securely by default.

subject: Weak digital signature algorithms
truth: Stop using weak algorithms for digital signatures; use strong algorithms like RSA-2048 or ECDSA with SHA-256.
quote: If a weak algorithm is used for digital signatures, an attacker may be able to forge signatures and impersonate legitimate users

subject: CSV formula-sensitive metacharacters
truth: Prefix entries starting with =, +, -, or @ with a single quote to neutralize formula injection in CSV output.
quote: Risky characters include =(equal), +(plus), -(minus), and @(at)

subject: JWT without key verification
truth: Stop decoding JWTs without verifying the cryptographic signature; always validate the key to prevent CWE-347.
quote: the key isn't verified in the process

subject: Using AES.GCM encryption for network transmission
truth: Use AES.GCM.seal() with a SymmetricKey to encrypt sensitive data before sending it over a network connection.
quote: decrypt sensitive information only at the point where it is necessary for it to be used in cleartext

subject: os.chmod with world-executable permissions (S_IXOTH)
truth: Stop using os.chmod with stat.S_IXOTH or any mask granting world execute; restrict permissions to owner-only.
quote: a HIGH warning is reported if a file is set world write or executable

subject: template.HTML() with variable input
truth: Stop passing untrusted variables to template.HTML(); only use template.HTML() with hardcoded string literals.
quote: Using a variable to initialize could potentially be dangerous

subject: text/template ExecuteTemplate with user data
truth: Stop using text/template.ExecuteTemplate with unsanitized user data; switch to html/template or escape input.
quote: text/template

subject: strcpy into fixed buffer
truth: Use strncpy() with a size limit and ensure null-termination, or use snprintf()/strlcpy() instead of strcpy().
quote: copies an input buffer to an output buffer without verifying that the size of the input buffer is less than the size of the output buffer

copy user input to local buffer

subject: strcpy stack buffer overflow
truth: Replace strcpy with strncpy or snprintf with explicit bounds checking to prevent stack-based buffer overflow.
quote: Do not use dangerous functions such as gets. Use safer, equivalent functions which check for boundary errors.
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

associative: copy a string into a buffer

subject: CBC IV with hardcoded zero bytes in Java
truth: Always generate IVs randomly using SecureRandom; never hardcode a fixed IV like all-zero bytes with CBC mode.
quote: predictable initialization Vector (IV) with Cipher Block Chaining (CBC) Mode
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: SHA-1 password hashing without salt
truth: Use a salted, adaptive hash function (like bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2) instead of a raw SHA hash without salt.
quote: does not also use a salt as part of the input
license_note: © The MITRE Corporation, reproduced with permission (CWE)

subject: Shared UserDefaults for App Group secrets
truth: Stop storing credentials, tokens, or keys in shared UserDefaults; use a shared Keychain Access Group instead.
quote: Store credentials, tokens, and keys that both the app and an extension need in a shared Keychain Access Group (the `keychain-access-groups` entitlement), not in shared `UserDefaults` or a shared file

subject: Bean Validation constraint placement
truth: Always use @Valid annotation when passing models across application layers to trigger validation constraints.
quote: you must add validation constraints

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: XSS defeating CSRF protections
truth: Remember that Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) can defeat all CSRF mitigation techniques; always prevent XSS first.
quote: Remember that Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) can defeat all CSRF mitigation techniques!
source: OWASP CS: Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet

subject: Login CSRF vulnerability
truth: Do not assume login forms are immune to CSRF; generate pre-session tokens and include them in the login form.
quote: Most developers tend to ignore CSRF vulnerabilities on login forms as they assume that CSRF would not be applicable on login forms because user is not authenticated at that stage, however this assumption is not always true.
source: OWASP CS: Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Using document or window for global variables
truth: Do not use document or window objects for storing global variables, as they can be clobbered by DOM elements.
quote: Avoid using objects like `document` and `window` for storing global variables, because they can be easily manipulated.

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Ad hoc queries in Entity Framework
truth: Stop building ad hoc queries in Entity Framework; they are just as susceptible to SQL injection as plain SQL.
quote: building your own ad hoc queries in Entity Framework is just as susceptible to SQLi as a plain SQL query
source: OWASP CS: DotNet Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Wi-Fi Management Frame Protection on drones
truth: Enable 802.11w Management Frame Protection (MFP) to prevent Wi-Fi deauthentication attacks against the drone.
quote: - Use **802.11w MFP (Management Frame Protection)** to prevent Wi-Fi deauthentication attacks.
source: OWASP CS: Drone Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: ZigBee encryption and key rotation
truth: Enable AES-128 encryption on ZigBee and deploy network keys with frequent rotation to prevent key compromise.
quote: - This has a way to enable **AES-128 encryption** to secure transmissions.
source: OWASP CS: Drone Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SQL injection via string concatenation
truth: Use Query Parameterization with PreparedStatement instead of concatenating user input into SQL query strings.
quote: Use *Query Parameterization* in order to prevent injection.
source: OWASP CS: Java Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: container updates in Kubernetes
truth: Do NOT directly update running containers; always update the source image and redeploy using rolling updates.
quote: Try to avoid direct updates to the running containers as this can break the image-container relationship.
source: OWASP CS: Kubernetes Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: MCP server binding to 0.0.0.0
truth: Bind MCP HTTP/SSE servers to specific interfaces (e.g., 127.0.0.1), never 0.0.0.0 unless explicitly required.

subject: Node.js Mongoose mass assignment without field filtering
truth: Use _.pick() with a whitelist of safe fields to prevent mass assignment of protected attributes like isAdmin.

subject: Android SharedPreferences for sensitive data
truth: Stop storing sensitive data in SharedPreferences; use Android Keystore or EncryptedSharedPreferences instead.

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Bearer token audience restriction
truth: Bearer tokens must be restricted to a single audience (Resource Server) to limit the impact of token leakage.
quote: bearer tokens must be restricted to a single audience (Resource Server) to limit the impact of token leakage.

subject: Retrieved content injected without delimiters
truth: Wrap retrieved content in delimiters that instruct the model to treat it as untrusted data, not instructions.
quote: Implement retrieved content delimiters that the model is instructed to treat as untrusted data, not instructions.
source: OWASP CS: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: input validation in code review
truth: Check for missing server-side validation, improper sanitization, and weak input filtering during code review.
quote: Check for missing server-side validation, improper sanitization, and weak input filtering
source: OWASP CS: Secure Code Review Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: force session logout on browser close
truth: Use JavaScript to capture browser tab/window close events and terminate the session, emulating manual logout.

source: OWASP CS: Session Management Cheat Sheet

subject: JWT algorithm confusion / none attack
truth: Stop accepting 'alg':'none' or any unverified algorithm from the JWT header; enforce a server-side allowlist.
quote: "enc" header specifies the same algorithm that is used for the

subject: Request processing before complete headers
truth: A server MUST NOT apply a request to the target resource until it receives the entire request header section.
quote: server MUST NOT apply a request to the target resource until it receives the entire request header section

subject: AES as preferred block cipher
truth: Use AES (AES-128, AES-192, or AES-256) as the preferred block cipher for new products instead of DES or TDEA.
quote: TDEA and is the preferred block cipher algorithm for new products.

subject: DSA (Digital Signature Algorithm)
truth: Stop using DSA for digital signatures; its approval has only been proposed for legacy retention, not new use.
quote: approval for DSA has been proposed to FIPS 186-5

subject: Downgrade protection in TLS 1.3
truth: Do not override or strip the downgrade protection bytes in the server random value when implementing TLS 1.3.
quote: TLS 1.3 implementations include a downgrade protection mechanism embedded in the random value

subject: TLS client certificate EKU extension
truth: Include id-kp-clientAuth in client certificates used for TLS authentication; omit it from other certificates.
quote: the id-kp-client-auth key purpose OID should be included in the certificates to be used for TLS client authentication and should be omitted from any other certificates

subject: subscriber-provided authenticator strength
truth: Assume the weaker authenticator strength unless the stronger can be verified with the issuer or manufacturer.
quote: the CSP SHOULD assume the use of the weaker authenticator unless it is able to establish that the stronger authenticator is in fact being used

subject: DataSet/DataTable deserialization
truth: Stop using DataSet and DataTable with untrusted input; they are not safe even with default type limitations.
quote: DataSet and DataTable are in general not safe when populated with untrusted input

subject: Path traversal defense via prefix check
truth: When checking for a trusted prefix, also verify that path traversal ("..") is not used to erase that prefix.
quote: check for a trusted prefix, ensuring path traversal is not used to erase that prefix

subject: JSZip decompression bomb
truth: Check the uncompressedSize field on the internal _data object before extracting any user-supplied zip entry.
quote: check uncompressedSize Object Field before extraction

subject: URL substring host check
truth: Stop checking if a host string is a substring of a URL; parse the URL and check the parsed hostname instead.
quote: treating the URL as a string and checking if one of the allowed hosts is a substring of the URL is very prone to errors

subject: LDAP connection without SSL
truth: Always set use_ssl=True when creating an LDAP Server object, otherwise the connection defaults to cleartext.
quote: The first one sets use_SSL to true as a keyword argument whereas the second one fails to provide a value for it, so the default one is used (False).

subject: Ruby Marshal.load deserialization
truth: Stop using Marshal.load on untrusted data; there is no safe way to deserialize untrusted data using Marshal.
quote: there is no safe way to deserialize untrusted data using Marshal

subject: Returning dangling raw pointer from function returning *const T
truth: Stop returning raw pointers to stack-local values; use Box<T> to heap-allocate and return ownership instead.

subject: Unbounded accept loop with fork per connection
truth: Limit the number of concurrent child processes or use a worker pool instead of forking for every connection.

subject: Exported deep link handler activity
truth: Set android:exported="false" on deep link handler activities that do not need to be reachable by other apps.

subject: iOS NSLog sensitive data logging
truth: Stop using NSLog for production logging; wrap NSLog in a DEBUG-only macro or use os_log with privacy levels.

subject: Browser extension XSS via innerHTML
truth: Stop using innerHTML with unsanitized user input; use textContent or sanitize with DOMPurify to prevent XSS.

subject: X-Frame-Options via meta tag
truth: Stop using meta tags for X-Frame-Options; set the X-Frame-Options directive as an HTTP Response Header only.

subject: missing upgrade-insecure-requests during HTTP to HTTPS migration

subject: Forgot password URL token generation using Host header
truth: Stop using the Host header to build reset URLs; hard-code the URL or validate against a trusted domain list.

source: OWASP CS: Forgot Password Cheat Sheet

subject: REST attack surface hidden from UI inspection
truth: Do not rely on UI inspection alone; collect full requests using a proxy to discover the REST attack surface.
quote: Collect full requests using a [proxy](https://www.zaproxy.org/) - while always an important pen testing step, this is more important for REST based applications as the application UI may not give clue
source: OWASP CS: REST Assessment Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: SAML certificate lifetime maximum
truth: Set SAML signing certificate maximum lifetime to two years; do not ignore the certificate's validity period.
quote: the maximum lifetime of a SAML signing certificate should be two years

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Docker secrets at build time
truth: Never hardcode secrets using docker ENV or docker ARG commands; let the orchestrator inject them at runtime.

subject: dependency version pinning
truth: Stop pulling unpinned dependency versions; use lockfiles to pin dependencies to a specific verified version.
quote: limit the applications dependencies to a specific version that has been previously verified as legitimate and secure

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: wildcard certificate scope
truth: Never use a wildcard certificate for systems at different trust levels; limit wildcard scope to a subdomain.
quote: Never use a wildcard certificates for systems at different trust levels
source: OWASP CS: Transport Layer Security Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: virtual patching false positives
truth: Do not ever block legitimate traffic under any circumstances — virtual patches must have no false positives.
quote: **No false positives** - Do not ever block legitimate traffic under any circumstances.

license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Version negotiation in TLS 1.3
truth: Do not use the legacy_version field for version negotiation; use the 'supported_versions' extension instead.
quote: Version negotiation is performed using only the handshake versions

subject: CCM mode is an approved authenticated encryption alternative
truth: Use AES CCM (SP 800-38C) for authenticated encryption when GCM is not suitable; avoid unauthenticated modes.
quote: SP 800-38C, Recommendation for Block Cipher Modes of Operation: the CCM Mode for Authentication and Confidentiality

subject: 0-RTT data
truth: Do not accept 0-RTT data by default; if allowed, implement single-use ticket replay protection per RFC 8446.

subject: Pre-shared keys (PSK) in TLS prior to 1.3
truth: Do not use pre-shared keys in TLS versions prior to TLS 1.3 or for initial session establishment in TLS 1.3.

subject: password entropy estimation
truth: Stop using entropy-based complexity estimation for user-chosen passwords; use length-based approach instead.
quote: estimating the entropy for user-chosen passwords is difficult and past efforts to do so have not been particularly accurate

subject: NULL DACL grants full access

truth: Always verify that user input is safe before passing it to a command execution function like Process.Start.

subject: Header checking disabled in HttpClient

subject: DataContractSerializer with DataSet/DataTable

subject: Java regex Pattern.compile with ambiguous repetitions

subject: JWT encoding with None algorithm

subject: Flask debug mode

subject: math/rand/v2 as a drop-in for crypto/rand

truth: Block public read access on S3 buckets containing sensitive data and enable server-side encryption at rest.

subject: Unclosed SQLite cursor triggering StrictMode
truth: Always close SQLite cursors in a finally block or use use() to avoid resource leaks detected by StrictMode.

subject: Deep link parameter bounds checking
truth: After type conversion, verify numeric deep link values fall within acceptable limits before acting on them.

truth: Stop using ECB mode or omitting the mode entirely; always specify a secure mode like GCM or CBC with an IV.

subject: Django CSRF protection

subject: file upload storage naming

truth: Do not configure TLS 1.1 for government-only applications; servers should not be configured to use TLS 1.1.

subject: Elliptic curve support in TLS

subject: AAL3 hardware-based authenticator requirement
truth: Use a hardware-based authenticator that provides verifier impersonation resistance for AAL3 authentication.

subject: IaC secrets management
truth: Stop storing secrets in plain text files or SCMs like Git; use dedicated secrets management tools instead.

subject: Secure cookie flag
truth: Mark all cookies with the 'Secure' attribute to prevent them being sent over unencrypted HTTP connections.

source: OWASP CS: Transport Layer Security Cheat Sheet

subject: authorization check on redirect targets
truth: Ensure the supplied redirect value is valid, appropriate for the application, and authorized for the user.

subject: XML maxOccurs unbounded
truth: Set a finite maxOccurs instead of unbounded to prevent denial-of-service from extreme numbers of elements.

truth: Do not leak padding or MAC validation failure timing; implement constant-time comparison for the HMAC tag.
quote: But see Section 11.5 of [JWE] for security considerations on thwarting timing attacks.

subject: Header field order preservation
truth: A proxy MUST NOT change the order of field line values with the same field name when forwarding a message.
quote: proxy MUST NOT change the order of these field line values

subject: DES (Data Encryption Standard)
truth: Do not use DES; it is a deprecated, withdrawn standard and must not be used for any cryptographic purpose.

subject: 0-RTT data replay protection

subject: Truncated HMAC with variable-length padding
truth: The Truncated HMAC extension shall not be used in conjunction with variable-length padding due to attacks.
quote: Truncated HMAC extension shall not be used in conjunction with variable-length padding

subject: Device unlock as authentication factor
truth: Do not consider device unlock (PIN/biometric to unlock a smartphone) as one of the authentication factors.

subject: Multi-factor cryptographic software authenticator activation memorized secret

subject: challenge nonce length for cryptographic device authenticators
truth: Stop using challenge nonces shorter than 64 bits; the challenge nonce SHALL be at least 64 bits in length.
quote: The challenge nonce SHALL be at least 64 bits in length

subject: ASP.NET Core global cookie security policy

subject: Go HTTP client TLS verification

subject: Missing @Valid on controller parameters

source: OWASP CS: Bean Validation Cheat Sheet

subject: Docker --privileged flag
truth: Do not run containers with the --privileged flag; it adds ALL Linux kernel capabilities to the container.

subject: secrets: inherit in reusable workflows
truth: Eliminate secrets: inherit when calling reusable workflows; explicitly pass each required secret instead.
quote: When using the `inherit` keyword while invoking a reusable workflow, all the calling workflow’s secrets (organization, repository and environment secrets) are passed to the called workflow, even if th
source: OWASP CS: GitHub Actions Security Cheat Sheet

subject: npm 2FA enforcement
truth: Enable 2FA with auth-and-writes mode for npm accounts to protect package publishing and token management.

subject: TLS for authorization responses
truth: Authorization responses must not be transmitted over unencrypted network connections; use end-to-end TLS.

subject: SAML RelayState Open Redirect
truth: If the RelayState parameter is a URL, validate it against an explicit allowlist to prevent open redirect.

subject: DBMS OS account privileges
truth: Stop running your DBMS as root or system; change the DBMS's OS account to one with restricted privileges.

subject: storing tokens in browser local storage
truth: Do not store tokens in insecure locations like local storage in a browser; use secure, HTTP-only cookies.

subject: hardcoded secrets in ML notebooks
truth: Never hardcode secrets in source code or notebooks; use secret managers or environment variables instead.

source: OWASP CS: Secure AI/ML Model Ops Cheat Sheet

subject: CSRF token validation argument order
truth: Pass the token ID as the first argument and the token value as the second argument to isCsrfTokenValid().

subject: XML parser recovery mode for malformed documents
truth: Disable recovery mode in XML parsers; never allow parsers to attempt recovery of malformed XML documents.

source: OWASP CS: XML Security Cheat Sheet

subject: Permanent admin rights
truth: Remove always-on administrative privileges; provide elevated access only when needed for a specific task.

subject: HSTS host noting for IP addresses
truth: Do NOT note a host as a Known HSTS Host when the host substring matches IP-literal or IPv4address syntax.

subject: TLS record layer version number
truth: Stop using TLSPlaintext.legacy_record_version for any purpose; it MUST be ignored by all implementations.

subject: 0-RTT data with older servers
truth: Do not send 0-RTT data unless you know the server supports TLS 1.3; older servers will fail to handle it.

subject: Security strength below 112 bits
truth: Stop using cryptographic algorithms and key lengths that provide less than 112 bits of security strength.

subject: SHA-1 for non-digital-signature applications
truth: SHA-1 is acceptable only for non-digital-signature applications that do not require collision resistance.
quote: For non-digital-signature applications, the use of SHA-1 is acceptable for applications that do not require collision resistance.

subject: TDEA (Triple DES) authenticated-encryption
truth: Do not use TDEA for authenticated-encryption; no authenticated-encryption mode has been defined for TDEA.

subject: SHA-224 minimum for certificate signatures

subject: External API sink with untrusted data

truth: Use a regular expression literal or double-escape backslashes when building RegExp from string literals.

subject: malloc using sizeof without accounting for element type size
truth: Always multiply by sizeof(element_type) when allocating arrays; malloc(3) allocates 3 bytes, not 3 ints.

subject: Race condition on shared static variable without synchronization

subject: Double Free via dangling pointer reuse
truth: After freeing a pointer, set it to NULL immediately so that subsequent free() calls are harmless no-ops.

truth: Stop writing sensitive files to the shared container without encryption; apply NSFileProtectionComplete.

truth: Configure the application to deny access by default when no access control rules are explicitly matched.

subject: Pre-session to real session transition

subject: DOMPurify sanitization without SANITIZE_NAMED_PROPS

subject: Environment variables for secrets

subject: code signing verification

subject: DNS records pointing to deleted cloud resources

subject: HSTS superdomain expiry modification

subject: RSA key transport without OAEP

subject: confirmation code expiration
truth: Set confirmation codes sent by non-postal means to expire at a maximum of 10 minutes, not hours or days.

subject: bzero for clearing secrets
truth: Stop using bzero to clear sensitive buffers; use memset_s, SecureZeroMemory, or explicit_bzero instead.

subject: SSRF hostname construction

subject: scanf %s into fixed buffer

subject: SBOM generation timing

source: OWASP CS: DOM Clobbering Prevention Cheat Sheet

subject: Symmetric key size less than 128 bits
truth: Stop using symmetric encryption keys smaller than 128 bits; use at least 128-bit symmetric keys.

subject: Kernel.send with user input in Ruby
truth: Stop using Kernel.send with user-controlled method names or arguments; call the method directly.

subject: TLS MinVersion on Go 1.18+

subject: HTTP URL connection for sensitive data
truth: Use HTTPS (https://) instead of HTTP when transmitting sensitive data over a network connection.

subject: Absolute Path Traversal in Python

subject: key rotation deferred until after compromise

subject: XmlDocument in .NET prior to 4.5.2

truth: Stop using TLS 1.0 and 1.1; use TLS 1.2 or 1.3.

subject: Hardcoded credentials in mobile app
truth: Do not hardcode credentials in the mobile app.
quote: Do not hardcode credentials in the mobile app.

subject: UIWebView usage in iOS apps
truth: Stop using UIWebView; use WKWebView instead.
quote: The test fails because the app uses the deprecated `UIWebView` class.

subject: Using TripleDES cipher via cryptography library
truth: Stop using TripleDES; replace with AES.
quote: Use of insecure cipher or cipher mode. Replace with a known secure cipher such as AES.

subject: Django DEBUG mode in production
truth: Never run DEBUG = True in production.
quote: - Ensure that the application is never in `DEBUG` mode in a production environment.
source: OWASP CS: Django Security Cheat Sheet

subject: Sensitive data via SMS
truth: Avoid sending sensitive data via SMS.
